The design also reduces the input and output current ripple. Until a certain state of charge is reached, the charger runs in continuous current mode. A PIC microcontroller provides precise control and safe operation, while a flyback converter creates electrical isolation between the input and output.

title: SkewFuse: Unsupervised Confidence-Guided Rank Fusion for Multi-View Image Retrieval
authors: Enis Teper, Mustafa Keskin, Emre Rençberoğlu, Yusuf Hüseyin Şahin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: In image retrieval systems, the use of multiple embedding models to capture diverse visual features often improves retrieval performance. However, traditional rank aggregation methods generally assume uniform reliability across retrieval models, neglecting the variability in model confidence across different queries. This paper introduces SkewFuse, an unsupervised rank fusion approach that dynamically weights retrieval results based on statistical skewness of similarity score distributions, effectively capturing query-specific confidence. We extend the Dowdall positional voting method by integrating these confidence weights, achieving adaptive and robust fusion. Evaluations conducted on the In-Shop Clothes Retrieval dataset demonstrate that SkewFuse consistently outperforms existing unsupervised and supervised fusion methods, notably improving early precision metrics, including a 4.7% relative gain in Recall@1 over the strongest baseline. SkewFuse offers an efficient, parameter-light solution ideal for real-world applications where top-ranked retrieval precision is critical.

title: MediVault - Your Medical Wallet
authors: R. Jegatha, Jayani Nirupa V, Saivarshaa S, Krithika G
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In critical care scenarios, timely access to accurate patient data is paramount. This paper introduces MediVault, a comprehensive, end-to-end digital solution designed to securely consolidate and manage electronic health records (EHRs). Developed as a cross-platform mobile application using Flutter with a Supabase backend, MediVault centralizes a patient's medical history, including diagnoses, prescriptions, and reports–enabling healthcare professionals to retrieve essential information swiftly. The system employs device-based biometric authentication to uphold stringent privacy standards. By providing a unified platform with role-based access for patients, doctors, and administrators, MediVault reduces data fragmentation and improves continuity of care. The core objective is to support medical personnel with immediate access to vital health data, thereby minimizing clinical errors and improving patient outcomes in both emergency and routine healthcare settings.

title: Wireless Electric Vehicle Charging Powered by Solar Monitoring
authors: Malathika A, Dharun P.K, M. Shyamalagowri
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The fast adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) has created an urgent need for more efficient, available, and sustainable charging solutions. This paper proposes a novel prototype system that integrates Dynamic Wireless Electric Vehicle Charging with a Solar-Powered Monitoring Mechanism, aiming to revolutionize how EVs are powered in real-time, in particular in urban and highway environments. A microcontroller-primarily based transmission unit is powered thru a solar-assisted battery financial institution, ensuring that the strength supply remains smooth, renewable, and impartial of traditional grid systems. The middle charging mechanism employs switching circuits to manipulate the inductive energy drift correctly, with system parameters which includes voltage, modern, and strength switch fee being monitored continuously. A real-time comments machine and show interface enhance visibility and manipulate, at the same time as a secondary embedded unit within the car manages reception and safety. Furthermore, remote monitoring competencies are included via Wi-Fi conversation protocols to ensure clever diagnostics, device indicators, and performance analytics. This prototype addresses key boundaries in conventional plug-in and static wireless charging systems by presenting flexibility, sustainability, and scalability. Field-stage validation of the prototype demonstrates solid performance and promising energy switch performance, marking a huge step towards clever transportation ecosystems. The integration of sun electricity with dynamic Wi-Fi charging affords a feasible and eco-friendly course forward for the future of electric mobility.

title: Electricity Rate Analysis for Remote Alaskan Microgrid Communities: Impact of Renewable Energy Integration and Electrification
authors: J. R. Yadav, I. Mathiasson, B. Panikkar, M. Almassalkhi
abstract: Alaska’s remote communities face some of the nation’s highest electricity rates due to reliance on diesel generation, prompting state through the Power Cost Equalization (PCE) program and growing interest in renewable energy and tribal Independent Power Produce (IPP) models. This paper presents an analysis of the complex dynamics of electricity rates within this context, focusing on how renewable penetration (α), IPP sales rate (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IPP</inf>), electrification, and fixed costs influence retail rates (R) and stakeholder outcomes. This analysis identifies combinations of α and R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IPP</inf> that keep retail rates within the PCE threshold ($/kWh) and demonstrates strategies to bring the retail rate back into the eligible range when it exceeds the PCE threshold. The key finding is that when renewable energy is procured at a price lower than fossil-fuel generation, it benefits both the utility and the state by reducing overall production costs and lowering the use of PCE funds, while simultaneously generating revenue for tribally owned IPPs. This collaborative framework promotes cleaner operations, enhances system efficiency, and stabilizes retail rates against fluctuations in diesel fuel prices.

title: Comparing Patent Network Approaches for Extracting Technology Intelligence
authors: Leehee Kim, Sungjoo Lee
abstract: Patent network offers advantages for extracting technology intelligence, such as analyzing technology trend and identifying emerging technologies. Despite the significant value of patent network methods, few studies compare similarity measures used to construct a network, which can have a substantial impact on analysis results. Because technology intelligence extraction depends on contextual relevance, understanding the unique characteristics of each similarity measure enables more appropriate and effective insights. This study first conducts a network structure analysis using multiple patent similarity measures. Basic network statistics and comparisons of network similarity are performed, along with sensitivity analyses that vary similarity measurement levels and threshold settings to examine how network characteristics change under different analytical conditions. The study then compared three similarity measures—citation-, classification-, and text-based approaches—across two perspectives: patent clustering and key patent identification. For clustering, we further evaluate robustness, assessing stability across hyperparameter configurations and consistency of pairwise assignments. The findings provide guidance on the selection of patent network construction strategies for analytical objectives.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Cooperative Control for Nonlinear Multiagent System With State and Control Input Constraints and Guaranteed Convergence Performance
authors: Zipeng Cui, Gang Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: The cooperative tracking control problem for the multiagent systems with unknown dynamic models, state constraints, control input constraints, optimal performance index, and convergence rate constraints is investigated in this article. A novel design framework is proposed to cope with these constraints and requirements. More specifically, the mean value theorem is utilized to transform the control input constraint to the unconstrained form. The performance function combined with a barrier Lyapunov function is leveraged to achieve the guaranteed transient tracking performance, finite-time convergence, and state constraints. To satisfy the optimal performance index, while considering the unknown dynamics of agents, we employ the actor–critic neural network architecture to get the near-optimal solution. Our control scheme is completely model-free. The rigorous Lyapunov stability analyses show that the full-state constraints and control input constraints are always satisfied, and the cooperative tracking errors can be made as small as possible in a finite time at a desired decay rate by appropriately setting the parameters in the performance function and barrier Lyapunov function. Finally, the simulation and hardware tests are conducted to clarify the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy.

title: Secure Synchronization of Complex Dynamic Networks via Adaptive Event-Triggered Control Against Dual-Channel Cyber Threats
authors: Zhong Yuguang
abstract: This article develops an adaptive event-triggered control (AETC) scheme for achieving global synchronization in discrete-time complex dynamical networks (CDNs) subject to Markovian switching with partially unknown transition probabilities (TPs). The study establishes an enhanced CDNs model that simultaneously accounts for nonlinear dynamics, time-varying delays, and probabilistic switching uncertainties. A novel resilient control architecture is designed to mitigate dual-channel cyber threats, including Bernoulli-distributed denial-of-service (DoS) attacks and deception attacks occurring in both sensor-to-controller and controller-to-actuator communication links. The proposed AETC mechanism introduces dynamically adjustable triggering thresholds that respond to both network state variations and attack intensity levels. Through the construction of advanced Lyapunov–Krasovskii functionals, delay-dependent stability conditions are derived, culminating in tractable linear matrix inequality (LMI) formulations for synchronization and explicit controller synthesis. Numerical results demonstrate superior performance in communication efficiency and attack resilience.

title: U-NEXT: A User-Centered New Prototype for Walking Excavator Simulator
authors: Long Cheng, Gilberto Mazzola, Samuel Knüsel, Michael Schreiner, Andreas Kunz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The walking excavator is a highly complex construction machine, characterized by challenges in control, instruction, and operational safety. In this work, we implemented a virtual reality (VR) walking excavator simulator that integrates real hardware components with kinematic physics simulation. Using the simulator, we conducted two user studies. The first involved 45 participants and examined the effects of user perspectives on performance and preference. The second involved 12 participants and investigated the influence of video passthrough of the controllers. Results show that the choice of perspective should be task-dependent. Third-person view outperformed first-person view in the undercarriage task in which complex leg movements are needed. It supported better planning and allowed complete body visualization. However, it did not reduce motion sickness and was less preferred than first-person view for training. From the second user study, passthrough significantly improved controller learning compared to animated controllers, with participants making fewer errors when using the passthrough design. Based on these findings, we provide design recommendations for user perspectives and controller representation in VR-based training simulators.

title: Interaction Under Whole-Body User Rotations in VR Space_supp1-3680596.pdf
authors: Filip Skola
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) enables interactions beyond real-world limitations, yet many such capabilities remain underexplored due to concerns about user comfort and increased disorientation and cybersickness. This study examined how altering the user's virtual pitch orientation influences interaction performance and subjective ratings. In a within-subject design, 30 seated participants experienced 12 virtual tilts from moderate to extreme (±180°). We assessed comfort, simulator sickness, perceptual responses, and performance. Our results revealed no significant increases in nausea, disorientation, or decreased comfort, with mild tilts performing equivalently to baseline. Notably, even extreme tilts maintained low nausea levels. Performance effects were mixed: forward tilts yielded similar or slightly improved performance, whereas backward tilts modestly impaired performance, though not significantly. These findings suggest that VR experiences featuring virtual body orientations distinct from the user's physical posture are feasible without compromising comfort or performance, enabling novel opportunities in simulation, interface design, visualization, creative content creation, and VR gaming.

title: Passive Fingerprinting of ICS Devices Using Aggregates of Modbus Packets
authors: Andres Galiasso, Peter J. Hawrylak
abstract: Industrial Control Systems (ICS) serve as the foundation for the operation of industrial processes by monitoring and controlling physical systems. Fingerprinting of ICS devices is a crucial first step in maintaining the security of industrial networks. Active scanning may have adverse effects on an industrial network, so using passive fingerprinting is important. In this paper, we propose a technique for fingerprinting ICS devices that utilize the Modbus protocol. Our technique is based on analyzing an aggregate of Modbus query and response packets within a fifteen minute window. The Modbus registers accessed are recorded for each Modbus query, and the Shannon Entropy and hexadecimal sums of chosen segments of the message are calculated for each Modbus response packet. The distribution of these values are used to create 17 statistical features that a multinomial logistic regression machine learning algorithm uses to fingerprint the ICS devices. Two experiments were performed to evaluate the fingerprinting model. The model achieved an accuracy score of 90.55% in classifying a set of five devices during our most challenging experiment.

title: Design and Validation of a YOLOv8 Synthetic Data Generation Pipeline for Surface Defect Detection in Manufacturing
authors: Armin Moghadam, Sahib Bhatia, Fatemeh Davoudi Kakhki, Hiroyuki Ichikawa
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Surface defect detection is essential for maintaining quality in manufacturing as it helps ensure product reliability and reduces material waste. While AI-driven visual inspection in manufacturing offers promising automation, to achieve high performance, it demands large, diverse and annotated datasets, which are often impractical to collect specially in low-volume or additive manufacturing contexts. Traditional defect detection methods relying on manual inspection and physical sensors can be labor-intensive, costly, and subject to variability. This study introduces a validated scalable pipeline for generating synthetic image datasets tailored for AI-based surface defect detection. Our pipeline integrates 3D modeling in Blender, procedural defect simulation, randomized rendering, and annotation generation to produce synthetic datasets with controlled variations in lighting, camera angles, and defect morphology. Cylindrical components were selected as the test geometry due to their industrial relevance and predictable curvature for defect propagation. To validate the pipeline, synthetic images were used to train a YOLOv8-based instance segmentation model, which was then applied, without retraining, to images of 3D printed physical parts. The trained model achieved 88% accuracy and 90% precision on the real object test dataset, demonstrating strong generalization and validating the realism and utility of the generated synthetic data. This research provides a scalable, low-cost approach for developing AI inspection systems without the need for labor-intensive real-world data collection. By bridging simulation and deployment through 3D printed physical validation, this pipeline contributes a practical foundation for synthetic-data-driven quality assurance in smart manufacturing environments.

title: Intelligent Air Quality Forecasting and Pollution Prediction using Hybrid Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Rajarajeshwari B, Sankari S, Harivel GK, Muthupandi G, S. Pavithra
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Urban air pollution is the next global emerging problem thrown in by rapid industrialization, urbanization, and booming vehicular emissions. A novel machine learning-based model for the estimation of air quality and prediction of pollution in support of sustainable environmental management for smart city infrastructure is proposed. The model performs temporal dependency analyses and multi-horizon forecasting of pollutant levels using the Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT). The model first applies Random Forest algorithms to identify significant environmental drivers and secondly uses CatBoost to determine pollution severity class levels with a superior rationale regarding complex interactions of features. As a multi-algorithm model, it deals better with noisy, incomplete, and non-linear IoT sensor network data with reasonably high prediction accuracy with regard to traditional models. It provides policymakers and city planners with real-time actionable inferences from which they may decide most efficiently for pollution abatement. Experimental results show substantial improvements in forecast accuracy of R2=0.9809. By integrating advanced machine learning techniques to real-time data streams, this study takes strides toward envisioning more intelligent and healthy cities and thus advocates sustainable policies that will attempt to stem the environmental and health implications of air pollution.

title: Universal Onboard Complex of an Unmanned Ground Vehicle
authors: I. A. Pestova, A. A. Gorbenko, V. M. Zuev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The article provides a detailed analysis of the onboard systems of autonomous transport vehicles. The architecture “sensors-computing-actuators” is examined, and the operating principles as well as the trade-offs in selecting various types of sensors (lidars, radars, cameras) are analyzed in detail. Special attention is paid to AI-based computing platforms and software stacks for perception, prediction, and path planning. Key technological challenges are identified, such as ensuring fault tolerance and handling “edge cases”.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Approach for Fraud Prevention and Health Insurance Claim Verification
authors: Talari Srinivasa Rao, Myneedi Chetan Viswa Kumar, Janapati Malathi, Shaik Mujahid
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Insurance fraud is a loss for both the insurers and the honest insured. To counter this, a hybrid model was created combining XGBoost and SVM, supported by Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) for extracting key information from claim documents and accelerating the verification process. In order to deal with the class imbalance problem, both SMOTE and random undersampling were implemented which in turn improved the performance of the model. The overall accuracy was 98.48% in the case of the developed system. For the legitimate claims, it achieved 98% precision, 99% recall, and a 99% F1-score, while for the fraudulent claims the scores were 99% precision, 97% recall, and 98% F1-score. The AUC and ROC score of 0.98 indicates the robustness of the model and reveals its strong potential for real-time, scalable fraud detection.

title: Robot Learning Framework using Behavioral Cloning and Gaussian Mixture Model (BC-GMM) for Sorting Tasks
authors: Hambal Tella, Prajyot Patil, Maria Kyrarini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: As collaborative robots (cobots) become available in industry, it is crucial to enable non-coders to program cobots easily. For example, in sorting tasks that may require adjustments over time, workers could easily adjust the cobot’s behavior by utilizing learning from demonstrations (LfD) approaches. This paper presents an LfD approach for enabling a cobot to learn sorting tasks by integrating the Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM), validated in Mujoco for policy training. Quantitative analysis shows that the improved BC-GMM achieves the lowest mean squared error (MSE = 0.00207) and highest R-squared score (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.89), outperforming other methods, such as standard BC, diffusion policy, and implicit behavioral cloning (IBC).

title: Full-Wave Simulation and Experimental Validation of Antennas Using Complex RF Components
authors: Anthony Eldridge, Alastair Wiegelmann, Samuel Drake, Shengjian Jammy Chen
abstract: Full-wave antenna simulations typically exclude complex Radio Frequency (RF) components because accurately modeling their intricate performance is challenging. However, this work demonstrates an approach to include accurate modeling of complex RF components in full-wave antenna simulation using both Touchstone<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> data (S-parameters) provided by the supplier and a Computer Assisted Drawing (CAD) model with material properties which can provide a more comprehensive antenna performance prediction. As an example, we illustrate the process using a half-wave dipole antenna fed by a transformer-based balun, where the manufacturer's multi-port Touchstone<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> file (S-parameters of a 4 port network) for the transformer is incorporated into a full-wave simulation workflow in conjunction with a CAD file of the transformer with appropriate material properties applied. Good agreement between simulation and the measured results of an antenna prototype proves that the proposed approach efficiently predicts complex antenna behavior and supports reliable design of advanced antenna structures.

title: Intelligent Control Strategies for EV Motor Drives: A Comparative Study of BLDC and PMSM Systems
authors: Sujatha Banka, Chava Sunil Kumar, Arakala Harshitha, Theegala Anupriya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Electric Vehicles (EVs) mainly use Brushless DC (BLDC) motors and Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM) because of their features like efficient, reliable, and compact. Motor drives that use Fuzzy logic controllers (FLCs) can make sure optimal energy use. They provide smooth acceleration and under various operating conditions improves efficiency. EVs use FLCs to control BLDC and PMSMs. FLC offers an optimised solution with different principles, which removes the need for complex mathematical models. In BLDC motors, FLC improves speed control and reduces torque ripples, which will make the operation smoother and more stable. For PMSM, FLC works together with direct torque control (DTC) to reduce torque ripples and improving response times and overall efficiency. MATLAB/Simulink software supports modelling and simulation. Simulations and real-time tests show that the FLC-based control system is more efficient than traditional Proportional-Integra (PI) controllers. It responds very fastly to changes in motor behavior, ensuring reliable, flexible, and energy-efficient performance. This makes FLC the best choice for the future of electric vehicles, ensuring the best motor performance in various conditions and also improves overall vehicle efficiency and durability

title: The Future of VLSI Testing: Deep Learning Models for Accurate Fault Detection – Review
authors: Balkees Marliyya S, Saumiya S, Kirubasankar K
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The growth in digital VLSI circuits has increased the complexities in fault detection. Traditional methodology such as ATPGs and SAT-based algorithms suffer from space explosion and limited scalability fails to detect the faults such as hardware Trojans. Enhancement in machine learning especially in deep learning algorithms such as SSAE (Stacked sparse autoencoder), and DSAE promises effective fault detection. These models guarantee efficient feature extraction, test pattern recognition and efficient fault classification in sequential and combinational circuits. The main objective of this paper is to review and compare machine learning-based fault detection, deep learning-based fault detection with conventional method in digital VLSI. It highlights their comparative strength, limitation, scalability, research gaps and methodology used in each method. The novelty of the paper lies in its structured comparison of unsupervised, semi-supervised and supervised models for fault detection.

title: Deep Reinforcement and Federated Learning-Based Joint Spectrum and Power Allocation in D2D-Enabled Cellular Networks
authors: Srinivasarao Yarlagadda, Telaprolu Dhanushyasri, Kolakani Hansika Bose, Lebaka Kyathi, Thati Jaya Sarayu, Ajay Bhardwaj
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In underlay cellular networks supporting Device-to-Device (D2D) communication, efficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex> esource allocation remains a key challenge due to spectrum sharing and crosstier interference. This work proposes a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-based framework for joint spectrum and power allocation aimed at maximizing system throughput while limiting interference to cellular users (CUs). The network environment is modeled as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) that captures essential wireless characteristics, including path loss, interference, and signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR). Two DRL algorithms, namely Deep Q-Networks (DQN) and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), are implemented using the Stable Baselines3 framework to dynamically assign resource blocks and transmission power levels to D2D pairs in a shared spectrum setting. Simulation results demonstrate that the PPO-based agent consistently outperforms DQN and baseline allocation schemes in terms of average throughput, SINR distribution, energy efficiency, and convergence stability. Overall, the proposed DRL-based approach achieves an effective trade-off between throughput maximization and interference mitigation under dynamic network conditions. While federated learning is discussed as a promising direction for distributed and privacy-preserving optimization, this study focuses on centralized DRL-based resource allocation and leaves federated extensions for future investigation.

title: MADE-PI: Framework for Effective Anomaly Detection in Federated Learning Applications
authors: Raman Zatsarenko, Sergei Chuprov, Dmitrii Korobeinikov, Leon Reznik, Adrian Peña
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: In this research, we address a critical vulnerability of Federated Learning (FL) in practical applications: their susceptibility to adversarial attacks from malicious participants, which can severely degrade a global model’s performance. We propose MADE-PI, a novel defense mechanism designed to identify and exclude malicious clients early in the training process. Our method introduces a pre-aggregation step where the server evaluates each client update using a Proportional-Integral (PI) score. This score is a unique distance-based metric inspired by the PID controller, where a proportional term P measures the immediate deviation of a client’s update, and an integral term I tracks its behavior over time. By analyzing the distribution of these scores, our technique effectively flags and removes malicious contributions. We validate our approach through an empirical study on three datasets representing various applications of FL, including intelligent transportation, medical image analysis, and handwriting recognition. We concentrate on data poisoning as a highly relevant class of attacks. The results demonstrate that MADE-PI outperforms conventional defenses by providing superior malicious client detection and improving both learning efficiency and final model accuracy.

title: SmartLib: Next-Generation IoT Access Control for Libraries
authors: Sushant Naik, Swarup Magar, Sneha More, Sakshi Murade, Tejas Pandure, N.S. Patankar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: In response to the limitations of the traditional library system, which is still supervised manually, this paper presents SmartLib, an IoT architecture for an automated library management system. The system’s primary feature is an automated access control system which is secure and RFID enabled and uses a solenoid lock to free the access control system from manual entry logs. To assist the administrators, SmartLib provides an analytics dashboard which is robust and presents real-time visitor information and provides usage reports on resources. Also, the framework enables students to access an online portal to browse the library catalogue and remotely reserve books which are still on the library stack. The effectiveness of SmartLib shows a large improvement in operational efficiency, security and resource management in academic libraries.

title: Design and Analysis of a Stack-Enhanced LECTOR-Based Power Gating Scheme with PMOS Sleep Transistors
authors: Mahesh Bidarkote, M. Suresh, Deep Chandra Upadhyay, Ankur Beohar, Pallabi Sarkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a novel hybrid leakage reduction scheme named SL-PG (Stack-Enhanced LECTOR with PMOS-only Power Gating) for CMOS digital circuits. By synergistically combining Forced Stack, LECTOR, and PMOS sleep transistor techniques, the proposed approach achieves superior leakage suppression in deep-submicron regimes. Through comprehensive SPICE simulations on 2 -input NOR gates and 4:1 multiplexer in 90nm technology, we analyze static/dynamic power, total dissipation, and Power-Delay Product (PDP). Results demonstrate that SL-PG achieves 99.47% leakage reduction <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.00369 \mu ~\mathrm{W})$</tex> for NOR gates with optimal PDP of 1.78 fJ for multiplexers. Forced Stack offers 99.1 % leakage suppression with moderate overhead, while LECTOR increases power by 167 % in NOR gates. Based on extensive analysis, we provide design guidelines for selecting appropriate techniques across applications.

title: Enhancing Earthquake Prediction with Ensemble Learning: A Hybrid XGBoost and LightGBM Approach
authors: Manni Kumar, Nivedita Sharma, Mahatava Saxena, Samaira Handa, Tamanna Sarawag, Tamanna Kashyap
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Anticipated prediction of earthquakes is also a critical component of disaster management that requires accurate and timely forecasts to minimize the possible damages. The study adopts a two-machine learning system that comprises of XGBoost and LightGBM to attain better accuracy in the process of predicting earthquakes. The data of historical seismic activity is merged with seismic parameters such as magnitude and depth and location-based information which was acquired at Kaggle. The combination of XGBoost and LightGBM functions in an ensemble model allows more sophisticated seismic patterns to be detected that eventually adds value to the prediction quality. When ensemble learning approaches are applied, the results of improved generalization and reduced overfitting issues are achieved, which in turn allows the application in the real world. The mixture of XGBoost and LightGBM works in the ensemble model permits to rent finer seismic patterns and is more enhanced than individual models in prediction. Ensemble learning through the assistance of a special preprocessing pipeline of unequal seismic data and strong hyperparameter optimization that improve generalization and reduce overfitting. The quick detection of earthquakes that will be evident through the assistance of this strategy will assist the authorities in charge of disaster management in undertaking pre-emptive preventive measures in order to minimize the number of victims and safeguard infrastructure. As has been shown, the proposed ensemble outperforms the classic machine learning baselines, and it means that the methods of AI- based ensemble are applicable in the real world when it comes to earthquake prediction.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Dynamic Queue Management Approach for Network Traffic Scheduling
authors: Xiaoliang Zhang, Yinan Dou, Mei Yan, Shuyu Han, Chenyang Xu, Jian Wu, Bei Ou, Yitong Liu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: With the growing scale and heterogeneity of network traffic, efficient network traffic scheduling has become a critical challenge in modern communication systems. Existing methods often show limited adaptability under resource constraints and dynamic traffic conditions. This paper proposes a deep reinforcement learning-based dynamic queue management approach for network traffic scheduling, termed DRL-DQM, which formulates traffic scheduling as a queue-level sequential decision-making problem to achieve multi-dimensional performance optimization. Extensive simulations under diverse traffic loads and bandwidth configurations demonstrate that DRL-DQM significantly outperforms baseline methods in queueing latency and packet loss rate. Moreover, the proposed approach exhibits strong stability and robustness across different network scenarios.

title: On the Problem of Interpretability of Neural Algorithms for Predicting Microscopic Traffic Flow Data
authors: Mikhail Gorodnichev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper develops and experimentally validates a method for interpreting driver behavior based on inertial sensor data. The relevance of the study stems from increasing road safety requirements and the development of telematics services and driver monitoring systems. A dataset consisting of multidimensional accelerometer and gyroscope time series was generated. Preprocessing was performed, including feature standardization, sequence segmentation into observation windows, and preparation of training samples. An interpretable algorithm for aggregating classifier predictions is proposed. Based on the distribution of recognized maneuvers, it generates a final driving style assessment based on threshold rules. The developed approach can be applied in telematics, driver monitoring, and behavioral insurance, and can also serve as a basis for further expansion with additional data sources and adaptation to various operating conditions.

title: H-OTFS: An Anti-Jamming Transmission Scheme for OTFS via Hadamard Spreading
authors: Yongning Zhuo, Yuhan Qiu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Aimed to enhance the ability of robust transmission in jamming environment for OTFS system, a novel scheme termed H-OTFS is proposed, which spreads the sparse user data using Hadamard transformation to Delay-Doppler (DD)domain. At the receiver, after converting the received signal into the DD domain, a screening process is employed to excise DD domain bins contaminated by jamming signals whose power exceeds a predefined threshold. The original user data are subsequently recovered from the partial observation using a multi-constraint compressive sensing (CS) with Alternating bidirectional multiplier algorithm (ADMM). Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly outperforms conventional OTFS systems under strong dense jamming conditions, achieving a substantial reduction in bit error rate.

title: Joint Radio-Computing Resource Allocation Modeling for Next-Generation Networks
authors: Tammam Z. Dawood, Hanin N. Karhili, Mikhail S. Stepanov, Ghanshyam Singh, Artwell R. Muzata
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Next-generation networks face the challenge of efficiently managing both radio and computational resources, especially under the demands of bandwidth-intensive services such as $\mathbf{4 K}$ video streaming. This paper presents a joint radio and computational resource allocation model for a single $\mathbf{5 G}$ access node equipped with Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC), serving homogeneous 4K-video streaming sessions. The model describes the interplay between finite radio and computing resources, deriving expressions for steady-state probabilities, blocking probability, and average resource utilization. A recursive algorithm is proposed to determine the minimal radio and computing resources required to meet a target quality of service (QoS). Numerical experiments illustrate the applicability of the model, showing how blocking probability varies with traffic load and resource availability. The results provide a practical framework for planning next-generation access nodes with joint resource constraints.

title: One-Bit Consensus of Controllable Linear Multi-Agent Systems With Communication Noises
authors: Ru An, Ying Wang, Yanlong Zhao, Ji-Feng Zhang
abstract: This paper addresses the one-bit consensus of controllable linear multi-agent systems (MASs) with communication noises. A consensus algorithm consisting of a communication protocol and a consensus controller is designed. The communication protocol introduces a linear compression encoding function to achieve a one-bit data rate, thereby saving communication costs. The consensus controller with a stabilization term and a consensus term is proposed to ensure the consensus of a potentially unstable but controllable MAS. Specifically, in the consensus term, we adopt an estimation method to overcome the information loss caused by one-bit communications and a decay step to attenuate the effect of communication noise. Two combined Lyapunov functions are constructed to overcome the difficulty arising from the coupling of the control and estimation. By establishing similar iterative structures of these two functions, this paper shows that the MAS can achieve consensus in the mean square sense at the rate of the reciprocal of the iteration number under the case with a connected fixed topology. Moreover, the theoretical results are generalized to the case with jointly connected Markovian switching topologies by establishing a certain equivalence relationship between the Markovian switching topologies and a fixed topology. Two simulation examples are given to validate the algorithm.

title: ERB-TransUNet: An Enhanced Residual Transformer-Based UNet for Accurate Polyp Segmentation
authors: Rohan Vaghela, Avani Khokhariya, Jigar Sarda, Tae Soo Yun, Mangal Sain
abstract: Worldwide, colorectal cancer ranks as the third most prevalent malignancy, making the reliable detection and segmentation of colon polyps crucial for early intervention and prevention. Although advances in endoscopic imaging have improved visualization, manual polyp identification remains labour-intensive and prone to inter-observer variability, highlighting the need for robust automated methods. Hence, this study conducted comparative experiments on Kvasir-SEG using several state-of-the-art (SOTA) segmentation architectures such as UNet, DeepLab, UNet++, FCN Transformer and introduces an Enhanced Residual Block with Transformer by modifying the UNet architecture (ERB-TransUNet). The Enhanced Residual Block consist of the Channel and Spatial Attention with the Squeeze and Excitation (SE) block which enhances the feature extraction capabilities of the model. To promote transparency and interpretability, study further complements the model with Grad-CAM visualizations and feature-extraction maps, offering insights into the regions and patterns that drive each prediction. When evaluated on Kvasir-SEG, a standard benchmark for polyp segmentation, ERB-TransUNet achieved a mean IoU of 86.39%, a Dice coefficient of 92.58%, and an accuracy of 96.19%.

title: Lower-limb exoskeleton trajectory and structure co-optimization for maximum assistive efficiency
authors: Tianyi Sun, Zhenlei Chen, Qing Guo, Yao Yan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Improving assistance efficiency is paramount for booster exoskeleton design, requiring comprehensive consideration of the human-exoskeleton dynamics, with coupling mechanics playing a central role. This study proposes a purely simulation-based co-optimization framework that simultaneously considers both trajectory and structure parameters of the exoskeleton to maximize assistive efficiency under various coupling conditions. The framework integrates a two-layer optimization strategy for identifying coupling parameters and employs a cooptimization scheme based on the Neighborhood Field Optimization algorithm, utilizing surrogate models for inverse dynamic analysis. Four coupling models across five coupling positions and three surrogate models were examined. The nonlinear coupling model and Bayesian Neural Network surrogate achieved superior accuracy. Optimal exoskeleton segment lengths found to be shorter at varying degrees along the shank, but slightly longer at the thigh compared to corresponding human segment lengths. Furthermore, the optimized results significantly enhanced assistance efficiency, with the linear coupling model achieving torque reductions of 29.65% (knee) and 69.29% (hip). The most effective coupling positions were identified at 70% (shank) and 60% (thigh) of segment lengths. By simultaneously optimizing both the structure and trajectory parameters of the exoskeleton within the proposed framework, effective assistance to the wearer can be achieved. This study offers valuable insights and design strategies for the development and optimization of booster exoskeletons, contributing to enhanced human-exoskeleton coupling and performance.

title: LiveMatte: Dynamic Scene Background Restoration and Selective Portrait Patch Enhancement
authors: Jiayu Sun, Zhanghan Ke, Lihe Zhang, Huchuan Lu, Rynson W.H. Lau
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Real-time and accurate portrait matting in videos is a challenging problem in computer vision research. Recent approaches have explored incorporating prior conditions for accurate inference. Notably, some methods ask the user to provide the background image, which requires extra effort from the user to capture the background image and is limited to videos with static backgrounds only. We note that real-time video motion segmentation methods often train a background model to detect the foreground. Our insight of this work is that if we can directly restore the background content from the input video as a prior, we may be able to achieve more precise portrait matting. In addition, this approach could potentially work even with dynamic backgrounds, without requiring additional user input. However, automatically restoring the background content is not straightforward due to the difficulty in distinguishing between foreground and background. While it may seem that stationary pixel values represent the background, these values can vary across frames. To this end, we propose a novel dynamic scene background restoration (DSBR) module that learns a background model by accumulating background content from each input video frame. It restores the current background content, which serves as a matting prior for alpha prediction of the subsequent frame. DSBR is extremely lightweight and can be easily integrated into existing matting models. Based on it, we present a real-time portrait video matting framework, LiveMatte. To more efficiently process high-resolution videos, we also introduce a selective portrait patch enhancement (SPPE) module. Extensive experiments and user studies demonstrate that our method is better and faster than existing methods.

title: A Two-Stage RF-SVM Model Against SSDF Attack in Cooperative Spectrum Sensing
authors: Renjie Yu, Jun Wu, Hongru Zhang, Rui Zhao, Chenpu Li, Yanrong Zhai, Fan Li, Xiaorong Xu, Jianrong Bao
abstract: With the increasing use of wireless devices, available spectrum resources are becoming increasingly precious. Cooperative spectrum sensing (CSS) is a key technology in cognitive radio (CR) networks. It enhances spectrum utilization efficiency by enabling multiple secondary users (SUs) to jointly detect the spectrum occupancy of the primary user (PU). However, its inherent data fusion mechanism makes it vulnerable to spectrum sensing data falsification (SSDF) attack. To enhance the system’s SSDF fault tolerance, this paper proposes a novel two-stage hybrid malicious user (MU) detection model integrating random forest (RF) and particle swarm optimization-support vector machine (PSO-SVM), denoted as RF-SVM. This model first employs an RF for preliminary screening of SUs, followed by support vector machine (SVM) for fine-grained classification, thereby combining the strengths of both algorithms. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed RF-SVM model outperforms both the PSO-SVM and standalone RF models, as well as advanced methods such as the enhanced support vector machine (ESVM) and isolation forest-spectral clustering (IFSC), in terms of both detection accuracy and false alarm control. This model maintains stable performance even under scenarios with high-intensity attacks and limited training samples, while also achieving efficient computation. These advantages fully verify its effectiveness, robustness and practical application value.

title: Wideband Low-RCS Phased Array Antenna Based on Hybrid Reduction Mechanism
authors: Xin Yue, Jinbo Liu, Qingxin Guo, Fei Liu, Zengrui Li
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A novel integrated phased array is proposed to achieve wideband low radar cross section (RCS) and maintain its high-gain scannable beam, addressing the trade-off between RCS reduction and radiation performance. It adopts a hybrid reduction mechanism that combines phase cancellation and wave absorption. Each element comprises a resistive metasurface (RM), an antenna, and a stripline-based optimum load impedance network. The structural-mode and the antenna-mode scattering fields of the element have nearly equal amplitudes and are 180° out of phase, which lead to phase cancellation and thereby achieves in-band RCS reduction. The RM exhibits an absorption–transmission–absorption frequency characteristic to reduce out-of-band RCS. An 8×8 array is designed and fabricated, with a 30.77% 10-dB impedance matching bandwidth (5.5–7.5 GHz). Measured results show a peak gain of 22.4 dBi and a scanning capability of ±45° in both xoz- and yoz-planes, demonstrating no degradation in key radiation performance. Meanwhile, under normal incidence, the 7-dB RCS reduction bandwidth is 96.1% (3.3–9.4 GHz) for x-polarization and 47.6% (3.7–4.9 GHz and 7.8–9.5 GHz) for y-polarization.

title: Retraction Notice: Home: IOT based home automation using NFC
authors: P. Vagdevi, Divya Nagaraj, Golla Vara Prasad
publish: 2017 International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: One of the emerging technologies in today's World having spread its usefulness in a wide variety of domains is near-field communication. From automation of student's attendance monitoring to the ticketing system, it has been widely accepted across many countries. Its main feature lies in automation like automating car for locking/unlocking car doors, switching in GPS, turning on radio/speakers etc. The internet of things has given us opportunity to automate our home appliance and control it from a certain distance. A home automation system has become a trend, which gives a convenient way for users to personalize artifacts in home using sensors, Bluetooth and android system. This paper proposes the architecture for home automations using near field communication and general packet radio service along with a mobile application. The near-field communication is used as the central system in this paper to automate home environment for a user by locking and unlocking doors, lighting, air conditioning, switching on/off fans, personal computer, mobile, television as near field communication helps in providing energy efficiency and required security.

title: Retraction Notice: RTIDS: A Robust Transformer-based Approach for Intrusion Detection System
authors: N Saranya, T Abbinavu, P Arun Kumar, R Gnanasekar, P Guru Prasanth, R Subha
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (FIDSs) with foamy disturbance are crucial for preventing security breaches in the constantly growing world of the internet's interconnectivity across many domains. Among the many applications are supply chains, manufacturing, smart homes, smart grids, healthcare, and environmental tracking. Traditional intrusion detection methods, while effective in conventional contexts, require adaptation and enhancement to cater to the unique challenges, including devices with limited resources, memory, and battery capacity, as well as particular protocol stacks. In this study, we develop a lightweight attack detection method that detects and stops attackers from injecting superfluous data into the network using a supervised machine learning-based FIDS. Our simulation results demonstrate that the suggested FIDS- based classifier works well in terms of classification accuracy and detection speed when it is enhanced by a combination of two or three complex features.

title: A System to Detect Unconscious Bias Using Virtual Patients: System Specifications
authors: Md. Nasfikur R Khan, Sean Walker
abstract: Unconscious bias training fosters diversity, safety, and respect within the workplace by assisting individuals in recognizing, managing, and reducing their biases. Recently, simulation-based training has become increasingly popular among healthcare professionals due to its effectiveness in replicating real-world scenarios. This approach allows for the opportunity to make mistakes, learn, reflect, receive feedback, and improve clinical skills while maintaining patient safety. Nevertheless, immersive technology simulations have certain limitations, including latency issues and challenges related to spatial awareness. This research outlines the system specifications and use cases, accompanied by an activity diagram and data analysis process, while identifying the primary users for implementing virtual patients to trigger unconscious biases in future physicians. This document constitutes the third part of a five-paper series focused on developing a system to trigger unconscious biases in medical students through interactions with virtual patients.

title: Retraction Notice: Bosom Malignant Diseases (Cancer) Identification by using Deep Learning Technique
authors: Timmana Hari Krishna, C. Rajabhushnam
publish: 2019 Third International conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: In recently observed that breast related diseases affects women present all over the globe, where it emerges as the second most common disease in the world. In 2012, 12 % cancer patients were present and from these patients 25 % are breast cancer patients. In the traditional method to cure the breast cancer is malignant tumor. Most of the doctors manually presumed the bosom malignant growth region. Various examinations have referred that this manual presumed requires more time and it relies upon the operation and machine. Therefore, it is necessary to design a perfect algorithm for the identification of bosom diseases. In this report, we have developed an algorithm to identify the breast cancer patient automatically. This algorithm can automatically detect the tumor of breast cancer by observing the biopsy pictures. Also, the calculation must be very precise, as the lives of individuals are at risk. All the performance operations are done on the microscopy pictures and the data set for this microscopy pictures is designed for the clustering analysis of a picture. The experimental results of the proposed scheme show accuracy 98.3 %, precision 0.65, Recall 0.95, F1 score 0.77 and ROC - AUC 0.692.

title: Retraction Notice: Voice Controlled Human Assistence Robot
authors: M.Bhanu chandu, Kirupa Ganapathy
publish: 2020 6th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Individual automated colleagues help diminishing the manual endeavours being put by the people in their everyday errands. In this paper, proposed a review on voice-controlled human right hand robot. The human voice directions are given to the human automated colleague remotely, by utilizing a voice order of client. The robot can perform various developments, turns, start/stop activities and migrate an article starting with one spot then onto the next and offering things to the matured individuals in the home. The voice directions are handled, utilizing an advanced mobile phone. The individual human right hand robot is created on a smaller scale controller based stage and can know about its present area. The viability of the voice control conveyed over a separation is estimated through numerous examinations. Execution assessment is completed with consequences of the underlying investigations. The developments to be forecasted are possibly referring to the applications in ventures, medical clinics and how, including the environmental laboratories.

title: Retraction Notice: Enhanced hybrid quality prediction model for 8K UHD H.265 video using ANFIS
authors: A. Dhanalakshmi, G. Nagarajan
publish: 2017 4th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Enhanced video coding standard: H.265/ High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) elaborated by using SHVC reference software model (SHM), has an aim to develop performance above the current H.265 standard. This paper represents a Quality of Experience (QoE) and Quality of service (QoS) estimate model in a video streaming from the origin of network visual application which is developed in recent years and also 8K video streaming illustration based on the approaching standard of SHVC (Scalable High efficiency Video Coding). SHVC decoder is utilized below the monitor to decode moreover demonstrate in real time the expected SHVC layers. Conversely, ANFIS technique is carried out to interface the Quality of Experience and Quality of service and real time streaming is approved in an unfeasible model which entails an original video sequence. The fundamental objective of this paper is to represent a wireless domain considering 8K UHD H. 265-coded video of the hybrid estimate model of excellent quality. The main contribution of this paper are split up as the examination of the quality impact of the service parameter on the video transmission of 8K UHD H.265-coded and the objective based upon development of quality of visualization by QoS parameters using ANFIS (Adaptive neuro fuzzy interference system). The results showed that the prediction of accurateness is obtained better using an enhanced hybrid model in the series of 50 percentage bit rate reduction and Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) for the same quality of video, reliability of QoE and 8KUHD video streaming using QoS.

title: Retraction Notice: Spam Detection for Social Media Networks Using Machine Learning
authors: V Niranjani, Y Agalya, K Charunandhini, K Gayathri, R Gayathri
publish: 2022 8th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: People frequently examine internet product reviews before purchasing a product. More merchants aim to deceive users in order to earn a profit. Because customers are misled in this way, it's critical to be aware of and delete fraudulent reviews. This study examines machine learning-based spam detection approaches and discusses their general perspectives and outcomes. Knowing how important customer reviews are to a product's success, marketers frequently try to fool customers by publishing phoney remarks. Merchants have the option of posting updates themselves or hiring others to do so for them. Comment or review spam is the practice of sending out false updates. Spam senders might be recruited to leave favorable or negative reviews that harm competitor business. By 2020, the Canadian Competition Bureau gave a warning to its citizens officially, stating that they should be careful of fraudulent reviews and estimating that one off three of online reviews is fake. Poll fiction taken from more than twenty-five thousand participants by 2020 claims that more than seventy percent of consumers trust online reviews. As a result, spam reviews are a major source of concern nowadays. Based on the goal of the proposed techniques, the majority of the published articles dealing with this subject can be segregated into three. Tactics can be used to get spam reviews, individual spam senders, or spams sent by groups. Because the spam sent in group methods haven't been thoroughly investigated; they aren't discussed in this work. Spam detection is a machine learning issue that requires supervision. This means you'll need to give your machine learning model a set of spam and ham message examples and tell it to look for the relevant patterns that distinguish the two groups. Most email service providers hav

title: Cross-Corpus Speech Emotion Recognition Based on Dynamically Filtering Multi-Stage Diffusion Model
authors: Jingjie Yan, Boyan Sun, Yuebo Yue, Xiaoyang Zhou, Ying Liu
abstract: In cross-corpus speech emotion recognition (SER) tasks, although generative methods have shown certain potential in alleviating the scarcity of target domain samples, the generated samples often lack precise alignment with the acousticsemantic features of the target domain. This results in pseudo samples of uneven quality and even introduces noise, thereby weakening the effectiveness of transfer learning. To address the problem of insufficient quality control over pseudo samples in existing methods, we propose a Dynamically Filtering Multi- Stage Diffusion Model (DFMDM). The proposed DFMDM model establishes a three-stage iterative optimization process of “Generation– Filtering–Enhancement.” In the first stage, the conditional diffusion model is guided by an adversarial domain classifier to generate pseudo samples with target domain characteristics. In the second stage, a dynamic filtering module is presented to eliminate low-quality pseudo samples that significantly deviate from the target domain features. In the third stage, the filtered pseudo samples are mixed with source domain samples and reused to regenerate target-style pseudo samples. In this way, a progressive diffusion-based enhancement loop is constructed, which improves sample quality through iterative generation and dynamic adjustment of similarity thresholds. Experimental results conducted on CASIA, EmoDB, eNTERFACE and IEMOCAP demonstrate that the proposed DFMDM model achieves significant improvements over existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) approaches, fully showcasing its application potential and practical value in cross-corpus speech emotion recognition.

title: Proceedings of the 2026 10th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Automation and Control Technologies
authors: N/A
abstract: None

title: Ways to Increase the Energy and Ecological Efficiency of Intelligent Transport Systems of the Region’s Support Road Network
authors: Roman S. Runets, Vladislav A. Pastushkov, Yuri V. Trofimenko
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article examines ways to improve the energy and environmental efficiency of intelligent transport systems being implemented on the region’s core road network. The relevance of the study stems from the increasing energy consumption and environmental impacts associated with the digitalization of road transport infrastructure, particularly in regions with pronounced seasonality in transport demand. Using the Krasnodar region as an example, a systems approach is substantiated based on identifying transport and infrastructure sources of energy losses, developing a rational ITS architecture, and gradually introducing renewable energy sources for autonomous power supply to facilities. An integrated indicator for assessing energy and environmental efficiency is proposed, allowing for the justification of investment decisions and development programs for the region’s core road network.

title: Intelligent Vehicle Damage Inspection: Detection, Multi-View Reasoning, and VLM-Based Report Generation
authors: Andrei Sergeev, Sergei Gaponenko, Ksenia Polyantseva
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper presents a general framework for automated vehicle handover inspection based on multi-view image analysis and a staged machine-learning pipeline. The system concept combines a backend layer for request orchestration and lifecycle control, a dedicated inference service for vehicle-presence validation and initial damage detection, and a VLM component for context-aware defect interpretation with structured assessment output. The driver uses a mobile application to create the report by initializing a request, capturing four mandatory views, and submitting the images for processing. The architecture is designed for practical deployment and supports decision traceability, reprocessing workflows, and operator-assisted final verification. The main contribution is the formalization of an end-to-end pipeline from image upload to final resolution with a unified JSON representation suitable for auditing and integration into fleet-management operations. Target system properties include accuracy, interpretability, scalability, and acceptable response time, establishing a basis for subsequent comparative evaluation and further development.

title: Comparative Analysis using Power-Aware Design of Traditional and Low-Power ALUs
authors: Sai Mani Deepika S, Asritha Venkata Naga Hasini B, Saikeerthi Abburi, Swaminadhan Rajula, Kiranmai P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This work presents a comparative study between a traditional Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) and a low-power ALU implementing operand isolation, approximate arithmetic, and power gating. Both designs were implemented in Verilog HDL and synthesized using Vivado 2023.1 targeting a Xilinx FPGA. SAIF-driven post place-and-route analysis shows that the proposed low-power ALU achieves 38.46% reduction in slice LUTs, 41.67% reduction in slices, and 58.01% reduction in total on-chip power compared to the traditional design. Dynamic power reduces by 58.42%, and junction temperature drops by 23.5 °C, indicating significant improvement in thermal efficiency. The design achieves these gains without compromising functional correctness for all tested operations, making it suitable for energy-sensitive digital systems.

title: EEG Signal Analysis for Epileptic Seizure Detection using Principal Component Analysis and Gradient Boosting
authors: C. Gethara Gowri, M Amanullah, G. Kavitha, Lakshminarayanan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: In order to diagnose and treat epilepsy, a neurological disorder characterized by abnormal brain activity, it is crucial to first identify epileptic episodes. Because of their high dimensionality and noise, EEG data can provide valuable information on brain electrical patterns, but accurate classification is challenging. Using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction and Gradient Boosting for classification, this research proposes an efficient paradigm. After preprocessing EEG recordings to remove artifacts, both statistical and spectral properties were recovered. PCA preserved the most informative aspects while lowering computational complexity. This was followed by training the Gradient Boosting Classifier to distinguish between seizure-free and non-seizures. Experimental findings show that the proposed method achieves 93.8% accuracy, 92.5% precision, 94.1% recall, and a 93.3% F1-score. exceeding several baseline models. The method’s exceptional robustness makes it a feasible choice for real-time seizure monitoring systems.

title: Performance Analysis of a Race Reduced Data Flip Flop Under Wide Thermal Variations for Future High-Speed Counters and Prescalers
authors: Shilagani Shivaprasad, Daddanala Nagavarun, Janamanchi Rathna Koushal, J Ajayan, Ponnaboina Charan Sai, MD Badhesha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The minimal building blocks of digital integrated circuits are flip-flops (FFs) although ensuring they are highly effective (as measured by performance), low power, and extremely reliable is a major challenge. The results of this paper consider the validity of high-speed, dynamic, D-flip-flop (DFF), which is another model of a flip-flop called the Race-Reduced Flip-Flop (RRFF). RRFF design assumes a master-slave kind of arrangement with dynamic pre-charge-evaluate logic to enhance speed with fixed keeper latches to enhance robustness and race conditions. The circuit was also tested in terms of its performance and reliability by simulation at the 16 nm technology node. Performance factors involved assessment of the transient characteristics of the circuit, Fault (Short Circuit) power, total power, delay, and the switching energy (PDP) and determination of the Delay-Power Ratio (DPR) at the range of temperatures 27° C and 107° C and intervals of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.7 ~\mathrm{V}, 0.8 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> and 0.9 V. The super-threshold performance of the circuit was established to be constant at 0.7 V and 0.8 V when subjected to the transient performance, as well as the conclusion was made that movement at 0.6 V was not consistent. The most alarming was that the propulsion delay is also increasing 0.395%, as the ambient temperature increases, more unexpectedly, the overall power dissipated decreases 14.19% at 0.7 V which previously was believed to be the most energy consuming. The temperature rise with this prompted an increase in temperature- power product (PDP) by 13.88% at 0.7 V. The motivation was mainly the Reduction of SC power 35.3% at 0.7 V within the temperature route examined. 0.7 V was found within the analysis as the most energy effective operating voltage and accurately represented the thermal power conventional and operational dependability.

title: Performance Improvement of Printed Monopole Antenna using Frequency Selective Surface
authors: Harshit Kaundal, Jasbir Kaur, Arun Kumar Singh, Gourab Das
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this paper, the performance of the printed monopole antenna is improved with the help of a Frequency Selective Surface (FSS) based reflector. The antenna used, three reflecting surface planes which are placed at an angle of 45° to each other. The antenna is placed at a certain distance from the reflector. The antenna is radiated at the frequency 5.2 GHz. The radiation is then reflected back by the frequency selective surface (FSS) which leads to increase the unidirectional gain of the antenna. The Simulation of the respective reflection coefficient and transmission coefficient of the antenna and frequency selective surface (FSS) illustrates that an increase in gain of the antenna is achieved by adding Frequency Selective Surface. Integration of the frequency selective surface (FSS) layer improves the antenna gain from 5.43 dB to 10.78 dB and achieved the gain improvement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 98 \%$</tex>.

title: Intelligent Medical Decision Support System Using IoT and CNN-LSTM-Based Predictive Modeling
authors: Ravinder L C, Mohammed Ali Shaik
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The emergence of illness, especially severe illnesses like chronic kidney disease (CKD) and heart disease (HD), is precisely forecasted, but this is a formidable and frequently unattainable endeavor. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning architecture that combines long short-term memory (LSTM) networks with convolutional neural networks (CNN) to enhance feature extraction and temporal pattern recognition, thereby improving diagnostic accuracy. We evaluate the proposed model against established benchmarks, including DLMNN, EO-LWAMCNet, and XGBoost Bi-LSTM, using HD and CKD datasets. The CNN+LSTM model outperforms the most advanced approaches across various metrics, including precision, recall, accuracy, and F1-score. It achieves maximum accuracy values of 95% for CKD and 94.5% for HD, surpassing the established diagnostic benchmarks. The model is consistent with a wide range of dataset characteristics, making it suitable for implementation in real clinical decision support systems.

title: SCD-YOLO: An Improved YOLOv11-Based Detection Algorithm for Rapeseed Sclerotinia Disease in Smart Agriculture
authors: Jiaxin Zhou, Jinghan Sun, Ziyi Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Rapeseed sclerotinia disease is one of the most destructive fungal diseases affecting oilseed rape production, causing severe yield losses and posing a significant threat to agricultural sustainability. Accurate and timely detection of sclerotinia-infected plants under complex field environments is crucial for precision disease management and intelligent crop protection. However, challenges such as irregular lesion morphology, partial occlusion by leaves and branches, and complex background interference significantly degrade the performance of conventional vision-based detection methods. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv11-based detection algorithm, termed SCD-YOLO (Sclerotinia Crop Detection YOLO). Two key enhancements are introduced. First, a Disease Morphology-Aware Feature Enhancement (DMAFE) module is designed to explicitly model elongated stem lesions and irregular white mycelial regions, improving the representation of partially occluded disease features. Second, a Field Background Suppression Decoupled Head (FBSDH) is proposed to reduce false detections caused by soil, residual straw, and overlapping vegetation by incorporating background-aware suppression into both classification and regression branches. Experiments conducted on a self-constructed Rapeseed Sclerotinia Disease Dataset demonstrate that SCDYOLO achieves an mAP@0.5 of 0.937, outperforming the YOLOv11 baseline by 1.5%. The results verify that the proposed method significantly enhances detection accuracy and robustness in real-world agricultural field scenarios.

title: Three-Dimensional UAV Path Planning Using Whale Optimization Algorithm with Lévy Flight and Elite Preservation Strategy
authors: Yanjie Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) integrating Lévy flight initialization and elite preservation strategies for three-dimensional UAV path planning in obstacle-dense environments. The algorithm formulates path planning as a multi-objective optimization problem incorporating path length, smoothness, altitude variation, and trajectory deviation. A hybrid initialization strategy combines Gaussian perturbations with Lévy flight distributions to enhance solution diversity. Comprehensive experiments conducted on a 450×400×50 3D grid map demonstrate superior performance: the proposed WOA achieves the shortest path length of 592.15±9.28m, representing 7.7% improvement over A* (641.72m) and 24.3% over GWO (782.14± 29.83m). The algorithm maintains exceptional smoothness with maximum turning angle of 6.34±1.12° and virtually zero sharp turns (0.1±0.3 occurrences), significantly outperforming A* (348 sharp turns) and PSO (38.42±5.67° maximum angle). Benchmark function tests validate robustness, with WOA surpassing GWO by 57 orders of magnitude on Sphere function (3.48×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−86</sup> vs 5.21×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−29</sup>), confirming its effectiveness for complex 3D navigation tasks.

title: Progressive Information Flow Non-Linear Dark Image Restoration Method
authors: Shatakshi Saxena, Shivam Taneja, Kriti Singhal, Arnav Sen, Naman Chaudhary, Kartik Koul, Taanisha Jain, Suresh Raikwar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Dark image restoration presents a significant challenge due to its inherently ill-posed nature. Existing methods often yield results with insufficient brightness and loss of fine details, limiting their practical applicability. In response, we introduce a novel framework designed to enhance brightness while preserving critical image information. This is achieved through a progressive non-linear flow of information that maintains finegrained details and produces perceptually brighter outcomes compared to existing state-of-the-art techniques. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated through comprehensive evaluation on benchmark datasets.

title: Design Study of a Fault-Tolerant Fiber-Optic Access Network Based on the Heat Supply Infrastructure
authors: Sergei A. Sidnev, Galina S. Artemeva, Liudmila Y. Krasikova, Vladimir A. Tsarenko
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The article discusses an approach to designing a fault-tolerant fiber-optic access network using urban heating infrastructure as a platform for laying communication lines. It is shown that heating mains often match the tracing requirements of communications, which supports the principle of “digging the earth once” and allows for faster commissioning of lines with less impact on the urban environment. The methods of optical cable placement during the construction of heating networks, their advantages for operation and access to maintenance are analyzed. The limitations and risks of integration are considered. The key technical challenge in designing such solutions is to align the tree topology of heating networks with the communication requirements for high fault tolerance. Methods of ensuring stability, including route redundancy, hybrid topologies, and monitoring/diagnostics, have been proposed to increase the stability of fiber-optic communication lines. The necessary ratios for estimating capital expenditures in the application of joint construction/use of infrastructure are obtained, the calculation results are presented, and the data obtained are analyzed.

title: Design of Cooperative Communication with Data Aggregation and Channel Selection in 6G Wireless Body Area Network based Applications
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Specialized networks known as WBANs use sensors affixed to the body to monitor vital parameters like oxygen saturation, ECG, and movement. These networks are essential for improving healthcare and telemedicine because they enable real-time monitoring and data analysis for disease management and forecasting. Incorporating 6G technology improves WBANs with ultra-low latency, extensive connectivity, and high data transfer rates, setting the stage for more dependable and effective health monitoring systems. Nevertheless, the heightened dependence on wireless communication and public networks raises major concerns about data security, privacy, and system stability. To tackle these issues, Cooperative Communication with Data Aggregation and Channel Selection in 6G WBAN (CDACWB) is developed. The simulation demonstration of the proposed CDACWB is performed in the ns3 software and the parameters which are calculated for the performance analysis are time estimation, computational cost and communication cost analysis. When compared with the baseline methods the proposed CDACWB performed much better in terms of time estimation and cost analysis which helps to maximize the performance.

title: PLER: A Novel Full-Reference Metric for Evaluating the Geometric Fidelity of 3D Assets
authors: Gleb A. Voronkov, Andrei V. Balobanov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper presents an enhancement of the previously proposed PLER (Peak Length to Error Ratio) metric for objective quality assessment of 3D models. The original PLER metric, which compares a reference and test 3D model by analyzing the distances of rays cast from a bounding sphere, suffered from non-uniform ray distribution. To overcome this limitation, a comprehensive study of methods for generating uniformly distributed points on a sphere was conducted. The Fibonacci (golden) spiral method was identified as optimal, leading to the development of PLER-2.0.
