title: Evolutionary Algorithm for Determining the Data Transmission Route Between Sensor Nodes
authors: Yaroslav Pyrih, Yuliia Pyrih, Taras Andrukhiv
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: A genetic algorithm is presented, the purpose of which is to minimise the weight of the route between sensor nodes. The total weight of the edges, which reflects the distance or delay value when transmitting data between nodes, is used as the optimisation criterion. The proposed approach involves representing the route as an individual and using genetic operators to form new generations. The fitness of individuals is assessed based on a function inversely proportional to the total weight of the route, which allows one to approach the optimal path with minimal costs. To study the operation of the presented algorithm, simulation modelling was carried out using the developed software product. Based on the results obtained, it is shown that the author's algorithm allowed determining the shortest route in terms of both distance and delay value, compared to the greedy one.

title: Ethical AIGC Pipelines for Film and Animation Production
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence-Generated Content (AIGC) has rapidly reshaped film and animation production by enabling automated asset creation, accelerated ideation, and complex multimodal storytelling. Despite these advancements, the integration of AIGC technologies introduces substantial ethical risks, including biased character representation, culturally insensitive outputs, unclear authorship, dataset opacity, copyright infringement, and weakened creative labour protection. This paper develops a comprehensive ethical framework for AIGC pipelines tailored to cinematic and animation workflows. Through an extensive review of current generative models-diffusion systems, multimodal transformers, neural animation generators, and large language models-the study identifies recurring ethical deficiencies that undermine responsible deployment. A technical methodology is proposed, featuring transparent dataset provenance tracking, embedded fairness auditing, cultural-sensitivity validators, copyright-risk scoring, and human-in-the-loop approval mechanisms as integral components of the AIGC pipeline. Experimental simulations across storyboard generation, character design, motion synthesis, and dialogue creation demonstrate that the Ethical AIGC Pipeline (E-AIGCP) significantly reduces bias propagation, minimizes copyright exposure, and enhances human-AI creative alignment. Results indicate a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 2 \%}$</tex> reduction in high-risk outputs and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 1 \%}$</tex> improvement in perceived creative integrity. The study provides a structured, technically rigorous blueprint for studios and regulators seeking to implement responsible AIGC systems in film and animation production.

title: The Improved Models for Calculation of Human Body SAR in the Case of Sitting Position
authors: Mykhaylo Andriychuk, Taras Nazarovets
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The calculation of Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) is carried out in the framework of the advanced modeling based on the improved model of linear antenna, which takes into account the seated posture of human, and the FDTD approach, based on the calculation of the internal characteristics of the EM field. The numerical data for both the approaches are proposed and issues related to the effectiveness and correctness of method are discussed. The simulation data confirm proximity of results obtained by both the methods.

title: Enhancing Research Administration Services in Universities: Research Grant Disbursement Online Tracking System
authors: Shisa Singkhamfu
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Higher education institutions encounter intricate and swiftly evolving obstacles, especially regarding research administration, a fundamental task influencing university rankings and academic reputation. This paper delineates a case study from the College of Arts, Media, and Technology (CAMT) of Chiang Mai University (CMU) regarding the development of an “Research Grant Disbursement Online Tracking System”. The research examines the integration of Lean Management concepts with Knowledge Management (KM) principles to transform tacit information into explicit and accessible knowledge. By delineating explicit six-step workflows, the system mitigates “administrative friction” and operational waste. Results indicate significant organizational impact in terms of time savings and improved user satisfaction.

title: Cebuano Speech Tutor Using Hybrid TDNN-HMM Phoneme and Prosodic Feature Recognition
authors: Christian Gabriel Ibaoc, Antonio Luiz Veloso, Nigel Kristoffer Valero, Shawne Michael Tumalad, Ronald Pascual
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Speech processing technology is a growing technology that relies on the effective use of available speech data. In the Philippines, most speech processing, from recognition to synthesis, is focused on either Tagalog or English, with Cebuano being often neglected. This study focuses on the Cebuano language and the prospect of developing a computer-assisted speech tutor using a TDNN-HMM hybrid speech model-based phoneme recognition, to teach language learners up to Level A1 CEFR in the Cebuano language. The model was evaluated using adapted WER and SER metrics as well as expert validation, and user improvement was evaluated through pre-tests, post-tests, model-based data, and expert validation. The speech model was calculated to have 2.64 percent WER and 8.00 percent SER, though expert assessments, on average, determined it to have a 17.82 percent error rate. Meanwhile, users of the website were found to have improved both their vocabulary and pronunciation, with an average score of 6.43 to 12.78 out of 15 from pre-test to post-test, and an increasing trend line of correct syllables per day with a strong coefficient of determination. User improvement was validated by an expert with 20 samples of final audio recordings. Recommendations for future research are discussed.

title: Effective English Communication for Food and Beverage Staff to Achieve Guest Satisfaction
authors: Aye Mon, Premchit Khurana
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: English communication is vital in the hospitality industry, especially for food and beverage (F&B) staff interacting with international guests. This study examines the challenges of English communication among food and beverage (F&B) staff in Thailand, its impact on guest satisfaction, and strategies for improvement. Data was collected through interviews as well as secondary research. The study highlights how language barriers, limited vocabulary, and cultural differences affect service quality and presents recommendations for enhancing staff English proficiency for better guest experiences.

title: A Logistic Regression Pipeline for Chiang Mai Air Quality Risk using Scaling, SMOTE, and Splines
authors: Sane Yu, Nina Bencheva
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Accuracy of air quality risk prediction in Chiang Mai is a significant challenge. There are acute <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> pollution in Chiang Mai, Thailand in every year from February to May. The interpretable model like standard logistic regression is beneficial for operational public health sectors, but the model performance is weakened by complex real-world data and extreme class imbalance. This study investigates how pipeline design specifically features scaling, nonlinear spline transformations, and the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) can materially improve logistic regression for the prediction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> in Chiang Mai. This study uses a public dataset of 29,078 hourly meteorological and pollution records for Chiang Mai with a severe class imbalance (approximately 3 percent high risk). This study evaluates four distinct pipelines against a deep learning benchmark. Results demonstrate that standard logistic regression fails to detect high risk events, yielding zero recall. However, the introduction of SMOTE successfully restores operational utility, increasing recall to over 0.81. The combined pipeline, integrating both SMOTE and cubic spline feature engineering, provides the best overall discrimination among all interpretable models. It achieves a Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (ROC AUC) of 0.823, balancing the need for high sensitivity in disaster warning systems with the requirement for model transparency. While the deep learning benchmark sets a higher performance ceiling, the results demonstrate that for highly imbalanced data, an engineered logistic regression pipeline offers the optimal balance of safety and auditability for municipal environmental monitoring.

title: Retraction Notice: IoT System Accomplishment using BlockChain in Validating and Data Security with Cloud
authors: J. Kingsleen Solomon Doss, S. Kamalakkannan
publish: 2020 Fourth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: In a block channel IoT system, sensitive details can be leaked by means of the proof of work or address check, as data or application Validation data is applied on the blockchain. In this, the zero-knowledge evidence is applied to a smart metering system to show how to improve the anonymity of the blockchain for privacy safety without disclosing information as a public key. Within this article, a blockchain has been implemented to deter security risks such as data counterfeiting by utilizing intelligent meters. Zero-Knowledge Proof, an anonymity blockchain technology, has been implemented through block inquiry to prevent threats to security like personal information infringement. It was suggested that intelligent contracts would be used to avoid falsification of intelligent meter data and abuse of personal details.

title: Retraction Notice: An IoT-Cloud Based Health Monitoring Wearable Device For Covid Patients
authors: T.H Hafsiya, Binet Rose
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: The biggest crisis facing the world today is the corona virus or covid 19. Most of the people in the Indian nation are ordinary people. Many tests for Covid19 are expensive and beyond the reach of the average person. With the advent of the novel Corona Virus, every country now places great importance on health care. So this year, the best solution for such infectious diseases is the IoT-cloud based health monitoring system. The Internet is a newcomer to the field of research, especially in the field of healthcare. With the proliferation of usable sensors and therefore smartphones, this remote health care monitoring has developed at such a pace. IoT health monitoring helps to prevent the spread of the disease and to make an accurate diagnosis of the patient's health condition even if the doctor is far away. In this paper, the portable physiological checking framework will be displayed, which will be able to constantly check the patient's heart rate, temperature, blood oxygen level and blood pressure, as the main symptoms of coronavirus are high fever, fatigue, and difficulty breathing. Sensor data is transferred directly to the IoT cloud using Wi-Fi. Any HTTP and MQTT protocols used in the IoT cloud to provide users with visible and timed sensor data. This data can be easily accessed by almost all smart terminals with a web browser. The patient's physical condition can also be monitored by the camera. Here we suggest a non - stop check and control device to check the patient's condition and store the patient's information in the cloud server, remote correspondence based on Wi-Fi module. A remote health monitoring system using IoT remotely diagnoses patients using the mobile/laptop application, based on these values obtained by authorized individuals who have access to data stored on a

title: Impact of Grid-Forming Inverters equipped with Virtual Inertia on Power System Stability: An Eigenvalue and Dynamic Simulation Study
authors: Maysam Abbasi, Yuchen Zhang, Firuz Zare, Arindam Ghosh, Kevin Paice, Sachin Goyal, Yateendra Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE Power &amp; Energy Society General Meeting (PESGM)
abstract: In modern power systems, grid-forming (GFM) inverters are increasingly used to enhance stability, especially in low-inertia networks dominated by renewable energy sources. This study investigates the influence of virtual inertia, denoted by the parameter H in virtual synchronous machine (VSM) models, on the dynamic and stability response of a single-machine infinite-bus (SMIB) system. In PowerWorld Simulator environment, the impact of varying H under different grid strengths is precisely examined by performing the steady-state, dynamic, and eigenvalue analyses. According to the obtained results, there is a trade-off between oscillatory behavior and transient recovery, which offers insights into optimal inertia design for GFM inverters.

title: Applying Artificial Intelligence in Telecommunication Networks
authors: E. A. Knigin, R. F. Rafikov, Z. M. Afanasyev, E. E. Malikova, V. M. Antonova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article explored the potential of artificial intelligence in telecommunications. Specifically, it described traffic analysis mechanisms and methods for improving energy efficiency in 5 G networks. A study was conducted comparing three variations of the Discontinuous Reception (DRX) energy efficiency algorithm: classic DRX, Q-aware DRX, and AI-DRX based on a convolutional neural network. To ensure the tests were as realistic as possible, real-world IoT traffic data was used. The sample included three types of loads: a constant data stream from industrial Modbus sensors, sudden load spikes from GPS trackers, and rare event signals from a Smart Thermostat. As a result, the AI-DRX algorithm reduced energy consumption by 49-80% compared to other models. At the same time, transmission latency did not increase, and service quality improved. The study revealed that conventional adaptive methods are too slow to respond to bursty traffic. However, the use of AI allows the system to prepare in advance, predicting the load in systems with IoT devices. The implementation of AI in radio interface management is not just a trend, but another way to improve energy efficiency and communication quality in nextgeneration networks.

title: NeuroScan: A Comprehensive Deep Learning Framework for Automated Brain MRI Tumor Detection and Multi-Class Classification
authors: Prathamesh S Vadatille, Dennis Singh Moirangthem, Khoirom Motilal Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Brain tumor diagnosis from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is time-consuming and requires skilled radiologists, which can be a limiting factor in many hospitals. In this work, we present NeuroScan, a deep learning system that automatically classifies brain MRI slices into four classes: pituitary tumor, glioma, meningioma, and no tumor. We finetune a VGG16-based convolutional neural network using transfer learning on a public dataset of 6,891 images, with 5,580 images for training and 1,311 for testing. Standard preprocessing, intensity normalization, and modest data augmentation are applied to improve generalization. On the held-out test set, the model achieves an overall accuracy of 93 % and class-wise precision, recall, and F1-scores close to or above 0.90 for most classes. To make the model easier to use, we integrate it into a simple Streamlit web application that allows users to upload an MRI image and view the predicted class along with confidence scores and basic visualizations. Our results indicate that a relatively compact VGG16-based model, combined with a lightweight web interface, can provide a practical proof-of-concept for automated brain MRI analysis in research and educational settings.

title: Lightweight Edge Intelligence: Adapting 1.5B LLM via LoRA for Log Analysis in Campus Networks
authors: Zhenpeng Li
abstract: The practical adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) in cybersecurity remains constrained by computational cost, deployment complexity, and increasingly strict data-privacy requirements. These challenges are particularly acute in resource-limited environments such as vocational colleges and similar resource-constrained institutional environments, where cloud-based security analytics may be undesirable or infeasible, while traditional rule-based intrusion detection systems often struggle with noisy alerts and limited semantic understanding of network events. To address this gap, I propose Edge-SecGPT, an on-premise framework for lightweight log screening under constrained campus deployment conditions. Built on the Qwen2.5-1.5B backbone, Edge-SecGPT adopts parameter-efficient adaptation through Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) together with 4-bit NF4 quantization. Rather than targeting maximal model scale, the framework is designed for deployment on resource-constrained single-GPU edge-oriented hardware and operates as a task-restricted binary log-screening module for privacy-conscious campus-network analysis. To improve methodological rigor, I evaluate the framework using a stricter temporal file-holdout protocol, where one earlier log window is used for training, a subsequent window is used for validation, and a later unseen temporal holdout window is reserved for final evaluation. Across 32,762 desensitized campus log records, the revised study frames Edge-SecGPT as a localized lightweight LoRA-based log-screening framework together with a controlled serialization study under the stricter temporal file-holdout protocol and an additional same-protocol conventional baseline. On the later unseen temporal holdout, the conventional logistic-regression baseline achieved 77.96% precision, 98.48% recall, and 87.03% F1-score, with an estimated 826.5 false alarms/day. Under the same temporal split, the TinyLlama-1.1B LoRA baseline achieved 94.37% precision, 100.00% recall, and 97.10% F1-score, while the raw-JSON Qwen2.5-1.5B configuration achieved the strongest empirical performance among the compared configurations under the present single-site temporal-holdout protocol and current label-construction regime (94.68% precision, 100.00% recall, and 97.27% F1-score). The corresponding structured configuration achieved 93.81% precision, 99.18% recall, and 96.42% F1-score. These results suggest that serialization choice materially affects deployment behavior in the present within-site setting, while broader attack-semantic generalization remains to be validated.

title: Three-Dimensional Circumnavigating Control of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles Using Range-Only Measurements_supp1-3681159.mp4
authors: Baoli Ma
abstract: This letter investigates the three-dimensional (3D) circumnavigating control for a single unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) using only range measurements in GPS-denied environments. The core challenge lies in the unavailability of bearing information, rendering traditional control laws ineffective. To formulate the control design, we propose a hierarchical motion decomposition strategy by introducing a virtual anchor, which conceptualizes the UAV's movement as a superposition of orbital and translational motions. Based on this formulation, a global circumnavigation control law is developed by fusing a nonlinear observer with the system dynamics. A unified stability analysis is presented to guarantee that the circumnavigation errors converge to zero from any initial state, without relying on the separation principle. Numerical simulations and field experiments are performed to validate the theoretical results.

title: GVF-MPC Depth Control Framework Motivated by Restoring-Moment Mechanism Analysis for Bionic Robotic Fish_supp1-3681186.mp4
authors: Zhongjing Ma
abstract: Longitudinal oscillations are commonly observed in depth control of low-speed underwater platforms such as bio-inspired robotic fish. To investigate this issue, this paper conducts a quantitative analysis of the vertical-plane dynamics. It reveals the speed-dependent coupling between the depth and pitch channels as the swimming speed decreases, and clarifies the underdamped mechanism induced by restoring-moment dominance. A critical speed condition for the onset of low-speed oscillations is also derived. Based on the dynamic analysis, a hierarchical GVF–MPC depth control framework is proposed, which structurally decouples geometric guidance from dynamic execution. The guidance layer employs a guiding vector field (GVF) to generate naturally smooth attitude references from geometric information, with reduced sensitivity to model degradation and pitch disturbances. The inner loop applies model predictive control (MPC) to track the guidance commands while explicitly accounting for dynamic constraints, balancing convergence speed and control-input smoothness to improve stability and safety under low-speed conditions. Simulation and experiments are conducted to evaluate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method. Simulations consider sinusoidal and V-shaped depth path and compare the proposed approach with LOS, PID, and SMC based controllers. Experiments are performed in still water and under simulated Sea-State 1 disturbances to assess steady state accuracy, disturbance recovery, and overall robustness.

title: Photonic Structuration of the Airy-Bessel Beam for High-Performance Fiber-Optic Refractive Index Sensing
authors: Bodem Indraja, Arijit datta, Sreenivasulu tupakula, Swagata Samanta, Saswat kumar ram
abstract: The emergence of structured light as a controllable photonic entity has transformed the manipulation of electromagnetic fields, facilitating advanced light-matter interactions that critically enhance the operational efficiency of diverse fiber-optic sensors. Within this emergent framework, the Airy-Bessel beam, characterized by its non-diffracting propagation and self-healing dynamics, stands as a paradigmatically superior candidate for fiber-based refractive-index measurements. In this investigation, we employed the Airy-Bessel excitation within a decladded multimode fiber to transcend the inherent constraints of conventional Gaussian illumination, whose efficacy is typically curtailed by shallow evanescent-field penetration and diminished mode-medium interaction. By tailoring the excitation conditions, higher-order guided modes are efficiently stimulated, thereby amplifying the evanescent-wave contribution and enabling a superior refractive index sensitivity. The proposed sensor architecture was validated using full-vector beam propagation method (BPM) numerical modeling performed in OptiBPM (v13.1.3, Optiwave Inc.), allowing a comprehensive analysis of Airy-Bessel beam evolution within the sensing structure. The sensor demonstrates absolute sensitivities of 1502.96 dB/RIU and 1878.72 dB/RIU for sensing lengths of 20 cm and 25 cm, respectively, with corresponding detection resolutions of 6.65×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup> RIU and 5.32×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup> RIU. These results affirm a notable advancement over Gaussian beam configurations and highlight that structured-beam excitation enables scalable sensing without the need for geometric alterations such as tapering, side-polishing, U-bending, surface-functionalized designs, etc. Therefore, the proposed sensing method furnishes a potent pathway for futuristic fiber-optic sensing across chemical, biomedical, and environmental domains where ultralow refractive index detection is imperative.

title: Blind Radio Map Construction via Topology Guided Manifold Learning_supp1-3680610.pdf
authors: Edith Ngai
abstract: Constructing radio maps traditionally requires extensive site surveys with precise location labels, resulting in costly and time-consuming calibration. Conventional approaches derive labels from inertial measurement units (IMUs), but are constrained by device-level access permissions and the need for pre-installed IMU hardware. In this paper, we present a calibration-free radio map construction method that relies solely on Channel State Information (CSI) measurements, thereby obviating the need for location labels. Our key insight is to embed CSI data into a two-dimensional geographic space using a neural network, without any label information. The primary challenge is to preserve the real-world topology in the embedded space. To address this issue, we propose a novel Topology-Guided Manifold Learning (TGML) approach that learns a low-dimensional embedding through self-supervision based on its mapping to a topological map in the geographic space. Specifically, we introduce an embedding network that learns locally smoothed proximity, thus creating a compact low-dimensional representation in the latent space. We further employ a regularized transport method with a differentiable Sinkhorn distance to establish an optimal mapping between the latent and geographic spaces. We use the resulting mapping deviation to supervise the training of the embedding network, enabling continuous refinement through self-supervision. Experiments in an office environment show that TGML achieves an average localization error of 2.37m and a relative beam estimation error of 6.68%, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.

title: Robust Information Bottleneck Guided Non-autoregressive Semantic Communication with Synonymous Mapping
authors: Mingtong Zhang, Haixia Zhang, Dongfeng Yuan, Ping Zhang
abstract: Semantic communication emerges as a pivotal technology for realizing the 6G vision. However, existing semantic-aware reconstruction systems rely on syntactic-level loss functions for optimization, failing to focus on the precise recovery of semantic information and resulting in suboptimal semantic fidelity. Meanwhile, the autoregressive decoding architecture adopted in text semantic communication introduces prohibitive high latency. To address these two issues, we propose a robust information bottleneck (RIB) guided non-autoregressive semantic communication (NASC) scheme, named RIB-NASC. First, we pioneer the integration of the RIB criterion into semanticaware reconstruction systems, formulating a direct optimization objective tailored for underlying semantic recovery and establishing an informativeness-robustness trade-off. Second, we derive a tractable variational lower bound for the RIB objective via variational approximation and a novel synonymous mapping-based semantic posterior estimation strategy. Third, we design a lightweight non-autoregressive semantic decoder architecture based on Transformer encoder, enabling high-speed parallel semantic decoding during inference. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the RIB-NASC scheme significantly outperforms baseline schemes in terms of semantic recovery performance (BLEU score and sentence similarity) and achieves a decoding delay reduction of nearly 96.7% compared to traditional autoregressive decoding.

title: Performance Analysis and Evaluation of the Dual Frequency Doppler Consistency Checking based GNSS Spoofing Detection for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Ziheng Zhou, Feifan Zhou, Yimin Deng, Mingquan Lu, Hong Li
abstract: With the growing reliance on Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) for navigation and positioning in autonomous systems, the vulnerability to spoofing attacks has emerged as a critical concern. The increasing deployment of dual-frequency GNSS receivers in vehicular applications has drawn attention to their potential for efficient spoofing detection. Dual-Frequency Doppler Consistency Checking (DFDCC) offers an intuitive approach by assessing the Doppler consistency between two frequency signals. This method not only enables the identification of spoofed signals but also exhibits robustness and flexibility in vehicular scenarios. Despite its practical advantages, DFDCC currently lacks a comprehensive theoretical framework and detailed performance analysis, particularly in the context of vehicular applications. To bridge these gaps, this study aims to develop a theoretical foundation and evaluate the real-world applicability and effectiveness of DFDCC in vehicular scenarios. The paper begins by deriving a Doppler observation model for both authentic and spoofing signals, deriving key sources of Doppler bias. These include the deviation between false and real user states, the spoofer clock bias, and the relative velocity between the spoofer and the user. Besides, extensive simulation experiments are conducted to analyze the influence of these factors on DFDCC performance, revealing an offset phenomenon when multiple factors coexist. This method is further validated through field tests under three typical vehicular motion patterns, demonstrating its feasibility across diverse vehicular scenarios. The results confirm that DFDCC is a viable solution for spoofing detection for autonomous vehicles, offering valuable insights for its practical implementation and serving as a reference for future research.

title: MTSP-LDP: A Framework for Multi-Task Streaming Data Publication under Local Differential Privacy
authors: Chang Liu, Junzhou Zhao
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Modeling of a GNSS Patch Antenna with a Metamaterial-Based Frequency-Selective Surface
authors: Margarita A. Mashkova, Andrey A. Yelizarov, Nikita O. Bulatov, Denis V. Kolyuzhnov, Oleg V. Arinin
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper investigates the design and performance of a right-handed circularly polarized (RHCP) antenna operating within the 1550-1610 MHz frequency range. The proposed structure uses a square patch with truncated corners on a dielectric substrate as the primary radiating element. To enhance bandwidth, a capacitive ring is integrated at the patch and centered around feed-pin. This design is further enriched by an additional layer of high-density thermoplastic and a frequency-selective surface (FSS) based on a metamaterial (MTM) formed as a periodic array of beveled frames. Antenna simulation demonstrates that it achieves an axial ratio (AR) less than 2.4 dB and a voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR) under 1.3 within the target bandwidth. Furthermore, the integration of the MTM superstrate broadens the operating bandwidth by a factor of 1.6 compared to the original antenna (under the conditions of VSWR ≤ 2 and AR ≤ dB).

title: A Dual-Branch Multimodal Object Detection Framework Based on Data Fusion
authors: Li-Zi Chen, Zhi-Lin Fan, Yu-Long Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Object detection based on visible images is sensitive to illumination changes and complex environments. To address this issue, this paper proposes a dual-branch multimodal object detection framework that integrates visible images and LiDAR point cloud data. By projecting point clouds onto the image plane, a visible and projected cloud data fusion method is constructed to embed geometric depth information into the RGB domain. A six-channel YOLOv11-based detector with channel-level dual-branch fusion is then employed for early multimodal feature interaction, and an enhanced adaptive feature network (EAFN) is incorporated into the multi-scale neck to improve structural representation. Experiments on the KITTI dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a mAP@0.5 of 94.07% and a mAP@0.5:0.95 of 74.59%. The experiments indicate that the proposed method achieves superior performance compared with existing methods.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Dispatching in Flow/Job Shop
authors: I. S. Gavrilov, S. L. Yablochnikov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Operational dispatching in discrete manufacturing determines the sequence of operations executed on equipment under queues, setups, breakdowns, and due-date constraints. In complex flow-shop and job-shop systems, even small changes in shop-floor conditions can noticeably degrade on-time performance, order lead time, and work-in-process levels; as a result, fixed priority rules often lose robustness when operating mode shift. The increasing availability of production data and the development of digital shop-floor models create the prerequisites for using machine-learning methods as a decision-support layer at the execution level. This paper provides a structured comparison of dispatching approaches under uncertainty with an emphasis on practical integration into the production control loop: augmenting priority rules with forecasts of processing/remaining times and tardiness risk, training DRL policies to directly select the next operation in a shop-floor simulation. For each group, we identify typical requirements for data and simulation, target metrics (tardiness and on-time delivery, lead/cycle time, WIP, throughput), and operational constraints (decision latency, robustness across modes, and monitoring of performance degradation). The results indicate that, within an MES context, the most readily deployable solutions are those that preserve existing dispatching logic while adding adaptivity through forecasting or ranking, whereas DRL/RL policies offer high improvement potential but require verification on digital shop-floor model and safe-operation procedures.

title: Accelerating YOLOv8 on General-Purpose CPUs for Object Detection
authors: S. Sasikumar, M Rajmohan, Sivanantham E, C. Ezhilazhagan, Durairaj.M
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This paper presents a high-speed CPU-based accelerator for the YOLOv8 object detection model. The model is compiled using Open Neural Network Exchange (ONNX) then the design is implemented and evaluated in a simulation environment, aiming to maximize inference speed while efficiently utilizing CPU resources. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in processing speed demonstrating the effectiveness of CPU-based acceleration, highlighting the potential of CPU-based deep learning accelerators for real-time applications.

title: Modality-Mix Learning: Promoting Multimodal Learning Through Multilabel Objective_supp1-3677806.pdf
authors: Nannan Lu
abstract: Multimodal fusion provides a comprehensive way to understand the world by integrating data from different sources. However, some studies believe that due to the optimization imbalance, partial modalities cannot be fully learned during multimodal learning. They attempt to achieve the balance between different modalities by controlling their learning process but ignore the function of the learning objective as an essential factor. The uniform objective for all modalities leads to the network being unable to sufficiently exploit discriminative information from different modalities. Therefore, we propose a new multimodal learning method, namely, modality-mix learning (MM learning), aiming to promote the sufficient learning of each modality via the designed multilabel objective. MM learning generates modality-mixed samples by combining modalities of different samples with varied labels, transforming the single label of a sample into a probability vector representing multilabel information. These modality-mixed samples are then fed into the network, which is trained to recognize the varying proportions of multilabel information. In addition, we introduce a bilevel learning scheme, where the network is first trained using standard learning to capture general features and select samples with strong prediction, followed by MM learning on these selected samples to further optimize the subexploration modality. MM learning forces different objective information to be learned from different modalities, avoiding the insufficient learning of modalities caused by the uniform learning objective. The experimental results show that the method can significantly boost different fusion strategies and methods in diversified multimodal datasets and improve the robustness of multimodal networks as well.

title: A Smart Pill That Can Sense, Sample, and Treat: Ingestible Electronics will Deliver Drugs and Take Biopsies
authors: Reza Ghodssi, Justin Stine, Luke Beardslee
abstract: One day soon, a doctor might prescribe a pill that doesn't just deliver medicine but also reports back on what it finds inside you—and then takes actions based on its findings.

title: Joint Optimization in Pinching-Antenna-Assisted NOMA-MEC Systems via DRL
authors: Yujie Ma, Wenqi Huang, Zhiguo Ding, Daniel K. C. So, Vimal Bhatia
abstract: This work investigates the integration of pinching antennas into latency-sensitive mobile edge computing (MEC) systems, where a hybrid non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) strategy is employed for task offloading to minimize energy consumption. An energy minimization problem is formulated to jointly optimize the pinching antenna position, transmit power, and time allocation. Due to the non-convexity caused by highly coupled optimization variables, an entropy-based deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approach is developed. Simulation results show that the proposed soft actor-critic (SAC) framework achieves near-optimal performance under dynamic channel conditions while maintaining stability and generalization. Meanwhile, with the integration of pinching antennas, hybrid NOMA outperforms other strategies in latency-critical scenarios.

title: Synthetic Bistatic ISAR Imaging for Moving Targets With Prescribed Trajectories and Scattering Regimes: Validation With Passive Radar Data
authors: Sandra Gutiérrez-Serrano, Javier Rosado-Sanz, M. Pilar Jarabo-Amores, David Mata-Moya, Nerea Del-Rey-Maestre
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The design of a bistatic Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar (ISAR) images simulator is described, as a response to the need of databases with classification purposes in bistatic radar scenarios characterized by numerous degrees of freedom. Main contributions are related to the use of 3D target models and electromagnetic simulations adapted to the scattering regime defined by the operation frequency and target size. The simulation process is driven by target trajectory and dynamic parameters, being able to mimic the variation of incidence and observation angles in the bistatic planes as the target moves along the trajectory. The simulation approach combines MATLAB and ANSYS HFSS (High Frequency Structure Simulator) tools. Shooting Bouncing Rays (SBR+) and full wave techniques are applied on 3D Computer-Aided Design (CAD) models with specified materials. Passive radar data acquired under different geometries and opportunity illuminators for aerial and maritime targets are used for validation.

title: Compact Silicon-Photonics Waveguide- Confined Receiving and Transmitting Antenna with Signal Remodulation for Indoor Optical Wireless Communication
authors: Yin-He Jian, Mikołaj Wolny, Chi-Wai Chow, Eduward Tangdiongga
abstract: We propose and demonstrate a compact silicon photonics (SiPh)-based waveguide (WG)-confined receiving antenna (RA) and transmitting antenna (TA) integrated with an enhanced direct intensity remodulation scheme. In a narrow-beam-steered system, the RA exhibits only 5.3-dB power variation over a 12° angular range, supporting data rates > 28.12 Gbps, while in an ultra-narrow-beam-steered system, a 6-dB field-of-view (FoV) of 6.4° enables data rates > 70 Gbps. The combined RA-TA architecture supports bidirectional channels without additional UL lasers. The proposed signal interference cancellation (SIC) algorithm further enhances UL performance. Experimental results show signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) improvements up to 2.37 dB and potential UL throughput gains up to 45% for higher DL modulation depths.

title: A Language-enhanced Supervision-reduced Analysis Framework for Multi-branching Vessels
authors: Xi-Yao Ma, Shi-Qi Liu, Xiao-Liang Xie, Xiao-Hu Zhou, Zeng-Guang Hou, Xin-Kai Qu, Wen-Zheng Han, Ming Wang, Meng Song, Chu-Tian Zhang
abstract: It is essential to quantitatively analyze coronary arterial angiography (CAG) images for coronary interventional procedures as they are the gold reference standard for assessing stenosis. However, mainstream methods cannot extract different vascular branches precisely due to inadequate annotated data by experts and difficulty in uniformly defining vascular branches. To address the issue, this paper presents a novel framework to analyze multi-branching vessels with simple annotation, which localizes the endpoints of the vascular branches first, and then extracts corresponding vessel segments based on the endpoints. Considering it is challenging for current image-based methods to distinguish sparse vessel keypoints, we propose a cross-modal Language-Enhanced Endpoint Detection (LEED) network to achieve better performance by providing linguistic knowledge obtained from clinicians. A medical term corpus is established to convert conversation contents to standard texts. Furthermore, we devise a Twin-Stream Vessel Segmentation (TS-Seg) algorithm to segment various vascular branches based on gray-level features and detected endpoints. Besides, clinicians are allowed to manually add new endpoints and apply the TS-Seg algorithm alone to analyze any variant vascular branch that has not been defined before. Extensive experiments on two in-house and one public datasets consistently demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed framework. Moreover, comprehensive vessel analysis reports are provided based on clinical prior knowledge to assist in preoperative surgery planning and postoperative treatment assessment, showing great potential to improve the efficiency and accuracy of clinical workflow.

title: Design of a Low-Profile Wideband Horizontally Polarized Omnidirectional-like Loop Antenna with Characteristic Mode Analysis
authors: Dazhi Piao, Juan Wang, Meiwei Zhang, Jiaojiao Song
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A low-profile wideband horizontally polarized (HP) omnidirectional-like loop antenna is proposed based on characteristic mode analysis (CMA). By analyzing the current distribution on a metal loop using CMA, the mode J<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub> is observed to exhibit a HP radiation pattern. To effectively excite mode J<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub>, nonperiodic and nonuniform slots are introduced into the loop structure to achieve capacitive loading, which enhances both the modal significance and the modal bandwidth. In order to further extend the bandwidth, a dual-loop structure is formulated by loading a parasitic loop. The proposed single-loop and dual-loop antennas are fabricated, and the measurement results show that -10 dB impedance bandwidths of 67.5% (2.75-5.55 GHz) and 88.7% (2.7-7.0 GHz) can be realized, respectively. The physical dimensions of the antennas are π × 28<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>× 0.8 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (π × (0.26λ°)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × 0.007λ°) for the single-loop and π × 30<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>× 0.8 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (π × (0.27λ°)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × 0.007λ°) for the dual-loop design, where λ₀ denotes the free space wavelength at the lowest operating frequency. Furthermore, the relative bandwidth-to-area ratios of the two antennas reach 318 and 387%/λ°², respectively, which are significantly higher than those of the previously reported HP omnidirectional antennas.

title: InferF: Declarative Factorization of AI/ML Inferences over Joins
authors: Kanchan Chowdhury, Lixi Zhou, Lulu Xie, Xinwei Fu, Jia Zou
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: A DQN-Guided Genetic Algorithm With Local Intensification for Agile Earth Observation Satellite Scheduling
authors: Jiang Zhu, Qingpei Hu, Jian Wu, Yonghao Du, Xiaolu Liu, Yanjie Song, Witold Pedrycz, Lining Xing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The agile earth observation satellite scheduling problem with time-dependent transition constraints is a complex combinatorial optimization problem that has been proven NP-hard. To address this challenge, a Deep Q-network guided genetic algorithm with a local intensification strategy (DQN-LIGA) is proposed. First, we adopt a hybrid initialization method that combines greedy and random strategies to generate a high-quality and diverse initial population. Next, multiple crossover–mutation operator pairs are designed. A Deep Q-network is then employed as a policy model to adaptively select operator combinations based on individual and evolutionary states. Moreover, a simulated physical oscillation strategy (SPOS) is proposed to periodically perturb elite individuals, which is designed to refine solutions and strengthen local intensification throughout the evolutionary process. Finally, extensive experiments are conducted on 20 benchmark instances under both China and World scenarios. The results demonstrate that DQN-LIGA consistently achieves higher observation profits compared to state-of-the-art algorithms, and the integration of SPOS further improves solution quality, especially under complex scheduling conditions.

title: Making LSM-Tree-based Key-Value Store Practical and Efficient for Multi-Tenant Serverless Cloud Databases
authors: Yingjia Wang, Caixin Gong, Guoyun Zhu, Sheng Wang, Zhengheng Wang, Huan Liu, Junzhi Shi, Wu Qin, Wei Zhang, Feifei Li, Ming-Chang Yang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: CMOS Current Driver for Electrothermal MEMS Switches With Actuation and Diagnostic Circuitry
authors: Allan Riboullet, Glenn E. R. Cowan, Frederic Nabki
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents an integrated CMOS current driver designed for electrothermal micro-electromechanical-systems (MEMS) switches, fabricated using a 0.18 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m Bipolar-CMOS-DMOS (BCD) process. Unlike conventional constant-voltage drivers typically employed for MEMS actuators with positive temperature coefficient of resistance (TCR), this driver adopts a constant-current actuation. This approach benefits series-connected array components, applications seeking enhanced electromigration control, as well as actuators exhibiting a negative TCR. The system includes a 6-bit programmable digital-to-analog converter, current multiplication circuits, and feedback diagnostic capabilities, enabling precise control and monitoring of actuator performance within a core area of 1.5 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The driver supports pulse-width modulation (PWM) to significantly reduce power consumption compared to DC actuation and implements a double-pulse strategy for accelerating switch activation times. Diagnostic circuits measure actuator resistance within ± 0.5 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\Omega }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and switching states to enhance reliability in safety-critical applications. Experimental validation confirmed that the driver successfully provides currents up to 110 mA with a resolution of 1.65 mA. At a minimum output voltage of 1.7 V, it can power electrothermal actuators with up to 775 mW under an 8 V supply voltage, corresponding to a power density of 517 mW/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The driver was demonstrated to successfully actuate a chevron-style MEMS switch in 3.5 ms and maintain contact with a 76.7 mA, 50 % PWM waveform at 1 kHz, reducing power consumption by 30.72 %.

title: Topology of Out-of-Distribution Examples in Deep Neural Networks
authors: Esha Datta, Johanna Hennig, Eva Domschot, Connor Mattes, Michael R. Smith
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: A longstanding problem for deployed deep neural networks (DNNs) is their behavior in the face of unfamiliar inputs; specifically, these models tend to be overconfident and incorrect when encountering out-of-distribution (OOD) examples. In this work, we present a topological approach to characterizing OOD examples based on embeddings from DNNs. Our goal is to identify topological features that distinguish OOD inputs from in-distribution ones. Our experiments on benchmark datasets reveal that well-trained DNNs induce a topological simplification on training data, but not for OOD examples. More specifically, we find that the average lifetime (or persistence) of OOD examples is statistically longer than that of training or test examples. This indicates that DNNs struggle to induce topological simplification on unfamiliar inputs. Our empirical results provide novel evidence of topological simplification in realistic DNNs and lay the groundwork for topologically-informed OOD detection strategies.

title: NNICE 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)

title: Edge-Optimized Multimodal Generative Model for Clinical Report Generation
authors: S. Sujanthi, Arnesh R, Arsath Musammil M, Keerthika S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The medical imaging is critical in the diagnosis of different clinical conditions, and the interpretation of X-rays, CT scans and MRI scans are usually time consuming and involve expert skills. This is especially important in large volume hospitals and resource-limited areas, in which timely reporting may be problematic. This paper introduces an edge optimized multimodal generative artificial intelligence system to design structured clinical reports using medical images automatically. The system incorporates a vision encoder to extract both the global and fine-grained image features with a language model which interprets these features into clinically significant reports such as Findings and Impressions. In order to facilitate accuracy and robustness, the model is trained on various and large-scale datasets like MIMIC-CXR, IU X-Ray, and Chexpert. The framework involves the use of explainability methods, such as attention maps, heatmaps, and 3D visualization to indicate image areas that impact predictions. Its main characteristics are dual-level reporting of physicians and patients, multilingual and speech-enabled reports, and a continuous learning module that integrates the feedback of radiologists. The system is optimized to operate in edge deployment and can work offline, which means it can be used in resource-limited healthcare centers and rural ones. This work can show the potential of multimodal AI to revolutionize clinical report generation by reducing the workload of radiologists, enhancing their accessibility, and supporting medical education in an efficient, interpretable, and inclusive way.

title: Robust Prescribed Control of an Electro-hydraulic System with Modified Observer
authors: Jae Ung Yu, Van Chuong Le, Dinh Tu Duong, Phuc Ngoc Nguyen, Quoc Cuong Phan, Van Du Phan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: The electro-hydraulic system (ELHS) plays an important role in modern industry. However, various adverse factors—such as disturbances, strict constraints, and nonlinearities—make the tracking control problem more challenging. In this paper, a robust prescribed control (RPC) strategy integrated with a modified sliding mode observer (MSOB) is proposed for the ELHS. Firstly, a prescribed function is employed to ensure the position error reaches to zero with the predefined constraint. Next, the MSOB is designed to handle the influences of lumped disturbances. The simulation trial research demonstrates the efficacy of the suggested control framework.

title: Exploring Predictive Models for Lung Cancer: A Machine Learning Perspective
authors: Sanhita Kar, Debrupa Pal, Meghna Das, Chayantika Roy, Debopriya Dey, Mrinmoy Guria
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Globally, lung cancer is responsible for a significant portion of cancer-associated fatalities, chiefly resulting from late-stage identification and inadequate early detection approaches. This study presents an intelligent lung cancer prediction model leveraging the XGBoost algorithm, trained on a dataset of 1,000 individuals incorporating demographic, lifestyle, and medical attributes such as smoking habits, air pollution exposure, and genetic risk factors. The suggested model obtained a high test accuracy of 98.18%, demonstrating its ability to capture complex, non-linear patterns in the data. Comparative analysis with traditional classifiers like K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) reveals the enhanced performance of XGBoost in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Additionally, a novel feature importance analysis combining clinical and texture-based features, along with hyperparameter optimization, enhances both interpretability and predictive power. The results highlight the potential of machine learning, particularly ensemble methods, in improving early lung cancer detection and supporting clinical decision-making.

title: Hybrid DNN and SVM: An Automated Camouflaged Weed Detection using UAV Imagery
authors: Kathirvel K, Gopika D S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Infestation of weed is a serious challenge on agriculture particularly in sugarcane farms where weeds usually pass unrecognized because of the close similarity in the colour and texture of the weeds with that of the crop. This camouflage brings about problems in manual identification, which is always time-consuming. To address this issue, an object detection tool (DL-based), which would automatically detect small and multiple weed patches using high-resolution UAV (drone)-acquired imagery, was created. A DJI Phantom 3 drone was used to take pictures with an altitude of 50 meters to produce a ground resolution of 213 cm per pixel. These images were mapping into detailed ortho mosaic maps through its application such as Drone Deploy and Pix4D. Manual labelling and synthetic data augmentation were employed in marking the weed and non-weed areas, such as lightening, contrast, and saturation levels, to train the model. These annotated pictures were useful towards the development of a large and heterogeneous training set. The model employs the deep neural networks (DNNs) that can learn patterns automatically based on the colour and texture features in the images. Other features such as RGB and HSV colour value, grayscale variations, and texture properties (through gray level co-occurrence matrix) were applied to assist in separating the crop and the weed. The architecture of the network was created so that it had the fully connected structure and learning and enhancing within the layers of information processing as it progressed. The standard performance was used to test the model, and it classified with an accuracy of 92.3%. It spotted even under the various environmental settings small patches of weeds, more than the traditional classifiers such as SVM and Random Forest. The method offers a usable, quick and precise procedure in early detection of the weed in crops. The integration of deep learning with drone technology helps the farmers to maintain the control of the weeds effectively and accurately, without a necessity to monitor the situation manually as well as use polluting chemicals.

title: Energy Efficiency for Automobile A/C Systems Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Neha Ananthavaram, Taehyung Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Improving the energy efficiency of vehicle air conditioning (A/C) systems is becoming increasingly important as we work toward more sustainable transportation. While much attention has been given to electric and hybrid vehicles, there is limited research on how A/C systems affect fuel consumption in traditional Internal Combustion Engine (ICE) vehicles. This project focuses on addressing that gap by developing machine learning models that predict and optimize A/C-related energy use using real-world data collected from a 2006 Honda Civic. The study combines OBD-II sensor data with external weather information to create a clean and well-structured dataset. After preprocessing and feature selection, both traditional machine learning and deep learning models were evaluated, including Linear Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, GRU, and a hybrid GRU-XGBoost ensemble. The XGBoost model performed best, showing strong predictive accuracy with low RMSE and high R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> scores. GRU captured sequential patterns effectively, while XGBoost handled non-linear feature interactions. The results demonstrate that machine learning can help identify when A/C usage significantly impacts fuel consumption, offering insights that could support smarter, energy-aware system design. This work lays a foundation for using data-driven techniques to reduce emissions and improve efficiency in conventional vehicles, especially under varied real-world driving and environmental conditions.

title: 5G-Driven Telehealth Systems for High-Fidelity Remote Diagnostics
authors: Dal Singh, Chetan Khemraj Lanjewar, Mandeep Kaur Ghumman, Anand Prakash Dube
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: High-fidelity remote diagnostics require ultrareliable, low-latency communications (URLLC), edge intelligence, and compact, diagnostic-preserving compression. This study designed and evaluated a 5G-driven telehealth framework that combined URLLC network slicing, an asymmetric learned image codec, and edge-based inference to preserve clinical utility of ultrasound frames during transport. Two public medical-image datasets were adapted into a paired three-condition protocol (local / URLLC / stressed) and evaluations used Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM), diagnostic AUC, one-way latency, jitter, packet loss, and compute/time metrics (CPU/GPU ms, energy proxy). The proposed pipeline delivered consistent fidelity gains versus classical H. 264 streaming and strong learned-codec baselines, reporting a representative improvement of +4 dB PSNR and an approximate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+8 \%$</tex> relative AUC gain versus the H. 264 baseline under URLLC conditions while meeting E2E latency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<50 ~\text{ms}$</tex>. Results also showed favourable compute-latency efficiency (AUC per <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 ~ m s}$</tex> latency) by shifting complexity to an edge decoder. Implications include a deployable URLLC-aware tele-ultrasound architecture for remote screening and procedures, concrete clinical acceptability thresholds (PSNR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>35 ~\text{dB}$</tex>, SSIM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.90$</tex>, latency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<50 ~\text{ms}$</tex>), and a reproducible paired-dataset protocol for future research.

title: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
authors: N/A

title: iSeg: An Iterative Refinement-based Framework for Training-free Segmentation
authors: Lin Sun, Jiale Cao, Jin Xie, Fahad Shahbaz Khan, Yanwei Pang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Stable Diffusion has demonstrated strong image synthesis ability to given text descriptions, suggesting it to contain strong semantic clue for grouping objects. The researchers have explored employing Stable Diffusion for training-free segmentation. Most existing approaches refine cross-attention map by self-attention map once, demonstrating that self-attention map contains useful semantic information to improve segmentation. To fully utilize self-attention map, we present a deep experimental analysis on iteratively refining cross-attention map with self-attention map, and propose an effective iterative refinement framework for training-free segmentation, named iSeg. Our iSeg introduces an entropy-reduced self-attention module that utilizes a gradient descent scheme to reduce the entropy of self-attention map, thereby suppressing the weak responses corresponding to irrelevant global information. Leveraging the entropy-reduced self-attention module, our iSeg stably improves cross-attention map with iterative refinement. Further, we design a category-enhanced cross-attention module to generate accurate cross-attention map, providing a better initial input for iterative refinement. Extensive experiments across different datasets and diverse segmentation tasks (weakly-supervised semantic segmentation, open-vocabulary semantic segmentation, unsupervised segmentation, and mask generation on synthetic dataset) reveal the merits of proposed contributions, leading to promising performance. For unsupervised semantic segmentation on Cityscapes, our iSeg achieves an absolute gain of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.8\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> in terms of mIoU compared to the best existing training-free approach in literature. Moreover, our proposed iSeg can support segmentation with different kinds of images and interactions, and also be used as a post-processing, or in different frameworks, to improve training-free segmentation. The project is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://linsun449.github.io/iSeg</uri>.

title: CVLS-ST: A Cross-View Long-Short Framework for Traffic Risk Prediction in Intelligent Urban Transportation Systems
authors: Yachao Yuan, Xingyu Chen, Zixiang Peng, Muting Li, Zhen Yu, Yingwen Wu, Thar Baker
abstract: As a cornerstone of smart city consumer ecosystems, accurate traffic accident risk prediction is vital for proactive safety services in intelligent transportation systems. However, current solutions often fail to integrate heterogeneous relational features (e.g., historical risk correlations) or effectively balance long-term periodic patterns with transient dynamics. To address these issues, we introduce a Cross-View Long-Short Spatial-Temporal Framework (CVLS-ST) for traffic risk prediction, which innovatively decouples long- and short-term dependencies to achieve more precise urban traffic risk prediction. Specifically, we design a cross-view attention graph network to effectively capture complex and diverse interactions across multiple relational graphs. Besides, we develop a Mamba block that incorporates state-space modeling into LSTM’s gating architecture to effectively capture global spatial semantics and temporal dependencies. Moreover, we introduce a state-inherited strategy to jointly and accurately model the long-term trends and short-term dynamics of traffic risk. Extensive experiments on two real-world datasets show that the F1-score of CVLS-ST outperforms the state-of-the-art by 20.37% on CHI and 5.83% on NYC, validating its effectiveness in complex urban environments.

title: Dynamic Incremental Tucker Decomposition for Sparse Tensors on Heterogeneous Platforms
authors: Xiaosong Peng, Laurence T. Yang, Xiaokang Wang, Shijie Lv
abstract: Tucker decomposition approximates the original tensor using a set of factor matrices and a core tensor, thereby reducing the storage and computational cost. Existing incremental Tucker decomposition techniques have limitations in efficiency and are not applicable to diverse dynamic incremental tensors. This paper proposes a dynamic incremental Tucker decomposition method based on heterogeneous computing, which efficiently handles complex dynamic tensors while preserving computational efficiency. Specifically, the target optimization function of Tucker decomposition is reconstructed to decouple the intrinsic correlation between factor matrix rows and fixed tensor dimensions, and the optimal solutions for the factor matrix rows are derived. This enables the algorithm to adapt to arbitrary forms of incoming incremental data, including time-series tensors, without recalculating existing data, thus enhancing data utilization efficiency. Then, a lightweight precision optimization strategy is introduced, which iteratively updates factor matrix rows using the most element-rich dimensions of each tensor order based on recorded observable data volumes. This strategy improves precision while significantly reducing computational costs. Furthermore, a task-characteristic-based heterogeneous computing scheduling strategy is proposed. By extracting highly parallelizable intermediate operators and leveraging the high throughput of GPUs, the algorithm is accelerated, leading to a 10 to 40 times speedup in incremental tensor decomposition compared to state-of-the-art methods, while maintaining accuracy.

title: Corrigendum: Burr: A Benchmark for Ontology Learning from Relational Databases [Experiments & Analysis]
