title: MaliVD: Source Code Vulnerability Localization via Attention-Based Multi-Modal Learning
authors: Enze Dai, Shuo Wang, Xi Xiao, Qing Li, Sheng Wen, Tianqing Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Source code vulnerabilities represent a critical threat to software security, potentially leading to severe consequences such as data breaches and system failures. Traditional static analysis tools, while widely used, suffer from high false positive rates and struggle to adapt to the increasing complexity of modern software. Deep learning-based approaches hold promise for automated vulnerability detection, but they face challenges including limited dataset quality, inadequate feature extraction, and lack of precise vulnerability localization capabilities. To address these limitations, we propose MaliVD, a novel vulnerability detection method in source code that leverages a multi-modal attention mechanism. MaliVD not only identifies vulnerability types but also pinpoints the specific lines of code where vulnerabilities are triggered. The model extracts sequential, tree-based, and graph-based features from source code and employs specialized neural networks to learn these diverse representations. By strategically focusing on Points of Interest within the code, MaliVD effectively prioritizes potentially vulnerable code regions, enhancing both detection accuracy and localization precision. Experimental results show that when compared with eight advanced vulnerability detection models across three large datasets, MaliVD demonstrates superior vulnerability detection and localization capabilities, maintaining high F1 scores and localization precision. Particularly on the ReliVul dataset, the F1 score is improved by 21.82%, and the Top-5 localization accuracy is 18% higher than other methods, with lower false positives across six mainstream vulnerability types, validating MaliVD’s practical application value in real-world environments.

title: An Object Recognition and Classification Method for Dexterous Robotic Hands Based on Tactile Perception
authors: Zhen Li, Peng Zhang, Huazhen Chu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To enhance the tactile perception capability of a robotic dexterous hand, this study constructs a tactile dataset termed NTD-25, which contains contact signals from 25 different objects and covers multi-dimensional tactile information such as pressure and micro-vibrations. To address the noise interference introduced by tactile sensors under no-load conditions, the acquired tactile data are subjected to filtering-based preprocessing. Furthermore, a multi-scale interactive convolutional neural network (MSICNN) is proposed to perform object recognition and classification based on tactile perception, and is trained and evaluated on the constructed dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the MSICNN achieves accuracies of 98.8% and 98.5% on object hardness recognition and object category recognition tasks, respectively, significantly outperforming comparative models such as ShuffleNet V2 and MobileNet V2, thereby validating the effectiveness of the proposed method for tactile perception in robotic dexterous hands.

title: Enhancing Agricultural Productivity with Machine Learning: A Crop and Fertilizer Recommendation System
authors: Hariom Nabira, Harsh Tiwari, Mayur Kawale, Mudassir Ahmed Siddiqui, Khushboo Khurana
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Agriculture is a critical sector for food security and economic stability; however, conventional farming often depends on intuition, causing suboptimal crop choices and fertilizer use. This study presents an intelligent recommendation system that leverages machine learning to optimize both crop selection and fertilizer application. It uses a Random Forest classifier to predict the most suitable crops based on soil nutrients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}, \mathrm{P}$</tex>, and K), pH, and climate (temperature, humidity, and rainfall). Given the chosen crop, it recommends fertilizers tailored to soil nutrient deficiencies. Importantly, the system integrates economic data, such as Minimum Support Prices (MSP) and relevant government subsidy schemes provided by India, to guide farmers' decisions. Experimental evaluation shows that the Random Forest model achieves 99.32% accuracy, outperforming other classifiers. These results align with those of recent studies demonstrating the high accuracy of ensemble methods in agriculture. By combining agronomic predictions with economic insights and visualizations, our system bridges precision farming and financial sustainability, aiming to empower farmers with data-driven decision-making support.

title: Deep Feature Fusion: A Hybrid Lightweight CNN and Transfer Learning for Micro-Expression Detection
authors: Anshika Rani, Bam Bahadur Sinha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Micro-expressions are very quick and involuntary movements of the face that show the true emotions and real feelings of a person in situations where they wish to hide their emotions. The detection of micro-expressions is a very hard and complex task because they last for only a fraction of a second and involve very subtle facial movements. While recent research has used temporal and spatial information, traditional techniques struggle to identify these delicate, hidden emotions. This paper implements an efficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$h$</tex> ybrid d eep <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$l$</tex> earning <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$m$</tex> odel <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$f$</tex> or microexpression detection, integrating a Lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) head with a DenseNet121 transfer learning backbone. The model is trained on a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48 \times 48$</tex> pixel grayscale facial images covering six emotion categories: fear, anger, happiness, surprise, disgust, and sadness. Preprocessing includes image resizing, normalization, and grayscale conversion to enhance critical edge and texture features. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid system achieves a high recognition accuracy of 96.97%, with a final validation accuracy of 85.35%. The lightweight, computationally efficient model is ideal for real-time mental state analysis, deception detection, and human-computer interaction.

title: Design and Development of an Arduino-based Automated Wire Cutting System for Enhanced Industrial Efficiency
authors: Shruti Thapar, Ravi Joshi, Gori Shankar, Akansha Sharma, Yash Kumar Katara, Ravinder Singh Maan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The industrial sector aims to make processes simpler, faster, more reliable, and highly automated to improve efficiency and productivity. This work focuses on designing and developing an automated wire-cutting system for industrial use, using an Arduino Uno and an ATmega328p microcontroller. The system reduces the need for manual labor by automatically measuring and cutting wires to the required length, saving time and effort. The Arduino Uno controls the entire operation, ensuring accuracy and precision. The system's accuracy was tested, and the mean absolute error before calibration was found to be ±0.73 cm.

title: Noise-Resilient Microwave Skin Tumor Localization Using Hybrid Compressed Sensing and Self-Organizing Maps
authors: Meisam Esfandiari, Majid Amiri, Xiaojing Lv, Yang Yang
abstract: Microwave imaging offers a non-invasive alternative for skin cancer detection but traditionally requires dense scanning arrays that increase cost and acquisition time. This study presents a hybrid framework combining compressed sensing (CS) with unsupervised learning techniques, specifically Self-Organizing Maps (SOM), integrated with delay-and-sum (DAS) beamforming for efficient tumor localization. Time-domain reflected data were acquired from a 40 by 40 mm pork skin phantom containing an embedded artificial tumor at a depth of 0.6 mm, using a waveguide-based measurement setup operating in the 25–45 GHz frequency range. The framework was evaluated under additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) at signal-to-noise ratios (SNR) ranging from 30 to 10 dB. Results demonstrate that reliable tumor detection can be achieved using only 25 scan points. At 20 dB SNR, the method achieved a 2.24 mm localization error, showing high accuracy, while at 10 dB SNR (very high noise), it still indicated the tumor position but with reduced accuracy.

title: Dual-robot Engine Assembly via Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning
authors: Junnan Zhang, Xiumei Yin, Longlong Sun, Ke Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In intelligent robotics, multi-robot coordinated assembly presents complex challenges including motion planning in shared workspace and sim-to-real policy transfer. This paper proposes a multi-robot collaboration method based on learned action masking. An engine cylinder-head assembly platform is built, where two robots with parallel grippers independently install a camshaft and a valve cover. Using deep reinforcement learning, trained policies are successfully transferred to real robots. Comparative experiments demonstrate that our approach completes the dual-robot assembly task effectively and robustly.

title: Evaluating the Impact of Compression Techniques on the Robustness of CNNs under Natural Corruptions
authors: Itallo Patrick Castro Alves Da Silva, Emanuel Adler Medeiros Pereira, Erick de Andrade Barboza, Baldoino Fonseca Dos Santos Neto, Márcio de Medeiros Ribeiro
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Compressed deep learning models are crucial for deploying computer vision systems on resource-constrained devices. However, model compression may affect robustness, especially under natural corruption. Therefore, it is important to consider robustness evaluation while validating computer vision systems. This paper presents a comprehensive evaluation of compression techniques—quantization, pruning, and weight clustering—applied individually and in combination to convolutional neural networks (ResNet-50, VGG-19, and MobileNetV2). Using the CIFAR-10-C and CIFAR-100-C datasets, we analyze the trade-offs between robustness, accuracy, and compression ratio. Our results show that certain compression strategies not only preserve but can also improve robustness, particularly on networks with more complex architectures. Utilizing multi-objective assessment, we determine the best configurations, showing that customized technique combinations produce beneficial multi-objective results. This study provides insights into selecting compression methods for robust and efficient deployment of models in corrupted real-world environments.

title: An Intelligent Ultrasound System using YOLO Baby for Real-Time Fetal Growth and Anomaly Detection
authors: M. Jeba Malar, V. Senthil Kumar, A. Nagadeepan, Sudeshna Baliarsingh, N. Rajesh, G. Sajiv
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Timely and accurate assessment of fetal growth and anomalies is essential for optimizing prenatal care. Traditional ultrasound examinations are often dependent on the sonographer’s experience, leading to variability in real-time interpretation. This study introduces a novel intelligent ultrasound platform leveraging a refined YOLO-Baby architecture, specifically engineered for the simultaneous, real-time identification of fetal biometric landmarks and suspected developmental defects. The detector is calibrated on a bespoke collection of annotated ultrasound sequences, allowing precise localization of critical fetal structures—including the cranium, femur, and abdominal contour—throughout the entire gestational spectrum. Quantitative evaluation reveals substantial gains relative to preceding detection pipelines, with the proposed architecture recording a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 91.7%, execution at 47 frames per second, and a 94.2% accuracy for anomaly categorization. These achievements underscore the algorithm’s prospective utility as a collaborative diagnostic aid, reducing the likelihood of operator misinterpretations and sustaining a high standard of fetal surveillance. By marrying rapid processing speed with diagnostic accuracy and clinical interpretability, the YOLO-Baby-based architecture sets a new operational reference for AI-integrated fetal echography.

title: A Knowledge Graph and Credibility Assessment-Based Intelligent Question Answering System for Power Grid Applications
authors: Depin Feng, Qing Xu, Zheng Wang, Xiang Song, Wenlei Xu, Shanzhi Yu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of power grid structures and the diversification of operational data, traditional knowledge management tools have proven inadequate for tasks such as dispatch support, fault diagnosis, and interpretation of regulatory procedures. To address these limitations, this paper proposes KG-CA-QA, an intelligent question answering system for the power grid domain that integrates knowledge graphs and credibility assessment. Centered around grid entities and their relationships, the system constructs a multi-source knowledge graph that enables unified modeling and querying of substations, transmission lines, equipment status, dispatch commands, and other key elements. It incorporates a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) model and a credibility scoring mechanism, combining semantic understanding, knowledge graph reasoning, and source authority to provide fact-based, interpretable, and credibility-scored answers. Furthermore, the system includes a dynamic knowledge updating module to ensure the timeliness of the knowledge base. Experimental results demonstrate that KG-CA-QA significantly outperforms traditional methods in terms of answer accuracy, semantic coherence, and user satisfaction, highlighting its practical engineering value and potential for widespread application.

title: Smart Notice Board with Proximity and Cloud Synchronization
authors: A Shanmugapriya, Selvabalakrishnan V, Sridhan KT, Subash K, Sugan MK
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Traditional notice boards in educational institutions, workplaces, and public spaces often require manual updates, lack interactivity, and consume resources such as paper. This work introduces SmartSense, an IoT-enabled notice board system with cloud-based synchronization, smart proximity activation, environmental monitoring, and alert functionalities. Built on the ESP32 microcontroller, the system integrates a TFT display, Firebase Realtime Database, and Firebase Storage for instant content updates via mobile devices. A PIR motion sensor activates the display only when a user is nearby, while a DHT11 sensor continuously measures and displays real-time temperature on the notice board. Additionally, a buzzer module provides audible alerts for high-priority announcements. The system supports both text and image notices, offering a low-maintenance, scalable, and eco-friendly communication platform. Testing demonstrated real-time synchronization within seconds, accurate proximity detection, reliable temperature readings, and effective alerts. SmartSense can be deployed in schools, offices, and public terminals, promoting sustainable and responsive communication infrastructure.

title: Network Attack Sample Generation and Detection Based on Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Haohan Xu, Heng Zhang, Jiawei Qi, Jiahong Dai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Network attack detection is a fundamental research topic in cybersecurity, yet existing methods face challenges including attack sample scarcity and class imbalance. This paper proposes a network attack sample generation and detection method based on Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), constructing an improved Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (CGAN) to synthesize high-quality attack samples integrated with a deep neural network classifier. Experiments on the NSL-KDD dataset demonstrate that generated samples exhibit high distributional consistency with real samples, achieving a Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) of 12.847, significantly outperforming conventional GAN approaches. The augmented detection model attains 94.325% accuracy and 93.178% F1-score, with improvements of 3.892% and 4.561% over baseline methods respectively. This research provides an effective approach for addressing data imbalance in cybersecurity applications.

title: Enhancing Secure Data Transmission in IoT-Cloud Systems using Deep Belief Networks
authors: S. Murugesan, N. Ramkumar, P. Usharani, S. Ramalingam, G. Kavitha, V. Sridevi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The security of IoT networks is becoming more important as the number of connected devices grows and data is exchanged across cloud services. These networks, which are often resource-constrained and scattered, are very susceptible to assaults. Robust security is required to ensure data integrity, confidentiality, and trust in IoT applications. This work proposes a new technique based on Deep Belief Networks (DBN) to forecast safe data transfer in IoT devices using cloud computing. The DBN model, recognized for its capacity for unsupervised feature learning and anomaly detection, is adapt at identifying possible security issues in dynamic IoT settings. The model was assessed using the Edge-IIoTSet dataset, which comprises data from both IoT and Industrial IoT (IIoT) devices and encounters various cyber-attacks, including DDoS and injection assaults. The accuracy of the proposed model was compared with existing approaches that include Artificial Neural Networks, Recurrent Neural Networks, Autoencoders, and Convolutional Neural Networks. As a result, the DBN model achieved 99% accuracy, 95% precision, 92% recall, and a 93% F1 score which is superior to previous techniques. The results show that the DBN model is efficiently used to support secure data transmission for IoT networks with its vast potential for widespread applications in real-time cloud-based IoT systems.

title: Multi-Channel Dynamic Convolutional Network for Knowledge Graph Completion
authors: Yanni Feng, Haitao Yu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Existing knowledge graph completion models based on convolutional neural networks still face limitations in feature representation. The unified convolutional perspective struggles to fully capture local variations within complex structures. And when feature dimensions increase significantly, redundant information may obscure key features. To address these issues, this paper proposes a knowledge graph completion model based on multi-channel dynamic convolutional network. The model first employs a reshaping combined with a dynamic convolution kernel mechanism to achieve local adaptive modeling and deep latent feature extraction. Subsequently, it introduces alternating reshaping and static convolution kernel structures to extract multi-scale surface features, thereby balancing feature diversity and global consistency. Finally, a self-calibrating attention mechanism selects the most representative features. Experiments on four public benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed model achieves significant performance improvements over existing methods in link prediction tasks, validating its effectiveness in modeling complex relationships and feature representation.

title: Redefining Systolic Efficiency with Pipelining ML Acceleration for High-Throughput AI Inference
authors: Suvitha PS, Aathmanesan T
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This paper proposes an optimized systolic array architecture tailored for AI acceleration, leveraging hybrid pipelining, Radix-8 encoding, and a CSA–PPA-based accumulation scheme. Distinct from traditional carry-portioned or operand-decomposed approaches, the design integrates a hybrid-pipelined dataflow with asynchronous summation, enabling high-throughput multiply-and-accumulate (MAC) operations with minimal clock-phase dependencies. The hybrid architecture includes delay-balanced logic, parallel prefix adder paths, and lightweight partial product registers that collectively reduce critical path delay and power consumption. The architecture was synthesized and evaluated on an Xilinx FPGA platform, achieving a delay of 13.353 ns and dynamic power of 154.05 mW, with a 26.4% delay improvement and 24.8% energy savings compared to baseline implementations. These results highlight the feasibility of the design for resource-constrained AI edge inference, offering scalable performance while minimizing area overhead and clock distribution complexity.

title: Microwave Characterization of Cork as a Sustainable Substrate for Antenna and Sensor Applications
authors: Amir Zahedi, Robert Salama, Karthick Thiyagarajan, Ranjith Liyanapathirana
abstract: In this paper, cork material is investigated as a sustainable substrate for antenna and sensor applications. The dielectric properties of cork, including dielectric constant, dielectric loss factor and loss tangent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\tan \delta)$</tex>, are measured using the Agilent 85070E Dielectric Probe Kit over the frequency range of 250 MHz to 20 GHz. Based on the obtained data, an inset-fed microstrip patch antenna is designed and simulated in CST Studio Suite at 2.45 GHz to demonstrate the feasibility of cork as a sustainable and feasible substrate for antenna and sensor applications. It is found that the cork antenna can achieve electrical performance comparable to a standard FR4 substrate microstrip patch antenna at 2.45 GHz with a reflection coefficient magnitude <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\boldsymbol{S}_{11}\right)$</tex>, of -24 dB, a gain of 8.18 dBi and a radiation efficiency of 93%.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Millimeter-Wave MIMO Antenna at 39 GHz for On-Body 5G (n260) Band
authors: Devraj Gautam, Abdul Quaiyum Ansari, Binod Kumar Kanaujia
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Design and performance evaluation of a compact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 2$</tex> MIMO antenna at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 9\ G H z}$</tex> frequency is offered for on-to-off body data transmission in the new radio (n260) band. The design is implemented on a Rogers RO3010 substrate of 0.72 mm thickness, enabling miniaturization and stable resonance. A slotted patch with parallel metallic strips is added to enhance impedance matching and significantly reduce coupling between elements. A bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.2 \text{GHz}(37.7-40.9 \text{GHz})$</tex>, a gain of 4.59 dB and excellent port isolation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{35} \mathbf{dB}$</tex> are achieved. Furthermore, the SAR value remains as low as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0621\ \mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> at 1 mW input power, confirming user safety in on-body applications. MIMO performance is validated, and the envelope correlation coefficient value is well below 0.01, confirming low inter-element correlation. It demonstrates the suitability of the antenna for reliable and effective mm-wave body-centric communication systems in healthcare and IoMT (Internet of Medical Things) applications.

title: AI-Powered Cybersecurity Models for Fraud Detection in IoT-Enabled Smart Healthcare Devices
authors: Nilesh Dnyaneshwar Bhandarwar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The fast expansion of IoT-enabled smart healthcare devices has raised the risk of financial fraud through unusual network transactions that in turn endanger patient data integrity and clinical operations. This research work targets the problem of identifying the most infrequent fraudulent cases in severely imbalanced IoT traffic of the real world. Traditional single models have a poor recall of the minority class, a high number of false positives that disrupt clinical workflows and limited adaptability to new attack patterns in resource-constrained environments. The stacked ensemble of XGBoost, TabTransformer and NGBoost with a logistic regression meta-learner trained on SMOTE-balanced data, as proposed, was able to achieve 96 % accuracy, 96 % macro-F1, and 0.986 AUC. By using the most important network features (DirPkt, DstJitter, Dur) it only allows 22 false positives and 14 false negatives, which makes it possible to deploy it in a lightweight manner at the edge and thus provides a reliable real-time safeguard of medical IoT ecosystems that are sensitive to security breaches.

title: Anatomy-Aware Deep Learning Approach for Schizophrenia Diagnosis Using Structural MRI
authors: L. Reetha, R. Gnanajeyaraman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Schizophrenia is a persistent mental condition which is characterized by severe structural and functional impairment in particular systems in the brain, which usually results in cognitive, emotional and behavioural disorders. Extensive use of traditional diagnosis is based on the subjective assessment of the symptoms, and it can lead to the late or inaccurate determination of the disorder. To cope with this issue, the current paper will suggest an effective hybrid model of deep learning that incorporates both anatomical segmentation and classification of identifying the presence of schizophrenia on the basis of high-resolution structural MRI data. The pipeline starts off with an intensive preprocessing step that includes skull stripping, bias field correction, normalisation to MNI space, resampling to a uniform <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$128 \times 128 \times 128$</tex> voxel grid, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$z$</tex>-score intensity normalisation, producing standardised and clean input data. An Attention U-Net architecture is then used to divide the important regions of the brain like the prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, and thalamus as well as to apply attention gates to select such features that are of much interest in schizophrenia and eliminate irrelevant business information. To train a fine-tuned ResNet-18 model to binary classification (schizophrenia vs. control) with dropout regularization and gradual unfreezing strategy to optimize learning is then trained using these anatomically segmented outputs. The decisions of the model are also explained with the help of Grad-CAM visualizations to provide an idea about important discriminative areas. The proposed method demonstrates excellent diagnostic accuracy (93.4 %), F1-score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(93.1 \%)$</tex>, and AUC-ROC (0.961) when evaluated on the OpenNeuro dataset with 176 subjects using 5-fold crossvalidation, and the result is significantly high in comparison with baseline CNN and U-Net models. This integrated assessment methodology shows both diagnostic validity and clinical elucidation and is a promising technique in objective diagnosis of schizophrenia on the basis of the image.

title: Research on the Performance of Wireless Optical Networks Using System DWDM
authors: A. N. Nabiyeva, B. G. Ibrahimov, V. P. Shuvalov
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article discusses a method for transmitting information over a wireless channel other than a radio channel. A wireless optical data transmission system implemented using wavelength division multiplexing technology is considered. The spectral efficiency modulation formats, optical system capacities, and signal-to-noise ratios in multiservice optical telecommunications networks using wavelength division multiplexing technology are analyzed. The purpose of this paper is to explore methods for calculating the performance characteristics wireless optical cellular networks based on a DWDM system. The operating principles wireless optical network nodes and the impact atmospheric factors and turbulence on transmission systems are examined. A study of wireless optical networks proposed a new approach for calculating their performance indicators. The calculation methods considered take into account the efficiency modulation methods, the transmission range optical signals, the received signal power taking into account atmospheric factors, and the frequency efficiency the optical transmission system.

title: Improving Robustness of Large Language Models Used in Healthcare Through Prompt Engineering
authors: Jonathan O’Berry, Indika Kahanda, Upulee Kanewala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been increasingly used in a variety of domains, including the medical field. Although much research has been done on improving the accuracy of medical LLMs, very little research has been done on the improvement of the robustness of medical LLMs. This study attempts to improve the robustness of medical LLMs through prompt engineering. Specifically, this study looks to utilize few-shot prompting, providing both positive and negative examples in prompts, as input into a medical question and answering application. This study found that robustness improved as more examples were provided, though there were diminishing returns. Five-shot prompting (providing five examples) performed the best compared to the prompting with fewer shots.

title: Multi-Modal Agricultural Perception: Fusing Deep Optical Networks with Software-Defined Acoustic Sensing
authors: Subham Kumar Beura, Mayank Godara, Mayank Paul, Saurav Kumar, Ravi Kumar, Amanpreet Kaur
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Robots in agriculture have a problem. They cannot easily find where a fruit is in 3D space. Expensive sensors are usually needed. Most systems use LiDAR or RGB-D cameras. These are too costly for small farms. This paper tests a different method. We combined a standard camera with a software-based acoustic sensor. This finds depth without adding new hardware. First, we used YOLOv11 to find the mangoes in the image. Then, we used a smartphone's audio hardware. We used the Phyphox tool to send sound waves. This measured the distance. We found that the phone's internal audio stack was better than an Arduino sensor. The Arduino had too much timing error. Our tests show this hybrid system works. It detects if a fruit is real or a picture. It also calculates the size accurately.

title: Problem of Machine-Generated Texts in the Academic Environment of Students
authors: Anastasia V. Ermakova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This article examines how students are using texts generated by neural networks in the educational process, and the associated problems of knowledge assessment. It considers statistics on the use of generative models in preparing coursework, laboratory work and tests, and in creating scientific materials. The structure and characteristics of the generated responses are analyzed to identify characteristic linguistic markers and template constructions. A comprehensive approach to identifying machine-generated text has been developed. This includes analyzing style and content, comparing with previous work, verifying facts and sources, using specialized tools and testing knowledge orally. The necessity of a critical approach to the use of neural networks, the importance of maintaining academic integrity and developing independent thinking among students in the context of the active introduction of artificial intelligence are emphasized.

title: Hybrid Anomaly-Based Intrusion Detection System for SDN-Enabled IoT Networks
authors: Olha Shpur, Taras Havryliv, Andriy Luntovskyy
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) deployments has increased the scale and complexity of modern network infrastructures, making them vulnerable to diverse distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks, including high-volume floods and low-intensity stealthy behaviors. Traditional signature-based intrusion detection approaches are often ineffective in such environments due to traffic heterogeneity, dynamic attack patterns, and limited computational capabilities of IoT devices. This paper presents a hybrid anomaly-based intrusion detection system for Software-Defined Networking (SDN)–enabled IoT networks that combines three complementary detection mechanisms: entropy-based traffic source distribution analysis, protocol-level heuristic inspection of TCP SYN traffic, and a machine learning–based behavioral detector using the Isolation Forest algorithm. All detection components are deployed within the SDN controller, enabling centralized traffic visibility and real-time telemetry processing without introducing computational overhead on resource-constrained IoT devices. A majority-voting scheme is employed to aggregate partial decisions from individual detectors and improve robustness against diverse attack characteristics. The proposed system is implemented using the Ryu SDN controller and evaluated in a cloud-based experimental environment under multiple traffic scenarios, including normal IoT operation, SYN flood and UDP flood attacks, botnet-like distributed traffic, and low-intensity stealth attacks. Experimental results show that the hybrid approach effectively detects volumetric and certain low-rate attack patterns while maintaining stable behavior during normal traffic conditions. The study highlights the benefits of combining heterogeneous detection methods within an SDN architecture and discusses limitations related to distributed botnet traffic and threshold selection.

title: Wearable Antenna Design for Medical WBAN Applications Supporting Next-Generation Wireless Systems (5G/6G)
authors: Ahmed Kudhair Daraj, Mahmood Farhan Mosleh, Raed Abd Alhameed, Ghalib Najm Radad, Inas Fadhil Jaleel
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative wearable antenna design for fifth and sixth generation wireless communications. The proposed antenna is designed for Wireless Body Area Networks (WBAN) and telemetry devices specific to biomedical applications. The principal challenge facing researchers is the flexible configuration and dimensions of antennas in relation to efficiency. The suggested antenna is made up of a single element with a unique rectangular shape. It is built on a Rogers RT/duroid substrate that is 0.29 mm thick and lossy. The dimensions of the proposed antennas are 10 mm x 10 mm for both the ground and substrate. The patch measures 4.12 mm by 3.13 mm. The proposed design features curves on either side of the antenna. Two circles in the antenna corner serve as pattern ratio balances that ascertain the optimal dimensions and positioning. Three slots are optimized to attain the requisite bands for 5G and 6G functionality. Three frequency bands have been designated: the initial band at 28 GHz for 5G applications and the subsequent bands at 68 GHz and 80 GHz for 6G applications. Furthermore, the ideal design optimizes both gain and bandwidth, achieving a VSWR of 1.006 and a gain of 9.75 dBi. The innovative design addresses the primary issue of varying antenna parameters due to human motion.

title: Method of Preventing Human Operator Errors Using Technology for Identifying Possible Problem Situations during Operation of ERP Systems
authors: Evgeniy Lavrov, Ihor Klymenko, Olga Siryk, Artem Bykov, Yana Chybiriak, Maksym Ostapenko, Tetiana Shovkoplias
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The problems of reliable operation of ERP systems are described. The influence of the human factor on failures and malfunctions in information processing is studied. It is proposed to move from the methodology of statistical analysis of human errors to the methodology of early warning of problem situations. To solve the problem of increasing the reliability of ERP systems, the methods of data mining are used, in particular the method of constructing associative rules. The principles of constructing a special software module aimed at preventing errors are developed. An analysis of the effectiveness of the method is carried out, which confirms the feasibility of its widespread use in the design and operation of ERP systems.

title: Enhancement of User Query Processing in Distributed Databases
authors: Olena Hordiichuk-Bublivska, Oksana Urikova, Andrii Masiuk, Oleksandr Androsiuk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper examines the problem of optimizing data processing in recommender systems operating within distributed PostgreSQL databases. It presents a systematic multi-layer SQL optimization framework that integrates indexing, materialized views, execution-level monitoring, and physical resource analysis. Experiments were conducted in a distributed PostgreSQL environment using key performance metrics, including execution time, CPU utilization, disk I/O operations, and system throughput. The experimental results demonstrate substantial performance improvements: query execution time decreased by 98%, CPU utilization was reduced by more than 50% (from 93.5% to 46%), and disk read operations were completely eliminated. System throughput increased from 9.26 million to 529.65 million rows per second. The proposed approach provides both theoretical and practical insights into the construction of high-performance distributed recommender systems, especially in cloud-based architectures. The results underscore the importance of multi-layer optimization and provide a foundation for future work involving stream analytics, automated ETL/ELT pipelines, and multi-cloud performance evaluation.

title: A Canvas Framework for Participatory Design of Cultural Products Using Generative AI
authors: Pruet Putjorn, Pattayaporn Unroj, Pollavat Prapattong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper introduces the “HHAI Canvas,” a novel participatory design tool for co-creating culturally grounded products using Generative AI (GenAI). The framework supports community engagement in the design of physical handicraft products by linking cultural identity (“Heart”), artisan skill (“Hand”), and AI-driven ideation (“AI Harmony”). The canvas guides local participants to articulate key product insights through storytelling, sketching, and coevaluation, enabling GenAI to serve as a tool for cultural expression rather than a replacement for human creativity. We demonstrate the framework's application in a Chiang Saen community workshop in Northern Thailand, where artisans applied the canvas to create new textile, food, and craft product ideas. The study utilized the Gemini 2.5 Flash Image model to generate high-fidelity visualizations while maintaining subject consistency. The resulting designs preserved cultural essence while gaining appeal for contemporary markets. The study positions the canvas as a scalable boundary object for community-based innovation, showing how local knowledge and AI can be harmonized to support sustainable design and cultural continuity.

title: Retraction Notice: Spatio Temporal Feature-based Weapon and Violence Detection from Video Surveillance using Deep Learning
authors: Anitha G, Siddharth Shanker M, Durgam Muni Soma Sukrith, Loakeshvaran S K, Venkatraman M, Sadam Mohamed Usman
publish: 2023 7th International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: The increasing incidence of violence in public spaces has made developing efficient and accurate weapon detection systems necessary. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model built on the MobileNet structure has been proposed for the detection of violence and weapons. It examines each frame of a video stream to look for indications of violence or the presence of weapons. The proposed system is trained on a dataset of simulated violent incidents. The dataset includes fights, gunfights, knife attacks, and diverse environmental and lighting conditions. The system is excellent for use in surveillance and security systems and 93.86% accuracy is achieved. It can be implemented on embedded systems or mobile devices due to its high efficiency and minimal computing demands. The proposed method can increase public safety by notably increasing the efficiency of security and surveillance systems.

title: Retraction Notice: An IoT Based Electromyography Signal Transmission from sEMG Electrodes to Client's Server with ICT Infrastructure
authors: P. M. Pavithra, M. Balakrishnan, A. Anandaraj, M. Ramya, R. Raja Sudharsan
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Bio-electric signal like Electromyography (EMG) can measure signal data from nerves and muscles separately in the particular region of affected nerve or muscles from a Human Being. For incorporating these measurements, can use a non-invasive technique. The Surface electrode can be used for measuring these problems in non-invasive fashion. Here, the idea of Internet of Things (IoT) can bring included advantage to applications with bio-electric signals to communicate to the doctors and patients such as human-PC cooperation by incorporating different advances for example, sensors, remote correspondence and information science. In this research, we worked on a remote bio-electric signals to another remote observing handling framework. A model with the contextual investigation of nerve and muscle signal acknowledgment utilizing 4-channel sEMG signals is executed. A Gaussian filter is prepared from one individual's sEMG signals with 4-nerve regions of human body such as ulnar, radial, median and radial. The introduced IoT application framework is actualized based on an 11-terminal bio-electric signal measuring device, with Arduino based Controller, Gateway, ESP8266 (Wifi) module, Server being the major mediums of remote transmission of signal data. The signal transmits at 35KB/s. In the framework, the ongoing sEMG signal from surface electrode is filtered from noise and other distortions using is given to the controller for performing the operation.

In our proposed technique the specialist customer requires administration with explicit useful and specialized necessities. By utilizing verification id specialists find the cloud administration dependent on administration motor. title: Retraction Notice: An Efficient Approach for Secured Data Aggregation Against Security Attacks in WSN
authors: R Menaka, R Dhanagopal, N Archana
publish: 2020 Fourth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: The information gathered from singular hubs is collected at a base station or host PC. To lessen vitality utilization, numerous frameworks additionally act in-organize the conglomeration of sensor information in the middle of the road hubs on the way to the base station. Most existing total calculations and frameworks do exclude any security arrangements, and subsequently, these frameworks are defenseless against a wide assortment of assaults. To address the security issue, propose one improved new technique. Secure data aggregation provides security to network from an attacker. Secure information accumulation procedure can likewise improve the power and exactness of data. Iterative separating calculation is one of the calculations to perform secure information accumulation and gives security to the whole system. Iterative sifting calculation all the while total information from numerous sources and give trust appraisal of these sources, through relating weight factor appointed to the information given by each source. Iterative sifting calculation with vitality model is utilized for giving more exactness to the Wireless sensor arrange condition.

title: Retraction Notice: MAGRISYS: A SMART AND UBIQUITOUS CONTROLLED – ENVIRONMENT AGRICULTURE SYSTEM
authors: M D Kamalesh, J Albert Mayan, Yovan Felix, B Sai Sumanth, B Sai Tej
publish: 2018 2nd International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC), 2018 2nd International Conference on
abstract: Horticulture is the significant control of numerous nations and robotization in this field can enhance creation of products. Computerization is principally required for observing the earth of harvests utilizing sensors and controlling the water system for better development. This robotization has is being adjusted gradually and another issue is security for the gadgets utilized for mechanization. On the off chance that every one of these criteria are served, at that point we can have a superior development of solid yields with powerful use of labor. This venture is one such method in which an outline of robotization in water system and security to the water system is given. Different sensors are utilized in the homestead arrive for checking different parameters of the dirt, water substance and interloper in the ranch arrive and so forth. Soil dampness sensor is utilized for observing soil dampness content; water level sensor is utilized for checking the water in the homestead arrive both amid typical atmosphere and amid stormy season. PIR sensor is utilized to screen gatecrasher in the ranch arrive room. Zigbee and GSM innovation is utilized for making three route interchanges for agriculturist, cultivate arrive and the engine room. Matlab is additionally included to catch the picture of plant and give the data of the plant stages to the agriculturist.

title: Retraction Notice: Monitoring System In Industry Using IoT
authors: M. Jagadesh, M. Saravanan, V. Narayanan, M. Priya Vadhana, K. Logeshwaran

IoT is utilized for transmitting and accepting information. This frameworks are utilized to screen mechanical machines. In this procedure little scale modern applications like fluid dimension control, vitality checking and so on are observed remotely by mobiles and workstations. The fundamental thought of this paper is to outline significance of IoT to screen mechanical applications.

title: Retraction Notice: Smart Access Control System For Covid Safety Smart Access Control System during Covid
authors: S G Rahul, D Subitha, S Velmurugan, R Chitra, Avinash Reddy, Grande Nishanth
publish: 2022 8th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: During the period of prevailing unsettled COVID pandemic, the countries and states started to plan reopening during which necessitates the non-contact temperature evaluation gadgets as a part of a preliminary look at access points to identify the humans with elevated body temperatures. Despite the utilization of these devices, temperature assessment restricted the impact on lowering the spread of COVID-19. Non-contact temperature measuring devices are used to measure the temperature of any person. Detection of a high temperature is one huge manner to pick out a person who might also have COVID-19 contamination. In this project, a room environment is created in which certain precautions are taken. A laser diode and receiver are used to detect the entrance of a person, and the system also detects the body temperature of the entering person. If the temperature is less than a threshold temperature entry for the person is permitted or else the entry is denied. This system also has a feature where it permits only a pre-determined number of persons inside the room. It also facilities to view the allowed temperature, the number of people to be allowed in the room and the number of people present actively using a Bluetooth App. This system aimed to be useful to combat the spread of COVID infections.

title: Junctionless FinFET-based Hydrogen Gas Sensors: A Comprehensive Review of Design, Performance, and Reliability
authors: Deborah Roseline P, Kavitha V P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This comprehensive analysis investigates current developments in junctionless FinFET-based hydrogen gas sensors, with an emphasis on design innovations, performance improvements, and reliability challenges. The research explores various methods, including ferroelectric gate stacks, negative capacitance integration, spacer-engineered SOI structures, and strained silicon designs. The study evaluates electrical characterization, sensitivity analysis, and reliability assessments across multiple studies, drawing attention to noise-dependent sensitivity and the impact of gate oxide on device reliability. This review offers a distinctive viewpoint on the present situation and potential future developments of junctionless FinFET-based hydrogen gas sensing technology by combining results from several study undertaking. The focus on junctionless FinFET architectures, a more recent development in gas sensing applications, is what makes this paper distinctive. The applicability of different sensor configurations for specific applications is evaluated, along with associated trade-offs. It provides a comparative analysis of several sensor architecture, such as JL, JLNC, MFIS/MFMIS, and SJL structures, assessing their suitability for particular use cases and performance attributes. The study examines simulation-based sensors, focusing on those that make use of Technology Computer-Aided Design (TCAD) tools. The identification and investigation of reliability challenges in nanoscale gas sensing, such as noise-related problems, aging effects, and gate-induced drain leakage (GIDL), is a major emphasis of this paper. The study investigates how different design factors and material selections affect the sensitivity, reaction time, selectivity, and long-term stability of sensors. For increased sensitivity in applications, the combination of Pt/Pd and SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> is given particular attention. This review provides engineers and researchers involved in the field of advanced gas-sensing technologies with a comprehensive understanding of the most recent advancements and potential research avenues in junctionless FinFET-based hydrogen gas sensors by combining insights from various research efforts.

title: Audio Feature Based Rotation Speed Extraction of Washing Machine's Dehydration Stage
authors: Binwen Luo, Cuiyun Gao, Yang Zhao, Zengyuan Wu, Mengting Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In the national standard GB/T 4288-2025, measuring a washing machine’s dehydration-stage speed requires attaching sensors to the drum, which necessitates machine disassembly and thus constitutes invasive, inconvenient detection. To address this issue, this paper designs an audio acquisition system with an amplifying circuit and high-order Butterworth low-pass filter, and proposes a non-contact, non-invasive speed measurement method that extracts speed information from dehydration-stage audio signals. Methods: Frequency-domain signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) was used to compare the self-designed low-cost system with high-fidelity recording equipment; Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) was then applied to extract signal spectra and calculate dehydration-stage speed curves. Results: Experimental verification on a pulsator washing machine showed that the self-designed system matched the performance of high-fidelity equipment and effectively extracted speed curves, achieving an SNR of 16–17 dB in the 500–2000 Hz band and a speed curve correlation coefficient of 0.9989 with the comparison device. Conclusion: The proposed system and method are expected to be extended to speed measurement of general rotating motor equipment.

title: Quadrifilar Helix Antenna Satellite Receiver Antenna
authors: Himanshu K H, Kanishk R M, Jagadish R, Mohammed Ataur Rahaman, Lakshmi C R, Nandini Kumari B
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The development of a Quadrifilar Helix (QFH) antenna for real-time weather data acquisition from Meteor-M satellites is a challenging area in the satellite industry. Design and Performance of a Quadrifilar Helix (QFH) Antenna for Meteor-M 2-3 LRPT Reception at 1 3 7. 9 MHz is presented in this paper. The Quadrifilar Helix (QFH) antenna optimized for receiving the Low-Resolution Picture Transmission (LRPT) signal from the Meteor-M 2-3 and Meteor-M 2-4 polar orbiting weather satellite is discussed. The system operates at the satellite's primary downlink frequency of 137.9 MHz in the VHF band. The QFH antenna is designed to efficiently receive the satellite's Right Hand Circularly Polarized (RHCP) signal. For the signal reception software defined radio, RTL-SDR is used with software such as SDR++, Sat dump to process satellite signals.

title: Deep Learning for Automated Lung Nodule Detection in Low-Dose CT Scans
authors: Santosh Reddy P, Geeta L S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: One of the biggest causes of cancer-related death globally is still lung cancer. Survival chances are greatly increased by early diagnosis of lung nodules with Low-Dose Computed Tomography (LDCT) screening. However, interpreting a hand LDCT scan is subjective, takes a lot of time, and is easily affected by differences between observers, especially when noise as well small tumours are present. It is the goal of this project to make a reliable as well as automated deep learning-based system for accurately identifying lung nodules in LDCT images. The study's goal is to create a 3-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (3D-CNN)) structure that uses volumetric spatial data to better detect small lumps and handle noise. This is how the network will be built and tested to make sure it can reliably find lung nodules. We test the suggested system using the open-source LIDC-IDRI dataset. The data is enhanced, and a thorough preparation pipeline is implemented, which includes filtering out Gaussian noise, segmenting the lungs, and normalizing the intensity. The Adam optimizer & binary cross-entropy loss are used to train a 3D-CNN model using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$64 \times 64 \times 64$</tex> voxel volumetric patches. The F1-score, AUCROC, sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy are the performance measures used. The suggested model has a great diagnostic performance, with an AUC of 0.94 as well as excellent sensitivity, especially for small nodules (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geqslant 3 ~\text{mm}$</tex>). Qualitative study backs up the idea that robust detection works even when LDCT is noisy. The suggested system is a dependable computeraided detection tool that may help radiologists find lung cancer early, cut down on the amount of effort they have to do to get a diagnosis, and help them make better clinical decisions.

title: Context-Aligned Large Language Models for Situational Awareness in Critical Grid Events
authors: Luciano A. Paniagua, Paras Mandal
abstract: Power system operators require real-time situational awareness to maintain grid reliability during normal and emergency conditions. During grid emergencies, the operator decision-making process is always high-stakes. Insufficient situational awareness, such as incorrect or untimely responses, can be the cause for major blackouts resulting in adverse impacts on millions of customers as well as huge economic losses. Traditional emergency management systems lack the ability to synthesize multi-source regulatory and operational information during time-critical scenarios. This paper presents a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system for power system operator decision support leveraging a Large Language Model (LLM) with domain-specific knowledge retrieval to address three operational queries: emergency procedure lookup, compliance interpretation, and event response. This involves hybrid retrieval combining sparse and dense searches over local documents, followed by prompting a local quantized LLM. Experimental results indicate that the proposed framework achieves an overall faithfulness of 0.83 and answer relevancy of 0.97, indicating a high degree of potential for improving situational awareness in critical grid operations.

title: AI-Enabled Innovation Management
authors: Aaryan Gupta, Divya Thakur, Kushagra Shekhar, Aditya Kumar Singh, Naman Sharma, Tushar Chahar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper provides a detailed examination of the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to transform innovation management practices. This paper examines how organizations adopt AI technologies like machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics into their innovation processes to boost efficiency and effectiveness. The paper starts off with explaining why innovation management in modern-day businesses is important and demonstrates how AI is supporting primary phases of the innovation life cycle, such as ideation, product development, and market intelligence. With an inclusion of history, existing patterns, and real-life examples, this research elucidates the game-changing potential of AI to lead innovation. Subsequently, the paper explores the organizational challenges, ethical implications, and technical restrictions on the deployment of AI. The paper concludes by highlighting emerging trends in AI and research gaps, providing useful insights for future AI-enabled innovation management advancements. The results highlight the need for companies to strategically implement AI technologies to remain competitive and promote sustainable innovation.

title: Design and calculation of LLC resonant converter
authors: Anima Kumari, Matta Mani Shankar, Mukhlesur Rahman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: This paper presents a comparison of PID, and Fractional Order PID (FOPID) controllers used in LLC resonant converters designed by Half Bridge (H-B) and Full Bridge (F-B). An evolutionary optimization framework based on Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and the traditional Ziegler-Nichols (Z-N) method are applied to tune both controller types. Closed-loop simulations based on MATLAB/Simulink are used to analyze dynamic performance considering rise time, settling time, and percentage overshoot as performance metrics. The findings show that PSO-tuned controllers significantly increase response speed and reduce overshoot, especially for FOPID control in Full-Bridge topology. These results support to improving power electronic converter control strategies through the application of soft computing techniques.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Unemployment in Indonesia with a Panel Data Approach
authors: Nur Azmi Karim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study examines the influence of artificial intelligence on unemployment in Indonesia. Comprehensive empirical studies are limited in the literature. Therefore, this study uses a dataset, which covers 34 provinces from 2013 to 2022. Researching the use of computers to explain AI and the unemployment rate. In an empirical approach, we control the dynamic effects of unemployment by using panel data to determine the effect of AI on unemployment. As a variable control, we use economic growth. We use unemployment as a dependent variable and AI as an independent variable. We assume that current unemployment is affected by unemployment in previous periods. All data in this study uses secondary data obtained from the website of the Central Statistics Agency for several years from 2013 to 2022. This study uses panel data which is a combination of cross section data and time series data using the eViews 13 analysis tool. To facilitate cross section data in this study, a sequence number is used to mention the name of the province being analyzed. To facilitate the use of provincial data in Indonesia, the author provides a sequence number code for each province The results of this study are that the unemployment rate of the previous year and economic growth have an effect on the current unemployment rate, while AI has not yet had an effect on the unemployment rate.

title: An Information System Analysis and Design of Private Tutoring at PT eBdesk Teknologi
authors: Durahman Firmansyah Alamsyah, Zalfa Gita Saputri, Angelina Ervina Jeanette Egeten
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Private tutoring is a form of non-formal education that provides personalized learning support to students outside regular school hours. PT eBdesk Teknologi, a software house and big data company with an open culture, welcomes ideas to address ongoing challenges. One major issue is the lack of an integrated system for scheduling, learning material management, attendance tracking, and student progress reporting, which are currently handled manually via WhatsApp. This often leads to scattered information, missed schedules, and miscommunication between tutors and parents. This study aims to analyze and design a mobile application prototype to help private tutors manage learning activities more systematically. The analysis methodology used is the System Development Life Cycle with the prototyping model, while the design methodology applied is the Unified Modeling Language. The resulting prototype includes scheduling, material management, attendance, and reporting features to support a more organized tutoring process and ease the workload of private tutors. The final stage involves evaluation through interviews with stakeholders and users to validate the design. This research also contributes to SDG 4 (Quality Education), as it promotes inclusive and equitable learning opportunities by improving access, structure, and transparency in private tutoring.

title: Physics-Based AR Framework for Realistic and Interactive Home Furnishing Visualization
authors: Anshuman Dhara, Neha Gode, Gitanjali Gangurde, Anant V. Nimkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Traditional AR tools often have functional limitations. They typically support placing only one object at a time and do not remember object positions between sessions. These tools usually depend on simple 2D images or poorly optimized models that do not interact realistically with the environment. This paper presents a framework that uses Augmented Reality (AR) and physics based graphic technologies to achieve realistic spatial visualization. The system combines edge detection, plane detection, spatial scanning, and real time rendering to improve physical-digital interaction in augmented environments. Built with Unity 3D and AR Foundation, the framework allows for accurate placement and anchoring of 3D models in a real-world space. A key feature of the system is the support for placing and positioning multiple virtual objects at once while remembering their position and direction. Our framework uses model optimization through level of detail (LOD), physics-based rendering, and lighting techniques like diffuse and specular reflection to enhance realism. The 3D assets created in Blender are optimized for mobile performance without losing visual quality. The framework supports 3D models with realistic interactions using Unity's PhysX engine, all managed through a responsive user interface for manipulating models.

title: Efficient Visual Cyberbullying Detection via Knowledge Distillation from EfficientNet to Lightweight CNNs
authors: Pal Dave, Subhram Dasgupta, Kushal Badal, Xiaohong Yuan, Kaushik Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The rise of social media platforms has resulted in a significant rise in cyberbullying, especially through visual content that can inflict considerable psychological damage on victims. Although current methods have shown effectiveness in identifying cyberbullying via textual analysis, the detection of image-based cyberbullying continues to be a significantly underexplored and important issue. This study introduces a framework that uses knowledge distillation to develop an efficient and interpretable system to detect cyberbullying in images. This study employs EfficientNet-B5, which incorporates dual attention mechanisms, as a teacher model to facilitate training of a lightweight enhanced sequential CNN student model. The knowledge distillation process allows the student model to achieve competitive performance while ensuring appropriate computational efficiency for real-time deployment. To improve transparency and trust in automated decision making, we incorporate various explainability techniques such as GradCAM, GradCAM++, and LayerCAM to offer detailed insight into model predictions. The experimental results on 19,300 images indicate that the EfficientNet-B5 teacher model achieves an accuracy of 98.34%. In contrast, the enhanced CNN student model maintains competitive performance with 96.59% accuracy, utilizing significantly fewer computational resources. This reflects a mere 1.75% decrease in performance while making considerable efficiency improvements. The explainability analysis demonstrates that our model effectively identifies pertinent visual cues, including threatening gestures, offensive symbols, and contextual elements associated with cyberbullying behavior. This study addresses the increasing demand for efficient and interpretable AI systems that improve online safety and ensure transparency in automated content management processes.

title: Effective Cleaning Process of Water Heat Exchangers and Water Jackets of Motors and Generators
authors: Henk de Swardt
abstract: High-capacity water-cooled electric machines are critical to modern wind energy conversion systems and hydroelectric plants, where superior thermal density and compact design are essential for sustainable power generation. However, the reliability of these renewable assets is compromised by the gradual accumulation of mineral scale, which acts as a thermal insulator. Previous analysis has shown that even a 0.5 mm limestone layer can reduce the theoretical insulation life of a wind generator by up to 30%, threatening the long-term sustainability of the installation. Addressing the maintenance gap where standard hydrostatic pressure testing fails to detect this efficiency-degrading thermal barrier, this paper presents a comprehensive technical protocol for the chemical cleaning and restoration of water jackets and heat exchangers. The methodology compares acid descaling agents – including citric acid, acetic acid, and commercial blends – based on effectiveness, safety, and material compatibility, with specific attention to metallurgy common in wind turbine generators (e.g., mild steel water jackets). Cleaning protocols are proposed for both routine preventative maintenance and severe fouling conditions, incorporating safety protocols and post-cleaning verification. Implementation of these protocols is essential to safeguard the operational lifespan, availability, and energy efficiency of critical renewable infrastructure.

title: DeepTime: Meta-Learning Temporal Dynamics for Salesforce Opportunity Forecasting
authors: Sharan Sukesh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: We present DeepTime, a meta-learning framework for forecasting Salesforce sales opportunities. DeepTime employs an inner–outer loop meta-optimization strategy to capture evolving temporal dynamics, enabling accurate prediction of deal closures and revenue projections. To enhance model robustness, auxiliary retrieval-based augmentations (QEXT and TQGEN) enrich input features, but the primary contribution is the forecasting pipeline. Empirical evaluations on Salesforce datasets show superior performance compared to baselines, improving sales resource allocation and strategic planning.

title: BRAIN-Net: CNN-based Brain Stroke Detection for Improved Diagnosis
authors: Meriga Kiran Kumar, G. Zayaraz
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Stroke remains a major worldwide cause of death and chronic disability, highlighting the significance of early and accurate detection to improve patient outcomes and reduce healthcare burdens. This paper presents BRAIN-Net, an advanced automated brain stroke detection system utilizing deep learning techniques, specifically a CNN-based approach, for enhanced diagnostic accuracy and reliability. The suggested framework makes use of machine learning algorithms to classify ischemic and hemorrhagic strokes based on imaging data from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Computed Tomography (CT) scans. By leveraging Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), BRAIN-Net efficiently extracts critical features for early stroke detection and precise classification. The model achieves an impressive 98% accuracy, with sensitivity at 97%, specificity at 96.5%, and an F1-score of 97.2%. These findings reveal better diagnostic accuracy than conventional methods, and thus BRAIN-Net is a potential tool to assist clinicians with swift and accurate stroke diagnosis, enabling timely intervention and significantly enhancing prognosis in patients. BRAIN-Net, an optimized CNN-based deep learning framework, detects ischemic and hemorrhagic strokes from CT and MRI scans with superior accuracy, outperforming traditional models.

title: Design and Validation of Low Power High Efficient CMOS Ring Oscillator using CMOS Technology
authors: K. Srilakshmi, Kiran Mannem, K.Jamal, Manchalla.O.V.P.Kumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Oscillator circuits enable precise and dependable clock signals, which enable long-distance communication, in devices ranging from basic wristwatches to complex satellites. may construct an oscillator circuit in a number of methods, using either active or passive components. At this stage of mobile communication's development, compatibility and low battery consumption are paramount, yet there are advantages and disadvantages to every potential choice. By fusing the two concepts, the proposed device reaps the benefits of both a negative-skewed delay and a current-starved ring oscillator. To meet the power and frequency requirements of 5G mobile communication, this study presents a five-stage CMOS ring oscillator architecture. The suggested technique will generate a near-frequency in the Ka band. On average, the proposed method consumes 3.9 mW, with 3.92 mW going toward dynamic consumption and 4.3 mW going toward static consumption. The proposed method has the potential to produce a frequency of 30.01 GHz inside the Ka band. This gadget manages to run at high frequencies with little power consumption by combining negative-skewed and current-starved delay methods. This 45GPDK CMOS oscillator is ideal for 5G applications that demand fast, low-power clock signals since it can run on a 5V supply voltage.

title: A Bipolar Stochastic Bitstream Generator Based on Linear Feedback Shift Register
authors: Jian Chen, Yujie Wang, Yi Wang, Yaohua Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Stochastic computing (SC) is an emerging approximate computing paradigm that processes data in the form of stochastic bitstreams. The bipolar representation is widely adopted in SC systems to handle data ranges that include negative domains. However, existing designs for converting binary real values to bipolar stochastic bitstreams often suffer from complex circuit structures, high hardware overhead, and low conversion efficiency. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel high-accuracy enhanced bipolar stochastic number generator (EB-SNG). By leveraging an optimized comparison logic circuit, the proposed EB-SNG can efficiently map binary values directly into bipolar stochastic bitstreams. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed EB-SNG maintains superior conversion accuracy while significantly reducing hardware resource consumption. Compared with traditional sign-magnitude-based SNG designs, the EB-SNG achieves a better balance between circuit area and power consumption, providing an efficient interface solution for low-power stochastic computing systems.

title: Advanced Synergetic and Lyapunov based nonlinear control for PEM Fuel Cell System
authors: Sheraz Ahmad Babar, Eleonora Riva Sanseverino, Rossano Musca, Marco Ferraro
abstract: Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell System (PEMFCS), working principle is highly nonlinear in nature. The existing literature strategies are mostly based on nonlinear Sliding Mode Control (SMC). Performance is compromised due to the SMC inherent chattering phenomenon in power generation. Although higher versions SMC handled the chattering problem but with much increased computational complexity. This article aims to provide nonlinear based control strategy with moderate mathematical complexity and improved stability for maximum power extraction from PEMFCS. In this paper, the nonlinear Synergetic Controller (SC), Integral Synergetic Controller (ISC) and Lyapunov Redesign Controller (LRC) have been proposed to stabilize partial pressures of hydrogen and oxygen to protect PEMFCS from membrane damage during power generation. The stability of proposed controllers has been proved using Lyapunov theory, while the simulation results in MATLAB/Simulink show the outperformance of the proposed controllers in comparison to the existing SMC approach.

title: Digital Pose Trainer for Posture Correction and Rehabilitation in Physiotherapy
authors: Vivek Kumar M, Monika Shree S M, Nandhini S, Nithiya R, Rajashree P
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The goal of the new developed website AI Physio Trainer is to assist doctors in organizing the patient’s physical therapy process by using the benefits of online tools. The use of body movement tracking through a camera in the system also safeguards the patients as the exercises are done correctly to avoid injury. Another essential part of the concept is that doctors would be able to set up personalized exercises for every patient due to employing AI Physio Trainer. The possibility to get feedback and track the results on the chosen treatment course also contributes to the fact that doctors can set measurable goals for the patients, which can be useful for assessment of the patient’s condition change in the course of time. But also, patients can be engaged with the AI Physio Trainer where the program is designed to have his or her own program, and this will keep the patient motivated and relaxed as they recover from their injuries.

title: Deep Hybrid Vision System for Automated Clinical Grading of Diabetic Retinopathy
authors: N. Subhash Chandra, Yerram Sneha, Lakavath Rajitha, Nitesh Gaikwad, Ashok Kumar Vootla, Kokkula Shiva Prasad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: DR is one of the most significant problems of diabetes and the largest causes of blindness in the world, so the correct assessment of its stages, Mild, Moderate, Severe and Proliferative, is critical in terms of ensuring timely treatment. The word manual grading of fundus images is labor intensive and subject to variability, which provides the need to rely on good automated systems. The proposed project will utilize a hybrid deep learning architecture that is based on a Vision Transformer (ViT) more than a ResNet to identify DR stages. ResNet takes the local features like the microaneurysms and hemorrhases whereas ViT uses the global retinal structure and allows the model to examine both the micro-level and the macro-level of the image. The system was also trained and tested on more than 500 images in one stage and preprocessing and data augmentation were used to improve the performance.
