title: CarbonWise CX: An Agentic AI Framework for Carbon-Aware Customer Support Analytics Using RAG and LLM-Based Code Generation
authors: B.M. Beena, Thotapalli Sri Surya Manideep, Sneha Saragadam, Sailesh Rathi, Ramaswamy Ramesh, Vijay Holimath
abstract: Organizations accumulate sufficient amounts of structured and unstructured customer support data, but current systems like SQL queries and fixed dashboards still generate actionable insights on a delay, increasing operational expenses, and inefficient use of cloud energies. This project aims at developing a cloud-native, agentic AI-driven analytics system that can be used to improve the decision-making processes of enterprises without violating the principles of green cloud computing. The proposed system is called CarbonWise CX, which incorporates Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to perform qualitative reasoning, the dynamic generation of Python code, which entails the use of LLM when making quantitative decisions, and cloud-deployed versions that are scalable. The queries in natural language are handled via, agentic decision layer that automatically identifies which analytics workflow to choose. One of such innovations is the carbon-conscious cloud routing, the dynamic choice of the execution areas according to the current carbon intensity, with maintained latency and SLA limitations. The system with a deployed infrastructure on AWS EC2 will exhibit a 34.6 percent reduction in the number of carbon emissions per query in the operation using a modular architecture and on multi-region workloads simulated in the BAR. Also, 8,469 actual customer support records were analyzed resulting in 62.3 percent less manual tasks and 41.8 percent faster query response time than traditional workflows. The results of CarbonWise CX yield to achieve a better SLA compliance (29.5% of the significant improvement), to optimize cloud resource usage (36.7% of the significant improvement), and to minimize the work of the analyst (they were decreased significantly). The architecture shows how agentic AI and green cloud computing can provide intelligent and scalable, as well as environmentally friendly customer experience analytics. The article corresponds to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals SDG 12 (Responsible Consumption and Production), SDG 13 (Climate Action).

title: EAP-LSTM: A Bi-LSTM-based Deep Learning Framework for Quantitatively Predicting Enhancer Activity in Drosophila and Human Cell Lines_supp1-3620986.pdf
authors: Hao Wu
abstract: Enhancer activity plays a critical role in gene regulation, influencing various biological processes such as development and disease progression. Accurate prediction of enhancer activity is essential for understanding the mechanisms underlying gene regulation and enhancer function. This study introduces a novel deep learning framework, EAP-LSTM (Enhancer Activity Prediction based on Bi-LSTM), to quantitatively predict enhancer activity across different species and cell lines. The model integrates multiple feature modules, including Word2Vec-based representations of DNA sequences, reverse complement k-mer, mismatch k-mer features, and epigenomic data. Evaluated on six cell lines, including five human cell lines (A549, HCT116, HepG2, K562, and MCF-7) and one Drosophila cell line (S2), EAP-LSTM consistently outperforms state-of-the-art models, such as DeepSTARR and HEAP, in all datasets. For example, on the K562 dataset, EAP-LSTM achieves a Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.7944, outperforming DeepSTARR and HEAP by 13.65% and 2.73%, respectively. In addition, EAP-LSTM demonstrates strong performance in small-sample learning scenarios, showing clear improvements compared with baseline models. Furthermore, the study investigates the role of transcription factor binding sites (TFBSs) within enhancer regions, identifying critical motifs associated with enhancer activity. These findings not only improve enhancer prediction accuracy but also provide valuable insights into the molecular mechanisms underlying enhancer function.

title: AquaFed: Ascending Quantized Federated Learning on Heterogeneous Devices
authors: Juntao Hu, Hong Huang, Kuan Liu, Huimin Lu, Bingyi Liu, Dapeng Wu
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaboration across distributed edge devices but faces severe challenges in cross-device settings due to stringent memory and computation constraints. While quantization offers a natural remedy, existing techniques primarily target inference acceleration and fail to improve training efficiency. Consequently, current quantized FL frameworks only mitigate communication overhead while suffering from memory inefficiency during on-device training. Moreover, they exhibit Heterogeneity-induced optimization instability, as devices with varying bit-widths jointly participate in federated learning, introducing inconsistent quantization noise that hinders convergence and degrades final accuracy. In this work, we propose AquaFed, a memory-efficient Ascending quantized Federated learning framework tailored for heterogeneous edge environments. We first introduce Memory-Efficient Quantization (MEQ), which quantizes both weights and activation caches during training to drastically reduce peak memory usage and memory I/O. To address instability arising from heterogeneous precision, we theoretically establish the Quantization Noise Equivalence (QNE) Hypothesis: Quantization error exhibits a behavior analogous to the stochastic noise induced by a large learning rate in quantized federated learning. Guided by QNE, AquaFed adopts an ascending quantization schedule, allowing low-bit devices to lead early coarse-grained learning, while high-bit devices refine the model in later stages for stable convergence. Extensive experiments on vision and language benchmarks demonstrate that AquaFed consistently delivers superior accuracy, generalization, and memory efficiency compared to state-of-the-art methods. On CIFAR-10 with ResNet-18, AquaFed achieves a 20% accuracy improvement and 39% memory saving over the best existing framework.

title: Torque Analysis and Optimization Procedure of Spoke-Array PM Vernier Machines Considering Flux Density Harmonic Contributions
authors: Junzhou Li, Yuting Gao, Xiaoming Zha, Zhaohua Hu, Guifen Li
abstract: This paper analyzes the torque sources of the spoke-array permanent magnet vernier machines (SPMVMs) before and after the introductions of asymmetric split-teeth and flux bridges, with emphasis on the role of flux density harmonics. It identifies a fact that their torque enhancements originate from the interaction among specific harmonics. Therefore, to maximize torque density, the key flux density harmonics responsible for torque production are first identified and set as design objectives. Then, a multi-layer high-efficiency optimization procedure that incorporates both the independent and coupled sensitivities of geometric parameters is proposed. By contrast, this method reflects the correlation among torque, key harmonics, and parameters, thereby achieving higher optimization accuracy. Besides, the harmonic products associated with the respective torque components are evaluated before and after optimization, which reveals a clear enhancement of positive-effect components and suppression of those with negative effects. Finally, a SPMVM prototype is built to verify the effectiveness of the proposed theoretical analysis and optimization method.

title: Dual-Attention Weighting for Multi-Agent Collaborative Perception
authors: Wei Li, Lin Ma, Run Zhang, Longteng Huang, Hao Huang, Danyang Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Multi-agent collaborative perception has shown significant potential in improving environmental awareness, especially in complex and dynamic environments where it enables a more precise and comprehensive understanding. However, existing collaborative perception systems still face several challenges, particularly during feature fusion, where issues such as information redundancy and noise interference arise. To address these challenges, this paper introduces the Dual-Attention Weighting Collaborative Perception (DAW-CoPe) framework. The framework integrates dilated convolutional techniques within the Multi-Scale Spatial Attention Module (MSAM), allowing for the capture of multi-dimensional object features across different scales and the effective integration of richer contextual information, thereby enhancing the model’s perceptual capabilities. Furthermore, the incorporation of local convolutions strengthens the model’s ability to extract fine-grained features, improving its capacity to capture local details during feature fusion and reducing the risk of losing important information. To further enhance perception accuracy, we introduce a dual-attention weighting (DAW) mechanism: a same-level, learnable gating module that jointly reweights channel semantics and multi-scale spatial cues, thereby emphasizing informative channels and critical spatial regions. By suppressing redundant and noisy signals, this mechanism delivers substantial gains in performance and robustness across perception tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that DAW-CoPe outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on three public datasets, significantly boosting the accuracy, reliability, and adaptability of collaborative perception systems. The code associated with this research will be made publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/HIT-K/DAW-CoPe</uri>

title: Allocation and Sizing of Distributed Energy Resources and Capacitor Banks Considering Network Reconfiguration through Multi-objective Optimization Algorithms
authors: Vanio F. S. Júnior, Renato S. F. Ferraz, Rafael S. F. Ferraz, Augusto C. Rueda-Medina, Eduardo S. Palmeira
abstract: The incorporation of renewable energies into existing energy infrastructures plays a significant role in advancing sustainable development. One of the tools that can be employed to operate distribution networks efficiently and yield various technical advantages is the integration of distributed energy resources (DERs) based on renewable sources. However, such benefits require their installation in appropriate locations with adequate selection of their dimensions. Another approach that can be employed to ensure efficient network operation is the reconfiguration of the distribution network. Reconfiguration is regarded as an operational approach to minimize power losses and enhance network characteristics. Thus, in this paper, a multi-objective optimization approach is proposed for the efficient allocation and sizing of DERs and capacitor banks (CBs) simultaneously with network reconfiguration to reduce power losses and to minimize investment and operation costs, while ensuring compliance with the physical and operational constraints of the distribution system. The multi-objective optimization problem formulation, which includes aspects such as daily analysis, associated costs, as well as other pertinent variables, is solved using the Non-Dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) and the Strength Pareto Evolutionary Algorithm 2 (SPEA 2), in addition to the Fuzzy Decision-making Method. Finally, the results demonstrate significant improvements in system performance as well as greater overall efficiency in energy supply compared to the original systems.

title: Reduced On-Resistance and Jitter of GaAs PCSS with Low Optical Energy in Quasi-Linear Mode
authors: Xule Li, Song Li, Jinhong Wei, Fanzheng Zeng, Chenglin Jia, Quan Sun, Lin Penghao, Baoliang Qian
abstract: In this letter, the effect of the trigger optical pulse on the switching performances of a gallium arsenide photoconductive semiconductor switch (GaAs PCSS) under quasi-linear mode is analyzed, both numerically and experimentally. It is found that, under identical optical pulse conditions, the PCSS operates in a unique quasi-linear mode under side incidence, with a 30% reduction in on-state resistance and an 83% reduction in time jitter compared to conventional front incidence. The positive effect of the quasi-linear mode on switching performance is attributed to the uniform illumination and absorption achieved by the device, resulting in a more uniform distribution of photogenerated carriers along the current filament channel.

title: Design of a Physics-Informed Neural Network for PCF-Based Multi-Analyte Biosensors
authors: Kawsar Ahmed, Md Mamun Ali, Md. Shohidullah, Francis M. Bui, Li Chen
abstract: The integration of deep learning (DL) and photonic crystal fiber (PCF)-based multi-analyte detection biosensors is increasingly used to improve measurement specificity in many crucial applications and to enhance detection sensitivity to the micro-range. For the first time in this paper, we introduce the Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) for four different multi-analyte biosensors, enabling detection and analysis with a single model. The model was trained on various datasets and input features (IF) for each biosensor. The proposed PINN model shows the lowest mean square error (MSE) of 0.0022(±0.0002) and the mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.0122(±0.0010). The performance R2 of the PINN model for four different biosensors is 0.9238(±0.0113), 0.9788(±0.0031), 0.9560(±0.0112), and 0.9883(±0.0025), respectively. A noticeable trend is that larger datasets lead to better model performance. The proposed PINN model is competent and flexible for designing and optimizing four different applied multi-analyte biosensors and can be used to effectively design and optimize other multi-analyte biosensors.

title: Retraction Notice: Enhanced DDOS Attack Detection Algorithm to Increase Network Lifetime in Cloud Environment
authors: R Sai Pavan Kumar, K Gopi Chand, M Vamsi Krishna, B Gowtham Nithin, A Roshini, K Swetha
publish: 2022 8th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: DDoS attacks, one of the oldest forms of cyberthreats, continue to be a favorite tool of mass interruption, presenting cybersecurity hazards to practically every type of company, large and small. As a matter of fact, according to IDC, DDoS attacks are predicted to expand at an 18 percent compound annual growth rate (CAGR) through 2023, indicating that it is past time to enhance investment in strong mitigation systems. And while some firms may assume they are limited targets for a DDoS assault, the amount of structured internet access to power corporation services and apps exposes everyone to downtime and poor performance if the infrastructure is not protected against such attacks. We propose using correlations between missing packets to increase detection accuracy. Furthermore, to ensure that these correlations are calculated correctly.

title: Retraction Notice: Artifact Removal using Elliptic Filter and Classification using 1D-CNN for EEG signals
authors: P. Nagabushanam, S. Thomas George, Praharsha Davu, P. Bincy, Meghana Naidu, S. Radha
publish: 2020 6th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Convolution plays major role in deep learning. CNN is the basic architecture and most of the other deep learning architectures ae built based on CNN. However, 1D-CNN is suitable for EEG signals as it is time-series one dimensional characteristic signal. In this paper, we have proposed a 6-layer 1D-CNN to analyze the classification of EEG. Elliptic filter helps to filter Multivariate EEG signal of sleep apnea patient and then classify sleep stages using CNN. Simulations are carried out using Mat Lab.

title: Retraction Notice: Enhancing Deaf Driver Safety: An IOT-Based Vehicle Sensing System
authors: B Padmini Devi, R Hariivarthini, S Kirithick Kannan, R Lathika
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Driving vehicle safety system used to enhance road safety, to reduce the risks of accident mainly for deaf drivers and also added benefit for normal drivers to prevent them from unpredictable accidents. The vehicle safety system uses a combination of sensors, cameras and the advanced algorithms are used to monitor the surroundings. The paper's goal is to provide a horn recognition system that can identify the direction and sound of a horn for safe four-wheel driving. Both people who are deaf and those who have trouble figuring out which side of the road a car is passing on can benefit from this approach. While driving, Deaf persons respond to visual signals such as flashing lights; nevertheless, blasting a horn does not block any visual indications. A gadget that alerts deaf drivers and indicates the direction of sound is necessary for their safety as well as to lessen societal discrimination against deaf persons who drive.

title: The Ga-Scso Algorithm Approach for Breast Cancer Classification
authors: Sakkayaphop Pravesjit, Krittika Kantawong, Natthaphong Aunruean, Phimwipha Thamod, Duangjai Jitkongchuen
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study introduces a hybrid algorithm combining a Genetic Algorithm (GA) with the attacking phase of the Sand Cat Swarm Optimization (SCSO), specifically developed for breast cancer data classification. The proposed methodology enhances the search process, wherein the new population positions are optimally selected based on the best classification efficiency. Evaluation of the algorithm was performed using the Wisconsin Breast Cancer Dataset (WBCD) sourced from the UCI Machine Learning Repository. Comparative computational results against established classifiers-GA, J48, SMO, Random Forest, Multilayer perceptron, and Naïve Bayes-demonstrate the proposed algorithm's superior performance, achieving a high classification accuracy of 98%, which surpasses the compared methods.

title: Development of a Smart Warehouse Logistics Virtual Reality (VR) Simulator for Experiential Learning in Logistics Education
authors: Apinanchai Yooyativong, Taksakorn Sittiwong, Teeravisit Laohapensaeng, Thongchai Yooyativong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: The present logistics and supply chain workforce needs staff members who can perform operational tasks while possessing digital technology skills. The current warehouse operation training methods encounter multiple barriers because they require expensive equipment and present safety hazards and insufficient adaptable training spaces. The authors present a Smart Warehouse Logistics Virtual Reality (VR) Simulator, which they developed to enhance practical learning experiences for warehouse operation training. The system duplicates complete logistics operations from receiving to shipping through sorting and error resolution in a physics-based 3D environment built with the Meta Quest 3 VR headset and Unity Engine and C# programming. The system enables learners to practice realistic warehouse operations through its combination of automated conveyor and sorting logic and forklift dynamics and error-handling mechanisms. The system architecture contains three separate layers (Input and Logic Core and Output) which allows for expansion and future connection to Industry 4.0 systems and digital twin platforms. The evaluation study employed a mixed-methods research design to evaluate system usability and student learning outcomes through a study with 32 undergraduate logistics students. The usability assessment through SUS reached 86.2, while participants demonstrated 48.7% better knowledge retention and their process errors decreased by 63.6%. The research participants gave feedback which showed their work engagement improved while they achieved better safety standards and operational performance.

title: Mapping Digital Public Opinion on Prabowo Subianto's Un Speech: a Computational Analysis of Sentiment and Political Discourse
authors: Frederik Masri Gasa, Sidharta, Nur Kholis, Hady Pranoto, Francesco Sudibyo, Ardvin Kester S. Ong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study analyzes Indonesian digital publics' responses to President Prabowo Subianto's 23 September 2025 speech at the 80th United Nations General Assembly. The speech, disseminated via online news and social media, elicited varied reactions, from national pride to ethical critique. Utilizing computational social science and communication theory, this research maps the emotional and thematic public sentiment. A dataset of 71,315 text entries from YouTube, Reddit, and Google News was collected. After cleaning, 62,282 units were analyzed. Sentiment classification used transformer models-IndoBERTweet and XLM-RoBERTa-for polarity and tone, while BERTopic enabled unsupervised thematic clustering. This methodology integrates computational modeling with interpretive discourse analysis to contextualize affective responses in political communication and digital nationalism. Results show polarized online discourse: positive sentiments focused on pride and global standing, while negative ones raised skepticism regarding policy and credibility. These findings illustrate how digital publics in emerging democracies engage in affective identification and moral deliberation when interpreting political leadership globally. By integrating AI analytics with critical communication theory, this study advances computational communication research and offers empirical insight into public opinion formation in Indonesia's digital era.

title: Enhancing Security of QUIC Protocol and IoT
authors: Parinya Utta, Baramee Saibut, Thawatchai Chomsiri
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This project aims to enhance the security of data transmission between IoT devices via the QUIC protocol by combining authentication mechanisms with hybrid encryption. Devices must authenticate using a device secret key combined with a nonce and timestamp, and then establish a secure connection with an AES key sent from the host encrypted with RSA. The result is a robust protection against replay attacks and session hijacking. Tested with ESP32/ESP8266 boards and air quality sensors, the system works properly with acceptable overhead.

title: SqueezeNet-Based Extraction of Compact Feature Vectors from Inspection Video Streams
authors: Oksana Karpinska, Lesia Dubchak, Anatoliy Sachenko, Oleg Zastavnyy, Bohdan Derysh, Ihor Romanets
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Automated visual diagnostics of renewable energy assets require efficient transformation of inspection video streams into compact and informative representations. Raw inspection videos of wind turbine blades contain a large number of redundant frames, while surface defects such as cracks, erosion, and corrosion occur only locally. Direct pixel-level processing of such data is computationally inefficient for streaming applications. This paper presents a SqueezeNet-based software module for processing inspection video streams and extracting compact four-dimensional class-confidence vectors. The proposed approach performs frame sampling, standardized image preprocessing, and convolutional feature extraction, followed by a lightweight classification head that produces 4D probability-based feature representations. The focus is placed on feature compactness and temporal stability rather than classification accuracy alone. Experimental results obtained on real inspection video data demonstrate clear class separability in the representation space and high temporal consistency, with an average cosine similarity of 0.988 between consecutive frames. The proposed representation is well-suited for integration into intelligent diagnostic systems operating in real-time or near-real-time conditions.

title: Recall-First Temporal Routing for Low-Base-Rate IDS: Debuggable Unified Preprocessing and Low-Base FPR Evaluation Protocol
authors: Rumen Doynov, Maria Nenova, Agata Manolova
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Security operations are constrained by analyst capacity, and excessive false alarms can overwhelm triage pipelines in low-prevalence traffic. This paper presents a debuggable methodology for (i) unifying heterogeneous IDS (intrusion detection systems) datasets into a single feature space and scalable memmap representation, (ii) training a compact temporal Transformer as a recall-first Stage-1 router (not a final alarm source), and (iii) validating operational risk via a multi-window evaluation protocol that explicitly selects low-base windows and reports worst-case low-base false positive rate (FPR) and alert rate. On a representative validation run, the Stage-1 model satisfies a strict supportedwindow recall gate (recall_min_supported <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.9997)$</tex> while exhibiting catastrophic low-base behavior (worst_fpr_low_base <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1.0$</tex>, alert_low_base <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=100 \%)$</tex>, with benign-score quantile diagnostics indicating an “always suspicious” bias <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{t}\_\text{benign} = 0.9147$</tex> as the benign p10 in a low-base window). The results highlight why aggregate metrics may understate operational risk and motivate router-only semantics for Stage 1 under low base rates.

title: Port-Hamiltonian Modeling and Control Design for Single-Phase Pwm Rectifiers
authors: Wachiravit Buaket, Sittisak Rojchaya, Kittikorn Khanklaeo
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design of a controller for a single-phase pulse-width modulation (PWM) rectifier, which exhibits nonlinear behavior due to the switching characteristics of PWM during AC-to-DC conversion. The controller is developed using the Port-Hamiltonian framework, enabling the design of a state feedback control strategy that employs all system state variables as feedback inputs. This approach ensures high system stability and a fast dynamic response under varying operating conditions. Simulation results confirm that the proposed controller successfully converts a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 1 5 ~ V}$</tex> peak, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0}$</tex> Hz AC input into a stable 800 V DC output. Additionally, under sudden load disturbances, the controller effectively regulates and maintains a steady DC output voltage, demonstrating robust performance and resilience.

title: RectLU: A Novel Activation Function Based on a Rectangle
authors: Thawatchai Chomsiri, Preecha Noiumkar
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper introduces RectLU (Rectangle Linear Unit), a novel activation function inspired by the geometry of rectangles. Its design begins by deriving a function representing the area of a rectangle with a constant diagonal slope (the ratio of its height to width). This area function is then multiplied by the input <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex> to form the core of RectLU's central segment. RectLU is structured into three distinct regions: a left segment returning 0, a right segment returning <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex>, and the aforementioned central function derived from the rectangle. We conducted experiments on two datasets: MNIST and CIFAR-100. For the MNIST experiments, a small Neural Network model with a limited number of hidden layers and nodes per layer was employed. Conversely, for the CIFAR-100 experiments, which involve a large number of classes, a larger and more complex model incorporating Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) was utilized. Experimental results reveal that RectLUs with a smaller diagonal slope are suitable for compact Neural Network models, while those with a larger diagonal slope are advantageous for extensive models, datasets with many classes, and CNN architectures. We designed RectLU with a single adjustable parameter: the diagonal slope (m). Furthermore, we performed comparative experiments against other standard activation functions, including GELU, Swish, ELU, and ReLU. The experimental findings indicate that when the slope parameter m is appropriately tuned to the model, RectLU consistently outperforms these other activation functions.

title: Assessing Image Similarity with Color Histograms to Reveal Security-Related Distortions
authors: Andriy Velhosh, Yuriy Furgala, Serhiy Velhosh, Petro Venheskyi
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper investigates the possibility of using color histograms generated in HS* spaces for identifying images that have undergone various types of distortions. Specifically, the research focuses on distortions caused by rotation, noise, blurring, and optical aberration. Histograms were compared using the Pearson cross-correlation coefficient, and the findings reveal that the correlation remains high for the same image despite the applied distortions. Conversely, the correlation coefficient between different images is low for most of the studied objects. These results suggest that color histograms can be effectively used for image identification tasks, even when images are significantly distorted, as is common in image forgery attempts. The use of correlation detection as a method for comparing histograms is suggested, despite its relatively simple computation. This technique may be useful in the creation of more faster image recognition systems, as well in detecting images anomalies that can occur in the case of malware and threat detection and steganography identifying tasks.

title: Unified Compression of Point Cloud Geometry and Attributes through Variable-Rate Conditioning
authors: Michael Rudolph, Aron Riemenschneider, Amr Rizk
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Multimedia Systems Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: RadixGraph: A Fast, Space-Optimized Data Structure for Dynamic Graph Storage
authors: Haoxuan Xie, Junfeng Liu, Siqiang Luo, Kai Wang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Uncertainty-Aware Federated Learning for UAV Predictive Maintenance: Confidence-Based Aggregation and Risk-Aware Scheduling
authors: Tanisha Pal Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Reliable health monitoring is essential for modern UAV fleets, yet real operational data are often heterogeneous, noisy, and difficult to centralise due to bandwidth and privacy constraints. This paper presents an uncertainty-aware federated learning framework for remaining useful life (RUL) prediction, combining LSTM-based temporal modelling with Monte-Carlo dropout, confidence-based aggregation, and risk-aware client scheduling. To emulate a distributed UAV environment, the NASA C-MAPSS turbofan dataset is partitioned into four clients per subset, introducing realistic non-IID behavior. Experimental results across all four datasets show stable improvements in global model performance, with RMSE and MAE consistent with dataset complexity and confidence-interval coverage ranging from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3}-\mathbf{8 9} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex>. Analysis of uncertainty indicates that aleatoric components dominate long-horizon predictions, while the risk-aware scheduler successfully down-weights low-quality clients. These findings demonstrate that the proposed framework can support distributed predictive-maintenance workflows and provide reliable uncertainty estimates even without centralised data. Future extensions will explore communication-efficient FL updates and transfer to real UAV propulsion datasets.

title: Adversary-Aware Synchronization Framework for Entanglement-Assisted QKD: Models, Taxonomy, and Adaptive Windowing
authors: Sanjay Kumar, Neeraj Kumar, Ketan Anand
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Synchronization is a fundamental requirement for the reliability and security of entanglement-assisted quantum key distribution (QKD), where accurate coincidence detection directly impacts both the secret key rate and robustness against adversarial interference. Existing synchronization approaches typically assume benign timing environments, leaving open the possibility of subtle yet impactful adversarial attacks on the temporal domain. In this work, we introduce an adversary-aware synchronization framework for entanglement-assisted QKD that systematically models timing threats and proposes resilient countermeasures. First, we present a formal system model capturing the timing dependencies of entangled-photon-based QKD protocols. We then introduce a taxonomy of timing adversaries, including passive noise amplifiers, delay injectors, jitter inducers, and desynchronization attackers, each characterized by distinct operational footprints and security implications. To mitigate these threats, we propose a preliminary adaptive windowing algorithm that dynamically adjusts coincidence detection thresholds based on realtime statistical estimates of photon arrival distributions. Initial analysis and simulations demonstrate that the proposed adaptive strategy outperforms fixed-window synchronization under both natural noise and adversarial timing manipulations, achieving improved key rates while maintaining security guarantees. These results highlight the need for adversary-aware synchronization as a critical component of next-generation entanglement-assisted QKD systems.

title: Synthetic Lipid Liquid Coolant Ratio Algorithm based on Multi-Objective Optimization and its Application in GPU Cluster Cooling
authors: Ruichen Bai, Zijie Lin
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: For the liquid cooling system of high-performance GPU clusters, this study proposed a new dynamic multi-objective optimization algorithm D-MOOSA based on Pareto front evolution. The algorithm is used for adaptive optimization of the ratio and flow distribution of synthetic lipid liquid coolants. The study first established a fluid mechanics and heat transfer coupling model of the liquid cooling system, and constructed an equivalent thermophysical property model of the synthetic lipid coolant. This model includes specific heat capacity, viscosity and thermal conductivity. Based on this, this study designed a multi-objective optimization function. This function covers three levels: minimizing thermal resistance, minimizing pump power consumption, and minimizing environmental impact indicators. D-MOOSA achieves dynamic response through sub-population co-evolution, boundary change criteria and "big blood transfusion" mechanism. This optimization can cope with GPU cluster load fluctuations and local hot-spot migration. The experimental platform was a four-node GPU training cluster (architecture: 8 GPUs per node, total power consumption approximately 11.2 kW). Tests were conducted under long-term full load (W1), peak load (W2), and memory-limited (W3) workloads. Static ratio screening results showed that optimizing the coolant ratio can achieve the best compromise between temperature rise and pump power. Under a 6-hour comprehensive workload, the proposed D-MOOSA method achieved Tmax=65.6°C and T99=67.4°C. This result is a reduction of 3.8°C and 4.5°C, respectively, compared to the fixed ratio strategy. At the same time, the thermal resistance Rth was reduced to 0.0150 K•W⁻¹, and the cooling energy consumption Ecool was reduced to 6.63 kWh. During the 72-hour continuous reliability test, the system only received two pump cavitation warnings during low-load switching. The research results show that D-MOOSA can achieve efficient thermal management of GPU cluster liquid cooling systems under the multi-objective constraints.

title: Calibration of a Six-Axis Force Sensor Based on a Bayesian Neural Network
authors: Jun Bao, Yuxiang Sun, Xuefei Du
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Six-axis force sensors are core components of robotic perception and control systems, and their measurement accuracy directly affects force-control performance. Traditional calibration methods based on linear least squares struggle to address inherent inter-axis coupling and nonlinear effects such as hysteresis. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a decoupling calibration method based on a backpropagation (BP) neural network with Bayesian regularization (BR). First, a comprehensive data preprocessing pipeline is established, including data cleaning, denoising, and bidirectional normalization. A conventional linear calibration model is constructed as a baseline for comparison, followed by the training of a dual-hidden-layer BP neural network. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms traditional linear calibration and existing decoupling data in terms of root mean square error (RMSE) across the force components (F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>,F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</inf>,F<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">z</inf>) and torque dimensions. The coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) approaches 1.0, effectively improving both decoupling accuracy and measurement stability of the sensor.

title: Data Imputation and Performance Prediction of xEV Batteries Integrated with DC Microgrids
authors: Saleha Tabassum, Pathan Adam Shafiullakhan, Sama Praneeth, Palukuri Gopichand, Shaik Imran Basha, M. Bharathi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Whenever merging electric vehicles (xEVs) with DC microgrids, accurate battery parameter monitoring and prediction are essential for efficient use of energy. However, real-time measurements are often impacted by missing data, leading to inaccurate control decisions. This work addresses data imputation and prediction in the absence of data in xEV integrated DC microgrids. The k -Nearest Neighbors (k-NN) approach and linear regression (LR) are used to recover incomplete battery properties, including voltage, current, temperature, and state of charge (SoC). MATLAB/Simulink is used to model and simulate the entire system in order to evaluate end-to-end performance. Simulation results show that both LR and k-NN effectively replace lost data, with k -NN showing superior prediction accuracy. According to quantitative study, k-NN obtains a coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}\right)$</tex> of 0.988, a mean absolute error (MAE) of 2.04 %, and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.028. In EV-integrated DC microgrids, the suggested framework promotes intelligent energy management, increases system dependability, and improves parameter estimate accuracy.

title: Lithium-Ion Battery Degradation in Coastal Climates
authors: Alex O’Sullivan, Ted K.A. Brekken
abstract: A battery health experiment was conducted to determine the effect of marine temperatures on battery degradation and lifetime. Battery health was measured by conducting a cycle life test and comparing the capacity of the battery with the nominal capacity. The internal resistance and open circuit voltage of the battery was also measured to serve as secondary health metrics. A variable temperature chamber was utilized to help maintain ambient temperatures at three different test conditions: room (77 °F, 25 °C), cold (41 °F, 5 °C), and hot (95 °F, 35 °C). It was found that the battery capacity degrades faster as temperature increases. In hot conditions, warm, and cold conditions, the battery degraded at 0.11 % loss of capacity per cycle, 0.08 % loss of capacity per cycle, and 0.06 % loss of capacity per cycle, respectively. These initial results suggest that, although the overall battery capacity is slightly reduced in cold conditions, its degradation is less than warm conditions, and thus the lifetime of batteries in cold and coastal conditions is not expected to be derated.

title: DuET-Shape: A Dual-Stream Evolutionary Topology Framework with Native Shapelets for Time Series Clustering
authors: Tianhao Hu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Balancing morphological interpretability with temporal dependency modeling presents a fundamental challenge in time series clustering. Traditional shapelet-based methods offer transparency yet often reduce data to a static "bag of patterns", discarding underlying chronological causality. On the other hand, deep generative models can capture these dependencies but often suffer from unstable training and "feature drift," where the semantic alignment of features shifts continuously during training. To address this, we propose DuET-Shape (Dual-Stream Evolutionary Topology), an end-to-end framework integrating static shape matching with dynamic logic inference. Subsequently, an Evolution Graph Builder converts unstructured sequences into structured Prototype Transition Matrices (PTM) using a Null-Class noise filtering mechanism and temporal pooling. Finally, a dual-stream co-clustering module jointly optimizes both the static shape similarity and the dynamic evolutionary logic. Extensive experiments on 7 UCR datasets demonstrate that DuET-Shape outperforms leading baselines, validating its efficacy in capturing non-linear process dynamics.

title: Superior Safety Helmet using a Standard Wearing Behaviour Detection Model based on Enhanced YOLOv5
authors: D. Bujji Babu, A. Suneetha
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This study presents YOLO-ESCA, an advanced standard helmet-wearing detection model designed to address incorrect helmet usage among workers. Based on an improved YOLOv5n model, the approach enables real-time compliance monitoring using UAVs and other methods, reducing detection errors in video streams. Incorporating EIOU-loss, Soft-NMS non-maximal suppression, CBAM, and a short target detection layer (ADL), the model was trained on a custom dataset consisting of 4,400 pictures. Experimental results demonstrate an mAP@0.5 of 94.7%, an FPS of 65.3, and a lightweight model size of 4.47 MB. Detection performance improved by 41.7% and 73% on custom and SHWD datasets, respectively. Extensions include integration with YOLOv5x6, YOLOv8, and YOLOv9 for enhanced accuracy, along with a user-friendly Flask-based front end featuring secure authentication.

title: Tuning of Integrated Terahertz Disk Resonators via Contactless Evanescent Loading
authors: Bryce Chung, Jordan Nedosyko, Withawat Withayachumnankul, Daniel Headland
abstract: We introduce a viable mechanical tuning technique for the dispersion of terahertz dielectric waveguide-based passive devices. Evanescent interaction with a movable conductive surface imposes a null on tangential electrical fields, which affects the dispersion of guided waves. Varying the position of this null allows us to tailor the waveguide's dispersion as-desired. As a proof-of-concept of this effect, we demonstrate motorized frequency-tuning of an integrated terahertz micro-disk resonator, yielding a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 2.5 \%$</tex> blue-shift in the resonance frequency. This span is greater than the frequency-spacing between adjacent resonances, meaning that a resonance can be tuned to any specific frequency within the operation bandwidth.

title: Closed Form Expressions for 2D Localization Problem in Electromagnetic Near-Field
authors: Georgii Reznev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Recent results on line-of-sight user localization with a uniform linear array (ULA) have shown that, as the number of antennas increases, the estimation error does not decrease as rapidly as predicted by the Cramér–Rao bound (CRB). It was conjectured in that this gap arises because the localization procedure does not exploit the full set of available estimators. In this paper, we confirm this conjecture. We propose a localization approach for ULAs that first generates a large collection of candidate estimates and then fuses them into a single final estimate using a median-based rule. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves an estimation error that is extremely close to the CRB and decreases with the same scaling as the CRB as the array size grows.

title: A Hybrid AI-Powered Framework for Accurate Urban Flood Prediction and Real-Time Early Warning Systems
authors: Prashantha Kumar Kemmannu, M. Udayakumar, V. Tamilselvan, Sree Lakshmi Lingineni, Ankit Kumar, P. Mano Paul
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Urban flooding presents increasing threats to densely populated cities as a result of climate change and urbanisation. Current flood prediction models often show deficiencies in real-time accuracy and do not deliver timely notifications, hence constraining disaster preparedness. This proposed study presents a hybrid AI system that combines Gradient Boosted Decision Trees with a CNN-BiLSTM ensemble to analyse spatial-temporal patterns from different datasets. The system, employing rainfall and water-level data from Kaggle, attains exceptional performance metrics: 97.83% accuracy, 98.15% recall, and an AUC-ROC of 0.993, markedly exceeding baseline models such as Random Forest and LSTM. The framework's real-time alarm distribution improves responsiveness, potentially minimising flood-related damages and fatalities. This methodology enhances flood prediction through the integration of explainability, accuracy, and operational security, providing it an essential instrument for urban disaster management.

title: Enhancing Crop Disease Identification Using an Ensemble of Pretrained CNN Feature and SVM
authors: Nadeem Akhtar, Manish Mandloi, Rehan Ahmad, Rashmi Bhattad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Accurate and timely detection of crop diseases is essential for enhancing yield and achieving food security, for smooth grains like Millet and Pearl Millet (Bajra). Many traditional image analysis techniques tend to be subjective, time-consuming and prone to inconsistencies due to the overlapping disease symptoms. Though there have been encouraging instances of the successful use of deep learning algorithms for the detection of crop diseases, these tend to pose issues related to their computational complexity and intra-class variability. This article uses five pre-trained architectures are used for feature extraction and an SVM is used for classification. The proposed approach is validated on two publicly available datasets of Millet and Bajra. The achieved result indicate that the Dense Net and Efficient Net outperform all other individual feature extractors with an accuracy of up to 99.41% on Bajra and 98.23% on Millet datasets. Moreover, the ensemble approach with the majority voting rule greatly increases the classification performance up to 100% on Bajra and 96.05% on the Millet dataset, decreasing the false positive rate. These experiments validate that combining these varied deep feature learning results using an ensemble approach has a significant enhancement effect on both the generalization capability and class-specific robustness, especially when diagnosing disease classes considered more challenging. The proposed approach provides a practical, time- and resource-efficient strategy for automated disease diagnosis on crops.

title: A-MARL: Attention-Enabled Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning For Scalable Cloud Load Balancing
authors: Swetha M, Suruthi K, Raja Lavanya L, Indira K
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Task scheduling inefficiency continues to hinder performance in heterogeneous cloud infrastructures. This paper solves this problem by introducing a novel scheduler built on a multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework. Its core innovation is an attention mechanism that directs scheduling agents to dynamically filter system state, focusing computational resources on high-priority tasks while accounting for real-time resource constraints. To transition from reactive to proactive management, we incorporate a lightweight predictive model for short-term demand forecasting. The output of this predictor directly governs a dynamic scaling module, which performs live migrations of tasks across the virtual machine (VM) pool to mitigate resource fragmentation and maximize parallel execution. We simulated a heterogeneous cloud environment to test our scheduler. The model achieved promising experimental outcomes such as 35 percent improvement in load distribution fairness, a substantial 61 percent boost in overall system performance, and an 8 percent reduction in average job response time compared to standard heuristic and rule-based baselines.

title: AI-Enhanced Soil Microbial Diversity Analysis for Sustainable Agriculture Practices
authors: R. Hema, S Murugan, Santosh Kumar Sahoo, R. Jagadeesh Kannan, Dennis Surendar, Lokeshwaran N
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Investigating soil microbial diversity is crucial for improving agricultural sustainability, given that soil microbes significantly contribute to nutrient cycling, pest management, and plant development. Traditional techniques for analyzing microbial populations are often arduous and protracted. Recent breakthroughs in artificial intelligence (AI), particularly Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), provide a viable method for enhancing the efficiency and precision of microbial diversity investigation. GNNs are engineered to represent complicated connections in data by modeling things, such as microorganisms, and their interactions as graphs, effectively capturing microbial communities' complex networks. This paper proposes using AI-augmented soil microbial diversity analysis using GNN to promote sustainable farming practices. Utilizing GNNs to create a framework that predicts microbial behavior, identifies changes in microbial communities, and provides insights into effective soil management practices. This system enables real-time monitoring and personal recommendations for soil health management. Incorporating AI-driven microbiological analysis may enhance crop yields, reduce environmental impact, and provide more effective resource utilization, fostering a sustainable and resilient agricultural future.

title: Deploying Large AI Models on Resource-Limited Devices With Split Federated Learning
authors: Xianke Qiang, Hongda Liu, Xinran Zhang, Zheng Chang, Ying-Chang Liang
abstract: Large Artificial Intelligence Models (LAMs) have delivered impressive capabilities, but deploying and fine-tuning them on resource-limited mobile edge devices remains challenging due to data privacy concerns, limited computation and memory resources, and prohibitive communication overhead. This paper proposes a novel framework, named Quantized Split Federated Fine-Tuning Large AI Model (SFLAM). By splitting the model across edge devices and an edge server, SFLAM assigns only lightweight computation to devices and offloads the remaining training to the server, substantially reducing the device-side memory footprint while keeping raw data local. However, high-dimensional intermediate activations impose a heavy uplink burden. SFLAM addresses this through activation quantization and derives a convergence bound showing that the upper bound decreases with increasing quantization bit-width. We further formulate an accuracy–energy efficiency objective jointly optimize transmit power, bandwidth allocation, and quantization bit-width to improve training efficiency over wireless links. Simulations under heterogeneous data and wireless conditions show that SFLAM improves training efficiency and scalability over baselines.

title: High-Sensitivity Dual-CSRR-Loaded SIW Sensor for Solid and Liquid Material Characterization at X-Band
authors: Qiyu Yin, Teng Li
abstract: This letter presents a high-sensitivity planar microwave sensor designed for precise dielectric characterization of solid materials. The variation of the loss tangent was also simulated and analyzed. The sensor configuration integrates a substrate-integrated waveguide and two pairs of complementary split-ring resonators to enhance measurement accuracy and sensitivity within the X-band. The operating mechanism is clarified by the TE30-like mode and equivalent circuit. To validate its performance, a prototype operating in the 7 GHz–13 GHz frequency range is fabricated and experimentally evaluated. The sensor maintains an accuracy within 3.73%, with a sensitivity of 862 MHz/ϵu, demonstrating its effectiveness in accurately measuring the dielectric properties of solid materials. With minor modifications, the proposed sensor can also be applied to liquid characterization. The evaluation of the liquid measurement results demonstrates satisfactory measurement accuracy, confirming the strong versatility of the proposed design.

title: DI3CL: Contrastive Learning With Dynamic Instances and Contour Consistency for SAR Land-Cover Classification Foundation Model_supp1-3678005.pdf
authors: Kai Wang
abstract: Although significant advances have been achieved in SAR land-cover classification, recent methods remain predominantly focused on supervised learning, which relies heavily on extensive labeled datasets. This dependency not only limits scalability and generalization but also restricts adaptability to diverse application scenarios. In this paper, a general-purpose foundation model for SAR land-cover classification is developed, serving as a robust cornerstone to accelerate the development and deployment of various downstream models. Specifically, a Dynamic Instance and Contour Consistency Contrastive Learning (DI3CL) pre-training framework is presented, which incorporates a Dynamic Instance (DI) module and a Contour Consistency (CC) module. DI module enhances global contextual awareness by enforcing local consistency across different views of the same region. CC module leverages shallow feature maps to guide the model to focus on the geometric contours of SAR land-cover objects, thereby improving structural discrimination. Additionally, to enhance robustness and generalization during pre-training, a large-scale and diverse dataset named SARSense, comprising 460,532 SAR images, is constructed to enable the model to capture comprehensive and representative features. To evaluate the generalization capability of our foundation model, we conducted extensive experiments across a variety of SAR land-cover classification tasks, including SAR land-cover mapping, water detection, and road extraction. The results consistently demonstrate that the proposed DI3CL outperforms existing methods. Our code and pre-trained weights are publicly available at: https://github.com/SARpre-train/DI3CL.

title: Modeling of Coupled Input-Force-Displacement Hysteresis in Pneumatic Artificial Muscles
authors: Yanding Qin, Tianying Dong, Haoqi Zhang, Jianda Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Pneumatic artificial muscle (PAM) has been widely applied in robotic systems due to its compliance and lightweight characteristics. However, it exhibits complex hysteresis, and strong couplings are observed between the output force and displacement, resulting in a complex three-dimensional (3D) nonlinear input-force-displacement relationship. Currently, most hysteresis models only describe the two-dimensional (2D) input-output hysteretic relationships and ignore the complex couplings between the outputs. This significantly degrades the system modeling accuracy and motion control performance, especially when the external load varies obviously. This paper proposes a 3D hysteresis modeling method based on a two-stage superposed Prandtl–Ishlinskii model, which captures the hysteresis among control input, displacement, and output force, and enables effective force estimation. Experimental validations under multiple scenarios and comparative analysis demonstrate that the proposed model can accurately predict the behavior of the PAM. The proposed method is independent of specific materials and input forms, allowing applicability to the 3D hysteresis modeling of other smart actuators

title: Design and Experiments of Magnetic-Driven Drug Delivery Capsule Robot based on Magnetic Spring Mechanism
authors: Shaohui Song, Shuo Zhang, Shicheng Luo, Shuang Song, Lihai Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: This article proposes a magnetic-driven drug delivery capsule robot based on the magnetic spring mechanism. The proposed robot primarily comprises a magnetic spring module and a flexible storage bin module. The flexible storage bin with a one-way valve stores liquid drugs, which has a good sealing ability to prevent liquid leakage and penetration. The magnetic spring module includes two radially magnetized cylindrical permanent magnets, which can be controlled remotely with an external magnetic field to achieve drug release. A prototype with a diameter of 13mm and a length of 26mm has been manufactured, which is capable of carrying 0.66ml liquid drug. Phantom and in vitro experiments have been carried out on our prototype robot and the results verified the effectiveness of the proposed design. Results show that the robot has good sealing performance, can release liquid drugs with different viscosities in a realistic environment, and the release rate can reach 92.7%.

title: A Lightweight RAW Video Denoiser with Coarse Alignment and Imbalanced Noise Pre-training
authors: Piotr Kopa Ostrowski, Anna Jezierska, łukasz Grzymkowski, Tomasz P. Stefański, Daniel Węsierski
abstract: Deep neural networks have significantly advanced video denoising. However, deploying accurate denoisers across platforms remains challenging, particularly under real-time constraints and with high-resolution inputs. While explicit motion estimation modules improve temporal alignment and enhance denoising quality, they are often omitted in lightweight models due to their computational burden. This paper investigates the trade-off between denoising quality and computational efficiency when using spatially coarse optical flow, and shows that reduced-resolution flow retains most temporal denoising benefits while significantly lowering computational cost. In addition, we introduce an inference-free imbalanced noise pre-training strategy that encourages effective temporal aggregation and complements coarse alignment without modifying the runtime architecture. The proposed Lightweight Video Denoiser (LVD) achieves state-of-the-art performance among lightweight models on RAW video denoising benchmarks while maintaining low latency suitable for real-time edge deployment. Our code is available at https://github.com/camalab-ai/LVD

title: A Machine Learning Framework for Identifying and Modeling Speed Governing Deficiencies in Hydropower Units
authors: Soumyadeep Nag, Richard Tapia, S M Shafiul Alam
abstract: A common notion is that all hydropower units have active speed governing capabilities. However, this capability can be deactivated due to varying reasons. To uncover and quantify the existence of such units in the Western Electricity Coordination Council (WECC), this paper performs a data-driven analysis utilizing open-source and restricted datasets. A decision tree ensemble-based machine learning algorithm is designed to estimate governor models for the identified units. After updating the WECC power system files with estimated governors the impact on frequency profile is quantified. Results indicate that there are 182 hydropower units in the WECC PSS/E format power system dynamic data file that do not have governor models (representing their in-field speed regulating incapability). 74% of these units are less than or equal to 20MW. It was observed that an additional 150MW to 200MW of response could be added which improved frequency nadir and frequency sensitivity.

title: iDiffODE: Learning Continuous-Time Latent Dynamics for Generative Spatiotemporal Modeling
authors: Sai Deepthi Yeddula, Abhijeet Bhattacharya, Chen Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Irregularly sampled time series are prevalent in domains such as healthcare, wearable sensing, and geospatial event monitoring. These datasets pose significant challenges for traditional time-series models, which assume uniform sampling and struggle with missing or delayed observations. In this work, we present iDiffODE, a novel continuous-time generative model that explicitly constructs smooth latent trajectories from irregular observations, enabling both accurate reconstruction and realistic simulation of multivariate system states. We evaluate our method on three representative datasets demonstrating consistent improvements over the existing methods. Our results demonstrate that iDiffODE and it’s model variants effectively learn continuous latent dynamics and generate time series data even under conditions of severe irregular sampling, positioning it as a highly promising solution for handling irregular time series data in the real world with enhanced generalizability. Github: https://github.com/Abhijeet399/iDiffODE

title: YOLOv8-Nano Driven Foot Stampede Prediction with Homography Mapping
authors: J Umamageswaran, CH. Gowtham Sri Satya, C. Karthik Reddy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Stampedes in crowded places may lead to serious injuries and deaths, and hence early detection and prevention are major concerns. This paper proposes a computer visionbased real-time foot stampede prediction system using YOLOv8 Nano and homography transformation. The system first uses YOLOv8-Nano: A Lightweight Yet Powerful Object Detection model to identify and track people in video streams. To account for perspective distortion, homography is used to project the camera view to a top-view representation of the scene, allowing spatial crowd distribution is to be estimated accurately. From the reconfigured viewpoint, the parameters include crowd density, velocity, and density-velocity ratio are computed to determine crowd dynamics. A possible stampede is anticipated when high density and low mobility are both present simultaneously, which indicates critical pressure build-up. The system is not using any other sensors or prior knowledge of the environment like traditional methods; on the contrary, it calibrates via homography every video stream and adapts to it. The system is scalable, lightweight, and it can be incorporated into real-time crowd monitoring systems, which will give authorities pre-warnings and enable them to take proactive measures to prevent stampedes.

title: WindFLOW: A Wind Foundation Model for Generative Temporal Super-Resolution
authors: Cong Feng, Rui Yang, Pranav Sharma
abstract: Wind power plants are increasingly expected to provide grid services such as operating reserves, ramping support, and headroom-based dispatch, all of which depend critically on accurate wind variability at minute-level time scales. However, most available wind datasets and operational forecasts are limited to coarser resolutions, creating a fundamental data gap for grid service quantification. To address this challenge, this paper introduces a novel temporal wind super-resolution framework based on time series foundation models, named WindFLOW. We leverage the pretrained TimesFM Transformer with a three-layer fully-connected neural network head to downscale 5-minute wind speed into 1-minute sequences. The WindFLOW method is evaluated using over 58 million minutes of wind observations from seven wind sites. Results demonstrate that WindFLOW consistently outperforms state-of-the-art deep learning models and classical interpolation methods across all sites, with a 0.39 m/s average mean absolute error. The WindFLOW model achieves superior pointwise accuracy while uniquely preserving empirical distributions and high-frequency spectral energy. These findings indicate that time series foundation models can reliably reconstruct minute-scale wind dynamics from coarse inputs and enable scalable high-resolution wind data generation to enhance grid reliability through grid services.

title: Semantic Segmentation of Drone Orthophoto Images
authors: Nikhileswar Reddy Ramireddygari, Hemanth Kumar Reddy Marappareddy, Deepak Krishnamoorthy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The problem that is discussed in this paper is the difficulty in segmenting high-resolution SVAMITVA drone orthophotos (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-10\ \text{cm} /$</tex> pixel) in rural areas of India, where incomplete labels and a variety of land objects prevent the use of conventional geospatial techniques to analyze the image. The goal is the creation of an AI-controlled pipeline that will preprocess and segment these orthophotos, which will further improve the identification of rural features to better plan and manage resources. The methodology transforms ECW raster and manually prepared shape files to GeoTIFF format with GDAL, inpainting to repair missing pixels, and creates ground-truth masks of five classes, which are background, buildings/houses, roads, agricultural land, and waterbodies, in six villages (Suragpur, Uncha, Uplarshi, Lalpur, Chhattisgarh, Amora). Two deep learning models, Land-Unet and DeepLabV3+, using ResNet50 as a backbone, take <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$256 \times 256$</tex> tiled patches, resulting in about 44,082 training and 7,371 validation samples of a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3,566 \text{MB}$</tex>. On the Amora test set, the results of the Land-Unet are 90.67% accuracy and 80.05% mean Intersection over Union (IoU) with per-class IoUs of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 8. 9 9 \%,\ } \mathbf{8 6. 7 4 \%,\ } \mathbf{6 2. 4 2 \%,\ } \mathbf{7 9. 3 0 \%,\ } \mathbf{9 2. 7 8 \%}$</tex>. DeepLabV3+ results are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1. 0 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0. 8 6 \%}$</tex> mean IoU with per-class IoUs of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 9. 5 3 \%}, \mathbf{8 7. 0 2 \%}, \mathbf{6 4. 2 5 \%}, \mathbf{8 0. 2 7 \%}$</tex>, and 93.23%. These two models are better than the traditional ones, and DeepLabV3+ performs slightly better, especially on roads, but Land-Unet is considered more suitable for practical deployment due to its lower computational requirements. The conclusion provides a solid segmentation framework in an effort to assist in the development of rural areas in India.

title: A Novel Metamaterial- Integrated Compact Multiband Antenna with Wideband Characteristics Using Intelligent Optimization Technique
authors: Sameer Purohit, Rakesh Singh Rajput
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The innovative metamaterial- Integrated inspired compact multiband antenna with wideband properties is reported in this paper, which was designed with the goal of optimizing the next-generation wireless communication systems using the intelligent Random Forest-based techniques. This is the case with the proposed antenna which will be able to effectively miniaturize through the incorporation of engineered metamaterial components and demonstrate multiband and extended bandwidth of impedance. To optimize the important geometrical parameters of the antenna, a datadriven optization framework based on the random forest is used to provide an incentive in the gain, return loss, and impedance matching at lower complexity. This antenna is modeled and analyzed on CST Microwave Studio and its structure is very compact that can be implemented in latest portable gadgets. It has been shown in simulation that it operates over the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0 - 2 8 ~ G H z}$</tex> millimeter-wave spectrum with discrete resonant frequencies at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.8 \text{GHz}, 24.26 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 26.53 GHz. The antenna has a voltage standing wave ratio of less than 2 and a better return loss (S11) of less than [?]10 dB in all bands of operation. The proposed antenna design has a better overall bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 5 2. 3 4 ~ M H z}$</tex> and stable radiating features compared to traditional designs. The above findings validate that, the proposed metamaterial- Integrated antenna is a potential candidate in the 5 G millimeter-wave and future application of high-data-rate wireless communication.

title: Evaluation of Companding Technique for Signal Integrity
authors: Pranav Arakkal, Rehan George Mathew, Sreyas Sreekumar, Anuraj K, Poorna S S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Maintaining signal clarity in analog communication systems remains a persistent challenge due to noise, distortion, and limited dynamic range. This work presents the design, simulation, and hardware implementation of a low-cost analog communication system that employs companding and amplitude modulation (AM) to preserve signal integrity. The architecture integrates a logarithmic amplifier for dynamic range compression, a transistor-based AM modulator, a diode-based envelope detector for demodulation, and an antilogarithmic amplifier for signal expansion. By compressing weaker signals prior to transmission and expanding them upon reception, the system improves Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) and reduces the impact of channel noise. Simulations in LTspice and hardware testing using discrete components confirm the system’s ability to reconstruct the original signal with high fidelity. Notably, an input signal with 28.5dB SNR yielded a recovered signal with 27.8dB SNR demonstrating minimal degradation despite the use of minimal circuitry. The simplicity and effectiveness of this design make it ideal for resource-constrained analog applications such as public address systems, wireless microphones, and educational setups, where real-time performance, noise resilience, and hardware feasibility are critical.

title: Examining Source Credibility and Parasocial Interaction as Drivers of Purchase Intention in Beauty Vlogger Content
authors: Finola Amanda Handritartha, Damalia Afiani, Naila Almira, Ridho Bramulya Ikhsan, Hartiwi Prabowo, Warniancy Ariesty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Businesses use beauty vloggers to increase purchases of local cosmetic products, but their effectiveness is hampered by low consumer trust and minimal parasocial interaction with beauty vloggers. This study aims to analyze the influence of source credibility on consumer trust, parasocial interaction, and purchase intention, as well as the relationship between consumer trust and parasocial interaction on the purchase intention of local cosmetic products. Research data was collected through a closed questionnaire and distributed online to 387 respondents residing in nine major cities in Indonesia who follow beauty blogger accounts. Therefore, purposive sampling and data analysis techniques were applied using PLS-SEM. The results showed that source credibility significantly and positively influenced consumer trust, parasocial interaction, and purchase intention. In addition, consumer trust and parasocial interaction also significantly and positively influenced purchase intention. This finding demonstrates how beauty vloggers are vital in creating consumer trust and parasocial interaction, affecting local cosmetic brands' purchase intention. Also, this analysis emphasizes the importance of parasocial interaction, where followers develop personal connections with the vloggers. Ultimately, these findings confirm that leveraging the influence of beauty vloggers is beneficial and essential for driving purchase intent and enhancing the status of local cosmetic brands in a competitive market.

title: Optimized Framework for Merging Navigable Small-World Graphs: Balancing Efficiency and Search Performance
authors: Priyanka Muppuri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Navigable small-world graphs have emerged as fundamental structures for efficient a pproximate n earest neighbour (ANN) search in high-dimensional spaces, with applications spanning recommendation systems, image retrieval, and natural language processing. However, the challenge of merging such graphs while preserving their navigability and search efficiency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex> emains <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$l$</tex> argely <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$u$</tex> naddressed. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T$</tex> his <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p$</tex> aper introduces a novel optimisation framework for merging navigable smallworld graphs that strategically balances computational efficiency with search performance. We propose three progressive merging algorithms-Naive Merge, Merge1, and Merge2-that employ localised proximity-based neighbourhood construction and adaptive exploration-exploitation strategies to minimise redundancy and ensure seamless graph integration. Comprehensive experimental evaluation on the Sift1M dataset demonstrates that our advanced merging algorithms reduce computational effort by up to 5x compared to baseline approaches while maintaining competitive search accuracy. The proposed framework provides a scalable solution for dynamic and distributed graph-based systems requiring efficient index maintenance and consolidation.

title: A Distributed Matching Framework for Low-Latency Cooperative Data Uploading in Smart Construction Sites
authors: Yiyi Zhang, Weiguang Jiang, Peng Guo, Cheng Zhou, Ke You, Chen An
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Smart construction sites are characterized by spatiotemporal data burstiness and heterogeneous uplink capabilities, which frequently result in network congestion and high data upload latency. To reduce this latency, the idle uplink resources of peripheral terminals can be utilized to assist in data uploading via Device-to-Device (D2D) communication. However, this collaborative scheme faces a complex resource contention problem where multiple source nodes may compete for limited helper resources. This transforms the scheduling challenge into a distributed many-to-one matching problem. To solve it, in this paper we propose the Distributed Preference-based Matching Algorithm (DPMA). DPMA leverages matching theory to construct comprehensive utility functions based on channel state, queue backlog, and uplink capability, enabling terminals to autonomously form stable source-helper pairs through a distributed proposal-acceptance mechanism. Extensive simulation experiments evaluate DPMA against Nearest, Greedy, and Centralized Optimal benchmarks. The results demonstrate that DPMA significantly outperforms heuristic baselines, reducing the average critical data completion latency by 28% compared to the Greedy algorithm and 56% compared to the Nearest scheme, while achieving performance within 4% of the theoretical centralized optimum. Additionally, DPMA reduces the overall makespan by 29% and 54% respectively, proving its effectiveness in time-sensitive industrial IoT applications.

title: YOLO-FM: A Method for Detecting Pine Wilt Disease Infected Timber Based on Optimized YOLOv11
authors: Ye Xie, Jianghua Li
