publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To address the challenges of large target scale variations, weakened early disease features, and frequent false and missed detections in complex forest backgrounds in UAV-acquired imagery, this study proposes an improved pine wilt disease–affected tree detection method, termed YOLO-FM, based on an optimized YOLOv11 framework. Taking YOLOv11s as the baseline, the network architecture and loss function are systematically enhanced while maintaining inference efficiency: a Feature Pooling Enhancement Unit (FPEU) is integrated into the C3k2 module to strengthen fine-grained and small-scale target representation, a CBAM attention mechanism is introduced between the backbone and neck to suppress complex background interference, and the ShapeIoU loss is adopted to replace CIoU for improved bounding box regression accuracy and training stability. Experimental results on the PWD dataset demonstrate that YOLO-FM outperforms YOLOv5s, YOLOv8s, and the original YOLOv11s in terms of precision, recall, and mAP, achieving mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 of 85.6% and 39.9%, respectively, with a reduced parameter count of 8.8 M. These results indicate that the proposed method achieves a favorable balance between detection accuracy and model lightweightness in complex forest environments, providing effective technical support for intelligent monitoring and precise control of pine wilt disease.

title: Knowledge-Injected Optimization Framework for Heavily-Constrained SPMT Layout of Offshore Decks
authors: Xiaorui Zhai, Shengli Wang, Qianran Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In modern offshore engineering, the layout planning of Self-Propelled Modular Transporters (SPMTs) for large deck modules is an extremely complex system engineering task, characterized by high dimensionality, heavy constraints, and computationally expensive "black-box" optimization features. Traditional trial-and-error methods struggle to achieve an optimal trade-off among multiple conflicting objectives—such as structural safety, load balance, and economic cost—and face the dual challenges of an extremely sparse feasible region and expensive Finite Element Analysis (FEA) costs. Therefore, this paper explores the innovative application of intelligent algorithms in the field of large-scale offshore transportation, and proposes an integrated intelligent framework based on "SACS-in-the-loop Simulation-Driven Design"—KI-NSGA-III. With a parametric knowledge base serving as the digital foundation, this framework constructs an intelligent optimization system featuring "One Architecture and Three Core Mechanisms": First, a two-stage hybrid optimization architecture is adopted to decouple feasibility exploration from performance optimization, effectively overcoming the "cold start" bottleneck caused by the sparse feasible region ; second, an evaluation and repair mechanism is established, maximizing the value of each simulation through a geometric firewall, layout fingerprint caching, and data-driven post-repair ; meanwhile, by integrating an evolutionary operator system with a dynamic control mechanism, full-process deep knowledge injection is achieved. Experimental results demonstrate that this framework not only overcomes the non-convergence defect of standard evolutionary algorithms but also automatically generates a Pareto-optimal set that significantly outperforms human expert baselines across the three dimensions of safety, stability, and cost , realizing a paradigm shift from passive verification to active generation.

title: A Streamlined Deep Learning Framework for Vineyard Yield Estimation
authors: P. Santhi, Aisha Suleiman Shaik, Yemineni Meenakshi, Komali Kadiyala, Suman Pandipati, V. Lakshmi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Accurate vineyard yield estimation is crucial mainly for harvesting, logistics, and production planning. Manual estimation of yield is time-consuming, labour-intensive, and error-prone. Many vision-based systems were proposed to overcome the limitations due to the manual mechanisms; therefore, complex systems that depend on costly sensors have been adapted. However, despite the use of expensive hardware, these systems failed to deliver promising results, also limiting their practical use. This paper proposes a pipeline that focuses on maintaining simplicity, eliminating the use of overpriced hardware, and also succeeds in delivering accurate yield estimation. This was achieved using the Wine Grape Instance Segmentation Dataset (WGISD). The WGISD dataset undergoes proper data pre-processing using CLAHE and denoising to improve image quality under varying illumination, which is then followed by grape bunch detection using the Faster R-CNN, where berry counts were refined using a CNN-based regression model optimized with Huber loss, achieving an accuracy of 94.7%. A YOLOv8 was also evaluated, which achieved an mAP@50 of 89.1%, showing reduced robustness under occlusion. In addition, a MINN model was introduced, combining visual and cluster-level features, which achieved the best overall performance, gaining an accuracy of 95.8%, proving that a simplified model, with proper data pre-processing, can deliver reliable yield estimation for real-world vineyard applications.

title: A Wavelet-Enhanced Convolutional TimesNet for Interval Forecasting of Thermal Power Generation
authors: Qingkai Yang, Xiaoliang Zheng, Hualiang Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of renewable energy sources, thermal power units are compelled to operate under frequent start–stop cycles and deep peak-shaving conditions, which substantially exacerbate the nonlinear, non-stationary, and multi-scale characteristics of power generation time series. Under such highly volatile operating regimes, conventional forecasting approaches often exhibit degraded predictive accuracy and insufficient adaptability to rapid dynamic variations. To address these challenges, this study proposes WTConv-TimesNet-QR, a short-term thermal power interval forecasting model that integrates learnable wavelet transform convolution with the TimesNet architecture. The model employs wavelet convolution to learn and extract multi-scale dynamic features, and further enhances sensitivity to local disturbances and high-frequency variations by building upon TimesNet’s periodic-domain modeling framework. Meanwhile, quantile regression is incorporated to construct predictive intervals, thereby improving the reliability and credibility of the forecasting results. Experiments based on real operational data from a thermal power plant demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms LSTM, GRU, TCN, and Transformer in MAE, RMSE, and MAPE, and achieves high PICP with narrow MPIW. These results confirm the superior accuracy, robustness, and uncertainty modeling capability of WTConv-TimesNet-QR under complex and highly dynamic thermal power operating conditions.

title: A Correlation-Aware and Dynamic Multi-Level Fusion Approach for Multimodal Relation Extraction
authors: Jing Liu, Jie Wang, Huili Zhang, Haifang Mo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Multimodal relation extraction integrates text and visual information to identify relationships between entities. However, in real-world scenarios like social media, many images or objects are irrelevant to the text, creating noisy interference that limits model generalization. To address this core challenge, this study proposed a Correlation-Aware Dynamic Multi-Level Fusion framework. The framework incorporated three key designs for effective noise suppression and feature fusion. First, a lightweight module assessed the semantic correlation between textual and visual features. Second, a dynamic weighting mechanism adjusted the fusion weight for visual features based on the correlation score. Third, a multi-level fusion architecture performed cross-modal interaction within intermediate Transformer layers, balancing modal heterogeneity and feature complementarity. Experiments on the MNRE dataset demonstrated that the proposed method significantly improved relation extraction performance across diverse scenarios. Ablation studies confirmed the collaborative effectiveness of each component.

title: Contextualizing Decisions in the Monarchy of Healthcare through Traceable Patient-Level Evidence
authors: Gaurav Sharma, Rahul Kumar, Yashi Tiwari, Sarthak Garg, Mohammad Ammar, Dattatraya Parle
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Clinicians often deal with lengthy, unstructured patient records extending across multiple PDF reports, laboratory findings, diagnostic descriptions, radiology summaries, medication prescriptions, and multi-visit clinical narratives. This type of information review is burdensome to do manually and is cognitively exhausting, especially in high-stakes environments with a short time window to make decisions. While Large Language Models (LLMs) are remarkably good at reasoning and summarising, directly applying them to entire medical documents is expensive, slow, and deprived of the traceability necessary in healthcare settings. We present a patient-personalised RetrievalAugmented Generation framework that incorporates metadataaware ingestion, Milvus-based vector retrieval, hierarchical clustering, and automated PDF evidence highlighting. Unlike generic RAG pipelines, our system retains patient-specific metadata, supports page-level explainability, and generates transparent, auditable responses. Extensive evaluations on multi-page clinical documents show significant improvements in response latency, token cost reduction, and clinician workflow efficiency. The system presents a practical and explainable solution for realworld clinical decision support.

title: Design and Implementation of a Hybrid IoT-Enabled Solar-Powered Wheelchair and Patient Monitoring System for Physically Challenged Individuals
authors: M. Vijayakumar, R. Arul, A. Divakar, N. Naveen, A. Jaya Prakash, R. Mathavan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Disability is a major challenge to completing the day-to-day activities. The majority of these disorders are acquired at birth or accidentally. To provide a smart solution with the help of digital technology development will help them for a better life cycle. This proposed work is to develop the hybrid wheelchair with the help of the internet of things (IoT). This hybrid wheelchair consists of mobile and gesture control and biomedical sensors that help to monitor the disabled person's health conditions. In this work a solar-powered gesture control wheelchair is developed. The customized smart wheelchair has the ability to move forward, right, left and stop based on the hand motion. The prototype model is completely tested both indoors and outdoors, and the obtained output results are significantly improved. This proposed work covers the two most relevant Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are SDG 3 - Good Health and Well-Being and SDG 9 - Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.

title: VehicleTracker: A Real-Time ALPR System for Multi-Point Vehicle Tracking
authors: Ishwar Tilkari, Anuj Sarda, Krishna Deokar, Nirmalya Mukhopadhyay, Kunal Kundu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of urban infrastructure and rising concerns over vehicle theft, designing surveillance systems that are not only accurate but also privacy-preserving and computationally efficient has become a critical area of research. Traditional Automatic License Plate Recognition (ALPR) systems often struggle to maintain a balance between real-time performance and environmental robustness, or they rely heavily on expensive cloud-based services that introduce latency and data sovereignty issues. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel, end-to-end surveillance framework named VehicleTracker, developed as an edge-native alternative to complex multi-stage pipelines. Our approach integrates the YOLOv5 architecture for rapid object detection with a hybrid Optical Character Recognition (OCR) engine to ensure robust multi-point tracking and geospatial visualization without internet dependency. We conduct a comprehensive comparative analysis by evaluating system latency, feature coverage, tracking continuity, and resource efficiency against state-of-the-art baselines, including GRU-based and multi-stage YOLO systems. Extensive simulations and realworld benchmarks validate the superiority of VehicleTracker, which achieves a recognition accuracy of 88.9 % and a low processing latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 35 \text{ms}$</tex>, outperforming traditional architectures in resource-constrained environments. The experimental results demonstrate that our system provides a scalable, cost-effective, and secure solution suitable for modern Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS).

title: Confusion and Diffusion Reinforced Image Encryption with Henon-Osprey Optimization
authors: Roopkamal K. Thind, Ashima Anand, Anamika Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Due to the proliferation of digital information technology, the people used the internet to communicate their data. This data is vulnerable to several types of attacks on the internet, and attackers utilize different strategies to steal the sensitive and confidential information of the people. The encryption technique applied adds a security layer for safeguarding the data. In this research, the focus is on data that is transmitted in the form of images. The image contains a large amount of information in the form of pixels, and the correlation between them is high. Therefore, in this paper, encryption method for images is proposed that utilizes the chaotic Henon map and employs the metaheuristic osprey optimization (OO) algorithm to hyper-tune its parameters. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{O O}$</tex> algorithm hyper-tuned the parameters by exploring the solution space of the parameter built on the entropy objective function and determining the best value. Further, in the encryption method, two random sequences are produced utilizing the chaotic Henon map. After that, these sequences are employed to achieve the confusion and diffusion in the proposed method. The simulation results showcase that the histogram of the encrypted images is equally distributed, entropy with a high value near to the ideal value is required in the encryption, and a low value of CC, SSIM, and PSNR is achieved by the proposed method.

title: Low-Latency Network Task Scheduling via Graph-Aware Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ziyi Huang, Yuzhe Wang, Na Pang, Xiangkun Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Edge computing can effectively support low-latency intelligent applications, but the heterogeneity of nodes in terms of storage, computing, and link bandwidth, as well as the dynamic changes of resources with task execution, make task routing and scheduling extremely challenging. The traditional shortest path and heuristic strategies are difficult to take into account both the topological characteristics of the network and multi-resource coupling simultaneously, resulting in limited scheduling performance in complex dynamic environments. To this end, this paper proposes an intelligent edge task scheduling method for GNN-PPO based on graph neural networks and proximal policy optimization. It models the correlation between nodes and link resources in the topology through graph convolution, and combines task locations and resource consumption processes to achieve globally aware next-hop adaptive decision-making. To approach the real system, the scheduling environment explicitly simulates the transmission, propagation and computing overheads, and adopts a delay-driven reward promotion strategy for optimization. The experimental results show that this method significantly improves the end-to-end delay performance in 8×8 heterogeneous networks compared with the MLP-DRL method, verifying its effectiveness in dynamic resource scenarios.

title: AI is a Memory Hog: Its Demand Threatens Whole Categories of Electronics
authors: Samuel K. Moore
abstract: If it feels these days as if everything in technology is about AI, that's because it is. And nowhere is that more true than in the market for computer memory. Demand, and profitability, for the type of DRAM used to feed GPUs and other accelerators in AI data centers is so huge that it's diverting memory away from the supply needed for other uses and causing prices to skyrocket. According to Counter-point Research, DRAM prices rose at least 80 to 90 percent in the first quarter of 2026. • The largest AI hardware companies are likely first in line for new memory, but that leaves everybody else—makers of PCs, smartphones, TVs, and everything else that needs to temporarily store billions of bits—scrambling to deal with scarce supply and inflated prices. • How did the electronics industry get into this mess, and even more important, how will it get out? IEEE Spectrum asked economists and memory experts to explain. Today's situation, they say, is the result of a collision between the DRAM industry's historic boom-and-bust cycle and an AI hardware infrastructure build-out that's without precedent in its scale. And, barring some major collapse in the AI sector, it will take years for new capacity and new technology to bring supply in line with demand. Even then, prices might stay high. • To understand both the supply and the demand ends of this episode, you need to understand its main culprit: high-bandwidth memory, or HBM.

title: News
authors: Ned Potter, Charles Q. Choi, Matthew S. Smith, Lawrence Ulrich
abstract: Artemis is NASA's bid to reclaim its heritage, to resume the business of human exploration, to take astronauts to the moon and beyond, and to win what's been billed by some U.S. politicians as a “new space race,” with China. Artemis II is planned as the project's first circumlunar test mission with a crew. If it succeeds, and if NASA can deliver on a December executive order from the Trump administration, Artemis astronauts will land near the moon's south pole by 2028 and start building a lunar base by 2030, steps to ensure, as the order says, “American space superiority.”

title: Exploring the Viability of Space on Wheels: Trends, Preferences, and Market Factors Shaping Mobile Science Education
authors: Hannah Mae R. Bertulfo, Julianne Grace C. Gomez, Jessica P. Tumambing, Aldrich Ralph C. Divinagracia, Veronica Jones M. Magno, Jaypy T. Tenerife
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The study is a market feasibility survey to assess the commercial potential of Space on Wheels, a mobile space education vehicle. The goal was to understand trends and factors that could guide the design and marketing of a tech-based, accessible educational museum aligned with user preferences. Using a mixed-method approach, we gathered both qualitative and quantitative data. The survey explored interest in space education, engagement with technology, support for sustainability, and perceptions of affordability and accessibility. Multivariate analysis examined how these factors relate and influence potential participation. Findings confirm the feasibility of the concept in the target market. Respondents showed strong interest in space education and a positive view of tech-enabled learning. Affordability, accessibility, and sustainability also emerged as key concerns. Several consistent and unexpected factors influenced willingness to engage in mobile science programs. Overall, the study points to a high level of market readiness and offers a foundation for expanding similar educational models to rural and underserved areas. Recommendations are provided for future initiatives and research on mobile, inclusive learning platforms.

title: Research on Indoor Obstacle Avoidance Method of UAV Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Juan Ding, Fei Hu, Quan Zhang, Zixi Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Aiming at the complex obstacle avoidance problem of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) operating in indoor environments without GNSS signals, this paper proposes an indoor UAV obstacle avoidance method based on deep reinforcement learning and the DAC-DPPO (Dynamic-Adaptive Clipping Distributed Proximal Policy Optimization) algorithm. This method adopts a distributed proximal strategy optimization algorithm with a dynamic adaptive clipping mechanism, which can accelerate training speed, enhance the adaptability of the strategy network model under different starting points (environmental conditions),so as to improve the overall obstacle avoidance ability performance of UAV. And an online UAV model parameter identification method is proposed, which can quickly identify the model parameters of the real UAV and import them into the simulation platform. The effectiveness of the method is verified by Simulink simulation experiments and real flight data experiments, which provides excellent technical support for the reinforcement learning training of UAV in indoor environments.

title: A Bidirectional Integrated Equalizer Based on CLLLC Converter for Series-Connected Supercapacitor String
authors: Lizhou Liu, Jingsheng Shang, Wangxin Zhang, Xueying Yu, Zhipeng Chen, Yi Zhou, Shi Chen, Tianlei Zang, Buxiang Zhou, Kaigui Xie
abstract: Traditional integrated voltage equalization circuits with compact size and low cost. However, each secondary winding can only balance individual cells, and although capable of charge/discharge balancing, cannot achieve autonomous self-balancing functionality. To overcome their shortcomings, an integrated balancing topology based on a CLLLC resonant converter is proposed in this article. First, each secondary winding attains dual-cell balancing capability by incorporating a voltage doubler circuit that capitalizes on the symmetrical voltage ripple characteristics inherent to full-bridge CLLLC resonant converters. This innovative configuration effectively doubles the voltage equalization capacity while maintaining identical transformer winding quantities. Second, the zero-voltage switching (ZVS) across all MOSFETs throughout charge and discharge cycles through meticulous topological optimization of the CLLLC converter topology is guaranteed based on the operational mechanism of the integrated balancer. This inherent soft-switching characteristic enables the system to maintain autonomous self-balancing functionality even under idle operating conditions. Experimental validation was conducted to demontrate that the transferred energy is effectively balanced during charging, discharging, and idle conditions by the proposed integrated balancing circuit on a system with six supercapacitors. Peak efficiencies of 92.47% (18.96 W), 92.97% (12.34 W), and 92.67% (9.87 W) are demonstrated in the charge, discharge, and self-balance modes, respectively.

title: GHz Rate, Watt-Level Femtosecond All-Fiber MOPA System based on Optoacoustically Mode-locked Soliton Fiber Laser with Apodization Filtering
authors: Jiachen Wu, Kaijun Wu, Benhai Wang, Shunxian Luo, Ruchen Lv, Yu Jiang, Qi Huang, Xintong Zhang, Xiaocong Wang, Wenbin He, Meng Pang
abstract: High-power ultrafast fiber lasers are essential tools for a wide range of advanced applications. We report in this paper a GHz-rate, Watt-level all-fiber master oscillator power amplifier (MOPA) femtosecond laser system, seeded by an optoacoustically mode-locked soliton fiber laser. We show both in experiments and numerical simulations that the ubiquitous Kelly sidebands could lead to detrimental splitting of laser pulses at the compression stage through causing some distortions of the seed pulse envelope, and the use of an apodization filter can highly mitigate this impact of Kelly sidebands, resulting in improved output-pulse quality of the MOPA system. The system can deliver ultrafast pulses at 2.56-W average power and 1.879-GHz repetition rate, each pulse being of  97-fs duration and 1.4-nJ energy. This work provides an effective spectral engineering strategy for MOPA systems seeded by soliton fiber lasers.

title: Fine Imaging Method of THz-ViSAR on Sub-aperture Range Migration Algorithm and Distinctive-point Map Drift Algorithm
authors: Xiaofan Li, Bin Deng, Qiang Fu, Hongqiang Wang, Jun Yi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Video synthetic aperture radar (SAR) is a new type of synthetic aperture radar. In order to achieve high imaging frame rate, the data collected by video SAR is divided into multiple units for imaging. Compared with undivided data, due to the shorter accumulation time of imaging units, the azimuthal resolution of the image decreases, resulting in blurred images. Compared with low-frequency video SAR, terahertz video synthetic aperture radar (THz-ViSAR) has a higher operating frequency and a larger difference in Doppler frequency rate between frames. The use of map drift algorithm cannot fully compensate for the quadratic phase error (QPE), resulting in image blur. To address the above issues, this paper proposes a THz-ViSAR fine imaging method that combines a sub-aperture range migration algorithm with a distinctive-point map drift algorithm. This method uses sub-aperture range migration algorithm to correct range drift in imaging. The residual QPE is compensated by constructing a QPE function using a distinctive-point map drift algorithm, and high-order phase errors are compensated for using a phase gradient autofocus (PGA) algorithm with an azimuthal sliding window. This method obtains clear and fine video SAR images through point target simulation and THz-ViSAR imaging of multiple imaging scenes, which verifies the effectiveness and progressiveness of this method.

title: High-temperature performance of all-diamond solar-blind UV photodetector with BDD electrodes
authors: Yuanyang Gao, Zhengjie Tang, Dan Zhao, Fengqiu Jiang, Zhangcheng Liu, Yang Li, Zhiwei Chen, Tao Liu, Xiao Wang, Jinping Ao
abstract: This work systematically investigated the high-temperature performance of an all-diamond UV detector utilizing heavily boron-doped diamond (BDD) as transparent electrodes. The dark current increases moderately from 5.65×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-11</sup> A at RT to 2.26×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-10</sup> A at 450 °C, while the photocurrent under 222 nm increases obviously, leading to the responsivity increasing from 0.07 A/W to 0.37 A/W. The UV-to-visible rejection ratio rises from 2.86×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> to 1.16×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup>. Dynamic response measurements reveals that the rise time increases slightly from 0.02 s to 0.1 s, while the decay time remains stable at 0.03 s, suggesting that the response speed is virtually unaffected by high temperatures. Furthermore, the optoelectronic properties of the detector remain consistent during continuous high-temperature operation and long-term tracking test, demonstrating excellent thermal stability. This study proves that the all-diamond detector with BDD transparent electrodes exhibits outstanding advantages in high-temperature stability and response performance, providing a promising pathway for the development of high-performance UV detection technology in harsh environments.

title: Post Fault Stability Assessment in Distribution System Using Sliding RMS and Phase Angle Methods
authors: Nimai Charan Patel, Bhabasis Mohapatra, Ritesh Dash
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Post fault stability assessment is a critical function in modern power distribution system more particularly in the presence of variable loads and increasing penetration of renewable energy sources. This research paper proposes a simple effective methodology to assess the stability of a single-phase distribution system during the fault condition. The approach is based on, two core signal analysis such as sliding root mean square voltage calculation and phase angle tracking by using dynamic phasor analysis. In order to showcase the realtime application line to ground fault is introduced in a single-phase system between 0.06 seconds and 0.11 second. Voltage waveforms are generated and analyzed in three different stages i.e. Pre-Fault, During Fault and Post Fault. The sliding root mean square method is applied over a moving window to detect sudden voltages dip and to monitor the recovery trend after the fault is cleared. Similarly, the phase angle variation is computed from the Fourier Spectrum to capture the dynamic behavior of the voltage signal. Cross correlation is applied to estimate the delay, which can indirectly indicate the location of the fault.

title: Miniaturized Wide Band Dual L-Slot on-Chip Antenna with Enhanced Gain for 5G Applications
authors: Harshavardhan Singh, Vipin Singh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Miniaturized on-chip antenna is an essential component in a compact and truly wireless Radio Frequency Integrated Circuit (RFIC). This paper presents a design of a CMOS based wide band on-chip patch antenna designed at 23 GHz band for 5G applications. The proposed antenna incorporates two identical L-shaped slots on the patch to achieve miniaturization with a wider bandwidth. Moreover, these two slots help to excite higher-order mode of operation i.e. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{TM}_{02}$</tex>. The antenna achieves a wide operational bandwidth of 7.55 GHz, spanning from 20.92 GHz to 28.47 GHz, along with a 54 % peak radiation efficiency. It also displays a gain of −6.8 dBi at 23 GHz. Moreover, the design also shows that the antenna's gain is increased by 6.9 dB after employing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{HfO}_{2}$</tex> layer. The simulated results show its potential for high-frequency, short-range applications, including fifth-generation (5G) technologies.

title: The Effect of Depth of E-Commerce Reviews on Trust through Utilitarian and Hedonic Values
authors: Bima Dwi Putra Widyanto, Ferrel Marcello Jonathan, Muhammad Itsar Zuhdi Afwashiyam, Artha Sejati Ananda
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Online reviews have been important in influencing purchasing decisions on customer-to-customer (C2C) e-commerce. Consumers often perceive these reviews through two main dimensions, utilitarian value and hedonic value. On the other hand, brands in C2C e-commerce often have trust problems as perceived by the customers. This paper has a purpose to investigate the effect of perceived values of the reviews -through utilitarian value and hedonic value- on trust, with depth of review as the antecedent variable, in the context of a fashion store (Erigo) on Shopee Indonesia e-commerce. It adopts a quantitative approach using the stimulus-organismresponse (SOR) framework, where depth of reviews function as stimuli, perceived values as organisms, and trust as a response. Data were collected through convenience sampling from 221 respondents who had shopped at the Shopee Official Store of Erigo. Data analysis was analyzed using the Partial Least Squares-Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) technique. The results of the study indicate that review depth has a significant positive influence on perceived utilitarian value and perceived hedonic value. Both values also positively influence trust. These findings provide theoretical and practical contributions, especially for online businesses on e-commerce, by emphasizing the importance of encouraging consumers to provide informative and accurate reviews to create long-term trust from future customers. This research represents one of the pioneering studies that explores the linkage between depth of reviews and trust, mediated by reviews' perceived utilitarian and perceived hedonic values within the C2C e-commerce context.

title: Scalar Precoding for Topological Interference Management with Heterogeneous Erasures
authors: Peixin Chen, Junge Wang, Syed A. Jafar
abstract: The problem of scalar topological interference management with heterogeneous erasures (STIME) is formalized, by adopting a one-shot model that favors ultra low latency communication, partially connected interference network topologies that are motivated by directional transmission, limited channel knowledge at the transmitters that is restricted to the network connectivity, erasures that model sporadic disruptions that are detectable at the receiver, and a performance metric that is closer to Hamming than Shannon, seeking the shortest multiuser-code that is robust to a set of specified erasure patterns for each user. In the absence of erasures, STIME reduces to the scalar TIM (STIM) problem which, based on prior work, corresponds to scalar index coding, and as such admits a well known minrank optimal solution. By the same token, STIME can be viewed as essentially a scalar index coding problem with heterogeneous erasures at each receiver. The optimal solution to STIME is bounded within a factor of 2 based on its connections to STIM for heterogeneous erasures. The bounds are shown to be tight in several cases, e.g., for topologies where alignment graphs have no internal conflicts, for neighboring interference models, and for arbitrary topologies if the erasure thresholds are identical across users. A mapping from any STIME setting to a corresponding STIM setting is identified such that the two problems have the same solution.

title: Phased UAV Swarm Mission Reliability Assessment Considering Cascading Failures
authors: Junxing Ren, Liudong Xing
abstract: In many critical real-world scenarios (e.g., emergency response, environmental protection, infrastructure surveillance), based on the Internet of Things (IoT) technology, multiple UAV swarms work together to acquire, transmit, and process information, subsequently taking action to provide the intended service. Collaborations and interactions among UAVs create interdependencies that can facilitate the propagation of failures, potentially leading to high-impact cascading effects. Leveraging the hierarchical architecture of IoT, this work examines the cascading failure mechanism triggered by the relay malfunction and its impact on mission reliability of a heterogeneous multi-phase UAV swarm system. To analyze mission reliability under cascading effects, a separable and analytical modeling method is put forward, which decomposes the original problem into independent, reduced reliability problems for efficient analysis. A detailed case study on a two-phase rescue mission showcases the model's ability to capture failure propagation and assess its impact. The suggested approach offers an efficient means of quantifying cascading effects, enabling mission planners to identify vulnerabilities and improve overall resilience strategies. Furthermore, based on the proposed mission reliability analysis method, resource allocation problems are formulated and solved to balance cost and mission reliability in practical mission design.

title: Unraveling the Degradation Mechanisms of AlGaN-Based UVC-LEDs: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: Pengyan Wen, Yu He, Meixin Feng, Jiahao Dong, Xinbo Zou, Jiaxiang Chen, Haolan Qu, Xiujian Sun, Qian Sun, Hui Yang
abstract: In the fields of medical diagnostics, virus inactivation, and air/water purification, ultraviolet-C light-emitting diodes (UVC-LEDs) have emerged as pivotal light sources, attracting substantial attention in the post-COVID-19 era. Nevertheless, their long-term reliability remains a critical area requiring further optimization. This study focuses on the degradation mechanism of AlGaN-based UVC-LEDs. Experimental results demonstrate that the aged devices exhibit a notable increase in both turn-on voltage and ideality factor. Concurrently, their electroluminescence (EL) characteristics reveal a redshift in peak emission wavelength and a significant reduction in luminescence intensity. By integrating noise analysis and cathodoluminescence (CL) characterization, we conclude that defect-related nonradiative recombination centers were generated in the n-AlGaN layer beneath the quantum wells (QWs), and these centers facilitate defect-mediated carrier tunneling, contributing to the observed performance degradation. This research provides valuable insights to guide the subsequent design and development of high-reliability UVC-LEDs.

title: RSMA-Enhanced Robust Weighted Secrecy Rate for HRIS-aided Near-Field Communications
authors: Sandeep Singh, Keshav Singh, Sandeep Kumar Singh, Fan-Shuo Tseng, Cunhua Pan
abstract: This study investigates the performance of rate splitting multiple access (RSMA) in a holographic reconfigurable intelligent surface (HRIS)-aided downlink near-field (NF) secure communication system with imperfect channel state information (iCSI) in the presence of an eavesdropper (Eve). In order to provide efficient resource utilization, a weighted sum secrecy rate (WSSR) maximization problem is formulated, where the transmit beamforming vector at the base station (BS) and the HRIS phase profile are jointly optimized under the strict constraint of quality of service (QoS) requirement and total power budget at the BS. Since the optimization problem is non-convex due to the coupling of the variables, we propose an iterative algorithm based on alternating optimization (AO) that jointly optimize transmit beamforming at the BS and beamforming at the HRIS. Various numerical results are shown to validate the effectiveness and convergence of the proposed algorithm. Furthermore, we also discuss the impact of the key system parameters, such as HRIS reflecting elements, minimum QoS constraint, transmit power budget, and number of users. The dominance of RSMA over its counterparts, non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and space-division multiple access (SDMA), is also demonstrated. It is shown that the use of RSMA can achieve up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$77\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> higher data rate than NOMA and SDMA. It is also highlighted that, under the proposed algorithm with NF assumptions, the achieved average WSSR is around <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$86\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> higher than the hybrid and far-field (FF) assumptions. In addition, we compare the system performance under the assumption of perfect CSI (pCSI) and iCSI.

title: Adaptive Multi-Kernel Attention Framework for Multimodal Healthcare Data Integration and Disease Classification
authors: Arun Singh Yadav, Surendra Kumar, Shubham Shukla, Soumya Shrivastava, Jaydip Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Disease subtype classification requires effective integration of heterogeneous medical data such as imaging, genomics, and clinical records. However, existing multimodal fusion methods treat all subtypes uniformly, limiting their ability to capture subtype-specific patterns essential for accurate diagnosis. This paper proposes an adaptive multimodal fusion framework that leverages subtype-aware Gaussian kernel learning and a KernelAdaptive Cross-Modal Attention (KACA) mechanism to model nonlinear, subtype-discriminative relationships across modalities. A Subtype Gating Network dynamically modulates modality relevance, while a multi-task objective with an MMD-based alignment loss reduces cross-modal distribution gaps. Experiments on ADNI, TCGA-BRCA, and MIMIC-III demonstrate an average Macro F1-score of 80.7%, outperforming MMHA (75.0%) and CentralNet (74.1%), including a 12.3% gain for BRCA subtyping. The model also achieves a 49.5% reduction in modality divergence, highlighting its robustness in heterogeneous clinical environments. Overall, the proposed approach enhances subtype-aware representation learning and improves diagnostic precision.

title: Modeling the Changes in the Current–Voltage Characteristics of Vertical GaN-on-GaN p-i-n Diodes Under Electrical Stress
authors: Ambra Maria Vianello, Simone Leonardo Longato, Manuel Fregolent, Fabiana Rampazzo, Matteo Buffolo, Dawei Wang, Bingcheng Da, Houqiang Fu, Shisong Luo, Cheng Chang, Yuji Zhao, Isabella Rossetto, Carlo De Santi, Gaudenzio Meneghesso, Enrico Zanoni, Matteo Meneghini
abstract: We analyze and model the changes in the electrical properties of vertical GaN-on-GaN p-i-n diodes submitted to electrical stress. The analyzed devices were treated with hydrogen plasma to minimize surface leakage. Constant-current stress tests were carried out to investigate the stability of the electrical characteristics of the samples. The results indicate the existence of two phases: 1) for short stress times, we detected a lowering in the turn-on voltage (V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {ON}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), with no significant variation in series resistance, which was ascribed to an increase in injection efficiency at the anode contact and 2) for longer stress times, the turn-on voltage stabilized, but the series resistance showed a measurable decrease (−35%). Based on TCAD simulations, the observed behavior is ascribed to the diffusion of hydrogen from the p<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> contact layer toward the junction. A model based on Fick’s second law was developed, finding good agreement with the experimental data.

title: Intent-Driven 6G Communication Framework for RIS and Spectrum Leasing
authors: Zawar Hussain, Naveed Ul Hassan
abstract: Intent-Driven Communication (IDC) is emerging as a key paradigm for autonomous 6G networks, where AI and Large Language Models (LLMs) translate high-level user intents into actionable network policies. Meanwhile, Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) and dynamic spectrum leasing are becoming essential for improving coverage and capacity in resource-constrained environments. This paper extends the IDC framework by integrating RIS and spectrum leasing into AI-assisted intent translation, policy mapping, and orchestration. A leasing-aware architecture is presented, and a Lyapunov-based Decision Support Framework is implemented as an illustrative mechanism for intelligent resource acquisition under time-varying prices and availability. Simulation results validate that the DSF achieves cost-efficient, delay-aware orchestration while exhibiting the expected Lyapunov stability properties. These findings highlight the feasibility of combining IDC with intelligent resource leasing in future 6G systems.

title: Hardware-Friendly Algorithm Optimization and Acceleration for 8K UHD Image and Video Broadcasting
authors: Hang Yuan, Wei Gao
abstract: Efficient implementation plays a critical role for real-time 8K Ultra-High-Definition (UHD) video broadcasting with the new generation video coding standards. Specifically, the more complex Coding Unit (CU) partitioning rules in new standards make it much more challenging to design efficient acceleration strategies. This paper proposes a light-weight hardware-friendly acceleration algorithm for CU partitioning, which is the bottleneck to implementing the Versatile Video Coding (VVC) based real-time intra codec for 8K video broadcasting on hardware platform. According to the CU partitioning rule, we first propose an novel One-Pass framework to determine CU split results, which can effectively reduce time complexity and hardware resources incurred by the original iterative decision process. Second, taking into account the characteristics of hardware platform, the efficient calculation methods based on Map-Reduce model and hardware-oriented optimizations are designed for gradient and histogram difference features required by our One-Pass framework. Finally, we design a reference hardware architecture to validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm to support real-time 8K video broadcasting. Besides, a light-weight 8K UHD video dataset including diverse content is also provided to more reliably illustrate the coding performance of our algorithm. Experimental results show that our method is capable of the real-time CU division on Xilinx VU440 FPGA at 200 MHz, where the single-path can support 8K videos at 45 frames per second (fps), and 135 fps also can be achieved with multi-path parallelism. As far as we are aware, the proposed algorithm has attained state-of-the-art performance for the hardware-friendly acceleration of CU partitioning with the least loss in VVC standard.

title: Path Planning Considering Artificial Potential Fields Based on Trajectory Prediction
authors: Yining Xing, Yiqian Tu, Yuning Wang, Mengchi Cai, Jianqiang Wang, Lu Geng, Miao Jiang, Kinoshita Masafumi
abstract: With the development of deep learning technologies, the accuracy of trajectory prediction has significantly improved. Leveraging predicted trajectories for motion planning can reduce the risk of collisions between vehicles more effectively. However, prior studies have shown that directly using predicted trajectories in planning often leads to suboptimal performance and may even cause collisions. To address this, we propose a novel framework that integrates trajectory prediction with artificial potential fields (APFs), enabling the effective use of prediction results in planning. In this paper, we introduce a trajectory planning framework that constructs a spatiotemporal risk (STR) field based on deep learning-based trajectory prediction and inter-vehicle interactions. The planning module then employs an optimization-based approach that considers multiple objectives, including safety, comfort, rule compliance, efficiency, and human likeness. Evaluated on a real-world dataset, our method achieves an 8.72% improvement in minimum Anticipated Collision Time (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{ACT}\_{\min }$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and a 36.0% gain in efficiency compared to the baseline Driving Safety Field (DSF) method. Compared with other competing approaches, our method improves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{ACT}\_{\min }$</tex-math></inline-formula> by 10.67% while maintaining competitive performance in terms of comfort, efficiency, and human likeness. The results from the NuPlan closed-loop experiments indicate that our method exhibits superior planning capabilities in complex scenarios, surpassing the performance of all baseline methods. Furthermore, closed-loop experiments demonstrate that our approach enables continuous and collision-free planning in highly interactive traffic scenarios.

title: ExplainChain-SE: A Lightweight Benchmark for Explainable Debugging with Reasoning-to-Repair Alignment
authors: Ilia Sedoshkin
abstract: Many automated debugging tools can repair faulty code but often fail to explain why their fixes make sense. This reduces developer trust and limits our ability to study how reasoning connects to repair success. We introduce , a lightweight framework and benchmark that links short natural-language explanations to verified fixes across well-known bug–fix datasets which has three main components: a Bug Analyzer that identifies likely fault locations and suggests possible causes, a Fix Generator that applies small, rule-based edits and records a concise rationale, and a Verifier that checks whether the fix works and measures how closely the explanation matches the result. We evaluate on QuixBugs, Defects4J, and a CodeXGLUE refinement split (Bugs2Fix). The system outputs detailed logs containing the buggy code, the reference fix, the generated fix, the reasoning text, and validation scores. In experiments, generated repairs closely matched reference fixes on Bugs2Fix (mean ≈ 0.97) and Defects4J placeholders (mean ≈ 0.87), while QuixBugs showed moderate variation (mean ≈ 0.80) due to its diverse algorithms. All runs finished quickly and produced reproducible metrics and plots. By combining reasoning with verification, provides a transparent, reproducible foundation for explainable debugging. It is simple to extend with more datasets, richer test cases, or large language model–based fix generators, paving the way for deeper research into reasoning-aware program repair.

title: Endogenous Interface Pricing for Consistent Transmission–Distribution Co-Optimization with Discrete Distribution Controls
authors: Seung-Gil Noh, Miguel Heleno, Yun-Su Kim
abstract: This paper proposes an endogenous interface pricing model for day-ahead transmission–distribution co-optimization that co-determines the interface locational marginal price (LMP) and the transmission–distribution exchange, ensuring price–dispatch consistency while optimally scheduling discrete distribution controls. The formulation couples a DC optimal power flow (OPF) with a branch-flow AC OPF that schedules distributed energy resources (DERs), tap-changer settings, capacitor banks (CBs), and multi-period energy storage systems (ESSs) under feeder voltage and current limits, and is solved as a mixed-integer second-order cone program (MISOCP). In a T14–D33 system, coordinated device scheduling recovers about 90% of the distribution-to-transmission export achievable in a reference case that ignores distribution network (DN) limits, while satisfying a 1.05 p.u. voltage upper bound. In a T39–D34/D37/D123 system, a sequential decoupled benchmark produces interface LMP distortions up to 12.5% and a 7.28% mismatch in net export energy, whereas the proposed model removes these distortions and the associated settlement mismatches. Second-order cone (SOC) relaxation gaps remain below 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> in all cases.

title: Adaptable Variational Information Bottleneck for Task-Oriented Edge Inference
authors: Exaud Tarimo, Huanlai Xing, Lexi Xu, Jincheng Peng, Li Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper studies task-oriented communication for cooperative edge inference in wireless sensor networks, where multiple edge devices transmit compact feature representations to a central edge server for joint decision-making. Recently, cooperative edge inference significantly improved the device-server system performance, however at the expense of increased communication overhead. To this end, an adaptive variational information bottleneck (A-VIB) framework that enables edge devices to extract and transmit task-relevant features under dynamic channel conditions is proposed. The framework jointly optimizes feature relevance, communication efficiency, and inter-device coordination through stochastic adaptive encoding and graph-based mutual-information suppression. To further reduce bandwidth consumption, a graph-coordinated feature decorrelation strategy is developed, enabling each device to adjust its feature dimensionality and minimize transmitting redundant information. It employs a local redundancy suppressor on the edge device to discard task-irrelevant features during extraction. We also adopt the information bottleneck principle and propose a dynamic rate control network module (RateNet) to accommodate the varying networking conditions. Extensive experiments on multi-view image classification and multi-view object recognition tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of A-VIB, achieving up to a 58.6% reduction in communication cost and up to a 13.2% improvement in inference accuracy compared with state-of-the-art semantic communication and distributed inference schemes under varying SNR and bandwidth-constrained wireless environments. In addition, A-VIB exhibits improved robustness to channel interference by 3.29%.

title: A Survey on Interaction-Aware Decision-Making for Autonomous Driving: Challenges, Solutions, and Perspectives
authors: Shen Li, Kai Yang, Zichun Wei, Yuan Zheng, Zhige Chen, Xiaolin Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Interacting with diverse and stochastic traffic participants is a critical challenge for autonomous vehicles (AVs), as it necessitates advanced decision-making systems to replicate the natural adaptability of human drivers. In particular, navigating safely and efficiently in dense traffic scenarios poses a significant challenge for decision-making, which is inherently an interactive task, i.e., nearby traffic participants will influence AVs’ action, and vice versa. Decision-making solutions that rely solely on unidirectional interaction schemes, neglecting the mutual influence between AVs and other traffic participants, may lead to overly defensive behaviors or the “freezing robot problem”. In recent years, researchers have been increasingly focused on incorporating bidirectional interactions into the decision-making process to make safe, intelligent, and socially compatible decisions. Currently, a comprehensive review of interaction-aware decision-making techniques remains lacking. To this end, this paper aims to provide a systematic review of interaction-aware decision-making methodologies for autonomous driving. Specifically, this paper analyzes the challenges in considering bidirectional interactions between AVs and other traffic participants. In addition, the state-of-the-art techniques for interaction-aware decision-making solutions are reviewed. More importantly, simulation and benchmarks for interaction-aware decision-making validation are also presented. Finally, research perspectives are highlighted to facilitate future studies for interaction-aware decision-making policy design.

title: Retraction Notice: Sensors Systems for Traffic Congestion Reduction Methodologies
authors: Thulasi Bikku, V. Lakshman Narayana, Arepalli Peda Gopi, Sk. Reshmi Khadherbhi
publish: 2019 Third International conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Nowadays the number of vehicles on the road has been expanded exponentially, but the limitations of roads and transportation frameworks have not created in a comparable method to effectively adapt with the number of vehicles going on them. Because of this, road congestion has expanded around the world. Sensor systems have increased by expanding consideration in rush hour traffic identification and maintaining a strategic distance from heavy traffic. WSNs are extremely smart because of their quicker exchange of data, simple establishment and for being more affordable contrasted with other systems. Remote sensor systems are an innovation which has assumed an enormous job empowering smarter city urban communities is utilizing this innovation to accumulate information identified with movement. The goal is to have an entire framework that empowers the observing of activity practices so choices on city advancement can be made smarter. This paper provides a survey on road traffic congestion control with the help of sensors which communicate with other vehicles nearby for avoiding traffic as well as road accidents. This paper performs a survey on various techniques on road traffic reduction methods of road accidents using sensors.

title: Retraction Notice: Solar Powered Automatic Irrigation Monitoring System
authors: J.Geetha Ramani, A. LakshmiPriya, S. Madhusudan, P.J. Rahul Kishore, M. Madhisha, U. Preethi
publish: 2020 6th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Being very founded on farming the economy requests for imaginative and solid techniques for water system. By utilizing the most supportable sun powered vitality the deficiencies of non-inexhaustible assets in water system can be amended. This paper displays the possibility of programmed water system strategy with solar as the primary vitality hotspot for power. The errand of programmed water system is done through help of soil dampness sensors. To make the procedure further developed moisture and temperature sensors are used additionally. The proposed plan has the component of GSM which makes this framework remote. The water substance is always noted and when the dampness level of soil levels to low values, the framework will sends a sign to engines requesting it to start. The engines would stop by itself once the soil achieves its greatest upper limit esteem that is chosen by client. Each time the engine turns over or stops naturally, the client will receive a SMS regarding the activity status. In addition this paper incorporates improvement of a framework through sensors utilizing Node MCU which can screen even the movement of creatures which may crush the harvests in horticultural field and furthermore catch a picture of those exercises and sent to the proprietor. The sun based boards provide the power required by parts thus this frees us from intruded on power supply because of burden shedding. The real preferences of the task utilizing sun oriented vitality is that it minimizes the power bills, requires less maintenance and gives an approach to stop global warming.

title: Retraction Notice: An Effecient Model for Plant Disease Identification
authors: Nithya Sri P, Sajitha D, Chancalviben N B, Babitha Lincy R
publish: 2023 9th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Plants are the world's food source. The country's rural development is depending on the productivity of crops. Specialists frequently inspect the plants to locate and identify illnesses. This approach, however, is typically time-consuming, expensive, and prone to mistakes. Automatic identification is essential. As an alternative to the usual time-consuming cycle, various research projects aim to discover viable methods for securing plants. Recently, advancements in invention have resulted in a few alternatives to traditional tedious methods. In order to allow the model to discriminate between sick and healthy leaves or between different climates, the strong leaves and foundational images are processed with various classifications. In classification challenges, advanced deep learning approaches are particularly helpful. This study uses deep convolutional networks to tackle an alternative method of dealing with the automatic identification of plant infectious recognition models.

title: Fault Analysis and Pilot Protection Scheme for Various Single-Phase Line Breaks in Resonant Grounding Systems
authors: Min Huang, Gang Wang, Jie Li, Haoming Wang, Shenquan Liu, Jican Lin, Dehui Zeng, Shaoyan Jiang
abstract: Due to small fault currents and different fault characteristics under various grounding types on both sides of the fault point, the existing methods are facing challenges in the analysis and protection principle for single-phase break faults (SPBFs) in resonant grounding systems (RGSs), including inaccurate fault identification, low sensitivity, and insufficient reliability under high-impedance grounding and intermittent arc faults. In this paper, fault characteristics of zero-sequence voltages upstream and downstream of the break point with different grounding modes are analyzed. The SPBFs with grounding at single-side and without grounding only exhibit the amplitude or phase characteristic, while SPBFs with grounding at both sides incorporate the two features. On this basis, a pilot protection scheme is developed for various SPBFs in RGSs, where internal and external SPBFs are determined by combining the features of the amplitude and phase of dual-terminal zero-sequence voltages. The effectiveness of the proposed methodology is validated through a closed-loop hardware test by applying a real-time digital simulator (RTDS) and the developed protective devices. Experimental results prove that the protection scheme performs well under different fault types, fault locations, fault resistances, and the presence of inverter-interfaced distributed generators.

title: Retraction Notice: Automatic Detecftion of White Blood Cancer From Blood Cells Using Novel Machine Lerning Techniques
authors: Aldrin karunharan. K, Kanmani Pappa. C, E.D Kanmani Ruby, Sathyasri. B, Edwin Shanthakumar. W
publish: 2022 8th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: The precise recognition of cells is required for the diagnosis of numerous blood illnesses, such as Leukemia and Myeloma, using WBCs or Leukocytes. In order to assist oncologists an automated system is necessary to minimize time and improve accuracy. The segmentation and classification of immature white blood cells are the most crucial phases in such systems. In this aper we have discussed the methodology for detection of white blood cancer caused because of immature leukocytes by using preprocessing, segmentation by Otsu algorithm, Salp swarm algorithm and random forest algorithm. In this paper we have explored the performance of three techniques such as random forest algorithm (RSA) Salp swarm algorithm (SSA) and Statistically enhanced Salp swarm algorithm (SESSA). We have achieved better segmentation and detection accuracy compared to other peer soft computing algorithms. The results shall be beneficiary for hematologists and oncologist as diagnostic reference before proceeding t the treatment strategy

title: Exploring Tourist Behavior and Usability Improvement for Smart Tourism in Secondary Cities: A Case Study of Lamphun Province
authors: Pimsiri Suangswang, Thepparit Sinthamrongruk, Chalermpon Kongjit, Korawan Sangkakorn
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study explores tourist behavior and usability improvement for tourism applications in Thailand's secondary cities, focusing on Lamphun Province as a pilot case. Data from 50 tourists who used or expressed interest in tram-based sightseeing services were analyzed using the 5A's of Tourism framework (Attraction, Accessibility, Accommodation, Amenities, and Activities) and ISO 9241-11 usability standards. Results show that most visitors travel for leisure and cultural purposes. Attraction and Accessibility performed strongly, while Activities and Amenities emerged as key areas needing enhancement. Usability analysis showed that Satisfaction scored slightly lower than Effectiveness and Efficiency, highlighting the need to strengthen engagement and emotional appeal. Correlation analysis identified exploratory associations between Accessibility and Satisfaction, and between Activities and both Satisfaction and Effectiveness. Based on these findings, four priority areas—Accessibility, Activities, Accommodation, and Amenities— were identified for design and functional improvement. Integrating the 5A's model with Design Thinking and usability frameworks offers a practical approach for developing user-centered smart tourism applications in secondary cities.

title: Slice-and-Align for Clothes-Irrelevant Features: A Clothes-Changing Person Re-Identification Approach Without Additional Input
authors: Yuwei Zhao, Guozhen Peng, Annan Li, Yunhong Wang
abstract: Clothes-changing person re-identification (CC Re-ID) focuses on recognizing pedestrians in a long-term with changes in clothes. Prior arts extract clothes-irrelevant features either by introducing extra modality or clothing labels, having their respective limitations. Instead, we seek to extract clothes-irrelevant features without additional input. We first analyze and find that one impediment to extracting clothes-irrelevant features is the co-occurrence of samples with the same clothes and the same identity. Inspired by this observation, we propose a novel CC Re-ID approach using no additional input. We introduce the Slice-and-Align Framework (SA), which employs a straightforward and intuitive prior: the upper and lower clothes of a person are usually different. SA is a dual-stream framework that slices the original image into upper and lower halves, and then aligns them to extract clothes-irrelevant features. On image CC Re-ID datasets, SA outperforms methods without additional input by a large margin and is comparable to or even better than methods with additional input. Besides, SA also outperforms state-of-the-art on video CC Re-ID task.

title: Enhancing Recruitment Efficiency through a Performance-Driven AI Agent Workflow for Automated Resume Screening
authors: Phasuth Pennivessuk, Thanapat Kangkachit
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper presents the design and development of a low-code AI multi-agent workflow for Automated Resume Analysis to enhance recruitment efficiency and transparency. The proposed system integrates multiple AI Agents: email classification, resume information extraction, job description extraction, and AI resume and job description matching within a single workflow connected to external services such as Google Drive and Gmail. An AI output approval by human mechanism is incorporated to maintain reliability and applicability in real HR contexts. Experiments on a dataset of resumes, job descriptions, and mock recruitment emails show that the system can analyze one candidate in about 2.12 minutes at an average cost of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\$ 0.03(\boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{1} \mathbf{~ T H B})$</tex> per record. Among the tested large language models (LLMs), ChatGPT-4o Mini achieved the best performance in data extraction and email classification, while ChatGPT-5 Mini delivered the most human-aligned resume matching results (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.95$</tex>, F1 score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.96$</tex>). The study demonstrates that combining multi-agent LLMs with workflow automation enables faster, more accurate, and cost-effective resume screening while providing explainability and supporting informed human decision-making for modern recruitment and selection process.

title: Surveillance Data Processing in a Multi-Radar Localization System
authors: Ivan Ostroumov, Daria Tkachuk, Nataliia Kuzmenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Surveillance is a key element of ensuring the safety of air traffic management. Different sensors are used in civil aviation to provide surveillance data: primary (PSR) and secondary surveillance radars (SSR), multilateration, and automatic dependent surveillance-broadcast. Ground network of radars provides a reliable surveillance function. A network helps to extend radar service for wide areas. In a paper, surveillance data processing is considered. A specific algorithm of data fusion from different ground radars of each airspace user is proposed. Fusion is based on a weighted approach, minimizing total error. Algorithm works with polar coordinates of airspace users, accompanied by performance of provided data. Proposed algorithm could be useful for implementation in surveilance data processing unit of a particular air navigation service provider.

title: Revisiting Vulnerability Patch Identification on Data in the Wild
authors: Ivana Clairine Irsan, Ratnadira Widyasari, Ting Zhang, Huihui Huang, Ferdian Thung, Yikun Li, Lwin Khin Shar, Eng Lieh Ouh, Hong Jin Kang, David Lo
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: Doppler-Domain Warping and Resampling for Semi-Static Human Occupancy Detection Using Low-Resolution SIMO FMCW Radar
authors: Huy Trinh, Elliot Creager, George Shaker
abstract: Radar-based human activity recognition is a promising privacy-preserving alternative to vision and wearable sensors, especially in sensitive environments such as long-term care facilities. However, reliable detection of quasi-static human presence, where subtle micro-motions such as small postural sway, breathing, small limb readjustments, etc, concentrate their energy around near-zero Doppler using low-resolution radar, remains challenging. In this work, we propose Range-Angle based Semi-Static Occupancy (RASSO), an invertible Doppler-domain non-linear remapping layer that resamples the slow-time FFT (Doppler) axis to densify the sampling grid around near-zero Doppler before adaptive Capon beamforming. This produces Range-Azimuth (RA) feature maps with higher Signal-to-Noise ratios (SNR), resulting in sharpened target peaks and reduced background variance. On a real dataset collected in a nursing home using short-range SIMO FMCW radar, RASSO consistently yields higher-contrast RA maps that are easier to threshold and more informative for downstream models. Under cell-averaging constant false alarm rate (CA-CFAR), RASSO-enhanced RA (RASSO-RA) achieves the highest detection-curve area under the curve (AUC = 0.981) and the best recall at false alarm rates (FAR) of 1% and 5% (0.920 and 0.947), compared to conventional Capon beam formation and a recent state-of-the-art baseline. Training on RASSO-RA further improves data-driven methods: a frame-based SimpleCNN reaches 95–99% accuracy across subjects, while a sequence-based CNN–LSTM achieves 99.4–99.6% accuracy, with paired session-level bootstrap analysis confirming statistically significant macro-F1 improvements of 2.6–3.6 percentage points (95% confidence intervals strictly above zero) over the non-warped baseline.

title: Methodological Aspects of Financial Competence Formation in the Structure of Supra-Professional Skills of Engineering Personnel
authors: Tatyana Y. Salutina, Yulia V. Gnezdova, Asiya R. Kaberova, Elena E. Volodina, Polina A. Zholtikova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The relevance of developing financial competence among future engineering personnel is determined by fundamental shifts in the global economy and industry. The purpose of this article is to develop and test the methodological foundations of financial competence formation as a structural element of the supra-professional skills of future engineers. To achieve this goal, the following tasks were solved in the study: to diagnose the demands of employers and the initial level of financial competencies of engineering students.; to develop a structural and substantive model of an engineer’s financial competence; to propose and experimentally test a methodological construct based on the principle of contextually embedded learning. The methodological basis of the study was based on systematic and competence-based approaches. The work used a set of theoretical (analysis of scientific literature, regulatory documents, modeling) and empirical (questionnaires, pedagogical experiment, methods of mathematical statistics) methods.

title: Object Identification Under Known Dynamics: A PIRNN Approach for UAV Classification
authors: Nyi Nyi Aung, Neil Muralles, Adrian Stein
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: This work addresses object identification under known dynamics in unmanned aerial vehicle applications, where learning and classification are combined through a physics-informed residual neural network. The proposed framework leverages physics-informed learning for state mapping and state-derivative prediction, while a softmax layer enables multi-class confidence estimation. Quadcopter, fixed-wing, and helicopter aerial vehicles are considered as case studies. The results demonstrate high classification accuracy with reduced training time, offering a promising solution for system identification problems in domains where the underlying dynamics are well understood.

title: Design of an on-Chip Microring Resonator Based Silicon Photonic Sensor
authors: Jenila C, Aarthi V.P.M. B, Sai Kiriti V, Sri Durga Prasad B, Ganesh Y
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This work proposes the design and numerical analysis of a silicon photonic microring-resonator-based biosensor for the detection of the Human epidermal growth factor 2 (HER2) cancer biomarker in an aqueous environment. The sensor is implemented on a standard Silicon on Insulator (SOI) platform using a silicon bus waveguide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.45 \mu ~\mathrm{m} \times 0.22 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>) and an 8 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>-radius ring resonator coupled through a 200-nm gap, with water acting as the upper cladding. A thin biolayer (10 nm) representing HER2 binding is modeled on the ring surface to evaluate refractive-index-induced resonance shifts. Using 3D Finite-Difference Time-Domain (FDTD) simulations, the sensor demonstrates a measurable resonance shift of 11 nm for an index change from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=1.38$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=1.45$</tex>, corresponding to a sensitivity of 110 nm/RIU (nanometers per refractive index unit). The resonance around 1524 nm exhibits a Full width at half maximum (FWHM) of 17 nm, resulting in a simulated Q-factor of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim$</tex> 89.6. Comparative E-field and H-field distributions confirm stronger modal confinement and enhanced perturbation at higher HER2 refractive indices. These results validate the proposed microring architecture as a compact, Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor (CMOS)-compatible platform for labelfree cancer biomarker sensing, demonstrating clear resonance signatures suitable for future optimization toward high quality (high-Q) and ultra-sensitive biomedical applications.

title: Single Event Upset Characterization in the Control Path of RISC-V Processor Core
authors: Shreyas R, Subir Kumar Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Functional Safety has emerged as a critical design requirement for modern-day SoCs operating in safety-critical environments. Single-event upsets (SEU) induced in hardware due to radiation or other transient disturbances pose a serious threat to the normal functionality of the design. Several SEU faults on the memories and data-path have been extensively analyzed and handled using both architectural and micro-architectural techniques. However, the control path is equally vulnerable and can potentially cause cascading failures, ultimately manifesting as a hardware malfunction. This paper focuses on extensively analyzing the SEU faults on the Open Source PULPino RISC-V core's control path, specifically for a motor control algorithm running on the CPU core. A specific methodology of fault analysis is introduced, and a vulnerability metric called Transition Architecture Vulnerability Factor (T-AVF), which maximizes the fault coverage on the control path for a given application running on the core. The results of Fault injection simulation on the core's control machines and the resultant T-AVFs are highlighted in this paper. This provides a foundation for designing future efficient, low-power, minimal area, and performance overhead-based mitigation techniques.

title: Scalable Clustering Over High Dimensional Vector Streams
