abstract: Forecasting air pollution is important for improving environmental health and safety. Most existing forecasting models are based on supervised learning approaches requiring vast amounts of labeled historical data, making them fundamentally limited in terms of scalability and adaptability. This paper presents a new method for forecasting air pollution based on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) techniques. In this case, we model the forecasting of air pollution as a dynamic decision-making problem, where the DRL learning agent observes real-time environmental information including pollutant concentrations, meteorological variable and geographical variables integrated from governmental environmental data to forecast future pollution levels. The DRL learning agent will optimize short and long-term forecasts of air quality (contaminant emissions and concentrations) as it explores, exploits, and learns from its continual interaction with the open world environment. Results from experiments using data from urban air quality monitoring stations indicate that the proposed DRL-based air quality forecasting model achieved higher levels of accuracy, compared to traditional machine learning methods. Overall, the results indicate that DRL learning agent can learn and adjust models based on and adapt to continued changing environmental conditions. Therefore, we concluded that the approach presents a more reliable and easier to implement solution with actionable and scalable predictions to support pollution control policy strategies and interventions for the protection of public health.

title: Transformer Bushing Health Monitoring via Embedded Nanoparticle-Coated Micro-Sensors
authors: Naveen Garg
abstract: Transformer bushings are vital insulation materials in high voltage power transformers, and their failure may have devastating effects of fires, transformer outages, and grid-wide crashes. Initial deterioration processes within bushings like partial discharge behaviour, ingrained moisture, hydrogen production and pressure fluctuation, can be hard to monitor through the conventional offline diagnostic services. This paper is a focused review of literature in the area of transformer bushing monitoring technologies and also a proposal of an embedded, multi-parameter health monitoring system, using nanoparticle-coated micro-sensors. The proposed system combines a Pd-Ni nano-film hydrogen sensor, temperature and pressure and micro-water sensors in one small module that can be directly mounted onto the bushing oil interfaces. The principles of fabrication, sensing mechanisms, and calibration models are addressed, and experimental validation of the fabrication property is performed with specific laboratory-simulated bushing operating conditions. The sensitivities indicated by the results show that it is very sensitive to dissolved hydrogen, has less drift in measurements, and is stable with long-term performance. The most important real issues, such as cost, scalability of manufacturing, long-term stability and electromagnetic interference are also under analysis. The suggested solution offers a modular way to in-situ real-time bushing monitoring, and thus the protocol will offer a more precise diagnostic and situation-based maintenance in contemporary power plants.

title: Efficient Jamming Detection in IoT Networks using 2D CNN Models
authors: Y. Pavan Kumar Reddy, V.Anusha, M.Senthil
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Industrial and mission-critical settings often employ Wi-Fi-based communication for the IoT because of its widespread availability, low cost, and high bandwidth. Internet of Things systems are especially vulnerable to jamming attacks because they disrupt communication and cause protocol layer failures. This paper extends previous research by incorporating a 2D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN2D) for enhanced jamming detection and forecasting in Wi-Fi-based IoT systems. The proposed CNN2D model efficiently extracts optimized features using 2-dimensional neurons, enabling more precise identification of jamming patterns and anomalies in transport and application layer parameters. The experimental setup records the connection and quality of service metrics in server-client industrial IoT configurations under both normal and jammed conditions. When it comes to feature extraction, CNN2D is superior to LSTM-based models. It achieved 100% accuracy on 20% test data. When it comes to real-time Internet of Things applications, CNN2D improves detection accuracy, processing speed, and resilience. Confronting the security issues surrounding the IoT, this study demonstrates that CNN2D may reliably and scalably defend industrial IoT settings against jamming attempts.

title: AI-Enabled Learning and Critical Thinking: A Study among Indian Engineering Students
authors: Manish Singh, Deepak Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The growing presence of generative AI-based tools in higher education has brought up urgent rhetoric questions as to the impact on students (higher-order thinking) and especially critical thinking. The authors investigate the dependence between attitude to AI use and the critical thinking skills of Indian engineering students. The data was obtained by means of a structured survey among undergraduate engineering students, which included the validated Critical Thinking Questionnaire (CThQ) and questions addressing the trends in the use of generative AI. High-frequency and low-frequency AI users were identified based on self-reports of frequency. There was the independent-samples t-test, which was done to compare the critical thinking scores of these groups. The results show that in spite of the fact that low-frequency AI users achieved marginally higher mean critical thinking scores with a high-frequency user, the difference was not significant in statistical terms. These findings indicate that the frequency of AI usage is insignificant in forecasting critical thinking skill. Rather, the results highlight the need to investigate qualitative aspects of AI use, including patterns of dependence and cognitive offloading, which, rather than usage frequency, determine usage frequency. The work helps in providing context-dependent empirical data in the Indian engineering education and makes suggestions to AI literacy programs and the responsible introduction of AI tools within the educational process.

title: Autonomous joint multi-factor sensing platform design for lunar space environment
authors: Yu-Qing Wang, Zheng-Yi Ren, Da-Cheng Ao, Le-Yuan Yu, Wen-Jie Jia, Zhen-Hua Xi, Hu-Zhong Zhang, Jiang Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: To address the critical need for collaborative sensing of multiple factors of the lunar space environment, this paper developed and implemented an autonomous joint intelligent sensing platform for multiple factors of the lunar space environment. The platform adopted an integrated technical architecture that seamlessly combined component sensing, whole-machine collaboration, model-driven analysis, and software integration, deeply merging high-performance sensing payloads such as Laser-induced Breakdown Spectroscopy-Raman Spectroscopy-Infrared (LIBS-RS-IR), Confocal Laser Scanning Microscope (CLSM), Time-of-flight mass spectrometry (TOF), and a Dust Analyzer (DCA). Key technologies such as high-precision spatiotemporal alignment of multi-source heterogeneous data and autonomous intelligent collaborative detection and control were emphasized. By constructing an autonomous decision-making algorithm based on deep reinforcement learning, the platform achieved tightly coupled collaborative detection and intelligent scheduling of multiple environmental factors, including lunar surface material composition and morphology, neutral and charged particles, and charged dust. Combining a volatile matter migration dynamics model with multi-source data fusion and inversion technology, the platform possessed the capability for three-dimensional visualization and dynamic simulation of the spatiotemporal distribution of important lunar surface volatiles (i.e. H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O, He, Ar, K, Na etc.). The platform was helpful for effectively monitoring payload health status, performing intelligent fault diagnosis, data fusion analysis, and scientific visualization, providing advanced intelligent sensing and decision support tools for revealing the lunar surface volatile transport mechanism and supporting subsequent lunar exploration and resource development missions.

title: Edu-EmotionNet: Cross-Modality Attention Alignment with Temporal Feedback Loops
authors: S M Rafiuddin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Understanding learner emotions in online education is critical for improving engagement and personalized instruction. While prior work in emotion recognition has explored multimodal fusion and temporal modeling, existing methods often rely on static fusion strategies and assume that modality inputs are consistently reliable, which is rarely the case in real-world learning environments. We introduce Edu-EmotionNet, a novel framework that jointly models temporal emotion evolution and modality reliability for robust affect recognition. Our model incorporates three key components- a Cross-Modality Attention Alignment (CMAA) module for dynamic cross-modal context sharing, a Modality Importance Estimator (MIE) that assigns confidence-based weights to each modality at every time step, and a Temporal Feedback Loop (TFL) that leverages previous predictions to enforce temporal consistency. Evaluated on educational subsets of IEMOCAP and MOSEI, re-annotated for confusion, curiosity, boredom, and frustration, Edu-EmotionNet achieves state-of-the-art performance and demonstrates strong robustness to missing or noisy modalities. Visualizations confirm its ability to capture emotional transitions and adaptively prioritize reliable signals, making it well suited for deployment in real-time learning systems.

title: Improving Robustness of MRI-Based Brain Tumor Classification Via Multi-View Preprocessing and Hybrid VIT-CNN Learning
authors: Sabuj Maity, Jyoti Bharti, Vasudev Dehalwar, Anuj Khandelwal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Although magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is a critical tool in the clinical assessment of patients with suspected or confirmed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex> rimary <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{b}$</tex> rain <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{t}$</tex> umors, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{c}$</tex> omputerized i mage interpretation for automatic classification ofb rain t umors has remained a significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{c}$</tex> hallenge. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{T}$</tex> he <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{c}$</tex> hallenges <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{i}$</tex> nclude the availability of sufficient a nnotated i mages, d ifferences i n image acquisition between scanners, variations in image quality associated with acquisition parameters, and others. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have demonstrated excellent capabilities for extracting localized anatomical features from MRI images. However, CNNs are limited in their ability to capture long-range spatial relationships because they have small receptive fields. On the other hand, Vision Transformers (ViTs) can capture longrange spatial relationships using self-attention mechanisms but they are less efficient in terms of the amount of data required to train them and may be unstable in training on the heterogeneity of medical images. Therefore, this study presents a clinically motivated hybrid CNN-Transformer model combined with a targeted multi-view preprocessing method. Unlike many previous studies that relied on large sets of augmentation operations to enhance generalization to different acquisition protocols, the preprocessing module produces three complementary representations of each MRI slice, which are created through baseline intensity normalization, brightness normalization, and leastvariant filtering. The goal of these transformations is to account for the variability caused by intensity shifts due to different MRI scanners, variability in backgrounds, and non-informative homogeneous regions that are common in many routine clinical MRI exams, and therefore to improve the robustness of the model to variations in appearance due to the acquisition protocol. The proposed architecture uses a CNN as the backbone to extract discriminative local features from MRI slices. A lightweight Transformer encoder is then used to aggregate convolutionally embedded patches to model global contextual relationships while preserving spatial coherence. An experimental evaluation was performed on a four-class brain tumor MRI dataset (glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, and non-tumor) that demonstrates the proposed approach achieves an accuracy of 96.42 %. The computational cost of the proposed architecture is also low and it exhibits stability across preprocessing views. Attention response maps demonstrate that the model focuses its attention on clinically relevant tumor regions, providing evidence for the model's interpretability. Overall, the results provide support for the feasibility and effectiveness of combining selective and clinically grounded preprocessing methods with hybrid CNN-Transformer modeling for brain tumor classification in heterogeneous MRI environments.

title:
Performance Enhancement of Rice Bran Oil–based Transformer Fluid Enhanced with TiO
                    <sub>₂</sub>
                    Nanoparticles as Dielectric Additives
authors: Bharanisree V, Abirami V, Dr.B.Vigneshwaran, Aarthi M
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This study evaluates the dielectric properties and physicochemical strength of used transformer oil combined through blending with rice bran oil and enhanced with titanium dioxide (TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf>) nanoparticles. Different samples were taken in varying blend ratios, starting from 100% used transformer oil to 100% rice bran oil. In this, 0.1 g of TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf> nanoparticles was added to each 500 ml of blended oil sample. The key properties, such as breakdown voltage, viscosity, flash point, fire point, and acidity were evaluated and statistically analyzed. Results indicate that the incorporation of rice bran oil significantly improved the thermal and chemical properties of the used mineral oil, while TiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₂</inf> nanoparticles improved its dielectric strength. This method demonstrates a sustainable and an effective approach for used transformer oil reclamation process in extending the life time of mineral oil and reducing environmental impact. To evaluate, a paired t-test was conducted using a data analysis tool to compare values before and after nanoparticle addition across all samples to determine whether the sample has a significant impact.

title: Early Forest Fire Detection Using Enhanced YOLOv11 with Contrast and Dynamic Attention Mechanisms
authors: U. Vijay Kumar, Thirupati Jai Prakash, Gopavarapu Rama Krishna Prasad, M. Jayanthi Rao, R. P. Ram Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Early recognition of forest fires is necessary to minimize environmental loss and loss of human life by propagating wild fires. However, recognition of weak fires or weak smoke plumes in their early stages is challenging due to low visibility and lack of same-order-of-magnitude smoke signatures for natural atmospheric effects such as fog or mist. As a response to these limitations, in this paper, a more capable forest fire detection system based on effective YOLO architectures, i.e., YOLOv9 and YOLOv11, has been proposed for small-scale and complex fire detection. The proposed method incorporates strong preprocessing techniques, i.e., the Atmospheric Scattering Model (ASM) to remove haze and (CLAHE) for contrast enhancement. These significantly enhance weak smoke and fire details detection under low-light and foggy scenarios. Another improvement comes from long-range dependency modeling and attention mechanisms, which enable the network to learn subtle spatial relationships and improve multi-scale fire detection accuracy. Large-scale tests with two benchmark datasets, M4SFWD and FLAME, demonstrate that the proposed approach is effective. YOLOv11, especially, achieved superior Mean Average Precision (mAP) than baseline YOLO versions. These results demonstrate that the combination of the effective preprocessing with feature-augmented YOLO models can provide precise real-time and resilient detection of forest fires, making the system suitable for early warning and large-scale monitoring of wildfires

title: AI-Powered Urban Traffic Management using YOLOv11 and Meta-Learning-based Deep Q-Network
authors: K.Varada Rajkumar, Rallapally Vivek, Yejjavarapu Venu, Pola Santhosh, Bandlamudi S. B. P. Rani, Marlapalli Krishna
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Urban areas face significant challenges related to traffic congestion, leading to increased travel time, pollution, accidents and difficulties in clearing traffic for emergency vehicles. This project, "Smart Traffic Management using YOLOv11 and GBML-DQN" is aimed at resolving these problems through the creation of an innovative traffic system. The proposed systems leverage high-definition cameras, sensors, and a reinforcement learning model. Also, Other embedded applications are installed with AI algorithms to carry out vehicle detection and monitoring including YOLOv11 for object detection and BotSort for vehicle tracking. The system design provides for adaptive traffic signal controls using Reinforcement Learning model to manage the traffic efficiently in real-time. The expected outcome is to get a new twist in terms of efficiency of the traffic management and control system, through, reducing congestion and facilitating safer road usage while taking better care of the environment with smarter, and data-driven decision-making.

title: Control of dissolved oxygen and ammonium ion in constructed wetlands using multivariable integral state feedback controller
authors: Rishov Krishna Baruah, Sandipan Prasad Chakravarty, Prasanta Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Constructed wetlands (CWs) are artificially created wetlands. These are economic alternatives to the conventional wastewater treatment plant. A horizontal subsurface flow constructed wetland (HSFCW) is considered here. The dissolved oxygen (DO) and ammonium ion (AI) concentrations are the two most important parameters that decide the water quality in a CW. The chemical kinetics in a CW is highly nonlinear, uncertain, and coupled. This article presents the control of DO and AI concentrations in an HSFCW using a multivariable integral state feedback controller. The efficacy of the control strategy is tested using numerical simulations. Results show that the control objectives are met. Additionally, the proposed controller shows better performance than that of several other literature.

title: Investigation of Dual Active Bridge Converters for Electric Vehicle Battery Charger
authors: K Dhineshkumar, M Deepak, R Mythili, Sweety Kumari, Rishi Bala R
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: The rapid development of electric mobility calls for efficient, small-scale, and bidirectional power conversion systems for charging infrastructure. Isolated DC–DC converter topologies are increasingly popular for the provision of galvanic isolation and adaptive voltage flexibility between the vehicle battery and the grid. Out of all these, the DAB) converter is popular for its high-power density, bidirectional operation, and compatibility for both (G2V) and (V2G) operations. However, traditional control with the Single-Phase Shift method is constrained by a small soft-switching range as well as high circulating current at partial loading, causing overall system efficiency to decrease. To overcome this issue, this study explores the Extended Phase Shift control approach that adds extra phase degrees of freedom to improve zero-voltage switching conditions as well as reduce RMS current. The approach consists of analytical modelling of DAB power transfer characteristics, SPS and EPS modulation implementation in simulation, and performance analysis based on important parameters, including switching loss, efficiency, current stress, and transient response. The simulation results show that EPS significantly expands the ZVS operating region, increases light-load efficiency, and minimizes transformer and switch stresses over SPS. The research concludes that EPS control performs better without the need for additional hardware and is thus a more efficient and energy-saving solution for next-generation bi-directional EV battery charging systems.

title: HierTTF: Hierarchical Two-Stage Time-to-Failure Prediction for Datacenter SSDs Using Telemetry
authors: Geunrok Oh, Sohyun Han, Kijong You, Jongsung Na, Kyungsik Song, Sejeong Jang, Yuqi Zhang, Haonan Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Storage reliability is mission-critical in cloud and data-center environments, yet unexpected storage failures still cause outages, operational overhead, and data loss. This has driven ongoing efforts to predict storage failures, leading to threshold-based methods that use self-monitoring, analysis, and reporting technology (SMART) statistics and binary classifiers. However, these methods can only indicate whether a storage drive may fail, but do not predict when failure will occur. This limitation highlights the need for time-to-failure (TTF) forecasting to enable proactive and risk-aware maintenance. To overcome this limitation, we propose HierTTF, a hierarchical two-stage framework for TTF prediction. Stage 1 uses a CNN-LSTM classifier with multi-head attention to screen drives at risk of failing within 14 days. Stage 2 applies a DeepAR-based probabilistic forecaster to the screened cohort, yielding the conditional TTF distribution and 95% prediction intervals. We validate HierTTF on more than 5 million enterprise SSDs over 28 months in real-world datacenters. Stage 1 attains 84.8% precision at 74.7% recall, and Stage 2 achieves MAE 0.918 days, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.928 and CRPS 0.85 days. Compared with a non-hierarchical model, HierTTF cuts MAE by 76.7% and raises R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> by 0.556, demonstrating the value of the hierarchical approach. HierTTF has been deployed in real-world data centers, supporting proactive data migration and drive replacement, resulting in measurable improvements in operational reliability and efficiency

title: Enhancing NFT Ownership and Trading with Blockchain based Decentralized Marketplaces
authors: Inbamalar T M, Abarna S, Abinaya G, Dhanalakshmi R, Dhanushiya E
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: A decentralized Non-Fungible Token (NFT) marketplace website powered by block chain technology enables secure and transparent trading of digital assets has been proposed. Unlike traditional platforms, this system eliminates the central authority and provides full control over their NFTs to the users. It incorporates smart contracts to automate transactions, ensuring efficiency and security. As there is no limit to crypto currency, the marketplace works exclusively through crypto currencies, enhancing global access and minimizing transaction fees. The platform also features a bargaining system, allowing buyers and sellers to negotiate prices directly. By utilizing decentralized storage solutions like IPFS, the project ensures secure, immutable storage of NFT metadata and assets. This website enables users to buy or sell their NFTs. The process involves selecting the desired NFT from the list of NFTs. The next step is the "make offer" system, where the buyer and seller negotiate the price. After that, the price is fixed, and the transaction is processed with the crypto currency in their wallet. Finally, the ownership is updated by the smart contract on the block chain and the database. This website sets a new standard for efficient, secure, and innovative NFT trading. Thus, it motivates people to buy assets from the internet as they have ownership over their assets

title: Time-Coupled Optimal Power Flow Incorporating DER Volt–VAR Dynamics for Voltage Recovery and Stability
authors: Shadrack T. Asiedu, Zongjie Wang, Hossein Moradi Rekabdarkolaee, Timothy M. Hansen
abstract: The growing penetration of distributed energy resources (DERs) and the recent Iberian Peninsula blackout high-light the need for dynamic voltage support in modern power systems. Achieving this requires integrating DER dynamic behavior directly into grid operations. This paper proposes a novel model that embeds DER Volt–VAR dynamics within a time-coupled optimal power flow formulation. The simulation horizon is divided into pre-disturbance, disturbance, and recovery segments, with timesteps aligned to the DER inverter switching dynamics. The proposed approach is demonstrated on a simplified two-bus power system. The model successfully identifies generator setpoints and DER injections capable of resolving a voltage deviation caused by a sudden 50% increase in reactive load, illustrating the importance of explicitly modeling DER dynamics in voltage recovery.

title: X-PDResNet: An Explainable Residual Neural Network for Discrimination of Partial Discharge Defects in High Voltage Apparatus
authors: Sounish Maiti, Biswarup Ganguly
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Localization and categorization of partial discharge (PD) defects are essential for understanding insulation degradation and preventing failures in high-voltage electrical apparatus. Although numerous machine learning and deep learning approaches have been successfully applied for PD diagnosis, most existing models operate as black-box frameworks, offering limited transparency regarding their classification decisions. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an explainable residual neural network (X-PDResNet) for multiclass PD defect diagnosis. A customized residual network has been proposed to discriminate PD defects by employing time–frequency (TF) representations for effective extraction of both temporal and spectral characteristics. Further, an optimized explainable architecture is developed to generate fine-grained TF representations by modulating internal parameters of PDResNet. The proposed approach depicts its interpretability in terms of intermediate feature maps and relevance maps across different residual layers of the customized network. Feature activations extracted from shallow and deep layers reveal the progressive evolution from low-level texture patterns to highly discriminative representations specific to PD, allowing visual insights into the model’s decision-making process. Extensive experiments and ablation studies demonstrate the superiority of the proposed X-PDResNet with 98.67% accuracy, 98.68 % precision and 99.33 % specificity. The proposed explainable framework also shows strong potential for non-invasive condition monitoring applications in electrical insulation systems.

title: Beyond a Single Perspective: Neural Fusion of Lévy-Generated Super-Resolution Images for Robust Face Recognition
authors: Marcelo dos Santos, João C. Neves, David Menotti
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Despite significant advancements in facial recognition technology, these systems struggle in real-world surveillance scenarios, especially when dealing with low-resolution images. Super-resolution techniques have emerged as a natural solution to this issue, primarily using generative models such as generative adversarial networks or diffusion models. Despite their potential, these methods face critical limitations, including mode collapse and difficulty in preserving identity-specific features. To address these challenges, we propose Lévy Super-Resolution (LSR), a novel super-resolution approach leveraging Lévy processes as a noise source in a modified diffusion model. This stochastic process enhances diversity and mitigates mode collapse, enabling the generation of diverse super-resolved samples from a single low-resolution input. These samples are fused using an ensemble of neural networks trained with a triplet loss, producing robust face descriptors with reduced bias and improved identity preservation. To the best of our knowledge, LSR is the first method to utilize Lévy processes for super-resolution generation. We validate our approach on real and synthetic low-resolution samples from three standard face recognition datasets, and we demonstrate that our approach can surpass both general and face-specific state-of-the-art super-resolution (SR) methods. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/marcelowds/lsr.

title: A Triple-Loss Optimized U-Net Framework for Accurate Pancreas Segmentation in CT Scans
authors: Pulavar Kaleelurrahman Thariq Ahmed, Narayanan Ganesh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Accurate identification of a pancreas in CT scans is problematic, mainly due to the organ's small size, irregular shapes, and large amounts of class imbalances among abnormal pancreas images. An alternative segmentation method utilizes the 2D U-Net architecture to achieve greater precision through the use of a Composite Three Loss Function (Binary Cross Entropy, Dice-Loss, Boundary Aware Loss) and enhanced Boundary Fidelity (the sensitivity of detecting the actual anterior/ posterior/ lateral borders). To maintain image acquisition consistency, a pre-defined Preprocessing Pipeline was developed to produce uniform CT images across three distinct stages. Class imbalances were mitigated using Balanced Sampling Techniques and Lightweight Augmentation Techniques. The Adam Optimizer trained the model, and performance was evaluated using the Dice Similarity Coefficient, which resulted in a validation DSC Score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.8802$</tex>, indicating enhanced performance compared to traditional U-Net implementations. Crucially, the process has greatly increased Boundary Fidelity in areas with prior difficulty identifying anatomical structures. Thus, using Boundary Aware metrics as a guiding component in combining loss functions provides an additional metric for improving overall segmentation accuracy in challenging anatomical regions.

title: Federated Capsule Endoscopy Analytics for Privacy-Preserving Gut Disease Screening
authors: M. Madhavi., Ch Divya, K. Karthick, J Jesintha Princy, Pradnya A. Maturkar, Arshiya Begum
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Wireless capsule endoscopy (WCE) produces large volumes of small-bowel frames that demand automated lesion screening while preserving patient privacy. This study addressed the research problem of designing a compact, privacy-preserving federated learning (FL) pipeline for WCE that maintained clinical utility and feasible edge deployment. Capsule-FedNet-FL, a lightweight MobileNetV2-style encoder fused with a capsule routing block — trained in a cross-silo FL setup with secure aggregation and per-client differential privacy (DP). Experiments used Kvasir-Capsule and a Kyushu institutional subset partitioned into five non-IID clients. Under FedAvg, Capsule-FedNet-FL achieved accuracy 0.890 (95% CI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.880-0.900$</tex>), F1 0.888 (95% CI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.878-0.898$</tex>) and AUC 0.942 (95% CI 0.935-0.949). With DP (Gaussian noise <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sigma=1.0$</tex>, approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon \approx 3.2, \delta=1 \mathrm{e}-5$</tex>) accuracy dropped to 0.860 (95% CI 0.849-0.871) and AUC to 0.915 (95% CI 0.905 - 0.925) indicating a modest privacy - utility tradeoff. Secure aggregation combined with Top-k sparsification and 8-bit quantization reduced per-client communication by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 89 {\%}$</tex> while recovering most utility (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 8 2}$</tex>, 95% CI 0.873-0.891). Explainable overlays (Grad-CAM, Integrated Gradients) supported lesion localization with segmentation IoU <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.71 (95 \% \text{CI} 0.68-0.74)$</tex>. Findings demonstrated that privacy-enhanced FL provided near-centralized performance with practical communication and compute costs, and suggested DP budget tuning and compressed updates as viable operational knobs for clinical deployment.

title: A Sign Language Recognition Model for Transport Systems
authors: K. I. Gorokhov, L. A. Tremasova, D. V. Gadasin, D. D. Gadasin
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The article discusses the problem of organizing human-machine interaction in unmanned vehicles for people with speech and hearing impairments. Modern autopilot control interfaces increasingly rely on voice commands, which creates an insurmountable communication barrier for mute users. The aim of the work is to create and experimentally test a computer vision system capable of recognizing Russian sign language and converting it into control commands for a vehicle. The study uses a transformer-based architecture and the MediaPipe framework to accurately assess hand posture and recognize the dactylic alphabet. Based on the analysis of the Bukva dataset, a model has been trained that reaches an accuracy of $83 \%$ with an extremely small size of 1.18 MB, which allows it to be integrated into onboard systems with limited computing resources. The result of the work is a prototype interface that provides an inclusive environment and safety of driving an unmanned vehicle for users using sign language.

title: Hybrid and Gradient-Boosted Regression Models for Accurate Transistor Drain Current Prediction
authors: P. Sirish Kumar, A. Jayalaxmi, Ch. Rajasekhar, K. Kavitha, Ramya Krishna Isukapalli, M. Pravallika
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative study of machine learning techniques for predicting transistor drain current (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{D}$</tex>) from measured direct current—voltage (DC I–V) data. Four models—Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM), Hybrid Stacking Regressor (HSR), Neural Stacking Regressor (NSR), and Polynomial–Multilayer Perceptron Hybrid (PMLP)—were developed to capture the nonlinear relationship between drain-source voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{D S}$</tex>) and gate-source voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{V}_{\boldsymbol{G} \boldsymbol{G}}$</tex>). All models were trained and tested on the same experimental dataset to ensure a fair comparison. Among them, the HSR model achieved the best performance by effectively combining Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Regressor (SVR), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Xtreme Gradient Boosting (XGB) with a linear meta-learner. Its ensemble structure reduced prediction errors and improved consistency under varying bias conditions. LightGBM also provided high accuracy with faster training and smaller model size. In contrast, NSR and PMLP showed reduced stability and higher deviation in predictions. The study confirms that ensemble-based learning offers a robust and reliable approach for transistor modeling. Future work will extend this framework to temperature-dependent and dynamic I-V behaviors for compact device modeling.

title: Multimodal Foundation Models for Zero-Shot Water Stress Forecasting in Precision Agriculture
authors: Seyed Hamidreza Nabaei, Zeyang Zheng, Dong Chen, Arsalan Heydarian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Foundation models for time-series forecasting offer powerful new capabilities, yet adapting them to specialized domains without costly retraining remains a critical challenge. This paper addresses this challenge by demonstrating that for high-frequency multimodal data, a zero-shot inference approach can significantly outperform not only fine-tuned variants but also traditional deep learning baselines. We introduce a novel framework that uses the Segment Anything Model (SAM) for visual plant phenotyping (e.g., RGB values, mask area, and plant dimensions) and the Lag-Llama foundation model for water-stress forecasting. Validated on a dataset of over 43,000 images and corresponding minute-level IoT sensor data, our zero-shot model achieves a Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.0062. This represents a 58.1% lower error than a unimodal variant (MSE 0.0148) and a 99.95% MSE reduction relative to a CNN+LSTM baseline (11.91 → 0.0062). These findings highlight a robust and computationally efficient pathway for deploying foundation models in complex, real-world systems. The methodologies and open-source contributions presented advance the application of machine learning in precision agriculture and provide a valuable framework for multimodal, zero-shot time-series analysis.

title: State of Health estimation of Li-ion cells via internal degradation modes identification and physics-informed machine learning
authors: Quentin Bigouraux, Vincent Heiries, Saifeddine Aloui, Antoine Laurin, Marion Chandesris
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: This study introduces a two-step machine learning framework for estimating the State of Health (SoH) of lithium-ion batteries, using degradation modes as intermediate variables. The framework incorporates key degradation modes such as loss of active material in both negative and positive electrodes, loss of lithium inventory, and resistance change. To train the first step of the framework, an extensive synthetic dataset was created using a full-order Doyle-Fuller-Newman physics-based model. This dataset consists of voltage and temperature time-series recorded during a C/3 charge, used as inputs and degradation modes as outputs for the first step. These degradation modes are then used as inputs for the second step of the framework, which estimates the SoH of real cells. By structuring the framework in this manner, the latent space is aligned to reflect physical values known to be correlated with SoH; we expect this architecture to allow better interpretability and generalization. This study investigates various machine learning and deep learning architectures for both steps, demonstrating the potential of employing different architectures to achieve the same objective.

title: Reconstruction-Free Classification for Lensless Imaging Systems
authors: Pramil Paudel, Fengjun Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Lensless imaging offers a promising, privacy-preserving alternative to traditional cameras by capturing unintelligible diffraction patterns instead of direct scene images. This enables compact, lightweight, and energy-efficient imaging systems suitable for resource-constrained surveillance applications such as UAVs and wearable devices. However, conventional lensless systems require computationally intensive image reconstruction, which compromises both privacy and efficiency. In this work, we propose a robust, reconstruction-free lensless image classification framework based on a modified ResNet18 architecture. To address the lack of structure in lensless data, we introduce a frequency-domain learning strategy using block-DCT applied to non-overlapping spatial blocks. Our method achieves 97.61% accuracy on a real-world lensless face recognition dataset and 93.98% on a simulated remote sensing benchmark, outperforming prior reconstruction-free approaches. These results demonstrate the feasibility of high-accuracy lensless recognition without reconstruction and pave the way for practical, privacy-aware deployment in real-world surveillance scenarios.

title: Iterative Tensorization of Physics-Informed Neural Networks
authors: Carter Capetz, Ryan Solgi, William Sheppard
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) enable efficient approximation of solutions to complex physical systems governed by partial differential equations (PDEs). While increased model expressiveness typically requires larger architectures, the sensitivity of PINNs often complicates efforts at memory-efficient compression. To resolve this problem, we propose and systematically evaluate an iterative tensorization method to create a fully-tensorized PINN (ft-PINN) based on the Tensor Train decomposition and tensorized training techniques. This method enables both an extensive exploration of PINN architectures and aggressive parameter reduction to optimize compression ratios whilst maintaining or improving the model’s original expressiveness. To illustrate the benefits of our ft-PINN, we solve a non-linear PDE. Specifically, we select the (2+1)D viscous Burgers’ equation, a recognized benchmark for PINNs. Our ft-PINN accelerates both training and inferencing efficiency, maintains high accuracies when compared to analytic solutions, offers up to 98% overall parameter reduction, and significantly outperforms PINNs of similar size. These results establish clear empirical performance baselines and demonstrate the utility of iterative tensorization to inform and complement future studies on more complex and higher-dimensional PDEs.

title: Sensor-Based Portable Device for Soil Nutrients Monitoring, Communication and Intelligent Crop Recommendation System
authors: Sarthak Narang, Anamika Sharma, Vishvesh Lamba, Vishvam Salwan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The world of agriculture is evolving as new technologies are being integrated to meet the increasing demand for sustainable food production. This comprehensive view delves into the global outlook on the game-changing effects of combining sensor nodes and machine learning in agriculture. Specifically, it examines how this fusion equips farmers worldwide with the capacity to achieve optimal productivity. The main aim is to design and develop a real-time portable soil nutrient monitoring device to help farmers make farm management and crop selection decisions. A device with various sensors, a microcontroller, and a communication module is developed to sense and communicate various soil nutrient parameters such as NPK, temperature, and humidity. The developed hardware device is tested and validated on four soil samples, i.e. sandy, silt, clay, and loamy. The device displays and transmits the value of various soil nutrients, such as nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, temperature, and humidity, measured for five successive iterations. The recommender system generates suggestions for sensed data, and their performance is evaluated on a dataset to determine the most effective model for crop recommendations. The results show that the AdaBoost +RF model is leading among all models and outperforms in precision, recall, F1-score, and accuracy. This research aims to transform traditional farming practices through precise and data-driven agricultural solutions.

title: Integrated Navigation Research for Outdoor Multi-sensor Off-road Vehicle Based on PID-Pure Pursuit
authors: Yutao Xu, Xiuping Wang, Zhengye Hu, Yixuan Tang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated navigation solution for outdoor intelligent vehicles, utilizing the fusion of GPS, IMU, and odometer data to achieve high-precision and robust positioning. The system incorporates a kinematic model-based Pure Pursuit algorithm optimized with PID control for accurate path tracking. A complementary filter fusion strategy is designed to dynamically combine GPS and odometer data, effectively compensating for cumulative drift and filtering signal noise. Experimental results confirm that this scheme significantly enhances the vehicle’s navigation stability and accuracy, offering a practical reference for high-performance mobile robot navigation.

title: Cloud-IoT and AI-based Intelligent Waste Monitoring and Collection System for Sustainable Urban Development
authors: Suganya S, Priyadharsini N. K, Kommabatla Mahender, Rajesh Kumar A, Naitik S. T., A. Kathiravan
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Urban waste management has increasing problems due to rapid population increase and ineffective collection processes, resulting in overflow, environmental contamination, and decreasing resources. The proposed study handles these issues by offering an intelligent Cloud-IoT and AI-driven garbage monitoring and collecting system aimed at sustainable urban growth. The system features intelligent bins outfitted with sensors that relay real-time data to cloud platforms, where AI algorithms categorise trash types, predict waste generation, and dynamically optimise collection routes. The suggested framework utilises deep learning techniques, including CNNs and LSTMs, for enhanced accuracy and adaptability, in addition to previous study that depends on traditional machine learning algorithms. Experimental results indicate a classification accuracy of 97.8%, precision of 97.4%, and a R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.94 in trash forecasting, outperforming baseline methods such as SVM and Random Forest. Route optimisation decreases collecting time by 28%, so improving operating efficiency and reducing carbon emissions. This integrated strategy enhances trash management efficiency while advancing environmental sustainability objectives, providing a scalable and economical solution for smart cities. The research's practical consequences highlight its capacity to revolutionise municipal waste management, promoting cleaner and healthier urban settings.

title: LIGHT-HIDS: A Lightweight and Effective Machine Learning-Based Framework for Robust Host Intrusion Detection
authors: Onat Gungor, Ishaan Kale, Jiasheng Zhou, Tajana Rosing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The expansion of edge computing has increased the attack surface, creating an urgent need for robust, real-time machine learning (ML)-based host intrusion detection systems (HIDS) that balance accuracy and efficiency. In such settings, inference latency poses a critical security risk, as delays may provide exploitable opportunities for attackers. However, many state-of-the-art ML-based HIDS solutions rely on computationally intensive architectures with high inference costs, limiting their practical deployment. This paper proposes LIGHT-HIDS, a lightweight machine learning framework that combines a compressed neural network feature extractor trained via Deep Support Vector Data Description (DeepSVDD) with an efficient novelty detection model. This hybrid approach enables the learning of compact, meaningful representations of normal system call behavior for accurate anomaly detection. Experimental results on multiple datasets demonstrate that LIGHT-HIDS consistently enhances detection accuracy while reducing inference time by up to 75× compared to state-of-the-art methods. These findings highlight its effectiveness and scalability as a machine learning-based solution for real-time host intrusion detection.

title: Intelligent Binary Descriptor Generation through GA Based Dimension Selection in MPEG CDVS and LDVS for Visual Search
authors: Vikas D. Patil, Manesh B. Kokare
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Traditional challenges in CBIR systems, such as descriptor separability, retrieval recall completeness, and computational and memory efficiency, remain unresolved, especially with multi-class image databases. We introduce a new visual search system designed for flexibility and scalability, aiming to maximize retrieval quality while minimizing the descriptor space required for experiments. The proposed method combines hand-crafted features, CNN-based descriptors, and evolutionary optimization to improve retrieval results. In this paper, we present a hybrid approach that merges MPEG-CDVS (Compact Descriptors for Visual Search) and LDVS (Learnable Descriptors for Visual Search). Additionally, we incorporate a Genetic Algorithm for feature selection and apply PCA (preserving the number of principal components) for compression. Experiments on an eightcategory image dataset use metrics such as Precision @K, mAP, ROC, CMC, patch classification error, inlier matching, and run length distributions. Our results demonstrate that LDVS outperforms CDVS, achieving an accuracy of 0.89 at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{K}=1$</tex> and an AUC of 0.796. Furthermore, the genetic algorithmbased optimization achieved a precision of 0.95 at a recall of 0.25 when reducing from 4096-D to 256-D with minimal loss of accuracy.

title: Com-Analyzer: A Multi-Modal Artificial Intelligence Framework for Personalized Interview Skill Assessment
authors: Jhansi Vazram Bolla, Manukonda Sai Sudheer, Devisetty Eswar Vasanth Kumar, Tatiparthi Sanga Reddy, Azad Duppala, A. Samatha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: While communication competence is increasingly felt to be a critical employability skill, most training environments lack scalable and data-driven assessment mechanisms. This paper presents Com-Analyzer, a multimodal artificial intelligence framework for real-time interview communication assessment. To provide this functionality, the system leverages a microservice architecture that includes a frontend in React, a backend in Node.js, and a Flask-based AI service that integrates speech-to-text conversion, sentiment analysis, and emotion recognition through convolutional neural networks. By building on Google Gemini's large language model, Com-Analyzer dynamically generates domain-specific interview questions and interpretable feedback reports. Within controlled academic trials involving 25 undergraduate participants, the system achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 1. 2 \%}$</tex> emotion recognition accuracy, 84.1 % sentiment precision, and an average processing latency of 4.6 seconds per response. Participants demonstrated measurable improvements in fluency and confidence across multiple sessions, outperforming text-only and audio-only baselines by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}-\mathbf{9 \%}$</tex>. These results provide evidence that Com-Analyzer can offer an interpretable and pedagogically aligned enabling technology for formative assessment and reflective learning within educational settings.

title: Study of Different Filter for Altitude Estimation in UAVs using Sensor Fusion
authors: J.Saranya, James A Baskaradas, B.Vijaya Bhaskar, Rajesh Anbazhagan, Manikandan Chinnusami, Suba Renjani N
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Altitude estimation is required for autonomous navigation, especially for unmanned aerial vehicle during emergency conditions and in indoors. While GPS sensors do offer altitude data, satellite geometry and multipath contributions render them less precise. Barometer sensors offer high-resolution altitude data, while being affected by weather variations. The research work proposed here is to enhance the altitude estimation by fusing both the sensors in contrast to isolated performance. The suggested work contrasts a few fusion algorithms such as the classical kalman filter (KF), KF with expectation maximization, weighted fusion, dynamic weighted fusion, long short term memory (LSTM) based kalman filter and particle filter. The performance of the fusion algorithm is evaluated using ground truth reference data by calculating the performance metrics such as standard deviation, mean square error, root mean square error and mean absolute error.

title: Reading Between the Signals: A Comprehensive Tutorial on Harnessing Physical Side-Channels for Anomaly Detection
authors: Kurt A. Vedros, Hunter Squires, Constantinos Kolias, Domenic J. Forte, Daniel Barbará
abstract: Side-channel analysis (SCA), which involves analyzing physical emissions from devices to infer sensitive information, was traditionally regarded as a technique for enabling cryptographic attacks. In recent years, SCA has been increasingly applied to defensive purposes, with anomaly detection emerging as a prominent application. This survey systematizes the expanding field of Side-Channel-based Anomaly Detection (SCAD), which utilizes physical emissions such as electromagnetic radiation, power consumption patterns, acoustic signals, and thermal signatures to enable real-time system profiling, authentication, attestation, and malware detection. In contrast to software-based methods, SCAD provides non-invasive monitoring of devices, including embedded systems deployed in critical infrastructures. As a result, SCAD approaches enhance system robustness against sophisticated threats, including code injection, hardware Trojans, and micro-architectural attacks. This survey reviews over 50 SCAD methodologies across multiple domains and device types. The complete detection workflow of SCAD processes, encompassing signal acquisition, reprocessing, feature extraction, and model evaluation, is examined in detail, and for each phase, dominant methodologies are compared, including statistical and machine learning approaches such as shallow learning and deep learning, with an emphasis on their respective strengths and limitations. Publicly available datasets are also discussed, along with experimental practices. Lastly, common pitfalls and emerging challenges are identified, including issues related to signal synchronization, the impact of environmental noise, robustness against adversarial attacks, and the need for scalable and explainable detection models.

title: One-shot 3D Affordance Learning for Multi-stage Robotic Manipulation
authors: Hyunseo Kim, Yeon-Ji Song, Minsu Lee, Byoung-Tak Zhang
abstract: Human-assistant robots must understand human-object interactions to execute collaborative manipulation tasks described in natural language. While affordance learning addresses this need, current approaches face a fundamental trade-off: 2D methods capture action-relevant object semantics but lack robust 3D geometric reasoning, whereas 3D methods demand labor-intensive point cloud datasets. To bridge this gap, we propose AffoRo-GS, a one-shot 3D affordance learning framework that grounds action verbs directly into 3D Gaussian Splatting representations. Our key insight is that verb-centric affordances from sparse 2D signals can be lifted into view-consistent 3D representations without explicit 3D annotations. Specifically, AffoRo-GS requires only a single affordance-labeled image per scene during training, and zero reference images at inference. Operating seamlessly with natural language, this approach eliminates the need for explicit object- or part-level queries, enabling rapid inference crucial for multi-stage tasks. Extensive real-world experiments demonstrate that our approach outperforms baselines reliant on 2D affordances or part-level reasoning, particularly in challenging long-horizon multi-stage settings.

title: Quality of Service Enhancement and Security Assurance in Internet of Things Networks: A Survey and Integrated Blockchain Sidechain Architecture
authors: Anurag Sharma, Gurpreet Singh Chhabra, Bhabendu Kumar Mohanta, Ali Ismail Awad, Muhammad Khurram Khan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) enables various applications, including healthcare and smart grids, many of which demand a high-quality of service (QoS) and are subject to external and internal attacks. While blockchain-based approaches provide built-in security, they generally incur a significant computation burden, negatively affecting QoS. This paper adopts a hybrid approach that integrates a comprehensive survey with the introduction of a novel blockchain sidechain architecture. The paper begins with an extensive review of QoS in IoT networks, sidechain mechanisms for improving QoS, blockchain for IoT security, and the deployment of blockchain in IoT via sidechains. This study then proposes a novel architecture for a blockchain sidechain that features a dynamic-truncation sidechain management model to combat the QoS and security tradeoff problem. The proposed architecture enhances network QoS through the sidechain while providing essential security via a blockchain mechanism. The study utilizes the Tendermint consensus mechanism, which is specifically designed to mitigate threats such as Sybil, denial-of-service (DoS), and double-spending attacks. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed architecture yields significant QoS improvements compared to non-sidechain methods, including a 22.54% reduction in communication delay, a 28.11% decrease in energy consumption, and a 35.64% increase in network throughput. The model also achieves a 10.47% improvement in packet delivery ratio and a 22.37% reduction in delay jitter, while consuming only 2.11 KB of storage, representing a 30.37% reduction in storage usage. Additionally, the suitability of the proposed approach for resource-constrained IoT environments is demonstrated by both its ability to deliver faster block generation than state-of-the-art methods and by the robustness of the Tendermint consensus mechanism, which is confirmed through security analysis. Coupled with the comprehensive review and insights into future research directions, this work serves as both a foundational reference and a pathway for advancing QoS and security in IoT networks.

title: Evolutionary Hyper-Transformation for Multi-AAV Path Planning to Visit Moving Targets_supp1-3678144.pdf
authors: Bin Xin
abstract: This article addresses a novel path planning problem for multiple fixed-wing autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs) to visit a set of moving targets, originating from AAV cooperative missions such as emergency communication and target surveillance. This problem can be formulated as a multiple Dubins traveling salesman problem with moving targets (mDTSPMT). The key challenge lies in the strong cross-level coupling between target assignment, encounter sequences, and motion-constrained paths for multiple AAVs in the presence of moving targets. To solve mDTSPMT efficiently, we develop an efficient transformation method by sampling the access location and heading of each AAV to visit moving targets, constructing the mDTSPMT roadmap, and transferring it into an asymmetric multiple traveling salesman problem (AMTSP). This transformation allows the use of mature AMTSP solvers while preserving the essential motion and timing constraints of the original problem. However, the performance of the transformation method heavily depends on the quality of the samples. To improve the quality of samples, a hyper-transformation (HT) framework is proposed, which adaptively optimizes AAV sampling, guiding the search toward more promising configurations and enhancing both the solution quality and computational efficiency of the transformation method. Experiments with extensive instances show that the proposed method outperforms four competitive algorithms in generating coordinated and time-efficient Dubins paths for multiple AAVs encountering multiple targets.

title: TechRxiv: Share Your Preprint Research with the World!
authors: N/A

title: l 2 - l ∞ Proportional-Integral State Estimation for Stochastic Nonlinear Systems With State Saturations Under Decode-and-Forward Relay
authors: Xueyang Meng, Zidong Wang, Fan Wang, Yun Chen
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of l2-l∞ proportional-integral state estimation for stochastic nonlinear systems over decode-and-forward relay networks subject to packet losses. The system model is formulated to capture the effects of stochastic nonlinearities and state saturations, thereby reflecting practical engineering conditions. To alleviate transmission distance limitations, a decode-and-forward relay is introduced into the wireless channel to support communication from the sensor to the remote estimator, which enhances the reliability of long-distance data transmission. The objective is to design a proportional-integral state estimator that guarantees a prescribed finite-horizon l2-l∞ performance level for the estimation error dynamics, despite the presence of stochastic nonlinearities, state saturations, packet losses, and decoding errors. A sufficient condition for the existence of such an estimator is established, and the corresponding estimator gains are obtained by solving a set of matrix equalities. Finally, a simulation example is provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed state estimation scheme.

title: An Efficient Data Generation Method Based on Flow Matching for Bearing Fault Diagnosis Under Imbalanced Data Conditions
authors: Mohammad Hatami Kakesh, Akbar Rahideh, Gholam Reza Agah, Mohammad Reza Peyghan, Shahin Hedayati Kia
abstract: The process of fault diagnosis is greatly constrained by imbalanced datasets, as a lack of fault data results in models with poor diagnostic performance. To overcome this issue, generative adversarial networks (GANs) have been utilized. However, they often suffer from training instability and are prone to mode collapse, where this critical issue causes the generator to produce a very limited variety of outputs. Consequently, this article proposes an efficient data augmentation framework based on flow matching for the domain of bearing fault diagnosis under imbalanced data. First, the continuous wavelet transform of vibration signals is employed to train a conditional flow matching model. After training, the model can generate new synthetic data by transforming a random Gaussian noise vector into a high-fidelity time–frequency spectrum. A key advantage of this approach is its computational efficiency, where the model produces diverse and authentic samples with as few as 5-15 generation steps, significantly reducing the generation time compared to traditional diffusion models. Furthermore, to comprehensively evaluate the diversity and fidelity of synthesized samples, various evaluation techniques, such as kernel inception distance (KID), the GAN-test/GAN-train metric and t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) visualizations of the learned feature space, are employed. Ultimately, a series of imbalanced fault diagnosis experiments was conducted to validate the practical effectiveness of the proposed method, resulting in final diagnosis accuracies of 95.90% and 97.31% on two widely used datasets and 98.81% on an actual test bed.

title: Lateral Anti-Misalignment Performance Improvement of the Single-Transmitter/Dual-Receiver WPT System with a Single Load
authors: Xu Liu, Dezhen Chen, Mingrui Yu, Hui Qian, Cancan Rong, Chenyang Xia, Hui Zhang
abstract: With the rapid advancement of wireless power transfer technology, its application scenarios have expanded significantly. However, the misalignment issue during power transmission, primarily due to the difficulty in precisely aligning charging devices, exerts a non-negligible impact on system performance. Given that automated guided vehicle misalignment predominantly manifests as lateral displacement, this study proposes an improved compensation topology and coil structure based on a single-transmitter and dual-receiver coil configuration. Meanwhile, this research further develops algorithmic approaches for parameter design criteria and optimization strategies to effectively enhance the system's lateral misalignment tolerance. The proposed compensation topology and parameter optimization methodology are validated through a 1 kW experimental prototype. And the experiment result shows that when the lateral alignment remains within 1/4 of the coil's maximum side length, the system's output power fluctuation can be controlled within 4.7%, and the maximum DC-DC efficiency of the system can reach 90.87%, with the efficiency fluctuation being within 1.6%.

title: Automatic Dual-Battery Switching System for E-Vehicles using Solar Integration
authors: Barkavi M, Kodieswaran M, Gopalakrishnan R, Sri Vidhya D, Jaividhya S, Prabha K
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The growing worldwide call for sustainable transportation has elevated the adoption of electric automobiles (EVs). However, limitations which includes quick driving range, lengthy charging times, and reliance on charging infrastructure restrict their considerable application, especially in lengthy-distance or remote journey situations. This paper proposes a wise, twin-battery switching device to beautify the operational efficiency and variety of EVs by means of integrating sun electricity into the electricity architecture. The gadget is prepared with unbiased power sources: a number one battery charged through a traditional AC power deliver and an auxiliary battery charged through a rooftop solar panel. A microcontroller-primarily based control unit (ATmega328) is utilized to display the voltage degrees of each batteries and the sun panel in actual-time through committed voltage sensors. When the primary battery's voltage drops beneath a vital threshold, the controller routinely triggers a relay circuit to interchange the energy source to the auxiliary battery, ensuring uninterrupted motor operation. A 16x2 LCD offers actual-time voltage display and supply repute, even as an IoT module permits cloud-based tracking of machine parameters consisting of voltage degrees and battery switching activities. The device's conduct is verified using Proteus simulation, confirming the accuracy of switching logic and real-time overall performance. By intelligently combining solar electricity harvesting with computerized strength management, the proposed answer reduces dependency on grid electricity, helps longer tour distances, and promotes the adoption of renewable power in EV systems.

title: Deep Learning-Based Direction Prediction: A Comparative Study of Neural Architectures on XAU/USD 30-Minute Data with Win-Big-Lose-Small Strategy
authors: Ankit Kumar Singh, Ram Chandra Barik, Niranjan Behera, Surendra Kumar Panda
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this paper, Deep neural networks have been used to predict FOREX commodity price directions. After comparing seven neural network models on 60 days of 30-minute gold trading data (XAU/USD), with 1,897 trading records. The Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) was identified as the best-performing model, achieving a price direction accuracy of 53.41 %. As this accuracy is only 3.41 percentage points above random guessing, it has been demonstrated through real trading that this small edge when combined with a smart risk management strategy, generates a substantial amount of profit. It was implemented with a trading strategy based on the principle of “winning big while losing small”. After backtesting the model on TradingView Strategy Tester, it shows a profit of 3,752.91 USD across 94 trades with a profit factor of 2.518 and maximum drawdown of only 0.04 %. This good performance was achieved after winning only 39.36 % of our trades. The main factor in trading is that trading success depends more on managing risk in a trade rather than on prediction accuracy. This paper suggests that machine learning trading works best in real life market conditions when combined with proper position sizing and risk control.This paper also explains that the 30-minute timeframe represents the optimal timeframe for short-term trading. While profit factors generally increase with longer timeframes, 30-minute intervals always allows for faster capital accumulation by increasing the number of trades taken. Our approach is validated by using time-series methodology including stationarity analysis, feature engineering with 46 features extracted from the raw price data, and proper train-test splitting.

title: Explainable Multi-Modal AI for Brain Tumor Segmentation and Radiotherapy Planning
authors: Chetan Khemraj Lanjewar, L Suresh Kumar, Prakash Selvam, Abhishek Kumar Tiwari
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Precise delineation of intracranial neoplasms from multi-parametric MRI is a prerequisite for high-quality radiotherapy planning; however, inter-scanner heterogeneity and topological segmentation errors remain major sources of dosimetric degradation. This study aimed to evaluate whether persistent homology-based shape descriptors and neurosymbolic anatomical constraints improve 3D segmentation fidelity and downstream dose planning on a curated Indian multi-institutional mpMRI cohort (sites S1–S4). A 3D residual U-Net was fused with handcrafted radiomics and persistence-image features derived from persistent homology and trained with a composite objective where the topological term penalizes spurious 0 and 1 cycles. Neurosymbolic checks enforced coarse anatomical consistency and persistence attributions were inverse mapped to image loci. Explainability was provided by Grad-CAM SHAP and topology-based attributions. Compared with CNN only baselines persistent feature integration increased ET Dice (median <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \approx+0.028$</tex>) and the topology aware loss produced further gains (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \approx+0.046$</tex>) while reducing HD95. External validation preserved these improvements within bootstrap 95% confidence intervals. In radiotherapy planning topology aware targets increased D95 by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 1.8\%$</tex> of prescription and reduced spurious high dose islands with modest OAR sparing. Results support the use of TDA guided neurosymbolic pipelines to enhance geometric fidelity dosimetric quality and clinician interpretability.

title: An Intelligent Traffic Behaviour Prediction Method using Deep Learning Approach
authors: Syed Reshma, G RoslineNesaKumari
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Traffic behaviour prediction plays a vital role in smart cities by improving traffic flow, minimizing congestion and enhancing road safety. High-resolution aerial images captured by drones or satellites offer a powerful means of monitoring and analyzing traffic in real time. These images detect key elements such as vehicle positions, and road conditions. However, integrating this data poses challenges. To overcome these challenges, this work proposes a deep learning-based model that extracts valuable insights from both visual and contextual data. The model integrates advanced object detection and tracking with predictive analytics to forecast traffic patterns and identify potential disruptions in advance. Firstly, the needed video is collected from the standard dataset. Then, the collected videos are given to the Spatio-Temporal Attention-based YOLOv9 (STA-YOLOv9) for video object detection and tracking to capture real-time traffic data. Once the object detection and tracking process is performed, feature extraction is executed on the detected videos. Here, three sets of features are extracted. Deep features from the detected objects are extracted using VGG19, and the key features, such as vehicle position, speed, lane usage, traffic bottlenecks, and road conditions are taken. To further enhance prediction accuracy, external factors such as weather conditions, historical traffic data, roadwork schedules, and event data (e.g., public events, accidents) are integrated into the system. The integration of these diverse features is achieved through attention-based feature fusion, ensuring that the model effectively learns which features are most relevant at different times and locations. Finally, the system utilizes a Dense Recurrent Neural Network (DRNN) to perform the prediction process and provide the predicted output. Then, the experimental validation is conducted to ensure the traffic behaviour prediction effectiveness.

title: Design and Implementation of an IoT-Based Smart Safety Ring with Direct Cellular Link for Women's Security
authors: D. Thamizhisai, T. Sachin, R. Ragul
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Women's safety remains a global concern, necessitating discreet and reliable personal protection solutions. SmartRing is an IoT-enabled wearable safety device designed to detect distress situations and automatically alert guardians and authorities in real time. It integrates heart rate, motion, and skin conductance sensors to identify panic or physical distress. Upon detection, the system sends the wearer's GPS location via GSM and uploads data to a secure cloud. The device combines AIbased signal processing with IoT communication, providing a fast and autonomous safety response mechanism. The prototype testing demonstrated over 90 per cent detection accuracy, 3.5 second latency, and 16-hour battery endurance, making it a practical and reliable solution for real-world use. This paper presents the detailed design, implementation, and evaluation of the SmartRing, highlighting its contributions to wearable IoT technology for personal security. We discuss hardware and software architecture, sensor fusion algorithms, and performance metrics, along with potential future enhancements.

title: Weighted Ensemble Classification for Misinformation Detection using Optimized TF-IDF Features on a Large-Scale News Dataset
authors: Arpan Kumar Shill, Pushpita Roy, Shubhendu Banerjee, Shounak Sinha, Shreyashi Saha, Rishiraj Chakraborty, Debjit Mondal, Mouli Das
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: The swift spread of fake news across digital platforms is really shaking up how people view information and trust in the media. This study rolls out a cutting-edge machine learning framework that works like a team to tell apart real news from fake. It combines the unique strengths of several models: Support Vector Machines (SVM), Logistic Regression (LR), Multinomial Naive Bayes (NB), and Gradient Boosting (GB). We’ve put together a thorough dataset of 44, 898 news articles 21, 417 of which are true and 23, 481 that are fake. To make sense of the text, we preprocess it by tokenizing, removing stopwords, lemmatizing, and using TF-IDF vectorization to pull out key features. We train our individual models on 80% of the data (that’s 35, 918 samples), saving the other 20% (8, 980 samples) for testing. By using an ensemble approach, we blend the predictions from these models with optimized weights, which we fine-tune through a grid search to get the best accuracy. An impressive test accuracy of 99.82% is achieved. We dive into detailed evaluations, including classification reports and sample predictions, which show that our model holds up well across various types of news content. Our results highlight how effective ensemble learning can be for detecting fake news, providing a reliable and scalable solution for automated content verification—perfect for media monitoring and social media platforms. We also pinpoint some areas that could use improvement, like tackling the stylistic similarities between true and fake news, setting the stage for future research into better feature engineering and model designs.

title: AI-Driven Cross-Layer Optimization of Hybrid Optical-Wireless 5G Transport Systems
authors: Praveen Kumar Pal
