publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The convergence of optical and wireless networks is a fundamental enabler of 5 G and beyond communication systems, where stringent requirements on bandwidth, latency, reliability, and energy efficiency must be satisfied simultaneously. Traditional rule-based transport network management techniques are increasingly inadequate for handling the dynamic and heterogeneous traffic patterns generated by ultra-dense wireless access and cloud-native applications. This paper presents an AI-assisted resource optimization framework for hybrid opticalwireless 5 G transport networks, enabling intelligent, adaptive, and predictive control of end-to-end network resources. The proposed approach integrates machine learning and reinforcement learning techniques to jointly optimize optical transport parameters (wavelength allocation, modulation formats, and capacity provisioning) and wireless backhaul/fronthaul resources under time-varying traffic and channel conditions. By leveraging real-time telemetry and traffic prediction, the framework enables proactive congestion mitigation, improved spectral efficiency, and enhanced quality-of-service (QoS) assurance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed AI-assisted optimization significantly outperforms conventional static and heuristic-based methods in terms of throughput, latency reduction, and resource utilization efficiency. These results highlight the potential of AI-driven control as a key enabler for scalable, resilient, and autonomous optical-wireless transport networks supporting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> and future 6 G services.

title: Implementation of a Cyber Reflex Layer for Securing Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Mrigank Jain, Shravan M, Sheela Devi M
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: As autonomous vehicles (AVs) become more connected and intelligent, they also become more vulnerable to cyberattacks that can compromise safety, privacy and endanger lives. Traditional cybersecurity solutions often rely on cloud-based detection or centralized decision-making, which may not respond quickly enough to real-time threats on the road. This paper introduces a Cyber Reflex Layer (CRL)-a lightweight, real-time security layer inspired by the human nervous systems reflex actions. The CRL enables an AV to detect anomalies and respond immediately at the local level, without delay. This paper implements and tests the CRL framework using a co-simulation environment that integrates SUMO, MOSAIC and Cyber-Battle-Sim. The results show that the CRL cam effectively detect and react to attacks with minimal latency, helping the vehicle maintain stability and safety during cyber incidents.

title: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
authors: N/A

title: Interactive Force-Impedance Control
authors: Fan Shao, Satoshi Endo, Sandra Hirche, Fanny Ficuciello
abstract: Human collaboration with robots requires flexible role adaptation, enabling the robot to switch between an active leader and a passive follower. Effective role switching depends on accurately estimating human intentions, which is typically achieved through external force analysis, nominal robot dynamics, or data-driven approaches. However, these methods are primarily effective in contact-sparse environments. When robots under hybrid or unified force–impedance control physically interact with active humans or non-passive environments, the robotic system may lose passivity and thus compromise safety. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a unified Interactive Force-Impedance Control (IFIC) framework that adapts to interaction power flow, ensuring safe and effortless interaction in contact-rich environments. The proposed control architecture is formulated within a port-Hamiltonian framework, incorporating both interaction and task control ports, thereby guaranteeing autonomous system passivity. Experiments in both rigid and soft contact scenarios demonstrate that IFIC ensures stable collaboration under active human interaction, reduces contact impact forces and interaction force oscillations.

title: An Open-Source, Reproducible Tensegrity Robot that can Navigate Among Obstacles
authors: William R. Johnson, Patrick Meng, Nelson Chen, Luca Cimatti, Augustin Vercoutere, Mridul Aanjaneya, Rebecca Kramer-Bottiglio, Kostas E. Bekris
abstract: Tensegrity robots, composed of rigid struts and elastic tendons, provide impact resistance, low mass, and adaptability to unstructured terrain. Their compliance and complex, coupled dynamics, however, present modeling and control challenges, hindering planning and obstacle avoidance. This paper presents a complete, open-source, and reproducible system that enables navigation for a 3-bar tensegrity robot. The system comprises: (i) an inexpensive, open-source hardware design, and (ii) an integrated, open-source software stack for physics-based modeling, system identification, state estimation, path planning, and control. All hardware and software are publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://tensegrity.yale.edu/</uri> tensegrity.yale.edu. The proposed system tracks the robot using a static overhead camera and executes collision-free paths to a goal among known obstacle locations. System robustness is demonstrated through experiments involving unmodeled environmental challenges, including a vertical drop, an incline, and granular media, culminating in an outdoor field demonstration. To validate reproducibility, experiments were conducted using robot instances at two different laboratories. This work provides the robotics community with a complete navigation system for a compliant, impact-resistant, and shape-morphing robot. This system is intended to serve as a springboard for advancing the navigation capabilities of other unconventional robotic platforms.

title: Model-predictive Vibration Reduction of a Suspended Cable-Driven Parallel Robot
authors: Sophie Rousseau, Nicolò Pedemonte, François Chaumette, Stéphane Caro
abstract: This letter focuses on the vibration reduction for Cable-Driven Parallel Robots. A Non-linear Model Predictive Controller (NMPC) is designed and implemented to adapt the control inputs sent to the robot actuators. This NMPC is based on an elasto-dynamic model of the robot, which is discretized and included in a constrained optimal control problem. Two experiments are conducted and the performance of the NMPC is compared with a Computed Torque Controller and a NMPC with an inelastic model. The first one is a trajectory following task and shows that the proposed controller significantly reduces the amplitude of the vibrations of the Moving-Platform along the trajectory and the second one focuses on a sudden braking situation and shows that the proposed control scheme manages to divide the amplitude of the post-braking oscillations by factors ranging from one and a half to twenty-two, demonstrating the interest of this controller for perturbation rejection.

title: Two-axis transparent force plate for droplet collision measurement using high speed line-scan camera and sampling moiré method
authors: Yukitake Nakahara, Toshihiro Shiratori, Choongyeop Lee, YunJung Heo, Hidetoshi Takahashi
abstract: Force measurement and interface observation during droplet impact are essential for understanding droplet behavior in both scientific and industrial applications. However, achieving both backside optical transparency for interface observation and two-axis force measurement for quantifying shear-force effects has been challenging with conventional force-sensing methods using a single device. In this study, we propose a transparent two-axis force plate based on the sampling moiré (SM) method combined with a line-scan camera, which enables high-temporal-resolution force measurement while permitting optical observation beneath the impacted surface. The proposed device consists of a spring-supported, laser-cut glass plate with a periodic striped pattern and a prism-based optical system. A line-scan camera is employed to overcome the inherent trade-off between spatial resolution and frame rate associated with area-scan cameras. By duplicating the pattern image using a prism and analyzing the resulting displacements via the SM method, in-plane and out-of-plane forces are independently measured from one-dimensional images. Calibration experiments confirm linear force–displacement characteristics, with force resolutions of Fx = 0.39 mN and Fz =0.12 mN, respectively. Positional errors remained below 2% in-plane and 6% out-of-plane. The fabricated device exhibits a minimum resonant frequency of 656 Hz, which is sufficient to capture short-duration impact events. Finally, droplet impact experiments are conducted under both vertical and tilted conditions. Compared with the vertical configuration, the inclined condition demonstrates Fx values comparable in magnitude to Fz and a clear elongation of the droplet induced by shear forces. Overall, the proposed force plate provides a customizable platform for high-precision, high-speed force measurements in droplet dynamics.

title: MOFM: A Multiple-in-One Flow Mamba for Unregistered Multi-Modal Image Fusion
authors: Bo Yang, Zhaohui Jiang, Dong Pan, Zhiping Lin, Weihua Gui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Multi-modal image fusion aims to integrate complementary cues from different modalities into a single image, facilitating downstream tasks such as object detection. However, input image pairs are often misaligned due to rigid or non-rigid deformations in practical scenarios. Such misregistration introduces structural distortions and visual artifacts, reducing the reliability of the fused results and limiting their effectiveness for downstream vision applications. While existing methods demonstrate satisfactory results under specific deformation scenarios, they exhibit limited generalization to diverse and severe misregistrations. To this end, this study proposes a unified multiple-in-one flow Mamba framework for registering various image deformations and generating high-quality fused results. Specifically, a hierarchical flow Mamba is designed to model rigid and non-rigid flow fields and enhance adaptability to complex deformations by progressively refining misaligned features. To better distinguish between rigid and non-rigid types, a flow field classifier predicts rigid/non-rigid categories and provides prompts for high-level feature modulation. Furthermore, a flow-guided fusion Mamba module is developed to aggregate aligned multi-level modality features and generate fused images, while an iterative training strategy enables collaborative optimization by using fusion outputs to refine flow estimation. Experiments across three representative modality tasks demonstrate that the proposed method delivers superior fusion performance while maintaining applicability to object detection. The code will be available at: https://github.com/BOYang-pro/MOFM.

title: Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces-enabled Transceiver: Functional Coding and Data-Enhanced Deep Unfolding Detection
authors: Shuyi Chen, Yingzhe Hui, Yifan Qin, Chang Su, Jiancheng An, Weixiao Meng, Chau Yuen
abstract: Recent studies have shown that the stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIM) can exploit inter-layer electromagnetic (EM) wave transmission for wave-domain signal processing. This enables over-the-air computing with characteristics such as high-speed operation, low energy consumption, and support for multitasking parallel processing. By leveraging these advantages, SIM show great potential in substituting or augmenting customized communication functions in conventional wireless systems. To support various transmitter-customized communication functions, we design a novel multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) transmitter architecture based on SIM and develop corresponding detection algorithms. Initially, we present a SIM-enabled MIMO transceiver model. Unlike conventional wireless communication, the SIM-enabled transmitter automatically performs customized functions, such as precoding and channel encoding, as the EM waves propagate within the layers. At the receiver side, minimum mean square error (MMSE) and maximum likelihood (ML) detectors are adopted as benchmarks, and their theoretical performance bounds are derived accordingly. Furthermore, we propose a data-enhanced deep unfolding algorithm, namely orthogonal approximate message passing causal dilated convolutional transformer (OAMP-CDT), to demonstrate the performance of customized functions based on SIM. Simulation results verify that the proposed SIM-enabled MIMO transmitter supports customized functions via over-the-air signal processing, and the proposed OAMP-CDT algorithm outperforms the same series of algorithms, such as orthogonal approximate message passing (OAMP) and OAMP-Net2.

title: A Kind of Flexible Forceps With High Clamping Force and Large Opening Angle for Endoscopic Surgery
authors: Yuan Xing, Fengchuan Liang, Mengjie Chen, Kang Kong, Tao Liang
abstract: In endoscopic submucosal dissection, endoscopic forceps is subject to stringent requirements regarding flexibility, miniaturization, and mechanical performance. However, most existing endoscopic forceps suffer from insufficient clamping force and limited opening angle. In this letter, we analyze the influence of the jaw configuration on the clamping force and opening angle of flexible forceps, and develop an innovative composite-groove jaw configuration that achieves a high clamping force of 2.57 N and a large opening angle of 105°, while maintaining a compact outer diameter of 2.8 mm. Additionally, the forceps provides 360° independent distal rotation, allowing flexible orientation under bent configurations. The kinematic model of the forceps, established based on screw theory, defines a workspace that approximates a hemisphere with a radius of 15 mm. Its modular design facilitates rapid instrument replacement during surgery. The ex vivo experiment on a fresh porcine stomach confirms its ability to realize effective tissue traction and surgical field exposure during endoscopic submucosal dissection, demonstrating its potential to improve efficiency and safety in endoscopic surgery.

title: A Pneumatically Actuated Robotic Assistant for MRI-Guided Stereotactic Neurosurgery
authors: Gang Li, Atharva Paralikar, Pavel Yarmolenko, Ayush Nankani, Wolfgang Loew, Charles Dumoulin, Kevin Cleary, Chima Oluigbo, Reza Monfaredi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: This paper presents the development and cadaveric evaluation of a pneumatically actuated robotic assistant for MRI-guided stereotactic neurosurgery. The robot features two coaxial arcs that provide four degrees of freedom to position and orient a needle within an MRI scanner. It is driven by custom-designed MR-Safe pneumatic stepper motors and powered by in-room compressed medical air, enabling precise interventions under intraoperative MRI guidance. To improve anatomical visualization, two custom MR imaging coils were developed and integrated with the system. A dedicated clinical workflow was also proposed for robot-assisted stereotactic procedures under MRI-guidance. Targeting accuracy was first assessed in free space using an optical tracking system, yielding mean absolute errors of 0.97 ± 0.42 mm for tip position and 0.46 ± 0.23° for needle angle. System-level accuracy was then evaluated in MRI-guided phantom studies, showing errors of 1.38 ± 0.80 mm in tip position and 1.08 ± 0.67° in angle. Finally, a cadaver study validated the clinical workflow, with observed errors of 2.11 mm in position and 3.32° in angle. Quantitative imaging analysis confirmed minimal image degradation, with signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) variation below 12.26% and 2D geometric distortion under 0.20%.

title: A Multi-AAV Assisted Mobile Edge Computing Task Offloading Based on Dynamic Priority and Two Stage Multi-Agent Collaborative Optimization
authors: Jiaxin Yan, Zhiang Gao, Junling Shi
abstract: In emergency and other dynamic scenarios, although autonomous aerial vehicle (AAV)-assisted mobile edge computing (MEC) alleviates the computational limitations of ground mobile devices (GMDs), its performance remains constrained in complex environments due to insufficient consideration of priority and fairness, coarse grained resource scheduling, and the strong coupling between discrete and continuous decision variables. The innovation of this paper lies in the proposal of a hierarchical cooperative task offloading framework, which incorporates a dynamic priority mechanism, multi-level geographic task grouping, and auction-based resource allocation strategy to address the issues of task priority and resource scheduling in task offloading. Additionally, a two stage cooperative optimization framework is employed, utilizing offline deep Q-network (DQN) for optimal AAV quantity selection and multi-agent deep deterministic policy gradient (MADDPG) for optimizing trajectory and service strategies. Simulation results show significant improvements in system utility, high priority task completion, and energy efficiency in dynamic environments.

title: A promising movement-based prosthesis control tested on valid and arm-amputated participants using first-person teleoperation of humanoid robot Reachy
authors: Vincent Leconte, Effie Segas, Bianca Lento, Emilie Doat, Etienne Guillaud, Matthieu Lapeyre, Pierre Rouanet, Gaëlle Lannuzel, Antoine Pirrone, Rémi Klotz, Daniel Cattaert, Aymar De Rugy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: We showed recently that a novel prosthesis control, based on natural arm movement reconstructed from an Artificial Neural Network (ANN) that receives movement goal and stump motion as inputs, enables close-to-natural reaching control in Virtual Reality (VR). Here, we applied and tested this control in the physical world using first-person teleoperation of Reachy2, an open-source humanoid robot made by Pollen Robotics that ranked 2nd at ANA Avatar XPRIZE. Direct teleoperation reproducing all arm joints of able-bodied participants (n=14) elicited a 100% success rate for grasping objects at various locations, with best possible usability scores and small workload scores. High success rates (>92%) were also obtained when all distal joints from the elbow onward were operated with the novel prosthesis control, using a movement goal identified through gaze-guided computer vision. While usability and workload scores were slightly degraded when able-bodied participants used the prosthesis control as compared to direct teleoperation, both were scored similarly well by a sample from our target population (n=8 participants with transhumeral limb loss). This platform and control schemes offer broad perspectives for prosthesis control, but also for teleoperation robotics and human-robot interactions.

title: USIGAN: Unbalanced Self-Information Feature Transport for Weakly Paired Image IHC Virtual Staining
authors: Yue Peng, Bing Xiong, Fuqiang Chen, Deboch Eybo Abera, RanRan Zhang, Wanming Hu, Jing Cai, Wenjian Qin
abstract: Immunohistochemical (IHC) virtual staining is a task that generates virtual IHC images from H&E images while maintaining pathological semantic consistency with adjacent slices. This task aims to achieve cross-domain mapping between morphological structures and staining patterns through generative models, providing an efficient and cost-effective solution for pathological analysis. However, under weakly paired conditions, spatial heterogeneity between adjacent slices presents significant challenges. This can lead to inaccurate one-to-many mappings and generate results that are inconsistent with the pathological semantics of adjacent slices. To address this issue, we propose a novel unbalanced self-information feature transport for IHC virtual staining, named USIGAN, which extracts global morphological semantics without relying on positional correspondence. By removing weakly paired terms in the joint marginal distribution, we effectively mitigate the impact of weak pairing on joint distributions, thereby significantly improving the content consistency and pathological semantic consistency of the generated results. Moreover, we design the Unbalanced Optimal Transport Consistency Mining (UOT-CTM) mechanism and the Pathology Self-Correspondence Mining (PC-SCM) mechanism to construct correlation matrices between H&E and generated IHC in image-level and real IHC and generated IHC image sets in intra-group level. Experiments conducted on two publicly available datasets demonstrate that our method achieves superior performance across multiple clinically significant metrics, such as IoD and Pearson-R correlation, demonstrating better clinical relevance. The code is available at: https://github.com/MIXAILAB/USIGAN.

title: ISAC-enabled Beam Tracking for U2U Communication Systems under Jittering Effects
authors: Wei Liu, Yuhang Tang, Jinkun Zhu, Peng Yang
abstract: A novel jitter-robust integrated sensing and communication (ISAC)-enabled beam tracking framework is proposed for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-to-UAV (U2U) communication systems. In particular, a tethered UAV base station (UAV-B) serves a maneuvering UAV user (UAV-U), where jitter disrupts beam alignment and degrades communication rates. To enable beam alignment, a UAV-U angle tracking method is first proposed to estimate the relative angles between UAV-B and UAV-U. Specifically, the tethered UAV-B leverages sensing echo signals to obtain motion information of UAV-U, and integrates them with UAV-U’s prior motion models to enhance the accuracy of angle prediction. Meanwhile, through the communication link, UAV-U can receive the direction of UAV-B. Building on the obtained angle information, a jitter compensation algorithm is further developed to maximize the achievable rate of the U2U communication systems. In particular, UAV-B employs adaptive beamwidth control (ABC) to adjust its beamwidth and thus ensures robust coverage for UAV-U. Meanwhile, UAV-U employs beam steering refinement (BSR) to optimize the direction based on predicted angle information, enhancing the received signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and reducing beam misalignment caused by jitter. Finally, our numerical results unveil that: 1) higher angle prediction accuracy is achieved by the proposed UAV-U angle tracking scheme compared to conventional Kalman filter-based angle tracking methods; and 2) a higher achievable rate in the U2U communication systems is achieved by the proposed jitter compensation scheme compared with traditional beamwidth control-based methods.

title: Physics-Guided Multistage Segmentation for Structural Characterization of TRISO Fuel Particles in X-ray CT Imaging
authors: Ping Yang, Songyuan Li, Ligen Shi, Jigang Duan, Genwei Ma, Xing Zhao, Xiangang Wang
abstract: Accurate structural analysis of coated fuel particles is essential for assessing the performance and quality of advanced nuclear fuels. X-ray computed tomography (CT) provides a non-destructive means to visualize the multilayer structure of TRISO fuel particles. However, accurate layer segmentation remains challenging due to low interlayer contrast, imaging noise, and limited labeled data. This paper proposes a physics-guided multistage segmentation network (PMSNet) for automatic multilayer segmentation of TRISO fuel particle CT images. PMSNet integrates prior knowledge of the particle’s spherical geometry and coating hierarchy into a deep learning framework, and consists of three key components: axial-aware position encoding with layer-specific spatial priors, a multiscale perturbation strategy to enhance generalization, and a prior-guided conditional fusion mechanism to enforce interlayer consistency. Experiments on synthetic and real TRISO CT datasets demonstrate that PMSNet achieves mean Dice coefficients above 0.98 and mean IoU values exceeding 0.97 across multiple coating layers. Compared with state-of-the-art methods, PMSNet improves Dice scores by 3–8 percentage points on challenging layers and achieves boundary F1 scores close to 1.0 for key interfaces. While the primary focus of this study is TRISO fuel particle segmentation, additional evaluations are performed to examine the general applicability of the proposed framework. Results on medical CT datasets further confirm its robustness and adaptability to radiation-based imaging tasks. The proposed method contributes a physics-based AI segmentation framework, enables accurate and fully automatic multilayer segmentation of TRISO fuel particles, and provides a practical solution for more efficient and reliable non-destructive nuclear fuel inspection.

title: Use Case Matters: Comparing the User Experience and Task Performance across Tasks for Embodied Interaction in VR_supp1-3680577.pdf
authors: Marie Luisa Fiedler

title: Descriptor: Solar and Kinetic Energy Harvesting Dataset for Human Activity Recognition (SKEH-HAR)
authors: Moid Sandhu, Sara Khalifa, Raja Jurdak, Marius Portmann, Brano Kusy
abstract: This paper presents the multimodal Solar and Kinetic Energy Harvesting dataset for Human Activity Recognition (SKEH-HAR), collected to support research in self-powered activity recognition and wearable systems. The dataset was collected using custom-built wearable devices equipped with solar cells and kinetic energy harvesters, alongside accelerometer sensors. It includes data from 40 adult and healthy participants performing 5 common daily activities, including walking, running, sitting, standing, and going upstairs/downstairs, in both indoor and outdoor environments. The SKEH-HAR dataset captures energy harvesting signals across diverse light and motion conditions, enabling not only the analysis of energy availability but also supporting HAR based on energy harvested signals. This unique combination enables novel investigations into sustainable HAR systems and adaptive sensing strategies based on energy availability. The dataset is expected to serve as a valuable resource for research community working on activity recognition, low-power and batteryless sensing, and self-powered wearable technologies.

title: Tag-Along Virtual Windows Increase Perceived Resistance and Task Load in Augmented Reality
authors: Motoki Kagami, Yuta Kataoka, Yutaro Hirao, Monica Perusquia-Hernandez, Satoshi Hashiguchi, Hideaki Uchiyama, Kiyoshi Kiyokawa, Shohei Mori
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Augmented Reality (AR) can enhance accessibility by anchoring virtual windows to the user's body. Among common approaches, head-following windows help maintain floating virtual windows within the user's field of view. Previous studies have actively explored this new design space to improve user experience and efficiency. In contrast, this study focuses on the perceived resistance of head-following windows in AR, despite their lack of physical mass. We conducted a within-subject experiment with 24 participants, manipulating Follow-Up Delay, Window Size, and UI Type. We measured subjective resistance ratings, NASA-TLX (Raw TLX Scores), and the gaze–head angular offset. The results showed that both a certain level of Follow-Up Delay and the Tag-Along elicited significantly stronger perceived resistance as well as task load. Although Window Size alone did not show a significant effect on resistance ratings, we observed an interaction between the size and UI Type. These findings extend existing pseudo-haptics research by revealing the previously unexplored domain of resistance in head-based interactions with head-following virtual windows. We further provide design implications for head-following windows in AR.

title: A Generic Construction of q -ary Near-MDS Codes Supporting 2-Designs with Lengths Beyond q + 1
authors: Hengfeng Liu, Chunming Tang, Zhengchun Zhou, Dongchun Han, Hao Chen
abstract: A linear code with parameters [n, k, n – k + 1] is called maximum distance separable (MDS), and one with parameters [n; k; n – k] is called almost MDS (AMDS). A code is near-MDS (NMDS) if both it and its dual are AMDS. NMDS codes supporting combinatorial t-designs have attracted growing interest, yet constructing such codes remains highly challenging. In 2020, Ding and Tang initiated the study of NMDS codes supporting 2-designs by constructing the first infinite family, followed by several other constructions for t > 2, all with length at most q + 1. Although NMDS codes can, in principle, exceed this length, known examples supporting 2-designs and having length greater than q + 1 are extremely rare and limited to a few sporadic binary and ternary cases. In this paper, we present the first generic construction of q-ary NMDS codes supporting 2-designs with lengths exceeding q+1. Our method leverages new connections between elliptic curve codes, finite abelian groups, subset sums, and combinatorial designs, resulting in an infinite family of such codes along with their weight distributions.

title: Geographic and Statistical Analysis of EESS-Passive Satellite Overpasses for Spectrum Coexistence
authors: Nicholas Brendle, Jonathan Chamberlain, Joel T. Johnson, David Starobinski
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The passive Earth Exploration-Satellite Service (EESS) provides critical environmental measurements to support meteorological forecasting and climate monitoring. As demand for bandwidth increases among both active and passive users, spectrum sharing is becoming essential. Due to the high sensitivity of EESS-passive radiometers to anthropogenic interference, effective sharing strategies must account for satellite overpasses to limit measurement exposure to unwanted noise. This paper presents a statistical characterization of EESS-passive satellite activity by analyzing interarrival times and overpass durations across multiple geographic locations. Using observational datasets from 15 microwave radiometers, we compute complementary cumulative distribution functions (CCDFs) for both metrics across 15 cities and assess how latitude and longitude affect their statistical properties. While overpass durations are largely location-invariant, interarrival times show strong dependence on latitude, consistent with the polar-orbiting trajectories of the satellites. A key contribution of this work is the introduction of generalized models for the mean and standard deviation of both interarrival times and overpass durations for variable spatial cutoff distances (30–100 km), defined as the maximum horizontal separation between a fixed location and satellite measurements retained for analysis. These models are validated using regression analysis and support location- and scale-aware characterization of radiometer activity. Subsequently, we apply this framework to an economic spectrum-sharing model, demonstrating that minor changes in radiometer utilization —particularly at high latitudes — can lead to significant variation in provider profitability. These results provide valuable insights for policymakers and commercial stakeholders evaluating spectrum coexistence strategies involving EESS-passive systems.

title: DS²AF: An Iterative Detection to Spectral Similarity-Guided Augmentation Framework for Few-Shot Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Shih-Yu Chen, Yu-Cheng Wu, Kai-Quan Wu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral image classification (HSIC) has greatly benefited from deep learning; however, the high dimensionality of hyperspectral data and the costly acquisition of labeled samples result in persistent sample scarcity, especially in real-world applications. Few-shot learning (FSL) addresses this challenge by enabling classification with only a few labeled samples per class, yet existing methods often struggle with HSIs due to complex spectral-spatial characteristics and substantial intra-class variability. This paper proposes DS²AF (Detection-to-Spectral Similarity-Guided Augmentation Framework), an iterative FSL framework that incorporates a novel Hyperspectral Detection-to-Similarity Ratio (HDSR) algorithm. HDSR unifies three complementary perspectives—matched-filter, angular, and probabilistic—into a single ratio-based formulation, yielding a robust global similarity measure for reliable sample selection. Leveraging HDSR, a two-stage augmentation strategy is employed: an initial the regional augmentation leverages spatial locality to stabilize the initial model, preventing it from diverging when sample scarcity is extreme. This process establishes a strong foundation by enriching the local neighborhood of labeled samples. Once the model has developed preliminary discriminative capabilities, global augmentation then searches for more representative samples across the entire image domain, gradually expanding the training set without additional manual labeling. A multi-branch classification network is further developed, comprising a full-band 1D spectral branch, an attention-enhanced 2D spatial branch, and a hybrid 3D-2D spectral-spatial branch to extract complementary features. Moreover, an adaptive stopping criterion based on classification consistency is introduced to ensure stability and prevent overfitting. Experiments on the Indian Pines, University of Pavia, and Salinas datasets under the 5-shot setting achieve overall accuracies of 83.47%, 93.01%, and 96.16%, respectively, substantially outperforming recent FSL approaches and confirming the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed framework.

title: Successful Diversity Is Not What You Think: How to Build a Great Start-Up Team
authors: Anouk Hubrechsen, Claire Migliaccio

title: Hybrid Dynamic-Statistical Algorithm for Optimizing CMOS-Type System Configuration
authors: Iryna Kremer, Olha Shymchyshyn, Mariia Ivakh, Ruslan Tykhovetskyi
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper investigates the impact of technological variability on configurable CMOS GreenPAK systems and the limitations of conventional analysis techniques. Using empirical data from the SLG47011V chip, correlation links between key functional block parameters were identified and incorporated into refined statistical models. A hybrid dynamic–statistical algorithm is introduced, combining step-wise digital state-machine simulation with analytical variability estimation via a modified Delta method. Compared with Monte Carlo analysis, the proposed approach reproduces statistical parameters with an error below 2% while reducing computational cost by over two orders of magnitude. For a PWM generator, agreement with experimental measurements better than 0.2% was achieved. The method enables fast and accurate reliability evaluation and is well suited for early-stage design of configurable mixed-signal microsystems.

title: Retraction Notice: Automatic Breast Tumor Cancer Diagnosis Based on a Hybrid Densenet
authors: G. Jayandhi, J.S. Leena Jasmine, R. Seetharaman, S. Mary Joans, R. Priscilla Joy
publish: 2022 8th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: The breast cancer based image classification and division is proposed by utilizing a Deep learning (DL) technique. A few DL models is used to classify Mammographic information to forecast and analyze breast cancer. In this work, the mixture of two DL is named as Hybrid Auto encoder and DenseNet CNN (HAE-DNN). The Auto-encoder (AE) is likewise a NN that is utilized for a compacted portrayal of crude information. This AE has both encoder and a decoder for pressure and extraction process. Therefore DL of DenseNet is a sort of CNN that has thick associations among layers, through Dense Blocks. This organization can associate each layer with relating feature-map measures straightforwardly with each other. Therefore the proposed strategy is very well based on foreseeing performance in conditions of accuracy, Recall, F1 score, RMSE and MAE individually. The proposed technique is contrasted and the earlier DL strategy and accomplished a preferred outcome over existing.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)

title: Retraction Notice: Comprehensive solution of Scheduling and Balancing Load in Cloud - A Review
authors: N. Manikandan, A. Pavin
publish: 2019 Third International conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Cloud computing provides powerful methods for digital resources to share computing tasks. Congruent scheduling solutions for task and workflow are necessary in order to develop profitable executions and obtain availability, load balancing and consistency in cloud computing, The problem of planning, an NP-hard issue, is solved by the use of multiple metaheuristic algorithms. This paper elaborates on the scheduling schemes for task and workflow for the cloud environment. Cloud computing is instrumental in sharing data and offering a myriad of resources to users, wherein users pay only for the resources used by them. Cloud computing sanctions storage of data and allocated resources in an open environment, thus enabling an increase in the quantity of data storage. Several types of load balancing issues such as memory, CPU and network load are inevitable in cloud environment. To solve these balancing problems by locating the overloaded nodes and diverting the load to another node in the network, load balancing employed. This helps in delegating the incoming workload to various nodes in the system to assure that none of the nodes are overloaded.

title: Retraction Notice: Prediction of Juvenile Delinquencies in Correlation with Education
authors: R. Akila, J. BrindhaMerin, R.K. Vishal, Vishal Krishnan S.H.
publish: 2020 6th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Juvenile Crime is not a healthy trait for any developing country and certainly not for a widely cultured and populous country like India. India has been suffering from overpopulation and low literacy for many years now and it affects the growth of the nation. Education is the key to improve the state of the country and more juvenile crime, children receive lesser quality education and it keeps pulling down the nation. Not only that, it also poisons the next generation and is not healthy for the future of the nation. Even though education is provided for the convicted children, not everyone who is released continues to study which leads to nothing but more uneducated adults who are less productive in this competitive world. This project analyses the juvenile delinquency rate over the course of 30 Years and predicts the possible rate of juvenile crime in India in the forthcoming years, taking into account the various factors that would affect said juvenile crime rate, which include but not limited to: overpopulation, education especially between the ages 16 to 18, financial state of family, cultural differences, regional differences and past criminal history within the convicted juvenile's family. Since factoring in all the aforementioned features into account is near impossible, various machine learning (Linear Regression, Polynomial Regression regression, Back Propagation Network and Adam Optimization Algoritms) and deep learning models are created to primarily focus on the admission rates at different education levels in India. The results of said models provide us with insight on how important education is and how would the future generations shape up when the current trend continues, and also which of the selected learning models is the most accurate.

title: Retraction Notice: Vegetable Disease Detection Using K-Means Clustering And Svm
authors: U. Rahamathunnisa, M.K. Nallakaruppan, A. Anith, Sendhil Kumar K.S.
publish: 2020 6th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: India is the cultivating country and our country is the biggest maker in agricultural products. So, we have to classify and exchange our agricultural products. Manual arranging is tedious and it requires works. The automatic grading system requires less time for grading of the agricultural products. Image processing technique is helpful in examination and evaluating the products. In this paper we proposed a vegetable disease detection system for recognizing diseased vegetables. Here we utilize the Image processing system for reviewing the vegetables. Vegetables are recognized dependent on their features. The features are color, shape, size, texture. We extract these features utilizing algorithms to distinguish the vegetables. We develop a recognition system for 2D input images. The main aim of this work is detecting infected vegetable based on features with K-means clustering algorithm. Algorithm includes three main steps namely enhancement, segmentation and classification. Vegetable samples are collected as images from high resolution camera and data acquisition is carried out for database preparation. The image segmentation process is based on pixel of the image and is applied to get the segmented and infected vegetables using K-Means Clustering algorithm. The image classification is based on Support Vector Machine (SVM) which perform supervised leaning for classification.

title: Retraction Notice: IOT Based Health Tracking Shoe For Elderly People Using Gait Monitoring System
authors: K Jose Reena, R. Parameswari
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Modern Technology increases the economic needs of each person in their machine life. This Machine life reduces the time to take care of their elderly parents in the real world. This paper proposed techniques to monitor and track their elderly parents from their shoe embedded with sensors. Proposed Techniques that track their gait structure and blood pressure, heart rate, and information passed to the responsible person of the elderly parents through SMS. This Paper consists of strategies to implement the gait monitoring system through the wireless sensor with a mobile application using the GSM Sim module.

title: IEEE Industrial Electronics Society Publication Information
authors: N/A

title: Fidelity as Forgetting: Rethinking Reconstruction in Neural Volumetric Media
authors: Yin Bing, Shuyi Wang, Angela Chulei Tang, Siyun Wang, Yuan Zhang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Multimedia Systems Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: Neural Network Image Quality Enhancement Using Multiscaling, Attention, and Residuals
authors: Arkadiusz Talun, Pawel Drozda, Yuriy Romanyshyn, Sergei Yelmanov, Markiian Durkot, Oles Tehlivets
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The problem of image quality enhancement based on a neural network using multiscale, attention, and residuals is considered. The MIRNet neural network and its modifications were used as the basic neural network. The structures and functional features of individual MIRNet network blocks are presented including scaling blocks and channel attention block. The TID2013 database was selected as the basis for test images to study the characteristics of neural network training and functioning. Two options for enhancing images from the TID2013 database were considered: 1) training the network on images from the TID2013 database and enhancing the quality of images from this database, and 2) training the neural network on the LoL dataset and enhancing the quality of images from the TID2013 database using the neural network trained in this way.

title: A Pilot Study on the Feasibility and Preliminary Efficacy of a Virtual Reality System for Stress Reduction
authors: Pakakrong Panthanan, Banphot Nobaew
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Workplace and academic stress remain significant impediments to well-being, yet many Virtual Reality (VR) interventions face practical barriers due to specialized monitoring requirements and limited user agency. This pilot study examines the feasibility and preliminary efficacy of an accessible, design-driven VR stress management system integrating Apple Watch-based heart rate variability (HRV) monitoring with a customizable VR framework that allows users to actively design and configure their own virtual environments. 24 participants (14 employees and 10 students) completed a single 15-minute immersive session. Outcomes were assessed using HRV (rMSSD) and the SPST-20 perceived stress scale. The intervention demonstrated high procedural feasibility, with no reports of adverse effects or motion sickness. Following the VR experience, perceived stress decreased consistently, with the largest reductions observed in affective dimensions such as anxiety and frustration. At the physiological level, HRV increased at the group level, indicating enhanced autonomic recovery, while individual variability was evident, particularly among participants with higher baseline physiological arousal. The convergence of subjective and objective outcomes suggests that a user-centric VR framework integrated with wearable technology represents a feasible and promising approach to stress regulation. These findings provide a foundation for scaling personalized digital interventions within the field of Digital Arts and Media for Health.

title: Synthetic AI-Based Ultrasound Images of Breast Cancer on Small Datasets
authors: K. Onsasipat, U. Chaumrattanakul, W. Lohitvisate, S.S. Makhanov
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Ultrasound (US) remains one of the most reliable and inexpensive tests. Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have emerged as a promising tool for augmenting medical image datasets used by AI solutions. However, GANs trained on small datasets (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300-500$</tex> images) frequently encounter mode collapse, overfitting, and instability. Many GAN images look unrealistic. Enhanced Deep Convolutional GAN (EDCGAN) is introduced to generate quality synthetic images of breast ultrasound (BUS). The contribution consists of several modifications to the standard architecture and postprocessing based on the visual evaluation by radiologists. The Inception Score, the Structural Similarity Index, and the Mean Squared Error comply with the results obtained in the preceding works. The proposed idea to merge Deep Learning(DL) and Machine Learning(ML) is based on the experience of the radiologists, which considerably improves the outcome. The experiments demonstrate that EDCGAN outperforms selected state-of-the-art GANs on small datasets.

title: Enhancing Image Classification Performance Using Data Augmentation and Adaptive Training Strategies
authors: Murali Mohan Reddy M, S. Masood Ahamed
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Real-world Indian image collections exhibit large intra-class variance, severe class imbalance, and localized discriminative cues (for example rare traffic signs, regionspecific crop lesions, and food items), which reduce the effectiveness of generic augmentation policies and standard training schedules. The objective of this study is to evaluate a multi-scale, structure-aware augmentation pipeline, combined with adaptive training strategies, for improving classification accuracy, calibration, and robustness on Indian image data. This work proposes SAIA, a multi-scale augmentation module that produces global, regional, and feature-level variants, and then uses an attention-based patch scorer to rank and sparsify augmented candidates. SAIA is complemented by an adaptive training suite that applies class-aware learning rates, samplehardness weighting, curriculum scheduling, and jump-fusion of complementary backbone embeddings. Experiments compare the approach to strong baselines (ResNet variants with MixUp/CutMix and RandAugment) on curated India-focused datasets, with ablation and robustness tests under noise, occlusion, and illumination shifts. Across multiple tasks, SAIA and adaptive training yields consistent improvements with relative Top-1 accuracy gains of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-6 \%$</tex> over strong augmentation baselines, macro F1 gains of 0.04-0.10, and notably smaller robustness degradations. Minority-class recall improves by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 - 1 0}$</tex> points. Structure-aware augmentation, with adaptive optimization, produces more informative, augmented views, and fairer learning with extensions to semi-supervised and cross-region transfer as future directions.

title: Real-Time Accident Prevention, Detection and Alert System using Federated Learning: A Decentralized Framework for Road Safety
authors: K. Maharajan, Mymoon Sulthana K, Jai Praveena Sai J, Sai Ganka R
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Road traffic accidents are a serious worldwide problem, resulting in numerous deaths and financial losses. Federated learning provides a privacy-preserving and decentralized solution for accident detection without the need for centralized data storage. This paper proposes a real-time accident prevention, detection and alert system that combines deep learning and federated learning for improved road safety. The model proposed applies CNN, ResNet, and EfficientNet to detecting accidents from real-time traffic videos and Random Forest for identifying high-risk areas from dashboard metrics to warn drivers. Federated learning enables accident detection models to be trained on distributed data sources without data sharing, promoting privacy and scalability, unlike in conventional models. The system also automates emergency response by sending real-time alerts through Email and Whatsapp, which reduces delay in emergencies. Impressionally, the suggested federated approach accurately detects accidents with 95.8% accuracy when compared to centralized methods. The accident prevention model is 96% accurate. These results show the capability of federated learning in transforming realtime accident forecasting, providing a scalable, privacyoriented, and effective framework for enhancing road safety.

title: Interpretable Gender Classification from Malayalam Speech
authors: Suprajha Baskaran, V. Sidharrth, Sirisha Tadepalli
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Speech serves as a primary medium of human communication and conveys important paralinguistic information such as speaker gender. This work investigates acoustic featurebased gender classification for Malayalam, a low resource Dravidian language that remains underexplored in speech processing research. A multi-speaker Malayalam speech corpus has been used in this study, from which a comprehensive feature set comprising of spectral, energy based and voice based characteristics of speech such as Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficients, Jitter, Shimmer etc. have been extracted. Multiple machine learning classifiers are compared, among which Logistic Regression achieves the highest accuracy of 94.65 % and a weighted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F}$</tex>-measure of 94.6 % on unseen speakers under speaker independent evaluation. Statistical significance tests, including McNemar, paired t-test, Wilcoxon, and Friedman tests, confirm meaningful performance differences across models. Robustness analysis under additive white noise demonstrates that Logistic Regression is the model most robust to noise with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0. 8 4 \%}$</tex> accuracy at 20dB SNR, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 4. 9 0 \%}$</tex> at 15dB SNR and 82.88 % at 10 dB SNR. Feature-level interpretability using SHAP on the best model further highlights the dominant role of MFCC-derived and spectral features.

title: STEPCon 2025 TOC
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)

title: Evolution and Future of Decision Support Systems: Frameworks, Applications, and Emerging Trends
authors: Siva Subramanian R, K. Ramesh, P.Ruba Sudha, Nagarajan S, D.Lekha, S.Deepa
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Decision Support Systems (DSS) are of strategic, tactical and operational importance for facilitating strategic, tactical and operational decisions in a range of disciplines. This survey focuses on the development of DSS from the initial basic structural models up to the recent systems that include artificial intelligence, big data, and cloud computing. Model driven, data driven, knowledge driven and hybrid DSS frameworks are used to solve problems as wide-ranging as mathematical models to decide resource allocation and real time decision making in volatile conditions. Healthcare, business, environmental planning, and smart city sectors demonstrate the effectiveness of DSS for improving diagnostic, financial, disaster, and urban planning solutions. The new trends include; Artificial intelligence integrated DSS, real time analysis, and ethical issues that are transforming DSS roles, stressing on issues of openness, expandability and user orientation. As for the recent developments in the DSS field, we observed that there are still some issues with data quality, system size, adaptation to use, and adherent to ethical standards. Solving these problems implies the use of more sophisticated approaches including cognitive DSS, reinforcement learning, quantum computation to provide faster, accurate and sustainable decision support. This survey clearly points to the centrality of DSS in increasing organizational performance, creativity and providing timely, relevant solutions in a world that is turning into a maze.

title: A Classification based on Salp Swarm Algorithm for Incomplete Imbalanced Data
authors: Yi Liu, Yangsen Zhou, Qibin Zheng, Guoli Yang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Data classification is significant important for artificial intelligence applications, and its performance rely on the characteristics of data. Many useful methods are advanced to resolve it. However, some general reasons lead to the occurrence of incomplete imbalanced data, and deteriorate capability of conventional approaches. A novel approach called Salp swarm algorithm for Incomplete Imbalanced Data (SIID) is developed in this paper. It adopts feature selection to improve classification accuracy, uses multiple data imputation methods to fill in missing positions, and applies undersampling and oversampling simultaneously to balance data. Besides, it employs salp swarm algorithm to optimize corresponding parameters for obtaining high accurate classification outcomes. Five representative datasets and four outstanding algorithms are taken to evaluate our way. The results on four indicators demonstrate that SIID is superior to compared methods and the proposed components are effective.

title: Smart Fall Detection for Elderly using Wearable Sensors and Machine Learning Analytics
authors: M Arun Kumar, M.Kalimuthu, Jayasurya J, Kavinshankar K, John Sham A
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The Fall Detection System for Elderly Patients is a cutting-edge technology designed to improve the security and health of senior citizens, especially those who live alone or in assisted living facilities. In order to precisely identify falls and track patient movement patterns in real time, this system combines wearable sensors with intelligent algorithms. The primary feature is the combination of accelerometer and gyroscope data, which allows for the accurate detection of abrupt impacts, odd postures, and inactivity after a possible fall. In order to ensure prompt action, the system instantly notifies emergency contacts or caregivers via cloud-based platforms or mobile notifications upon detection. Advanced features like motion trend analysis, posture classification, and ambient condition monitoring help reduce false positives and improve situational awareness. The gadget is perfect for continuous monitoring because it is comfortable, lightweight, and made to be worn for extended periods of time without causing discomfort. The system provides smooth data transfer to healthcare providers for continuous evaluation and proactive care planning thanks to its wireless communication integration and battery-efficient operation. This technology dramatically lowers the risks of fall-related injuries, hospitalizations, and long-term health decline by tackling the crucial problem of unreported or delayed responses to falls. In the end, the Fall Detection System gives older patients more autonomy while bringing comfort to their families and caregivers.

title: Development of a Hybrid Piezoelectric and Solar Rainfall Energy Harvesting System with Water Collection Storage and Python-Based Performance
authors: Joshua Bryant B. Anilao, Gene Gabriel B. Payumo, Noel A. Salalila, Carlos C. Hortinela
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This study presents the development and validation of a novel, integrated hybrid system designed to enhance resource sustainability by simultaneously harvesting solar energy, converting raindrop kinetic energy via a piezoelectric array, and collecting rainwater through an integrated storage mechanism. The system was constructed as a compact, multifunctional prototype suitable for locations characterized by highly variable weather conditions. The methodology involved designing and fabricating the hardware, implementing smart control algorithms for power and water flow management, and evaluating the overall system performance through controlled experimental testing. Results from the piezoelectric subsystem confirmed a clear correlation between rainfall intensity (Light Rainfall at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.56 \text{mL} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> and Heavy Rainfall at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.25 \text{mL} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>) and instantaneous voltage generation, with peak outputs observed under high intensity conditions. Concurrently, the photovoltaic (PV) subsystem analysis characterized thermal and irradiancedriven derating effects on power output. Hybrid power flow measurements verified that the combined system can reliably operate despite the contrasting electrical profiles of the transient piezoelectric and steady-state solar sources. This research demonstrates the technical feasibility of a unified platform that enhances energy availability and environmental adaptability through the strategic integration of multiple ambient resources.

title: AI-Driven Personalization in the Classroom: Impacts on Intrinsic Motivation and Academic Performance
authors: Parul Dubey, Abhinesh Kumar Sahu, Anshul Sharma, Om Balram Sharma, Saurabh Mishra, Manjushree Nayak
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Primary school mathematics education is an important area where learner motivation and engagement play a significant role in academic achievement in the long run. Recent progress in artificial intelligence has raised the possibility of creating adaptive and personalized learning systems. Yet, conventional teaching methods are based on common sources that do not take into consideration the variety of interests present among students leading to lack of interest and reduced achievement. This gap can be bridged by scaling methods that dynamically accommodate different learner profiles. Data were derived from 114 German elementary school students who provided pre-test/post-test performance, as well as measures regarding intrinsic motivation and self-reported personal interest. It included both the measurable test scores and qualitative interest data, which created a broad base for examination. The design was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> mixed factorials (learning content: personalized vs. standard between subjects; learning type: personalized vs. standard within subject). Educational resources and exercises were as generated using a Generative AI implemented over school mathematics curriculum. The contribution of our study is to use the free-form nature of student interests for fine-grained personalization, instead of predened categories. Performance was assessed by test accuracy, intrinsic motivation self-reports and engagement indicators. Results indicate that AI-based personalization significantly increased intrinsic motivation, situational interest, learning performance and reengagement intention than the traditional materials and thus shows potential in scalable adoption.

title: Micro-defect Detection Based on Application of Mixing Frequency Ultrasound
authors: Ganlu Zeng, Shengzi Lu, Xufeng Li, Lei Wang, Pengan Zhu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: The insufficient detection capability of micro defects limits the research and application of high-strength metal materials in China. Due to the energy attenuation and low signal-to-noise ratio of ultrasound signals, it is difficult to detect micro defects in thick components using conventional ultrasound pulse reflection methods. Compared with conventional linear ultrasonic testing methods, nonlinear ultrasonic testing methods are more sensitive to micro defects and have become a new research hotspot in the non-destructive testing industry in recent years. In order to realize the identification and location of micro defects in thick components, the collinear anisotropic longitudinal wave mixing method is used to identify micro defects through sum and difference frequency signal characteristics. The influence of the change of defect buried depth on the mixing effect is studied. The depth location of micro defects is realized by measuring the amplitude change of differential frequency signal. The results show that this method can effectively identify the transverse hole micro defects with a buried depth of 80mm in 7075 aluminum alloy, and can realize the depth location of micro defects.

title: DistilBERT-Powered Optimized Sentiment Analysis for Next-Generation Natural Language Processing
authors: S.R.Menaka, S.Mohanasundaram, P.Sukumar, S.M.Ramesh, Ravikumar Gurusamy, S.Hemalatha
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This proposed system aims to assess the effectiveness and performance of DistilBERT, a streamlined transformer-based model, in sentiment classification tasks, in comparison to BERT. It centers on investigating whether DistilBERT can deliver comparable sentiment analysis results while minimizing computational resource demands, thereby make it suitable for real-time scenario and settings with limited resources. This study involves two distinct groups. Group 1 pertains to sentiment classification utilizing BERT, also with 26 samples. The power analysis is set at 80%, and the confidence interval at 95% the significance level at 0.05%, pertains to sentiment classification utilizing DistilBERT, which comprises 26 samples, while Group 2 pertains to sentiment classification utilizing DistilBERT, which comprises 26 samples. The DistilBERT model offers impressive accuracy while requiring much less computational power than BERT. DistilBERT's accuracy ranges between 91.34% to 97.89%, in contrast to BERT, which reaches an accuracy range of 88.12% and 96.45%. DistilBERT offers an effective approach to sentiment analysis, achieving impressive accuracy while requiring less computational power.

title: Assessing Operational Autonomy in Decentralized Energy Systems
authors: Ailén Kreizer, Julius Frerk, Michael Popp, Markus Zdrallek
abstract: Energy autonomy in decentralized, inverter- dominated power systems is commonly assessed using annual, energy-based metrics. Existing autonomy metrics remain largely energy-centric and focus on annual balances such as self-sufficiency ratios. While useful, these metrics fail to reflect operational independence during real-time operation. Systems can therefore appear highly autonomous on an annual basis while remaining dependent on upstream grids for significant portions of time. This paper introduces an operational autonomy framework that combines extended metrics with an optimized battery energy storage system operation. In addition to temporal self-sufficiency measured as continuous hours without upstream grid support, the framework enforces reserve-aware battery constraints to guarantee stability services. Crucially, the proposed control strategy optimizes BESS charging not only from local surplus but also from the upstream grid in anticipation of future deficits. Results show that operational BESS optimization is essential to achieve meaningful autonomy beyond annual energy balances.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks in Low-Data Regimes: A Comparative Study with Multilayer Perceptrons
authors: Farhad Pourkamali-Anaraki
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Multilayer Perceptrons (MLPs) have long been a cornerstone in deep learning, known for their capacity to model complex relationships. Recently, Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) have emerged as a compelling alternative, utilizing highly flexible learnable activation functions directly on network edges, a departure from the neuron-centric approach of MLPs. However, KANs significantly increase the number of learnable parameters, raising concerns about their effectiveness in data-scarce environments. Hence, this paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of MLPs and KANs from both algorithmic and experimental perspectives, with a focus on low-data regimes. We introduce an effective technique for designing MLPs with unique, parameterized activation functions for each neuron, enabling a more balanced comparison with KANs. Using empirical evaluations on two real-world data sets from medicine and engineering, we explore the trade-offs between model complexity and accuracy. Our findings show that MLPs with individualized activation functions achieve higher predictive accuracy with only a modest increase in parameters, especially when the sample size is limited to around one hundred. For example, in a classification problem within additive manufacturing, MLPs achieve a median accuracy of 0.91, significantly outperforming KANs, which only reach a median accuracy of 0.53. In addition, we analyze key factors affecting KAN performance, including the polynomial order and grid size of spline-based activation functions.

title: Radix-8 Booth Encoded Approximate Multiplier Design for Energy-Efficient Image Processing Applications using a 5:3 Compressor
authors: Manju I Kollannur, Oudaya Coumar S
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Low-power design of the Arithmetic Logic Unit heavily relies on approximate computing. Applications involving image processing, like multimedia and signal processing, are its best fits. However, approximate computing is less accurate with area and power consumption. To overcome this, a novel high-performance multiplier architecture is proposed using Radix-8 Booth Encoding, and an approximate 5:3 compressor has been proposed. The proposed Radix-8 Booth encoding reduces the number of partial products. Signed bits of the multiplication are generated by grouping four multiplier bits using Radix-8 Booth encoding. Three bits are new bits and one is an overlapping bit at a time, and encodes them. The proposed 5:3 compressor efficiently compresses the partial product, leading to a reduced critical path and area. The proposed 5:3 compressor efficiently compresses five bits into three outputs, resulting in improved speed and reduced power consumption. By attaining high PSNR and MSSIM values, the proposed method is verified through image sharpening. Excellent image quality and structural similarity are provided by the high PSNR and MSSIM values. Using Xilinx ISE, the proposed multiplier is implemented on a 28 nm FPGA and assessed in terms of area, power, and delay. The proposed structure achieves up to 57% area, 51% power, and 40% delay reduction in comparison to existing approximate compressors and multipliers. Therefore, the proposed design is highly efficient for image-sharpening applications.

title: Synthetic Data Generation with GANs for Rareevent Detection in Medical and Traffic Vision
authors: Bhanu Prakash Battula, P Sumana, R Lavanya, Juhisha J, Subha Balamurugan, Navyatha Ravi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Rare-event detection in safety-critical vision systems such as video capsule endoscopy (VCE) and traffic surveillance for vulnerable road users (VRU) remains challenging due to extreme class imbalance and limited annotated examples. Class imbalance and high intra-class variability caused supervised detectors to miss uncommon but critical events and to produce costly false alarms. A conditional, diversity-preserving generative adversarial network (cD-GAN) was developed to synthesize plausible rare-event images and short clips and to augment training of a transformer-based detector (ViT-D) with temperature-scaled calibration. Experiments used contemporary 2024-25 testbeds-Galar VCE and Capsule Vision 2024 in the medical domain, and Waymo VRU resources plus TU-DAT and TAD in traffic-and evaluated exclusively on real hold-outs. Compared with a strong pretrained ViT baseline, the augmented pipeline increased recall by +18 percentage points in the medical setting and +10 percentage points in traffic, and improved AUPRC by +0.27 (medical) and +0.17 (traffic), while reducing expected calibration error (ECE) by roughly 44%. The approach also reduced false positive rate at operational TPRs. The results suggested that carefully constrained, diversity-aware synthetic augmentation could improve detection sensitivity and reliability; future work will extend temporal coherence, multisite validation, and uncertainty-aware decision policies for deployment.

title: Ultra-Reliable Receiver for Asynchronous SCMA in Satellite-Terrestrial Communication
authors: Chunjie Li, Ke Zhang, Jian Jiao, Ye Wang, Xiao Ma, Qinyu Zhang
abstract: This paper proposes an iterative detection and decoding (IDD) scheme for asynchronous sparse code multiple access (aSCMA), referred to as aIDD, in satellite-terrestrial uplink communication scenario with the low earth orbit (LEO) satellite equipped with uniform planar array (UPA) antenna. In detector design, we first develop the extended factor graph for aSCMA by considering the memory induced by asynchronous transmission, and an asynchronous message passing algorithm (A-MPA) is proposed. In A-MPA, the noise whitening on the sampled symbols is performed to mitigate the correlation among the noise samples due to the matched filtering, and the updating rules are then designed to achieve superior performance. Furthermore, we propose an asynchronous expectation propagation algorithm (A-EPA) by exploiting the diversity gains induced by UPA, where the means and variances of the transmitted SCMA codewords are updated with high reliability. Simulation results show that the proposed A-EPA can achieve the same performance as that of A-MPA but with lower complexity at a high number of receive antennas. In decoder design, a soft-output ordered likelihood decoder (S-OLD) is proposed to generate the soft information with high reliability compared with the belief propagation (BP) decoder under low-density parity check (LDPC) code. By combining the proposed A-EPA/A-MPA and S-OLD, the proposed aIDD scheme iteratively exchanges the messages between the detector and the decoder until the maximum number of iterations of the outer loop is achieved or the decoding results of all the users are converged. Simulation results show that the proposed aIDD/A-EPA and aIDD/A-MPA have the same performance and are better than that of the synchronous IDD and joint detection and decoding (JDD) schemes.

title: IEEE Standard for Wearable, Cuffless Blood Pressure Measuring Devices
authors: N/A
abstract: A normative definition of wearable cuffless blood pressure (BP) measuring devices and the objective performance evaluation of this kind of device are established in this standard. This standard is independent of the form of the device or the vehicle to which the device is attached or in which it is embedded. All types of wearable BP measurement devices, including epidermal and unobtrusive devices with different modes of operation (e.g., short‑term, long‑term, snapshot, continuous, beat‑to‑beat BP, or BP‑variability measurement), are covered by this standard. This standard is, however, limited to the evaluation of devices in which a cuff is not used during measurement, and the evaluation of all sphygmomanometers that are used with an occluding or inflatable cuff for the indirect determination of BP on the upper arm or wrist is not covered. Guidelines are provided in this standard for manufacturers to qualify and validate their products, potential purchasers or users to evaluate and select prospective products, and health care professionals to understand the manufacturing practices of wearable BP devices.

title: Bridging Machine Learning and Generative AI for Intelligent Vulnerability Detection and Remediation in Source Code
authors: Atharva Deokar, Mayank Chauhan, Saur Deshmukh, Paarth Mahadik, Debjit Ghosal, Mahadev Balla, Megh Doshi, Shrey Dedhia, Sujal Chaudhari, Aniket Atole, Anant V. Nimkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Software vulnerabilities remain a major concern in modern development, where increasing code complexity and rapid release cycles make manual reviews and traditional static analysis insufficient. Existing automated approaches primarily focus on detecting vulnerabilities but rarely provide actionable, context-aware remediation, resulting in delayed patching and increased developer effort. To address this gap, this research proposes an AI-driven architecture that combines traditional machine learning with large language models (LLMs) for both precise detection and guided repair. The system employs an LSTM-based pipeline for accurate vulnerability localization and an LLM-based recommender module capable of generating targeted fixes and explanations. By constraining the LLM's operational scope, hallucinations are minimized and the reliability of recommendations improves. This hybrid architecture ultimately supports faster, more intelligent, and developer-centric vulnerability mitigation during the development phase.

title: Triple-Stream Ensemble Fusion for Resilient Multi-Modal Human Action Recognition: Addressing Generalization in Co-Located Sensing
authors: Suraj Shandilya, Mahendra Siyag, Muskan Kumari, Rajkishor Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Human Action Recognition (HAR) with high accuracy in private areas is a hard task. Even though camera-based systems are accurate, they create serious privacy issues. On the other hand, RF sensing (WiFi) is passive and protects privacy, but it suffers from signal noise and environmental changes. In this paper, we introduce the Triple-Stream Multi-Modal Fusion Framework, which uses the combined benefits of skeletal vision data and WiFi Channel State Information (CSI). We study three different architectures: a lightweight Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) for spatial data, an Adaptive Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) for structure, and a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network for time sequences. Experiments on the MMFi dataset show an unexpected result: although complex GCNs train well, they fail to generalize to new people. The simpler MLP works better on unseen subjects <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(72.25 {\%}$</tex> vs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70.62 {\%})$</tex>. To fix this, we use a Probabilistic Ensemble Fusion approach. By optimizing weights on a validation set <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.5 W_{M L P}+0.4 W_{G C N}+0.1 W_{L S T M})$</tex>, we raised the final accuracy to 75.64%, beating the single best model by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+3.4 {\%}$</tex>. These results show that combining models can reduce overfitting and offers a practical way to do privacypreserving sensing on edge devices.

title: Past Forward: All Alone in the Abyss
authors: Allison Marsh
abstract: Deep Rover was a one-person submersible designed to satisfy human curiosity about the underwater world. As the rover moved freely through the water down to depths of 1,000 meters, its operator sat in relative comfort in the cab, inside a clear acrylic bubble with panoramic views–an inverted fishbowl, with the human immersed in breathable air while the sea creatures looked in.

title: To be Healed or Hacked? - User-Centered Ethical Design for Embodied AI in Mental Health Care_supp1-3680608.mp4
authors: Paula Friedrich
