title: An improved driving power supply method for a portable electromagnetic impact source with a short acceleration distance
authors: Wei Wang, Xian Liao, Xinglin Lu, Jingjie He, Zhihong Fu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Portable electromagnetic impact seismic sources, which use reluctance coil emission technology, have been partially applied in seismic engineering and geophysical exploration. This technology offers significant advantages in equipment stability and the simplicity of the electromechanical structure. However, the short acceleration distance inherent to their portable design limits the hammer's ability to achieve high ejection kinetic energy. In this study, a new driving power supply for the acceleration coil was enhanced and redesigned based on the previously established architecture. By leveraging the complementary advantages of commonly used driving power supplies (capacitor and battery), a method combining both for a time-sharing drive was proposed. The results of comparing and analyzing the output kinetic energy of the seismic source under five different driving power supply methods indicate that the ‘battery driven first, capacitor driven later’ outperforms ‘battery driven alone’, ‘capacitor driven first, battery driven later’ and ‘capacitor driven alone’, increasing the hammer's kinetic energy by 20.49%, 48.96%, and 109.23%, respectively. Based on an analysis of the reasons for these improvements, a scheme named ‘battery driven first, two capacitors driven later sequentially’ was proposed. Compared to ‘battery driven alone’, this scheme enhances the hammer's kinetic energy by 41.57%. The improved driving power supply significantly enhances the performance of the electromagnetic impact seismic sources, achieving an output kinetic energy of 1910.35 J.

title: An Extensive Experimental Study of Indexes in Continuous Subgraph Matching: [Experiments & Analysis]
authors: Xiangyang Gou, Lei Zou, Jeffrey Xu Yu, Wenjie Zhang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Descriptor: The WiFi-Based Human Activity Recognition Under Everyday Life Changes Dataset (WiFi-Wild)
authors: Amany Elkelany, Robert Ross, Susan Mckeever
abstract: WiFi-based Human Activity Recognition (HAR) provides a non-invasive alternative to camera- and wearable-based sensing, but model performance often degrades under environmental and subject-specific variations. This work introduces the WiFi-Wild dataset, a publicly available collection of WiFi Channel State Information (CSI) signals designed to capture everyday life changes such as furniture rearrangement, transmitter/receiver placement, device interference, and subject diversity. The WiFi-Wild dataset was collected in a residential environment using ESP32 devices, with a single participant performing a range of daily activities under multiple spatial and environmental scenarios, and additional participant appearances were included to evaluate subject variability. To demonstrate dataset utility, a hybrid deep learning model combining a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with an attention-based Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) network was trained for activity classification and evaluated across different configurations. Results show that environmental and subject-related variations substantially affect model performance, with furniture rearrangements and TX/RX repositioning reducing F1-scores by up to 21%, and signal interference or clothing changes causing decreases of 7–12%. The WiFi-Wild dataset enables systematic, reproducible evaluation of WiFi-based HAR models under realistic conditions and supports the development of more robust systems.

title: Analysis and Design of Dual-Injection Injection-Locked Frequency Triplers With Wide Locking Range and High Harmonic Rejection
authors: Qingfan Zeng, Jingzhi Zhang, Yiming Yu, Huihua Liu, Yunqiu Wu, Chenxi Zhao, Kai Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a dual-injection injection-locked frequency tripler (ILFT) achieving a wide locking range (LR), high harmonic rejection, and low power consumption. The dual-injection ILFT consists of a tail injection path and a coupling injection path. The tail injection path leverages a hard-switching behavior to reshape the injection current into a pseudo-square waveform, which enhances the strength of the third harmonic to extend the LR. In addition, the coupling injection path utilizes both electric and magnetic coupling of the injection-current-boosting (ICB) transformer to further enhance the injection current. By combining the two injection paths, the third harmonic currents are combined in phase, while the other undesired harmonics are suppressed out of phase. Additionally, an injection-locked buffer (ILB) is cascaded to improve the flatness of the output power while further suppressing undesirable harmonics. Fabricated in a 65-nm CMOS process, the ILFT operates from 17.4 to 26.4 GHz with 41.1% LR. The harmonic rejection ratio (HRR) is above 40 dBc from 17.4 to 24 GHz, and the power consumption of the ILFT core is 3.2 mW.

title: Time-Varying Acceleration Constraint-Based Admittance Control for Robot-Aided Bilateral Human-Compliant Ankle Rehabilitation
authors: Jianfeng Li, Cong Wang, Shiping Zuo, Mingjie Dong, Yifeng Chen, Yongzhen Li, Mingming Zhang
abstract: Robot-assisted bilateral rehabilitation has demonstrated efficacy in facilitating ankle function recovery. This article develops a control framework to ensure safe bilateral human-compliant ankle rehabilitation training. First, a bilateral ankle-coupled rehabilitation training mode (BACRTM) is established, which dynamically couples the human–robot interaction (HRI) torques of both ankles. When one ankle performs active training, resistive or assistive torques are synchronously applied to the contralateral side, forming a coordinated bilateral movement mechanism. Second, a time-varying acceleration constraint (TVAC) strategy integrating error feedback is designed based on Lyapunov stability theory. By limiting the upper and lower bounds of reference acceleration, system stability is ensured during high-dynamic HRI. Theoretical analysis proves that the TVAC strategy can produce stable and bounded tracking errors. Experimental results demonstrate that under the BACRTM, the TVAC strategy can reduce the bilaterally rendered inertia by an average of 99.05%, while maintaining robustness to varying subject inputs and dynamics. Furthermore, the strategy features adjustable acceleration boundary effects, enabling adaptation to patients with ankle injuries at different rehabilitation stages.

title: PP-wave reflection coefficient approximation for seismic amplitude inversion in biaxial stress-induced anisotropic media considering microcrack closure
authors: Liangyi Deng, Xingyao Yin, Zhengqian Ma, Kun Li, Yaming Yang, Bing Wang, Guanjie Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Horizontal biaxial stress, a prevalent stress state of Earth’s crust, can cause preferential closure of microcracks within rocks, thereby triggering elastic anisotropy. However, the nature effect of biaxial stress on seismic reflection response has been devoted scant attention. Addressing this issue, an approximate PP-wave reflection coefficient in biaxial stress-induced anisotropic media incorporating microcrack closure is developed for seismic amplitude inversion. Based on the dual-porosity model, we treat the initial unstressed rock as a stress-insensitive background matrix embedded with randomly oriented microcracks. Under small biaxial stress, microcracks preferentially close, expressed as normal and tangential compliances decreasing exponentially as a function of the normal stress acting on the microcrack surface. A corresponding biaxial stress-induced anisotropy model is derived, and then experimentally validated using existing rock samples. We then use it to construct the effective stiffness tensor of the biaxial stress-induced anisotropic media through the effective medium theory and the weak anisotropy assumption. Two sets of stress-dependent anisotropy parameters (SDAPs), defined as the combination of elastic modulus, stress and normal/tangential compliance, are introduced to quantify the magnitude of stress-induced anisotropy. Furthermore, considering the scattering theory, a linearized PP-wave equation is derived in terms of P- and S-wave modulus, density and SDAPs. We exhaustively investigate the effect of biaxial stress on seismic response characteristics. Finally, within the Bayesian framework, the SDAPs are inverted using the azimuthal amplitude difference inversion method incorporating Cauchy prior information and low-frequency regularization. The inverted SDAPs then serve as inputs to predict the remaining isotropic parameters. The feasibility of the inversion approach is illustrated with synthetic seismic examples and a real data application. We determine that our study contributes to understanding stress-induced anisotropy and can produce potential stress indicators.

title: Corrigendum: Building Efficient Serverless Vector DBs via Block-based Data Partitioning: [Experiments & Analysis]
authors: Daniel Barcelona-Pons, Raul Gracia-Tinedo, Albert Cañadilla-Domingo, Xavier Roca-Canals, Pedro García-López
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Integrating Physiological Signals for Enhanced Sleep Apnea Detection Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Rudra Vyas, Parva Lunawat, Santosh Kumar Satapathy, Nibedita Das, Suren Kumar Sahu, Rajanikanta Mohanty
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Sleep apnea (SA) holds substantial clinical importance, as it markedly elevates the risk of cardiovascular and cerebrovascular complications. However, its diagnosis through full polysomnography (PSG) presents several drawbacks, including high costs and the use of numerous sensors that can cause patient discomfort and disrupt normal sleep. To address the above problems, this study proposes using single-channel photoplethysmography (PPG) to detect pediatric sleep apnea (SA) in an automated manner. One of the main issues lies in identifying small, non-steady-state apneic events in raw PPG records, while simultaneously reducing the large class imbalance exhibited by clinical data. These aims were achieved through the analysis of two deep-learning pipelines: (1) a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) that directly takes raw PPG timeseries data as input, and (2) a two-dimensional convolutional neural network (2D-CNN) that accepts time-frequency data (scalograms) created using continuous wavelet transform (CWT) as input. The Childhood Adenotonsillectomy Trial (CHAT) dataset, including 246 subjects, was used, and class imbalance was handled using controlled oversampling and class-weighted loss functions. The baseline 1D-CNN reached an overall accuracy of 80.0 %, but with a sensitivity of only 3.6 % to apneic events, exemplifying the accuracy paradox. Conversely, the optimised 2D-CNN using complex Morlet wavelets demonstrated significantly increased subject-independent performance: in a leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) study on a twelve-subject sample, the average accuracy was 86.8 %, the area under the AUC-ROC curve was 0.893, and the macro F 1 -score was 79.7 %. Future research can explore multimodal fusion, temporal aggregation, real-time signal quality, and clinical support. Overall, 2D-CNN of CWT scalograms from single-channel PPG is a promising wearable-based pediatric SA screening method, outperforming 1D-CNN, though temporal modelling and artefact robustness need refinement before clinical use.

title: AI based Chatbot for Students using RAG in Large Language Models
authors: Monica R, Mukhesh S, Mahalakshmi M, Madhumathi R
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This research paper presents a novel AI-powered educational platform designed to address the significant barriers faced by Indian school dropouts, particularly those from regional language-speaking backgrounds. The platform's core functionality provides personalized learning resources and scholarship recommendations. To optimize the system's retrieval and conversational capabilities, a performance evaluation is conducted on three open-source language models namely all-MiniLM-L6-v2, DistilGPT-2, and Mistral AI. The system incorporates a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) chatbot, implemented with the Gemini API, to deliver interactive, context-aware support. The platform's recommendation accuracy is enhanced by a custom-curated dataset, and PyPDF integration ensures seamless access to digital learning materials. The efficacy of the system is demonstrated through strong performance metrics, including a response relevance of 87%, a retrieval accuracy of 89%, and a low latency of 1 second. These results highlight the platform's potential to empower students to resume their education and access vital financial aid.

title: Event Detection in Dark Web and Surface Web Forums using Machine Learning
authors: Rodrigo Bernabé Silveira, Rodrigo Sanches Miani, Paulo Henrique Ribeiro Gabriel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Detecting malicious posts on the Dark Web and Surface Web forums is crucial for cybersecurity. Analyzing these posts can enhance intrusion detection systems and aid in identifying cyberattacks. This analysis also reveals new threats and provides other benefits. By investigating forums on cybersecurity topics, various types of attacks can be proactively prevented. Therefore, it is essential to identify emerging events in posts with relevant cybersecurity keywords. This work aims to analyze forum posts from both sources over time to detect new events – specifically, emerging topics of cybersecurity interest. To evaluate the significance of detected events, a malicious post classifier categorized them as high, medium, or low relevance. The proposed algorithm proved promising. It identified 767 new events from a dataset of 7,643 analyzed posts. Approximately 0.9% of the detected events were classified as highly relevant.

title: AQUA-LLM: Evaluating Accuracy, Quantization, and Adversarial Robustness Trade-offs in LLMs for Cybersecurity Question Answering
authors: Onat Gungor, Roshan Sood, Harold Wang, Tajana Rosing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently demonstrated strong potential for cybersecurity question answering (QA), supporting decision-making in real-time threat detection and response workflows. However, their substantial computational demands pose significant challenges for deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. Quantization, a widely adopted model compression technique, can alleviate these constraints. Nevertheless, quantization may degrade model accuracy and increase susceptibility to adversarial attacks. Fine-tuning offers a potential means to mitigate these limitations, but its effectiveness when combined with quantization remains insufficiently explored. Hence, it is essential to understand the trade-offs among accuracy, efficiency, and robustness. We propose AQUA-LLM, an evaluation framework designed to benchmark several state-of-the-art small LLMs under four distinct configurations: base, quantized-only, fine-tuned, and fine-tuned combined with quantization, specifically for cybersecurity QA. Our results demonstrate that quantization alone yields the lowest accuracy and robustness despite improving efficiency. In contrast, combining quantization with fine-tuning enhances both LLM robustness and predictive performance, achieving an optimal balance of accuracy, robustness, and efficiency. These findings highlight the critical need for quantization-aware, robustness-preserving fine-tuning methodologies to enable the robust and efficient deployment of LLMs for cybersecurity QA.

title: Unified Adversarial Robustness: Combining Adversarial Training with Certified Defense
authors: Haijia Zhong, Fucai Xie, Shuanglei Wang, Xianpeng Wang, Silalai Phadboke
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Adversarial examples pose critical security threats to deep neural networks, yet existing defenses face a fundamental dilemma: adversarial training provides strong empirical robustness but lacks provable guarantees, while certified defenses offer formal guarantees but sacrifice practical performance. We propose a unified framework that bridges this gap through balanced adversarial training combined with randomized smoothing certification. By introducing a tunable clean/adversarial mix ratio α, our approach achieves simultaneous improvements on both fronts: 72.20% robust accuracy under AutoAttack and 92.19% certified accuracy at r = 0.5 on CIFAR-10, surpassing prior certified defenses by 23.8% while maintaining 93.93% clean accuracy. Through systematic ablation studies, we discover a remarkable negative gap phenomenon (−42.34%) where pure adversarial training achieves robust accuracy exceeding clean accuracy, revealing critical insights into training distribution mismatch and validating the necessity of balanced training. Our work demonstrates that empirical and certified robustness are complementary rather than conflicting objectives, providing a practical foundation for trustworthy AI deployment in safety-critical applications.

title: Digital Leadership Practices and Their Influence on Employee Productivity in IT-Enabled Workplaces
authors: Nirmala Gandla, M.Rajya Laxmi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The digital environment becomes meaningful in the information technology of today due to companies' desire for greater efficiency, innovation and collaboration. This shift is all about digital leadership practices in which employees can navigate this change in technology without sacrificing productivity. The authors of this study examined the possible impacts on individual productivity in the workplace and considered the role of digital culture as a moderator of the relationship between digital leadership practices and individual productivity. The survey data comprised 200 participants employed in IT-enabled work environments across different sectors and was compiled using a quantitative research design. The data was analyzed using multiple regression analysis to identify the relationships between digital leadership practices, digital culture and employee productivity. The analysis determined that digital leadership practices can have a positive and significant effect on employee productivity, and that digital culture is a moderator, based on its complementary effect to that of digital leadership practices. These discoveries affirmed a need to promote positive digital leadership behaviors and fostering digital cultures in order to improve employee performance in IT-enabled organizations.

title: Machine Learning Techniques for Voice-Based Parkinson's Disease Detection
authors: Nishvika Teja Reddy, Niharika. N, Jayashree K.R, J. Pallavi, Abdul Haq Nalband, Trupthi Rao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease represents a progressively debilitating neurological condition that fundamentally disrupts motor control, vocal production, and patient quality of life. The integration of artificial intelligence within modern healthcare paradigms have catalyzed innovative approaches for the objective disease identification. This investigation presents a comparative evaluation of machine learning architectures, including Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Support Vector Machines, Logistic Regression, and Multilayer Perceptron Neural Networks. Our methodology analyzes biomedical vocal recordings to establish discriminative patterns between healthy subjects and individuals with Parkinsonian symptoms. Performance assessment encompasses diverse statistical indicators, while model transparency is achieved through SHAP-driven feature analysis. Experimental outcomes demonstrate that computational intelligence applied to vocal pattern recognition offers a dependable, minimally invasive pathway for early-stage identification, with Random Forest achieving 94.87% accuracy. The framework exhibits substantial promise for augmenting clinical judgment and supporting therapeutic intervention.

title: SONAR: A Hybrid Explainable Emotion-Aware Music Recommendation Framework
authors: Harinarayanan U, Oneesa Faizal, Juben George John, Akash JP, K. Namitha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Music influences emotional control, cultural identity, and individual well-being significantly. But today's music recommendation systems lack cultural awareness, contextual intelligence, and transparency in decision making; they rely on collaborative filtering, audio similarity, or simple mood classification. This paper presents SONAR, a novel Explainable Hybrid Emotion-Aware Music Recommendation System that integrates contextual intelligence, audio feature psychology, cultural modeling, text emotion recognition, speech emotion analysis, and safety-aware mental health filtering. SONAR proposes a threepillar hybrid architecture that considers (1) Cultural Context, (2) Audio Feature Science based on music psychology, and (3) Real-world Contextual Intelligence together for personalized genre recommendations. To produce transparent playlist choices, weighted reasoning is applied using a multilayer scoring framework with adaptability to user preferences. An Explainable AI layer is integrated at every stage of the pipeline to generate human-understandable explanations for mood detection, genre selection, safety judgments, and ordering of playlists. Balancing technological intelligence with psychological and cultural realism, this work presents a strong, ethically sound, culturally sensitive system that raises the bar above conventional models in user trust and emotional fit.

title: Digital Semantic Communication with Channel Adaptation and Vector Quantization
authors: Yuyang Zheng, Weihong Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Semantic communication has demonstrated the potential to surpass the Shannon limit through joint source-channel coding. However, existing systems suffer from two critical limitations: the lack of adaptability to dynamic channels and insufficient compatibility with digital communication systems due to reliance on analog modulation. This paper proposes a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) adaptive digital semantic communication framework that achieves high-quality transmission under dynamic channels and compatibility with digital systems by integrating a channel-aware semantic codec with a learnable semantic feature quantization module. The system employs lightweight residual convolutional modules to extract semantic features, enables a single model to adapt to a wide SNR range through an SNR-adaptive attention mechanism, and utilizes a learnable codebook to map continuous features to discrete binary sequences. A three-stage progressive training strategy is adopted to optimize system performance. Experimental results on the test set demonstrate that the proposed system outperforms the traditional JPEG+LDPC scheme, validating the effectiveness and practical value of this framework.

title: Retinal Disease Classification Using a Multi-Stage Deep Learning Model on OCT C8 Dataset with Grad-CAM Support
authors: T. G. Ramnadh Babu, Bathula Timothi, Kannekanti Teja Vardhana Chari, Kurra Vamsi Krishna, Sireesha Moturi, Y. Sri Lalitha, Y. Sravani Devi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Early diagnosis of retinal-related disorders is paramount in ensuring from causing significant vision loss, especially where access is limited specialist medical treatment. The following study offers a short, multi-step procedure. Convolutional model assisting in the identification of different disorders of the retina on the Retinal OCT C8 dataset. This includes eight prominent categories that are widely employed by physicians in real scenarios. The model consists of a sequence of convolutional layers with special modules which assist in recognizing fine textures as well as large ones. These modules consist of different filters which are signs for potential retinal problems. The data set was were thoroughly cleaned and divided, and the model was trained only on these samples to ensure the results are reliable in a given real medical setting. On the test section of the data, The accuracy achieved by the model was 95.07%, with precision, recall, and F1-score all tend to be very close to each other, which is better than models such as it. The model has been designed to be lightweight, so that it A model such as it can perform well even on system with low computing capabilities. To in order to make the model more trustworthy, it also includes visual tools those that emphasize what aspects of the retina were most significant in arriving at a diagnosis, assisting physicians in employing it effectively in their practice.

title: DIVER: A Robust Text-to-SQL System with Dynamic Interactive Value Linking and Evidence Reasoning
authors: Yafeng Nan, Haifeng Sun, Zirui Zhuang, Qi Qi, Guojun Chu, Jianxin Liao, Dan Pei, Jingyu Wang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Respiration Rate Estimation Using ECG and PPG Data: Open Source Implementations Focused on Wearables
authors: Chryssalenia Koumpouzi, Matthew Pediaditis, Emmanouil Spanakis, Vangelis Sakkalis
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Respiratory rate (RR) is an indicator of various psychological and pathological conditions and needs to be monitored easily and reliably. Emerging trends and recent advancements indicate that wearables are the future of continuous noninvasive vital measurement for healthcare. Numerous recent studies have focused on estimating RR from the electrocardiogram (ECG) or photoplethysmogram (PPG), which can be acquired non-invasively with minimal hardware and without obstructing spontaneous breathing patterns. While easy and flexible in acquisition, such signals may suffer from poor signal quality, which in turn results in unreliable estimation of RR, an issue that can be addressed by evaluating the quality of the obtained estimates. In this work, we propose a novel method for RR estimation based on the cross-correlation function that demonstrates improved estimation properties compared to most existing open-source methods achieving 0-bias and limits of agreement (LOAs) from -5.37 to 5.44 bpm. We propose the use of variance as an estimate quality metric and demonstrate how this approach results in higher accuracy. Two types of fusion are designed and evaluated, stressing the importance of quality evaluation and estimate combination in improving accuracy, reaching LOAs between -1.44 and 0.9 bpm. Finally, we stress the importance of signal duration in estimation accuracy, and demonstrate how longer segments result in lower error, validating this finding on multiple open-source methods. Our results are evaluated on three publicly available datasets, including CapnoBase, a benchmark database on respiration.

title: Invisible Bullet-Magnetism Aim Assistance in VR Dart Throwing: Effects on User Experience and Performance_supp1-3680707.mp4
authors: Ren Boz
abstract: Aim assistance has been shown to be effective in traditional video games; however, its application in VR contexts remains relatively underexplored. We investigate the effects of invisible bullet-magnetism aim assistance applied at the time of throw on performance and user experience in a VR dart-throwing task. We corrected the trajectory by adjusting the initial throwing direction to reduce the landing position deviation from the target center by 50%. We conducted a within-subjects experiment with 29 participants, comparing the aim-assist condition with a control condition without assistance. Aim assistance with invisible bullet magnetism significantly improved objective and perceived performance, accuracy, and precision. Participants in the aim-assist condition reported significantly higher perceived competence and control, while perceived suspense and pressure were scored significantly lower. Enjoyment, internal attribution of achievement, and workload remained comparable between the two conditions. Participants attributed their success to themselves rather than external factors in both conditions. The invisible bullet-magnetism aim assistance in our study remained largely unnoticed, and participants favored the aim-assist condition.

title: Data-Driven Energy Optimization of a CPA and Gain-Managed-Nonlinearity Mamyshev Oscillator
authors: Ya Lin, Qiurun He, Peng Liu, Zhongxing Jiao, Yan Zhang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Mamyshev oscillators are well known for generating high-energy ultrashort pulses. However, further energy scaling is fundamentally constrained by stimulated Raman scattering, intracavity nonlinear accumulation, and gain-induced spectral narrowing. Conventional optimization based on generalized nonlinear Schrodinger equation simulations is extremely time-consuming and strongly dependent on manual parameter tuning in a multi-parameter cavity space, making it inefficient for systematic energy optimization. To address these challenges, a data-driven optimization framework is developed to enhance the pulse energy of a chirped-pulse-amplification and gain-managed-nonlinearity Mamyshev oscillator. The framework integrates a long short-term memory (LSTM) model with a particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm. The LSTM model reproduces intracavity pulse evolution with sub-percent accuracy and over three orders of magnitude speed-up relative to full simulations for generalized nonlinear Schrodinger equations. The PSO algorithm explores the multi-parameter cavity space efficiently. The PSO-LSTM framework identifies an optimal configuration yielding 558.9 nJ, representing a ~70% increase over the experimental result, demonstrating an effective approach for high-energy ultrafast fiber laser optimization.

title: Deployment of Attention-based VGG19 with Deep SVM for Soil Moisture and Vegetation Index Prediction in Agriculture Field using Satellite Images
authors: M. Ashok Nagasai, S.Maruthupermal
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Variation in soil moisture content can greatly affect crop growth and agricultural productivity. While soil moisture provides needed water for plants, excessive soil moisture can hinder crop growth. Therefore, it is necessary to develop effective methods for analyzing normal soil moisture levels before the harvesting period of crops to boost agricultural output. It helps predict and analyze water cycles, climate crises, water vapour transport, drought, water management, and overflow during rainfall. In particular, the conventional technique for measuring habitat structure has difficulty in understanding variation among habitat categories due to the complex patterns. In agricultural settings, the integration of aerial and satellite imagery techniques has improved the ability to monitor the yields. In this study, an effective attention-based deep learning method is proposed for the prediction of soil moisture and vegetation index analysis. Firstly, the required samples of satellite images are garnered from the benchmark datasets. Consequently, the input images are processed under Attention-based Visual Geometry Group 19 with Deep Support Vector Machine (A-VGG19-DSVM) for predicting the soil moisture and vegetation index level. Lastly, the experimentation of the proposed model is assessed with distinct evaluation metrics. On the contrary, the results are obtained by the proposed method to prove the efficacy.

title: Zero-DiDCE: Dual-Illumination Curve Estimation For Zero-Reference Low-Light Image Enhancement
authors: Yashwitha Reddy Chapala, Praveen Kumar Kalangi, Sriram Bodla, Aishwarya Tota, Nitisha Sharma, Bollipelly Pruthviraj Goud, G. L. Anand Babu, Gudepu Sai Krishna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: The challenge of improving images captured in insufficient light settings continues to be a difficult problem in computer vision, posing challenges in fields like surveillance, autonomous driving, and medical imaging. Many traditional improvement methods have problems with areas that are either underexposed or overexposed, with excessive noise, requiring paired ground-truth data, and other concerns. In this study, we introduce Zero-DiDCE: a reference-free deep curve estimation method leveraging dual illumination for boosting image quality captured in low-illumination environments via unsupervised learning. It expands the scope of Zero DCE by utilizing both the original and its inversion illumination versions to enhance the illumination range of the image. Our algorithms design and train a spatially-extensive pixel-wise formulation of enhancement curves to enhance the compact convolutional neural networks. These curves are applied in an iterative manner to improve exposure and contrast without any referenced images. The resulting image exhibits structure-preserving smoothing, accurately refining visibility in thoroughly dark conditions. The experimental results demonstrate that the method delivers impressive improvements across a wide range of low-light conditions, showcasing its robustness and adaptability for real-time applications.

title: Point Cloud Video Modeling With Progressive Prior Knowledge Guidance and Adaptive Neighboring Aggregation
authors: Jingkun Yan, Hongwei Ge, Mingze Cui, Chunguo Wu, Mingde Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Point cloud video modeling not only has to address the natural irregularity of point clouds, but also the challenge of capturing spatial and temporal representation simultaneously. Current methods attempt to approximate the temporal dimension using several 3-D point cloud frame sequences but struggle in sparser conditions. Accurate point trajectory tracking is crucial for effectively capturing temporal dynamics, as point positions across different frames are often inconsistent, especially during rapid motion or at low frame rates. Conventional point tube operations aggregate motion features over fixed time windows but fail to capture rapidly changing scenes. Implicit tracking techniques are limited by quadratic time complexity, which restricts their practical use. In this article, we propose a native 4-D framework (N4DF) that guides the network to learn spatio-temporal dynamics from a native 4-D perspective. Furthermore, we devise a dynamic point spatio-temporal (DPST) convolution to adaptively select the optimal point-tracking strategy, which constructs local plane regions in anchor frames and propagates them to neighboring frames to evaluate point cross-frame movement distances. To further enhance the global modeling power of N4DF, we develop a dynamic self-tracking re-encoding (DSTR) module that employs point-wise self-attention to search for relevant points across the entire video. Compared with the recent 4-D modeling methods, N4DF demonstrates superior performance on MSR-Action3D and NTU RGB+D for action recognition (+0.7% and +1.2% accuracy, respectively), on HOI4D for action segmentation (+1% accuracy), and on Synthia 4-D and nuScenes-lidarseg for semantic segmentation (+0.49% and +1.7% mIoU, respectively). Our N4DF shows greater robustness at low frame-rate settings due to native 4-D modeling and adaptive tracking, making it suitable for tracking fast-moving objects in future real-time scenarios.

title: Weakly Supervised Lesion Localization Through Classification Based on U-Shaped Backbone
authors: Qiye Chen, Bingtian Qiao, Yiwen Wang, Qifeng Lin
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Although deep learning-based segmentation provides significant contributions in medical imaging, traditional supervised methods still rely heavily on pixel-level annotations, which are both scarce and costly to obtain. To solve this problem, we propose Weakly Supervised U-Shaped Architecture (WSUSA), which integrates a Cascaded Fusion Module (CFM) into a U-shaped backbone to perform image-level lesion classification. Moreover, the outputs from the backbone decoder provide an interpretable analysis. We train WSUSA using image-level labels from the COVID-19 Radiography Database. Experimental results demonstrate that WSUSA balances model performance with interpretability while reducing reliance on pixel-level annotations, offering potential for segmentation-limited scenarios.

title: Real-Time Remaining Useful Life Prediction with LSTM Priority Resampling
authors: Tori Wright, Bulent Soykan, Sean Mondesire
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: With the advancement of digital twins for real-time predictive analytics, there is a growing demand for continual learning systems that can adapt dynamically and be retrained efficiently. In this context, we focus on a deep learning approach using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model to predict Remaining Useful Life (RUL). RUL, defined as the time or operational cycles remaining before a system fails, is central to predictive maintenance and digital twin applications, enabling proactive scheduling of repairs, minimizing downtime, and improving system reliability. However, as these real-time models are retrained, performance degrades due to an imbalance of prioritizing new, incoming data over previously learned knowledge. This presents a challenge in digital twin environments, where historical data often remains relevant for predicting future outcomes. Therefore, it is critical to adopt retraining strategies that are both efficient and mitigate forgetting.This work introduces a novel deep learning LSTM-based retraining framework that uses a priority resampling strategy to enhance forward learning while preserving past knowledge. Rather than focusing solely on mitigating forgetting, we evaluate how the inclusion of strategically selected historical data improves learning metrics such as convergence speed and prediction error. Our experiments on the NASA C-MAPSS jet engine dataset show that this approach achieves faster convergence on new data, yields lower prediction error, and demonstrates better generalization to unseen data. These results confirm that relevance-guided resampling is effective in continual learning settings, particularly for real-time, adaptive digital twin systems that require fast and reliable RUL predictions.

title: Integrating BRP and Aggregator Roles for Imbalance Management and Frequency Regulation using Demand-side Flexibility
authors: Arqum Shahid, Roya Ahmadiahangar, Argo Rosin, Tarmo Korõtko
abstract: Energy balancing costs significantly impact the operational efficiency of Balance Responsible Parties (BRPs), particularly in the context of increasing renewable energy integration and varying weather conditions that amplify forecasting uncertainties and frequency deviations in community-level energy systems. Imbalance settlement mechanisms often penalize deviations, exacerbating BRP operational costs. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a dual-role methodological framework for optimizing community-level flexibility resources when a balance BRP simultaneously acts as an aggregator providing frequency containment reserve for normal operation (FCR-N) services. The optimization is performed by minimizing the net operational costs, balancing day-ahead procurement costs, imbalance penalties, and maximizing FCR-N capacity and activation revenues simultaneously. Three distinct operational scenarios are analyzed: a baseline scenario without flexibility usage, flexibility dedicated solely to imbalance reduction, and flexibility fully committed to the FCR-N market. The analysis demonstrates that leveraging community-level flexibility for reserve services significantly reduces overall economic costs. Specifically, committing the same flexibility solely to FCR-N results in substantial economic benefits, achieving a 4.75% cost reduction compared to the baseline. These savings primarily stemmed from substantial FCR-N capacity and activation payments, despite occasional increases in imbalance volumes due to reserve activations. The research highlights important trade-offs and operational considerations regarding frequency-dependent activations can influence BRP imbalance costs.

title: Spatiotemporal Dynamics of Night-time Light in Sudan during the Ongoing Conflict
authors: Yuanxi Ru, Yingce Wei, Xubing Zhang, Xi Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The armed conflict in Sudan, spanning from April 2023 to the present, has escalated into a severe humanitarian disaster. Night-time light data can serve as a valuable proxy for monitoring the humanitarian conditions in conflict-affected area where ground-based data collection is severely constrained. This study utilized the Black Marble monthly night-time light product (VNP46A3) to analyze the spatiotemporal dynamics of night-time light in Sudan during the conflict period, with particular focus on major conflict hotspots such as Khartoum State and the Darfur region. Additionally, we examined refugee movements from Sudan to Chad by analyzing night-time light changes along the Sudan-Chad border. Furthermore, we employed a two-way fixed effects (TWFE) model to quantify the relationship between night time light change (NLC), conflict frequency, and internally displaced persons (IDPs). The results indicate that night-time light in Sudan exhibited a sharp and persistent decline following the onset of the conflict, with nearly half of the states experiencing a light reduction exceeding 50%. Along the Sudan Chad border, night-time light decreased on the Sudanese side but increased on the Chadian side, suggesting an influx of Sudanese refugees into Chad. The TWFE model demonstrated that NLC has a negative correlation with both conflict frequency and the net outflow of IDPs. These findings can provide the international community with a deeper understanding of the evolving humanitarian situation in Sudan, and highlight the value of night-time light remote sensing in effectively capturing conflict induced socioeconomic disruption and population displacement.

title: Exploring the Potential of Oil Concentration Estimation From Single-Polarization SAR Data
authors: Honglei Zheng, Yunhua Wang, Peng Ren, Weimin Huang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Quantitative estimation of oil concentration in marine oil spills is critical for effective emergency response. Previous studies on oil concentration retrieval have primarily relied on multi-polarization Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery. In this work, we explore the potential of using single-polarization SAR imagery to estimate oil concentration. Oil spills appear as low backscatter areas in SAR images due to two mechanisms: (i) damping of short Bragg waves and (ii) reduction of effective permittivity when the oil spill is sufficiently thick. To explore the feasibility of single-polarization SAR for oil concentration estimation, we assume that the damping effect on short waves is independent of oil concentration. Accordingly, the normalized radar cross section (NRCS) attenuation caused by wave damping can be separated from the total attenuation induced by the oil spill. The remaining NRCS reduction is then attributed to variations in effective permittivity associated with oil concentration. Based on this principle, oil concentration can be estimated from a single-polarization SAR image. The proposed method was applied to the Deepwater Horizon oil spill, and the retrieved concentrations showed good agreement with results from dual-polarized SAR when the oil concentration is sufficiently high (e.g., greater than 0.6 in our experiments). This study demonstrates that single-polarization SAR is applicable not only for oil spill detection but also for rapid estimation of oil concentration in thick, high-concentration marine oil spills.

authors: Yuan-Chun Sun, Guodong Chen, Sam Ziaie Kondori, Mallesham Dasari, Cheng-Hsin Hsu

title: The Principle of Matching Patch Elements in Broadband Antenna Array
authors: Yaroslav Gritsev, Olga Shcherbyna
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper discusses the advantages of using passive antenna arrays in the ground segment of a communications system for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). The focus of this paper was on matching methods for rectangular patch elements in antenna arrays. It was noted that there are no universal methods that would simultaneously ensure antenna array matching over a wide frequency band without changing its polarization characteristics. This paper presents the design of a 4x4 right-handed circular polarization (RHCP) antenna array, comprised of passive director patch elements for the 4.9-6.1 GHz operating frequency range. Thanks to the developed power supply circuit of the radiating elements, a stable VSWR value of less than 1.5 was achieved over the entire operating frequency range. The antenna array gain reaches 19.5 dB at high frequencies with an average half-power beamwidth (HPBW) of 13 degrees.

title: Explainable One-Class Machine Learning for Anomaly Detection in Hydro-Ecological Monitoring
authors: Maksym Palka, Nataliia Dziubanovska, Leonid Bytsyura, Anatoliy Sachenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Water monitoring data are often irregular, incomplete, and lack reliable quality labels, which limits supervised methods. This paper proposes a station-specific, label-free one-class pipeline for multivariate anomaly detection in water-quality monitoring. Isolation Forest is trained separately for each station after completeness-based feature filtering, median imputation, and robust scaling. Detected events are explained using robust deviations, reporting the top contributing indicators for each anomaly. Experiments on 2023–2024 data from river reaches, reservoirs, and an estuary demonstrate data-driven anomaly counts across stations and interpretable indicator-level profiles suitable for expert review and operational validation.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning–Based Test Selection for Improving Web Application Testing Accuracy
authors: Anna Kovalova
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents an approach for improving the accuracy of automated testing of web applications through adaptive test scenario prioritization using reinforcement learning. The proposed model enables an agent to learn from past test execution data and dynamically adjust its prioritization strategy to detect defects earlier under limited testing budgets. An experimental study was conducted on three open-source web applications using real test suites and several baseline selection strategies, including random choice, change-based prioritization, and coverage-based ordering. Quantitative evaluation, based on the proportion of defects detected within a fixed subset of tests, the average rate of early fault identification, and mutation testing performance, demonstrates that the proposed reinforcement learning method achieves significantly higher accuracy and faster defect localization compared to traditional approaches. These improvements were further validated using the Wilcoxon statistical test. The results highlight the potential of reinforcement learning for building adaptive test prioritization systems in modern web applications and for integrating such methods into CI/CD pipelines.

title: Deep Learning towards Intrusion Detection in IoT
authors: Olena Semenova, Andrii Semenov, Olha Voitsekhovska, Andrii Dzhus, Volodymyr Martyniuk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Internet of Things has transformed digital ecosystems by interconnecting a vast number of devices. However, this connectivity also introduces significant security risks, particularly vulnerability to sophisticated cyber threats. This study investigates the application of deep learning methodologies for the development of intelligent Intrusion Detection Systems in IoT environments. Three neural network architectures – Feedforward Neural Networks, Convolutional Neural Networks and Recurrent Neural Networks were implemented and compared in MATLAB to evaluate their effectiveness in detecting abnormal patterns and unauthorized access. Furthermore, a genetic algorithm optimization procedure was applied in MATLAB to enhance a model performance by fine-tuning network parameters. The study also outlines potential directions for future research in securing IoT infrastructures.

title: Enhancing Chinese Listening Proficiency Through an Interactive Learning Approach with Edpuzzle
authors: Mingjuan Wang, Kannika Daungcharone, Krittawaya Thongkoo
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study examines the effects of Edpuzzle, an interactive digital learning tool, on Chinese listening comprehension among Thai junior high school students. A quasi-experimental design with pre-test and post-test measures was employed to compare changes in listening proficiency between an experimental group that received Edpuzzle-based listening instruction and a control group that received traditional listening instruction. Listening proficiency was assessed using test tasks that involved both short and long dialogues, as well as comprehensive listening activities, with a focus on students' understanding of main ideas, details, and inferential meaning. The pre-test results indicated no significant difference between the two groups, suggesting comparable listening proficiency prior to the intervention. In contrast, the post-test results showed that the experimental group significantly outperformed the control group, indicating that the Edpuzzle-based intervention was associated with improved listening comprehension. These findings suggest that integrating interactive digital tools into conventional instruction may enhance student engagement and learning outcomes in Chinese listening. Future research is recommended to explore the longterm effects of such digital interventions and their applicability to other language skills.

title: NTL Detection: Multi-Modal Architecture for Smart Meter Fraud Detection in a Realistic Deployment
authors: Emiliano Acevedo, Martín Schmidt, Sara Silva, Camilo Mariño, Álvaro Gomez, Alicia Fernández, Alexander Martins, Gonzalo Caudullo, Mauricio Berois
publish: 2025 IEEE PES Innovative Smart Grid Technologies Conference - Latin America (ISGT LA)
abstract: Non-Technical Losses (NTLs) in power distribution pose persistent operational and financial challenges for utilities worldwide. This paper presents DeepDAICE, a multimodal deep learning architecture designed to detect NTLs by jointly modeling heterogeneous smart meter data, including low- and high-resolution consumption, alarm sequences, and contextual customer attributes. We compare DeepDAICE with a system based on a classical learning model currently deployed by Uruguay’s state-owned power utility (UTE), using two large-scale real-world datasets. We observe consistent improvements across both global and operational metrics. Additionally, results from field inspections validate the practical value of DeepDAICE, showcasing strong and complementary detection capabilities relative to the existing model. Its modular architecture and robust generalization make DeepDAICE a promising candidate for deployment and integration in real-world utility operations.

title: Design and Implementation of a Hybrid-Powered Automatic Agricultural Produce Oven System
authors: Suwannee Panyot, Wirot Ponglangka, Niwat Nualkan, Kamonlak Chaidee
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This research aimed to develop a Hybrid Automatic Agricultural Dryer using combination of solar and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) energy to enhance drying efficiency and reduce energy costs. The developed system measures <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$192 \times 236 \times 100 ~\text{cm}$</tex> and equipped with a 2,300 kilocalorie (kcal) infrared gas burner and four drying trays sized <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90 \times 90 ~\text{cm}$</tex>. Experimental results from drying 20 kg of lemongrass showed that the hybrid system required only 8 hours with a final moisture content of 16.3 %, using 2 kg of LPG, compared with 10 hours and 4.1 kg of LPG for gas-only drying. The hybrid dryer reduced the drying time by 11.1 % and LPG consumption by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 1. 2 \%}$</tex>, demonstrating its effectiveness and suitability for community-level agricultural processing.

title: Advancing Afghanistan Mineral Stone Classification Using Hybrid CNNs with Attention and Texture Learning
authors: Iqbal Samsoor, Siam Yamsaengsung, Mohammad Shuaib Zarinkhail
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This research developed an automated deeplearning system that classifies Afghan mineral stones from images. We created a novel, high-quality dataset of 5,840 images covering 42 mineral types, collected with formal permission from the Afghan Ministry of Mines and Petroleum. Our model is a hybrid that combines two pretrained backbones (ResNet50 and EfficientNetB3) and is enhanced with dual attention modules (channel and self-attention) plus a dedicated texture feature extractor to emphasize color, structure, and subtle surface textures that are key for distinguishing similarlooking stones. Both backbones were fine-tuned by training only their last two residual blocks to adapt to the dataset while keeping training efficient and reducing overfitting. On the held-out test set, the best model achieved 99.39% accuracy. The proposed system can help make mineral identification more accessible and efficient for the mining sector in Afghanistan and other resource-rich regions.

title: Air Quality Forecasting Using a Long Short-Term Memory Neural Network
authors: Ivan Rudavskyi, Halyna Klym
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Accurate air quality forecasting is critical for environmental regulation and safeguarding public health. This study presents an analytical framework which uses a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network, enhanced with data imputation via the Expectation–Maximisation (EM) algorithm, as well as standardised pre-processing. The EM procedure reconstructs incomplete monitoring records to ensure a consistent and reliable dataset, while standardisation strengthens model robustness and facilitates efficient training. The LSTM architecture is used to identify temporal patterns in pollutant time series, and its forecasting ability is compared with that of several alternative models. Experimental findings suggest that combining imputation, normalization and deep learning techniques significantly enhances the accuracy of air quality forecasts and improves the overall reliability of the model.

title: A Knowledge-Driven Framework for Cross-Border-Logistics Innovation: A Case Study from Southeast Asia
authors: Krittawaya Thongkoo, Sixian Lu, Kannika Daungcharone, Khanita Tumphasuwan
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Cross-border logistics in Southeast Asia continues to face inefficiencies arising from fragmented regulations, limited infrastructure, and heterogeneous information systems. This study proposes a knowledge-driven innovation framework that integrates digital platforms with structured knowledge processes to support cross-border logistics operations. Using a Thailand-based logistics provider as a case study, a web-based logistics platform was developed to connect Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and Transportation Management System (TMS) modules with IoTenabled tracking and multi-channel notifications, thereby enhancing real-time information flow and operational visibility. The platform supports automated reporting, standardized supplier data integration, and real-time stakeholder communication. Based on system prototyping, stakeholder surveys, and performance benchmarking, the evaluation indicates an observed reduction of approximately 15 % in data processing time, reporting accuracy of 98 %, and a 20 % increase in user satisfaction. The findings suggest that embedding knowledge-sharing and reuse mechanisms within digital logistics platforms may support more consistent coordination and organizational learning in cross-border transport contexts. This case provides empirical insight into how logistics providers can align knowledge management with digital innovation to enhance transparency and operational resilience in dynamic regional trade environments.

title: Retraction Notice: Artificial Intelligence based Anxiety Detection in Patients
authors: Likhita Chowdary Koneru, Routhu Ramya Dedeepya, Angadi Lakshmi, G Kranthi Kumar
publish: 2022 Sixth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Wellbeing and health refer to a condition of complete mental, social and physical prosperity that go beyond the absence of sickness or deficiency. When distanced from genuine prosperity, individuals will generally ignore material and mental concerns and illnesses. Depending on the depth of experiences and accidental complications faced by the individual, these ailments have a wide range of effects. Ideally, it makes it impossible for individuals to live normal, merry lives, and without doubt, and eventually leads to deterioration. It can similarly provoke steady mental degradation, obstacle, control, or self-hurt in specific conditions. This research study intends to design and develop an AI-based profound prosperity assist application with a straightforward UI, which can be used to explore and survey mental prosperity characteristics, for instance, pressure levels, following sensations of uneasiness and required treatment using data assessment procedures. Natural Language Processing (NLP) approaches are being used for serving an arrangement of infections, addictions, and various issues. These NLP ways of managing plan are utilized to train an AI-based application, which consolidates a Chatbot with the relational information index to attract clients dynamically and give them the best, speedy, and convincing responses for their inquiries.

title: Retraction Notice: Prediction of Air Pollution by using Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Jayant Kumar Singh, Amit Kumar Goel
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: controlling and defensive the higher air greatness has gotten one in everything about first imperative occasions in different creating and metropolitan districts at the present. The greatness of air is adversely contacting collectible to the different styles of tainting influenced through the transportation, power, powers consumptions, and so forth. In our country population is a big problem as day by day population is increasing, so the rapid increasing in population and economic upswing is leading environment problems in city like air pollution, water pollution etc. In some of air pollution and air pollution is direct impact on human body. As we know that major pollutants are arising from Nitrogen Oxide, Carbon Monoxide & Particulate matter (PM), SO2 etc. Carbon Monoxide is arising due to the deficient Oxidization of propellant like as petroleum, gas, etc. nitrogen oxide (NO) is arising due to the ignition of thermal fuel;Sulphur Dioxide(So2) is major spread in air, So2 is a gas which is present more pollutants in air, it’s affect more in human body. the predominance of air is overstated by multi-dimensional impacts containing spot, time and vague boundaries. title: Retraction Notice: Road Sign Recognition System for Autonomous Vehicle using Raspberry Pi
authors: K. Vinothini, S. Jayanthy
publish: 2019 5th International Conference on Advanced Computing & Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Road sign recognition is one of the important tasks of intelligent transportation systems (ITS). The project aims at implementation of road sign detection and control of an autonomous vehicle using Haar Cascade Classifier algorithm. In this proposed work, the system automatically detects the road signs, controls the vehicle and command certain actions. The system consists of Raspberry Pi 3 processor and web camera which automatically captures the video data and converts them into number of frames which are processed by the proposed algorithm in OpenCV to detect the road sign and control the vehicle. Based on the detected sign, the vehicle is controlled by two DC motors interfaced with Raspberry Pi. The experimental results for Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Minimum Mean Square Error indicate the proposed system gives more accurate results with higher PSNR value compared to Hough Transformation. The performance metrics of the algorithm implemented in ARM processor is much better compared to the results obtained using MATLAB software.

title: Retraction Notice: A Review of Machine Learning Techniques for Breast Cancer Diagnosis in Medical Applications
authors: Praveen Tumuluru, Ch. Prasana Lakshmi, T. Sahaja, R. Prazna
publish: 2019 Third International conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: During their life, among 8% of ladies are determined to have Breast cancer (BC), after Lung Cancer. Breast cancer is the second predominant cause for the increasing fatal rate. Breast cancer is exposed by the transformation of qualities, consistent agony, changes in the size, colour (redness), and the skin surface of the breast. Classification of breast cancer leads the pathologists to discover a specific and target prognostic, by binary classification (normal / abnormal). This work associates five of the most prevalent machine learning methods utilized for breast cancer exposure and analysis, explicitly Support Vector Machine (SVM) for breast cancer exposure and analysis, explicitly S.V.M, k-Nearest neighbours, logistic regression, decision tree, and random forest. The Wisconsin dataset is utilized to estimate and compare the accuracy of five different ML methods with the evaluation metric viz. accuracy. The best performance is obtained by decision tree and random forest with the highest accuracy.

title: Foundation Models for Autonomous Driving Systems: An Initial Roadmap
authors: Xiongfei Wu, Mingfei Cheng, Xiaoning Ren, Qiang Hu, Jianlang Chen, Yuheng Huang, Maxime Cordy, Yao Zhang, Xiaofei Xie, Lei Ma, Yves Le Traon
publish: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: None

title: PhishCrawl and Deeptrace: A Distributed and Explainable Framework for Cyber Threat Detection
authors: Gadha Saji Menon, Jyothika T Manoj, Pranav Krishna S, K Nimmy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Phishing attacks and deepfake media manipulation create serious and growing threats to online security, public trust, and digital integrity. This study focuses on developing a robust architecture to address both threats in a scalable, ethical, and explainable manner. The proposed framework includes two subsystems: PhishCrawl, a distributed phishing URL detection web crawler using Celery for task parallelism and Redis for shared memory, and DeepTrace, a real time deepfake detection system using a quantized Xception model. PhishCrawl employs heuristics derived from URL formats, domain characteristics, and content trends. Ethical testing is guaranteed by using mock phishing URLs. DeepTrace employs Grad-CAM for clarity and pHash for identifying perceptual similarity, implemented through a client-server model utilizing FastAPI. DeepTrace reaches a classification a ccuracy of 91.4 % ont he C eleb-DF v 2 dataset, with an average inference duration of under 200 ms for each frame. The pHash-based matching module achieves a 98 % recall in identifying reused content. PhishCrawl effectively classified phishing patterns in a distributed environment using lightweight rule-based algorithms. Together, these results demonstrate that distributed crawling with explainable deepfake detection offers a practical and reliable path to safeguarding digital ecosystems from modern cyber threats.

title: Length-Sensitive Summarization: a Stratified Approach to Abstractive Text Generation
authors: K.V. Narasimha Reddy, SK. Faheem Ahmed, R. Nandu, K. David Shalem, Bulipe Sankara Babu, Eragamreddy Gouthami
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In the era of information explosion, generating concise and human-like summaries from long-form text has become essential. Traditional extractive techniques often fail to maintain narrative coherence and semantic depth, motivating the need for stronger abstractive approaches. This work presents a dynamic multi-model summarization pipeline that selects the most suitable transformer model—PEGASUS, BART, or T5—based on input characteristics to enable scalable and context-aware text abstraction. The system is trained and evaluated on the BBC News dataset using ROUGE and BERTScore metrics. Experimental results show that the pipeline consistently produces coherent, fluent, and semantically rich summaries across varied article lengths. Overall, this study demonstrates a practical and deployment-ready framework for modern abstractive summarization while highlighting future opportunities in hybrid, efficient, and multilingual summarization systems.

title: Drone-Enabled Lift Zone Surveillance and Hazard Detection
authors: Chandrima Chakrabarti, Bingshati Mondal, Krishnendu Roy, Disha Basu, Rakesh Ghosh, Swapnendu Kolay
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: In construction areas, mining industries, automotive sectors, or warehouses where heavy crane lifting operations are performed, surveillance via drones is best suited for the detection of any type of hazardous situation and taking post-hazard measures based on data received through monitoring drones. This paper presents an integrated system model comprising multiple sensors, a LiDAR sensor, an RGB camera, and a thermal infrared camera, with advanced machine learning algorithms such as CNNs, K-means clustering, and SVMs, mounted on a lightweight drone connected over Wi-Fi to ensure safety protocols in crane lifting areas for monitoring purposes. This surveillance system is designed for the real-time, rapid, all-day, and non-contact acquisition of multi-source data in crane lift zones and their surrounding environments to gather data and transfer it to the nearest control station, which will be analyzed for quick hazard detection and taking the required measures in rapid rescue operations. The challenges faced in the traditional surveillance of crane lift zones are the limitations of high-resolution RGB image detection in complex terrains and adverse weather conditions. To address these challenges, this study developed a multi-sensor drone system model that allows for effective data collection and transfer through Wi-Fi networks and hazard detection using SVMs in crane lift zones that speed up rescue operations by identifying critical areas where emergency help is required. The integration of thermal infrared imaging is particularly valuable because it can penetrate cloud cover and rain, providing reliable information even under difficult weather conditions.

title: Intelligent UAV-Based Precision Agriculture Using Machine Learning
authors: Subhradip Mukherjee, G Petchinathan, Arun Francis G, M Thilagaraj, Gagandeep B
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: In India, precision farming methods have long been the cornerstone of agricultural endeavors. However, it is necessary to incorporate modern technologies for effective disease control and diagnostics, given the growing difficulties presented by plant diseases, particularly in crops. Through the utilizing unmanned aerial vehicles equipped with a camera and a GPS module, this research explores a novel approach to enhance crop health monitoring. Despite the continued widespread use of traditional farming techniques, precision farming with unmanned aerial vehicles presents a potentially useful strategy to mitigate the consequences of plant diseases. Through the use of machine learning techniques and high-resolution data captured by unmanned aerial vehicles, this research aims to create a solid framework for the early diagnosis of crop insects and illnesses. The research produces promising results with a validation accuracy of 93.55% and a training accuracy of 90.25%.

title: Modeling and Simulation of a MOSFET Biosensor for Early Stage Oral Cancer Detection
authors: Abha Dargar, Shashi Kant Dargar, Zayd Abdul Bhari, Shaik Sufiya Khatun, Vijeyandar S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Oral cancer is one of the most prevalent cancers worldwide, causing over 150,000 deaths yearly. The death toll has increased by more than 98 % in 27 years. This dramatic increase indicates the importance of early detection as a necessary precaution. Salivary biomarkers offer a non invasive path for diagnosis because they contain a host of enzymes, hormones and proteins. In this paper, investigations are conducted for the detection of oral cancer using MOSFET biosensors. MOSFET biosensors offer label-free detection, high sensitivity, and low power consumption. To this end, simulations are performed using TCAD Sentaurus, focusing on device geometry, doping, multilayer gate stack and surface charge based biomarkers. The scope of this work is to design and simulate a MOSFET based Bio-FET for early, noninvasive oral cancer detection by evaluating its electrical output to biomarker induced surface charge variations. Preliminary results reveal parameter optimization challenges, highlighting a need for further refinement. An overview of the methods of detection of oral cancer is discussed here. Recent developments in biosensors are summarized. The parameters for MOSFET simulation using TCAD are provided. Finally, future of biosensor based diagnostic application are presented. This work aims to provide a simulation-based framework. The goal is to advance MOSFET biosensors toward mainstream diagnostic applications.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Mental Health Classification: A Comprehensive Study
authors: Sameer Barwal, Shiv K. Sharma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The classification of mental health has become a prominent issue in the areas of digital healthcare and preventive medicine. This research is in line with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, especially SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-being) by supporting the early detection, and SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities) by pointing out the need for lightweight and interpretable models that are suitable for resourcepoor environments. The paper introduces such a wide-ranging comparative study of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques for mental health risk classification. The experiments were carried out on a dataset containing 2,000 individuals with 11 demographic, lifestyle, and psychological characteristics, and eight algorithms were evaluated: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Support Vector Machine, Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, K-Nearest Neighbors, Decision Tree, and Neural Networks. Logistic Regression model triumphed over all models with 81.25 % accuracy, 81.33 % precision, 81.25 % recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$81.11 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex> score. The dominance analysis of the features revealed that stress level (26.76 %), mental health history (14.42 %), and sleep hours (14.27 %) were the most prominent indicators of the risk of mental health. The findings indicate the need for a balance between the predictive performance and the interpretability of the model, showing that simpler models can outperform complex ones based on structured mental health data. This research encourages the use of medical screening tools based on transparent ML in clinical settings and demonstrates the possibility of incorporating AI-driven mental health risk classifiers into electronic healthcare systems for better patient monitoring and availability of psychological support services.

title: ViLT and Customized Classification Block for Remote Sensing Visual Question Answering
authors: Jaswanth Polarowthu, Bala Venkateswarlu Isunuri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Transformer models have been greatly improved recently, which has led to their extensive use in the process of captions for images, responding to questions regarding images, and the processing of both images and words. The current models use extensive computational power, time, and many configurable parameters for handling inquiries about remote working and detecting images. In this paper, we introduced an efficient and rapid visual question answering (VQA) system for satellite images using the Vision-and-Language Transformer framework (ViLT). Compared to the past models of remote sensing VQA, our approach improves the efficiency of the training process while maintaining robust image understanding. Our model utilizes a ViLT backbone consisting of immobilized transformer components. A customized block using dense layers with ReLU6 activation has been developed for categorization and the generation of answers from low-resolution images. This approach makes it easier to adapt to VQA tasks that involve several tunable parameters. Testing of the conventional remote sensing VQA (RSVQA) dataset shows that our system performs well with an overall accuracy of 86.26%. Moreover, this proposed model has lower operational expenses than the alternatives that require comprehensive retraining.

title: IEEE Transactions on Materials for Electron Devices Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Materials for Electron Devices

title: Supervised Contrastive Learning for Uncertainty-Aware Wireless Signal Analysis: A Case Study for Modulation Classification
authors: Han Zhang, Mohammad Farzanullah, Akram Bin Sediq, Ali Afana, Melike Erol-Kantarci
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI), and more specifically deep learning techniques, have demonstrated strong capabilities in processing wireless signals, enabling automatic modulation classification (AMC). However, existing AI-based AMC methods often produce unreliable predictions and lack robustness to out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs, which limits their deployment in real-world scenarios. This study aims to address the gap in simulations to real deployments by fitting predictions to OOD scenarios. We propose an uncertainty-aware AMC framework based on supervised contrastive learning (SupCon). The proposed framework aims to enhance classification reliability, particularly under OOD conditions. In this framework, a ResNet-based representation learning model that consists of a feature extractor and a projection head is first trained using a combination of SupCon loss and cross-entropy (CE) loss to produce class-discriminative embeddings. By decoupling the shared representation learning model from task-specific classifiers, the framework enables a modular and computationally efficient uncertainty estimation strategy, avoiding redundant computation during inference. The learned embeddings are then processed by a set of class-wise binary classifiers that provide both classification and sample-wise uncertainty estimates. A rejection mechanism is incorporated to improve decision reliability by discarding uncertain predictions. We evaluate the proposed framework on the RadioML 2018 dataset. Experimental results show that our approach significantly improves representation quality and classification reliability compared to conventional supervised learning. Specifically, the classification accuracy increases from 63.7% to 93.6% under in-distribution conditions, and from 30.8% to 92.5% under 50% OOD contamination, while maintaining over 85% recall on accepted predictions.

title: Research on Fault Diagnosis Algorithm of Liquid Permeability Measurement Equipment Based on CNN-LSTM
authors: Shuaishuai Teng, Zhejun Pan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
