Evaluated on the Avast-CTU Public CAPE Dataset, our framework consistently outperforms existing methods, highlighting the effectiveness of LLM-based behavioral embeddings and the overall design of BEACON for robust malware classification.

title: Model Reference Control for Grid Forming Inverters
authors: Debatirtha Deb, Ratnesh Kumar
abstract: The growing integration of renewable energy resource-based generation introduces power-electronics-based interfaces between generation and the grid, replacing traditional synchronous generators. Approaches to renewable energy integration can be categorized into grid-forming (GFM) vs. grid-following (GFL) inverters. This paper proposes a novel model-reference control framework for grid-forming inverters, where the control is designed to ensure that the overall system mimics a traditional synchronous generation system, treating it as a reference. A learning algorithm to emulate the operation of a traditionally controlled synchronous generator is proposed. A simplified two-bus test system is used to validate the controller’s grid-forming capability.

title: TTQA-RS- A break-down prompting approach for Multi-hop Table-Text Question Answering with Reasoning and Summarization
authors: Jayetri Bardhan, Bushi Xiao, Daisy Zhe Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Question answering (QA) over tables and text has gained much popularity over the years. Multi-hop table-text QA requires multiple hops between the table and text, making it a challenging QA task. Although several works have attempted to solve the table-text QA task, most involve training the models and requiring labeled data. In this paper, we have proposed a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) based model - "TTQA-RS: A break-down prompting approach for Multi-hop Table-Text Question Answering with Reasoning and Summarization". Our model uses an enhanced retriever for table-text information retrieval and uses augmented knowledge, including table-text summary with decomposed sub-questions with answers for a reasoning-based table-text QA. Using open-source language models, our model outperformed all existing prompting methods for table-text QA tasks on existing table-text QA datasets, such as HybridQA and OTT-QA’s development set. Our experiments demonstrate the potential of prompt-based approaches using open-source LLMs. Additionally, by using LLaMA3-70B-Instruct, our model achieved state-of-the-art performance for prompting-based methods on multi-hop table-text QA.

title: A Novel Method for Neuro Guard – Intelligent Seizure Alert Device
authors: Sujatha Banka, Chava Sunil Kumar, Sri Priya Rathod, Janani Nagarajan, Jessi Elina Sanaga, Advaitha Srigiri
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Seizure attacks chronically affect many lives globally, and their manifestations are frequent and call for urgent assistance. As such, the project’s aim is to develop a wearable seizure attack detection device that constantly monitors a patient’s movement, pulse, and body temperature. The Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32-C3 microcontroller will be used as the core processing unit due to its smaller size and reduced power consumption, built-in Wi-Fi and Bluetooth for real-time alerts, an ADXL345 accelerometer for capturing sudden, unexpected jolting movements, a heart rate sensor that is able to detect bolus beats, and an MLX90614 infrared sensor to track temperature fluctuations. After the seizure is diagnosed, the warning system is initiated, which activates a buzzer for prompt notice. For improved security, the system can connect via Bluetooth or the Internet of Things (IoT) to send instant notifications through mobile phone to the caregivers.

title: Bee Detection and Behavior Classification Using YOLOv5, YOLOv7 and YOLOv8
authors: Jason Elroy Martis, Suhail Ahamed Nisar Shaha, Shreesha, Riyaz Ahmed, Shrisha SK
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Monitoring honeybee behavior is crucial for ecological studies, agricultural productivity, and health evaluation of the colony. Conventional manual observation is timeconsuming and liable to errors that lead to bee stings and colony disappearance. Every year farmers incur losses in apiary due to these bee activities that require continuous maintenance. To avoid these situations and to induce automation, we propose a system based on object detection models, to identify bees and define their behavior types, including foraging (collecting food), defense (protecting the hive), fanning (cooling the hive), and wash boarding (cleaning/maintaining surfaces). Quantitative assessments indicate models like YOLOv5's possessed the highest detection accuracy at mAP@0.5 of 0.849, accompanied by better precision and recall. YOLOv8 also has shown better generalization and convergence of training with fewer oscillations, hence ideal for real-world deployment. While the metrics of YOLOv7 were incomplete, qualitative outcomes indicate competitive performance. Qualitative assessment also indicates issues like behavior class imbalance, subtle inter-class visual cues, and occlusions. This research sets a baseline for the deployment of state of art neural networks and edge-based bee monitoring systems. It also presents avenues for future enhancement with temporal modelling and data augmentation The working model of our prototype can be found https://github.com/RiaAug24/YoloBeeClassification

title: Structure-Adapter: Knowledge Graph Path Soft Prompts for Reliable Legal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Zhuoying Feng, Baiyang Song
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Legal question answering requires both strong evidence recall and faithful multi-hop reasoning, yet vanilla RAG pipelines often struggle to organize cross-article statutory structure and may produce high-risk errors. We propose a lightweight Structure-Adapter that augments a text-retrieval backbone with a parallel knowledge-graph branch: the system retrieves relevant subgraphs and high-confidence multi-hop paths, encodes each path into compact structural and textual representations, and injects the fused representations as continuous "knowledge soft prompts" into the LLM ’ s embedding space at predefined positions in the hard prompt. To keep training efficient, we freeze the LLM backbone and optimize only the path-level adapter and a small projector, enabling low-cost adaptation while preserving the model’s generation ability. We evaluate on a 488-sample benchmark randomly drawn from the LAW-GPT 92k dataset with reference-grounded supervision, using standard text-overlap metrics and a high-risk error rate (Badcase_Rate). Results show that the proposed method achieves the best overall performance (F1 0.5246, ACC 0.4068) and the lowest Badcase_Rate (0.0082), outperforming strong RAG baselines including RQ-RAG.

title: Accurate Classification of Soft Tissue Sarcoma using Hybrid Models
authors: Vineela Madireddy, HariKrishna Bommala
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Soft Tissue Sarcoma is an aggressive malignant tumor with more than 50 histological subtypes early detection of these tumors is also very crucial. Most of these tumors are undetected until it is progressed to a more advanced stage significantly Affecting treatment outcomes. The goal of the research is to utilize both the DL and ML algorithms to analyze metastasis with the help of MRI, X-Rays and CT-scan. This study focuses on classifying the image into viable tumor or nonviable tumor at the initial stages using CNN, LSTM, BiLSTM and compared with proposed model sarcomaNet. A Novel sarcomaNet integrates CNN, attention mechanism and sequential mechanism to enhance diagnostic performance. The proposed model Sarcoma Net achieves classification with accuracy of 87.5% which demonstrates superior performance when compared with traditional models which aids clinicians in early diagnosis.

title: Laboratory Evaluation of Federated, Hierarchical Controls for Distribution Power System Management
authors: Annabelle Pratt, Venkateswara Reddy Motakatla, Ismael Mendoza, Harsha Padullaparti, Jiyu Wang, Andrew Ingram
abstract: The connection of more flexible loads, generation and storage to the distribution power system brings challenges, such as increased variability in net load profiles, and opportunities to system operators. There are opportunities to aggregate flexible loads, generation and storage to provide transmission grid services, but the coordinated actions of these distribution assets being managed by independent, third-party aggregators to support transmission system operations can present challenges to distribution system operations. We developed a federated management architecture and control framework that aims to manage heterogeneous assets to deliver reliable transmission grid services while respecting distribution system constraints. The controls include stochastic day-ahead optimization, model predictive control, and a simple real-time management scheme. We present simulation results obtained from a realistic laboratory test bed of federated controls managing distribution assets within a substation service area to make the substation net power follow the optimal net power determined by the day-ahead optimization.

title: Centralized and Localized Voltage Control Strategies for Modern Distribution Systems with DERs and EVs
authors: Arun Sukumaran Nair, Aravind Ingalalli, Mohd Azrin Mohd Zulkefli, Biswajeet Rout, Sheroze Liaquat, Siddharth Suryanarayanan, Sudipta Chakraborty
abstract: The conservation voltage reduction (CVR) technique manages legacy voltage control devices in power distribution systems to keep network voltages near the lowest acceptable limits. However, these devices operate at a slower timescale compared to the faster dynamics of distributed energy resources (DER) and charging station (CS) loads. Frequent operation of legacy devices can shorten their lifespan, increasing maintenance costs. This paper presents a centralized volt-var controller with CVR, complemented by a local voltage controller for the distribution grid with DERs and CSs to address voltage violations. The impact of various local voltage controllers, such as P-priority and Q-priority, is tested while operating legacy voltage controllers using the CVR technique. DER voltage controllers generate reactive power compensation and/or active power curtailment to restore local nodal voltage within the nominal voltage range (0.95-1.05 p.u.). The proposed approach is verified using the IEEE 33 bus feeder. Results demonstrate a 30% reduction in overall legacy controller operations per day. Furthermore, CVR along with the DER voltage controllers can maintain network voltage near the lower voltage range (0.95 p.u).

title: Hybrid SVM and K-Means Clustering Model for Anomaly Detection in Wireless Sensor Network Environments
authors: Ashitha V Naik, Gayathri V, S. Menaka, J. Mrudula, Amarnath Prabhakaran A, M Sreedhar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Detecting anomalies in wireless sensor networks (WSNs) is essential for ensuring secure and dependable connectivity, particularly in applications related to critical infrastructures and real-time monitoring. Conventional detection techniques frequently have increased false-positive rates, restricted scalability, and challenges in managing unlabelled or partially labelled datasets. This research presents a hybrid machine learning framework that integrates K-Means clustering with Support Vector Machine (SVM) classification to overcome these limitations. The model first employs K-Means to identify behavioural patterns and create structural clusters, which are subsequently enhanced using SVM to classify anomalies with more accuracy. This dual-phase methodology improves both sensitivity and specificity in identifying malicious or abnormal actions. Experiments performed on benchmark WSN datasets demonstrate exceptional performance, attaining 98.4% accuracy, 98.1% precision, 97.9% recall, 98.0% F1 score, and a 99.2% ROC-AUC score. The suggested system demonstrates markedly enhanced classification performance relative to baseline models like Naïve Bayes, KNN, and standalone SVM, especially in high-dimensional and noisy contexts. The suggested approach is both theoretically efficient and practically deployable in real-world wireless sensor network applications, including smart agriculture, healthcare, and industrial control systems. The hybrid design provides a scalable and intelligent solution to a significant difficulty in WSN security.

title: Assessing Ethical Risks of Data-Driven Prediction Models in Healthcare AI
authors: Rachna Rathore, Rajendra Kumar Vairagi, Raghavaiah Bonam, Deepika, Rajesh Kumar Tripathi, Minakshi Poonia
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) to improve predictive modeling of healthcare has increased disease detection, planning of treatment, and risk assessment, but it also raises ethical issues concerning bias, fairness, transparency, and privacy of data. This paper analyses these concerns by comparing a traditional machine learning model with an ethical-enhanced model on a diabetes data. The model that was created with no bias-reducing measures had the accuracy of 75.76%. Comparatively, the ethically enhanced model, which contained the fairness-oriented technique, including the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE), achieved a precision of 7 5. 3 2%. Even though the improved model demonstrated slight decrease in the overall accuracy, it performed greatly improvements in recall among diabetic patients (74% vs. 66%), which minimized the risk of false diagnosis. Analysis of feature importance was done to enhance model transparency. The findings emphasize the need to incorporate fairness-based concepts of healthcare AI to reduce bias and improve ethical reliability and competitive predictive accuracy.

title: Lightweight Neural Architecture Search for On-Device Computer Vision Deployment
authors: Sandeep Gupta, Ankit Kumar Taneja, Nidhi, Khushbu Garg, Anita Mudgal, Ankur Raj
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: A Lightweight, hardware-aware Neural Architecture Search (NAS) framework is presented for automated discovery of compact, high-performance models tailored to on-device vision. The proposed pipeline combines a one-shot weight-sharing supernet with a learned latency predictor and a two-stage search (differentiable supernet training followed by evolutionary Pareto refinement) to jointly optimize accuracy, latency, and model size. Compression readiness is enforced during search by integrating LAMPinspired pruning-robustness estimation and channel-wise distillation (CWD) proxies, producing architectures that retain accuracy after pruning and quantization. Validation was performed on a cultural-heritage damage segmentation benchmark and standard vision datasets (COCO, Pascal VOC) using representative Android device profiles. Compared with a manually designed YOLOv8n-Ghost+LAMP+CWD baseline and other mobile networks (MobileNetV3, EfficientNet-Lite, FBNet, MnasNet), the searched models achieved a superior accuracy-efficiency Pareto front: models under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 ~ M B}$</tex>, sustained real-time operation (>200 FPS) on high-end devices, and up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 3 \% \text{mAP}$</tex> improvement over the compact baseline at comparable model sizes. The framework enables scalable, reproducible on-device deployment of compression-resilient architectures.

title: Quantum Machine Learning based Framework for Green and Sustainable Power Grids
authors: S. Naganandhini, Rabiyathul Bachiriya, M. Sowmiya, M. Parvathi
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The rising global need for renewable energy systems entails the constants call for research in ideal methodologies to enhance energy systems. As the problem of energy distribution, consumption and forecasting forecasting involves a number of inherent and intricate processes, conventional methods fail to combine with the dynamics of present day energy systems. With the help of efficient solutions by Quantum Machine Learning (QML), this paper aims to understand how QML solutions can transform Energy Management. The broad spectrum of QML techniques we consider includes quantum-enhanced optimization algorithms and quantum-assisted data analysis to discuss the use of the approaches in predicting energy demand, managing energy distribution, and enhancing consumption rates. In this work, we validate QML algorithms in simulation studies and consider real-world examples with other machine learning and optimization methods. They imply that, under certain circumstances, QML can produce notably higher performance with respect to the classical ML approaches in tasks of optimization, including the ones addressed in this paper at large datasets and complex, non-linear dependencies. The quantum algorithms demonstrated substantial gains in terms of energy prediction and the optimal use of energy with minimum lag, especially in situations where volatility and uncertainty are high. In addition, the possibility to identify the connections in mass energy consumption data that could not be found by using classical methods is one of the main advantages of QML in the activities of the company.

title: Solving Math Word Problems Using Estimation Verification and Equation Generation
authors: Mitchell Piehl, Dillon Wilson, Ananya Kalita, Jugal Kalita
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) excel at various tasks, including problem-solving and question-answering. However, LLMs often find Math Word Problems (MWPs) challenging because solving them requires a range of reasoning and mathematical abilities with which LLMs seem to struggle. Recent efforts have helped LLMs solve more complex MWPs with improved prompts. This study proposes a novel method that initially prompts an LLM to create equations from a decomposition of the question, followed by using an external symbolic equation solver to produce an answer. To ensure the accuracy of the obtained answer, inspired by an established recommendation of math teachers, the LLM is instructed to solve the MWP a second time, but this time with the objective of estimating the correct answer instead of solving it exactly. The estimation is then compared to the generated answer to verify. If verification fails, an iterative rectification process is employed to ensure the correct answer is eventually found. This approach achieves new state-of-the-art results on datasets used by prior published research on numeric and algebraic MWPs, improving the previous best results by nearly two percent on average. In addition, the approach obtains satisfactory results on trigonometric MWPs, a task not previously attempted to the authors’ best knowledge. This study also introduces two new datasets, SVAMPClean and Trig300, to further advance the testing of LLMs’ reasoning abilities.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>

title: A Multi-User Detection Algorithm for Phased Array Satellite Communication
authors: Yufeng Zhao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Inter-beam multi-user interference is a key factor limiting the performance of phased array satellite tracking telemetry & command (TT&C) communication system. To address this challenge, this paper applies the multi-antenna multi-user SIC detection algorithm to phased array satellite communication. To solve the problem of excessive computational complexity of this algorithm, an equivalent beam-level SIC multi-user detection algorithm is proposed. By demodulating interference signals, followed by spatial mapping and regeneration, the algorithm utilizes beamformed received signals to achieve interference cancellation, thereby significantly reducing computational complexity. Simulation results and practical applications verify the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Street2Air: A Framework for Synthesizing Aerial Vehicle Views from Ground Images
authors: Md Rubel Ahmed, Fazle Rahat, M Shifat Hossain, Sumit Kumar Jha, Rickard Ewetz
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Annotated aerial view images are often missing from fine-grained vehicle type classification datasets. This lack of data limits both the accuracy and robustness of models when applied to top-down views, which are essential for applications such as autonomous drones and aerial surveillance. Models trained only on street-level images often fail to generalize to aerial perspectives, requiring more time and multiple observations to recognize vehicles accurately. In contrast, models trained with both street-level and aerial views can perform more reliably and with faster inference in drone-based systems. However, collecting real aerial data at scale can be costly and logistically challenging. In this paper, we propose AVA (Automated Aerial View Augmentation), a framework for aerial data augmentation via 3D asset generation and contextual scene synthesis. Since standalone 3D vehicle models from 2D images are not directly usable for detection, we embed them in realistic backgrounds to enable learning of both object features and scene context. AVA first constructs 3D vehicle models from street-view images. To ensure data quality, we introduce a realism checker that discards incomplete or distorted assets. We then apply geometric transformations to generate aerial 2D views. The 2D views pass through a text-to-video generator that adds background context, mimicking typical drone imagery. We evaluate our data augmentation approach by fine-tuning several object detection backbones. Notably, the pretrained YOLOv11 model, when fine-tuned with AVA augmented data, achieves a significant mAP@0.5 improvement from 0.06 to 0.51 in classifying previously unseen vehicles from aerial perspectives.

title: An Approximate Method for Assessing Full-End Availability of Communication Networks
authors: Victor Netes
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The international standards IEC 61907 and IEC 62673 as dependability measure for communication networks recommend using end-to-end availability and full-end availability. The former characterizes viewpoint of end users, the latter reflects perspective of network operator or service provider. Full-end availability can be expressed as a weighted sum of end-to-end availabilities for all pairs of corresponding nodes. However, this way of its calculating is extremely time-consuming. The paper suggests an approximate method for assessing full-end network availability. It is applicable for networks where all links have approximately the same and low unavailability and is based on the consideration of cut-sets with a minimum number of elements. The presentation is illustrated with several examples.

title: The Digital Habitus of Generation Z as A Factor in the Transformation of Management Models in the Context of Sustainable Development (ESG) and Lean Manufacturing (LEAN)
authors: Natalya I. Obukhova, Galina P. Platunina, Olga G. Vandina, Polina A. Zholtikova
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The presented research is aimed at conceptualizing and empirically verifying the “digital habitus” of generation $\mathbf{Z}$, as well as analyzing its conceptual compliance with the principles of Lean and ESG (environmental, social and corporate governance). The work substantiates the crisis of traditional hierarchical management models in dealing with this generation and suggests the contours of a hybrid management model. The scientific novelty lies in the operationalization of the concept of habitus in relation to the digital generational cohort and in the development of an integrative model of “digital habitus - hybrid management”. The practical significance is defined in the recommendations on the transformation of managerial and educational practices.

title: TLBDNet: A detection network for tea leaf blight in unmanned aerial vehicle remote sensing images
authors: Gensheng Hu, Xue Lian
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In the complex tea garden environments, accurately detecting diseased tea leaves and promptly implementing control measures are crucial to preventing the further spread of tea plant diseases. Addressing challenges such as significant morphological variations in tea leaf blight spots and high similarity between spots and backgrounds in UAV-acquired images, this paper proposes a tea leaf blight detection network TLBDNet for UAV remote sensing images. This network is based on YOLOv11 and designs a multi-aspect information extraction component (MIEC) to enhance the model's feature discrimination ability for disease spots. An adaptive feature aggregation (AFA) module is constructed to integrate global contextual information and suppress false positives caused by complex backgrounds such as camellias and soil. At the same time, an adaptive threshold focus loss (ATFL) function is introduced as a confidence loss function to accelerate the convergence speed of the model. To verify the effectiveness of the designed network for detecting tea leaf blight in UAV remote sensing images, this paper conducted experiments on a self-built tea leaf blight dataset. The results showed that the detection accuracy of TLBDNet reached 80.3%, with an average precision (mAP) of 76.3%. Compared to existing detection networks, TLBDNet achieves the best detection performance for tea leaf blight in UAV remote sensing images.

title: Data-Driven Intelligent Retrieval for Stochastic and Hydrodynamic Traffic Modeling Using Video Analysis
authors: Ivan A. Kuteynikov, Marina V. Yashina
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven intelligent information retrieval (IIR) approach for modeling heterogeneous traffic flows, combining stochastic cellular automata (TASEP) and hydrodynamic principles with video-based verification. The methodology leverages video data from urban settings, such as Leningradskoye Highway in Moscow, to extract key traffic parameters–density, intensity, and velocity-using virtual detectors and computer vision techniques. Stochastic models, based on the Totally Asymmetric Simple Exclusion Process (TASEP), enable the retrieval of traffic patterns by simulating vehicle interactions on a discrete lattice, while hydrodynamic models capture macroscopic flow dynamics. Real-time video analysis enhances the verification of model parameters, achieving high accuracy in detecting traffic states (e.g., free flow or congestion). Experimental results demonstrate a robust correlation between empirical data and model predictions, with retrieval accuracy improved by 5-10% compared to traditional methods. The approach supports intelligent transportation systems (ITS) by providing reliable data for traffic optimization and safety enhancement.

title: Mathematical Modeling of the Propagation of Decameter Radio Waves in Magnetoactive Cold Plasma Based on the Bicharacteristic System and Spatio-Temporal Geometric Diffraction Theory
authors: Andrew S. Kryukovsky, Dmitry V. Rastyagaev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: An asymptotic approach, alternative to the Fourier method, for description of the radio pulse propagation in ionospheric plasma - the method of space-time geometric theory of diffraction-and the possibility of its numerical implementation are considered. Mathematical modeling of the amplitude structure of a radio pulse propagation in homogeneous ionospheric plasma was carried out. A numerical comparison of nonuniform and uniform asymptotics containing Fresnel functions is performed.

title: Carbon nanotube acoustic wave sensing and information processing system for intelligent perception
authors: Yilu Huang, Xiaoyu Mai, Yijing Chen, Yun Zhu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: This paper proposes a carbon nanotube acoustic wave sensing and information processing system for intelligent sensing. The system converts mechanical vibrations caused by sound waves into electrical signals using a multi-walled carbon nanotube array, achieving high-precision and high-sensitivity acoustic wave detection. The front-end acoustic wave receiving module consists of a high-sensitivity vibrating diaphragm and a waveguide structure. Utilizing the piezoelectric effect and conductivity changes of the carbon nanotube array, it converts weak mechanical vibrations into electrical signals. An integrated signal acquisition and embedded processing module enables real-time signal amplification, filtering, and feature extraction, allowing the system to accurately extract the frequency, amplitude, and phase information of the sound waves. Experimental results show that the carbon nanotube-based acoustic wave sensor can respond stably in the low-to-high frequency range, and its sensitivity is superior to traditional piezoelectric sensors and polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF) material sensors. This device provides a hardware foundation for constructing intelligent acoustic sensing nodes and has significant application prospects in industrial detection, environmental monitoring, and medical diagnosis.

title: A Multimodal Model for Analyzing Publications of Copyrighted Works
authors: S. A. Palibza, A. A. Rodina, D. V. Gadasin, D. V. Rulev
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: Currently, authors have the opportunity to publish their works on a large number of electronic resources. To perform a quick search, each publication is provided with a tag. Based on the tags, publications belong to a specific genre. The correlation process is quite complex and requires a lot of resources. One of the possible classification methods is the multimodal model, which is considered in this paper. This model should take into account the existing ONIX publication classification standards and the automatic document parsing algorithm with GROBID data extraction. The model is based on the principal component method. The paper considers a working example. The developed model can be used not only for working with text data, but also in machine learning and image recognition systems.

title: Toward Unified Moderation of Cyberbullying Across Social Media and Video Games
authors: David Cong, Keyan Guo, Hongxin Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The growing integration of social media and video games in adolescents’ daily lives has heightened the risk of cyberbullying, contributing to severe mental health challenges such as depression and suicidal ideation. Traditional moderation techniques, including manual review and rule-based filters, often fail to capture contextual nuances. Existing machine learning models also face limitations due to their reliance on large labeled datasets and lack of cross-platform adaptability. Our experiments further reveal that models trained on social media content perform poorly on video game data and vice versa, leading to inefficient moderation. To address these challenges, we propose a novel approach that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning for cyberbullying detection across both domains. This method eliminates the need for extensive training data while significantly improving accuracy. It achieves 90.3% and 91.1% accuracy on social media and video game datasets, respectively, offering a scalable and resource-efficient solution for cyberbullying moderation.

title: Advancing Environmental Disaster Classification using Deep Learning Models
authors: Varshitha Dodla, Sai Sreepad Punith Chindukuru, Rajesh Boppana, Srinivasa Rao Vankdoth
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Weather uncertainty is realized in the form of a major threat to regions like agriculture, transportation, and public safety, which makes effective disaster classification systems inevitable. The paper suggests using a deep learning approach in the Environmental Disaster Management Identifier for the classification of weather events using image data. Architectures were tested between CNN, AlexNet, VGG16, GoogleNet, ResNet, AutoEncoders, and a tailored Encoder+ResNet architecture, each exposed to optimization methods like SGD, Adam, and RMSprop. GoogleNet and ResNet kept the validation accuracy at the highest of 96.15%, while VGG16 was very poor at a rate of 30.77%, and AlexNet followed at 69.23%, therefore validating that the accommodating of deeper architectures is beneficial when learning intricate disaster patterns. The hybrid Encoder+ResNet model offers additional improvement to feature reconstruction and classification capability, hence promising towards scalable real-time disaster detection. These results highlight the need for accurate selection of optimizers and architectural inference and demonstrate that such models are viable for deployment in intelligent early warning systems. Through the combination of strong deep learning models and real-time image feeds, this strategy presents promising avenues for enhancing disaster response, risk reduction, and decision-making in dynamic environmental contexts.

title: ADODN: A Dropout-Based Occlusion-Aware Deep Network for Facial Landmark Detection
authors: Muhammad Sadiq, Junhao Wu, Yu Geng, Mohammad Sultan Mahmud, Amar Khelloufi, Hua Zheng, Yunsheng Zhang, Junwei Liang
abstract: This paper introduces a dropout-based occlusion-adaptive deep network (ADODN) for robust facial landmark detection under challenging conditions, including occlusions, extreme poses, and illumination variations. While convolutional neural networks have achieved high accuracy in Facial Landmark Detection (FLD), their performance often degrades under partial occlusions due to overreliance on a few highly discriminative facial regions. ADODN addresses this limitation through three complementary modules: (1) a geometry-aware module that captures spatial relationships and structural priors among facial components; (2) an attentive dropout module that stochastically alternates drop masks and importance maps to encourage balanced feature learning from both dominant and subtle facial cues; and (3) a low-rank learning module that regularizes the regression representation by exploiting inter-feature correlations to recover occlusion-missing information. Unlike deterministic reweighting schemes, the attentive dropout mechanism improves robustness by randomly suppressing prominent responses during training, which mitigates feature over-dependence and promotes holistic structural inference.The resulting framework remains end-to-end and does not require auxiliary classifiers or multi-stage training. Extensive evaluations on challenging benchmarks (300W, COFW) show that ADODN achieves competitive and consistent performance, especially under occlusion-heavy settings. For example, ADODN attains 2.80 NRMSE on the 300W Common set and 5.81 on the Challenging set, improving upon recent baselines including ODN, AODN, and RHT-R. We also report parameter efficiency relative to prior ODN-style designs, supporting efficient training and inference.

title: Toward Functional Testing of Autonomous Ships: A Structured Survey With a Hybrid Virtual-Real Implementation Framework
authors: Jialun Liu, Zhilin Dong, Shijie Li, Zhouhua Peng, Xinjue Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) proposed the concept of a maritime autonomous surface ship (MASS), which could integrate perception, decision-making, and control functions to implement autonomous navigation in complicated environments. It is recognized as the fundamental component of the future new generation of shipping systems. The development of MASS requires the use of specific test tools and use cases for the evaluation of the safety, reliability, and functional performance of its navigation system. At present, the lack of standardized, modular, and serial testing paradigms and methods for MASS impedes the development and improvement of related products and applications. Therefore, it is essential to develop a practical and applicable testing framework. This paper presents a functional analysis of autonomous navigation systems and provides a comprehensive overview of current research status, potential solutions, and future challenges. Moreover, this paper also takes a step towards facilitating the functional testing of autonomous surface vehicles by proposing a hybrid virtual-real framework consisting of scenario generation, virtual simulation, model-scaled physical experiment, validation and evaluation. The research opportunities and future aspects are also addressed. This work may serve as a reference for academic and industrial researchers to investigate new methods and to develop prototype systems for future autonomous surface ships.

title: The future of electric mobility between technology, business acceptance and policy making: lessons learned from the INCIT-EV project
authors: Alfredo Felix Cota, Guido Perboli, Paolo Guglielmi, Antonio Miguel Munoz, Gregorio Fernandez Aznar, Irene Maria Torres Alfonso, Gilney Damm, Hussein Al-Haj Hassan, Juan De Blas Pombo, Pedro Salazar
abstract: INCIT-EV was an European Project financed under the Horizon2020 programme, grant agreement number 875683, that involved over 30 partners, grouped in national consortia (Spain, France, Netherlands, Estonia and Italy) and developed 7 case studies aimed at experimenting with new charging solutions for Electric Vehicles (EV) and business models capable of fostering the adoption of EVs in the European Union (EU). The project successfully ended on 30/6/2024, and the present work is aimed at illustrating the results of the research activities and experimental tests carried out, with an in-depth outlook on the future of EV charging and EV adoption based on the information gathered and the experiences of this project regarding the technology development, the economics of the EV market and the importance of the policy making activities by the governance authorities and technical committees.

title: Continuous-Space Multi-Agent Path Finding via Enhanced Prioritized Search with Ackermann Kinematic Constraints
authors: Tianyuan Zhang, Lin Zhang, Qiyu Cai, Shoukun Wang, Junzheng Wang
abstract: Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) in complex environments remains challenging due to high computational complexity, frequent conflicts, and realistic motion constraints. Most existing methods focus on discrete spaces or idealized omnidirectional models, often neglecting or partially considering nonholonomic constraints, which limits their applicability to real-world robotic systems. This letter proposes a hierarchical continuous-space MAPF framework explicitly designed for Ackermann-steered robots, balancing computational efficiency, global coordination, and motion feasibility. In the path search layer, a spatiotemporal hybrid A* algorithm with an adaptive dynamic weighting factor improves the trade-off between computational cost and path quality, while a homotopy-group clustering mechanism provides structured agent grouping for conflict resolution. In the conflict resolution layer, a partial-order priority reconstruction and flexible priority-based dynamic adjustment strategy effectively reduce search space and conflict density. The trajectory optimization layer integrates a decentralized sequential quadratic programming method to ensure trajectory feasibility and smoothness. Comprehensive experiments, including ablation, comparative, and scalability studies, demonstrate that the proposed method achieves lower runtime, reduced execution cost, and better coordination than existing MAPF approaches, while maintaining strong scalability in high-density environments.

title: A Hybrid Blockchain Approach to Secure and Transparent Casino Betting and Payouts
authors: Jaehyun Kim, Murat Kantarcioglu
abstract: This paper proposes a blockchain-based decentralized casino system that ensures fairness and transparency through smart contract automation. The system is implemented using Solidity and features core functionalities including secure bet submission, on-chain random number generation, and automatic reward distribution. By recording all game outcomes and transactions immutably on the blockchain, the system prevents manipulation and ensures verifiability. Experimental results demonstrate reliable performance, with transaction costs ranging from 34,826 to 45,553 gas and execution times under two seconds. To address reviewer concerns about methodological rigor and local-testbed interpretability, we additionally report measurements over N=11,000 betting operations and summarize controlled-environment gas micro-benchmarks (e.g., placeBet() 148,532 gas and fulfillRandomWords() 121,884 gas) together with controlled-environment end-to-end settlement latency statistics (mean 23.84,ms, 95This approach highlights the potential of blockchain to establish trust in digital gambling environments through autonomous and tamper-resistant mechanisms.

title: Personalized Vehicular Health Diagnosis via Large Language Model
authors: Yushen Chen, Guan Qiang, Ximing Xie, Boyu Wang, Donghong Cai, Muhammad Diyan, Tianlong Liu, Fang Fang
abstract: In the past decade, vehicle on-board diagnostics (OBD) systems have significantly improved by leveraging numerous electronic control units (ECUs) to monitor a wide array of real-time sensor data and report faults. However, traditional OBD systems often confuse drivers with generic warning lights that provide no context on issue severity. Recently, large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated advanced capabilities in knowledge acquisition and human-centered interaction, offering a promising solution for personalizing warning contents within existing OBD systems. In this paper, we propose an LLM-based OBD (LLM-OBD) system, an intelligent agent designed to deliver not only precise diagnostic information but also personalized guidance when a vehicle fault occurs. Moreover, the proposed LLM-OBD system can be used in coverage-challenged scenarios, such as highway, maritime routes and disaster situations, where terrestrial infrastructure is unavailable or unreliable. In these cases, non-terrestrial networks (NTN) can be leveraged to maintain reliable diagnostic capabilities. Specifically, LLM-OBD deploys a lightweight LLM on the vehicle to parse complex sensor data and driver personalization profiles into a concise query with negligible transmission overhead. Upon receiving the query through the satellite links, a responding LLM hosted on a central server generates accurate warning messages tailored to individual drivers, which are then relayed to the vehicle via the NTN connection. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed LLM-OBD system reliably delivers personalized guidance to diverse drivers.

title: Improved Classification Accuracy of Convolutional Neural Networks with Connected Layers for Electroencephalogram Data
authors: Viet Quoc Huynh, Tuan Van Huynh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Objective: Advanced deep-learning techniques have been employed to enhance the field of brain-computer interfaces through the processing of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. Recent studies have shown application of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with manually extracted EEG features to be a promising approach to improve classification accuracy. However, these models require complex input features to generate output decisions. This study aimed to improve EEG recognition by developing novel CNN architectures based on branch CNNs: the step CNN (the base model) and the input- and layer-additive CNNs, which incorporate connected layers. The models exhibit exceptional performance across established WAY-EEG-GAL benchmark datasets. Additionally, the proposed method was evaluated and optimized through EEG dataset experiments, and surpassed other state-of-the-art techniques. Using connected layers in the models increased the effectiveness in recognizing EEG signals during grab-and-lift classification.

title: LUNA: A Low-SNR Acoustic Localization and Orientation Framework for UAVs
authors: Bo Yin, Guangyao Liu, Weimeng Cui, Xinwei Gao, Peixuan Hu, Naizheng Jia, Zhi Wang
abstract: Accurate indoor localization and stable attitude estimation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are essential for autonomous navigation, obstacle avoidance, and mission execution. However, existing indoor localization solutions, such as Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR), vision-based systems, and Ultra-Wideband (UWB) technologies, may fail in certain indoor scenarios. In this context, as one implementation within the indoor localization framework, acoustic localization has attracted increasing attention due to its low cost, high accuracy, and insensitivity to ambient lighting conditions. This paper presents an acoustic localization system named LUNA, Low-SNR UAV Localization via Acoustics, which achieves accurate indoor position and orientation estimation using commercial loudspeakers and an eight-element circular microphone array. Loudspeakers deployed in the environment emit near-ultrasonic signals, which are received by the UAV-mounted microphone array. Under strong UAV self-noise conditions, a reduced angular-spectrum method is applied to perform direction-of-arrival (DoA) estimation, and position and orientation are jointly optimized by minimizing an angle-of-arrival (AoA) related objective function. Experiments conducted in real indoor environments demonstrate that LUNA achieves a mean position error of 0.089 m, representing an improvement of approximately 30% over commercial UWB systems and 80% over traditional time-difference-of-arrival (TDoA) methods. The mean orientation error is 0.56°, demonstrating the system’s high precision, robustness, and strong potential for real-world indoor UAV navigation applications.

title: UAV-Assisted Shadow Removal Framework for Accurate Pavement Crack Segmentation under Complex Illumination Conditions
authors: Yu Tian, Bin Lv, Yingchao Zhang, Jianzhu Wang, Ziyi Zhang, Benhao Xie, Jianqing Wu
abstract: Timely and accurate detection of pavement cracks is crucial for ensuring road safety and prolonging service life. However, real-world pavement cracks often contain complex shadow occlusions from trees and vehicles, reducing detection accuracy. While existing methods perform well on standardized datasets, they frequently neglect shadow interference, limiting their effectiveness in practical applications. To overcome this challenge, this study proposes a novel UAV-assisted dual-stage shadow crack segmentation framework named DSCSNet. It initially employs a shadow removal module to preprocess images, effectively mitigating the impact of environmental factors such as shadows and lighting variations. Subsequently, a crack detection module performs pixel-level classification to accurately distinguish cracks from background. Additionally, this study also introduces a dedicated crack dataset that incorporates shadow occlusions to improve model generalization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DSCSNet achieves superior robustness and accuracy under shadow interference, outperforming state-of-the-art methods and exhibiting strong potential for real-world pavement inspection applications.

title: Stabilization of G-Stochastic Complex Networks via Asynchronous Hierarchical-Triggered Intermittent Control
authors: Junqing Ma, Junhao Hu, Jingwen Xue, Sen Li, Wenxue Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: For providing a more accurate description of probabilistic and model uncertainties, this paper studies the stabilization of G-stochastic complex networks (SCNs-G), where the normality assumption on noise is removed by accounting for non-Gaussian G-noise. Unlike prior intermittent event-triggered control (IE-TC), a novel asynchronous hierarchical-triggered intermittent controller (AHTIC) is designed, where the asynchronous self-triggered law determines the switching between intermittent control (IC) intervals and rest intervals, while the asynchronous dynamic event-triggered mechanism (E-TM) governs the sampling rule within the control intervals. The hierarchical-triggered feature enables the proposed AHTIC strategy to respond adaptively to unanticipated events while improving energy efficiency. Note that the sublinear expectation framework that the SCNs-G stems from, results in the existing IE-TC stabilization results inapplicable. Subsequently, a novel G-Lyapunov functional with assisting functions is constructed, and a new analytical technique is introduced to tackle the challenges arising from sublinear expectations. Specifically, the stability analysis relies on the estimation of sublinear expectations for several integrals relating to sampling errors, dynamic terms, and system states, which in turn enables the derivation of less conservative stability conditions. By means of Lyapunov functional method, and G-stochastic analysis techniques, two types of exponential stabilization criteria, including mean-square and quasi-sure exponential stability, for the control system are developed. Finally, the effectiveness of theoretical results is validated by numerical simulations on autonomous land vehicle networks.

title: Honeypot-Driven Proactive Detection Network for Attacks in Smart Grids
authors: Yi-Xuan Chen, Xi-Ming Sun, Xue-Fang Wang, Tianju Sui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper proposes a honeypot-driven active detection framework to address the growing cyber threats targeting smart grids. To address the limitations of existing detection methods and the lack of proactive defense capabilities, the framework integrates interpretable decision mechanisms with an adaptive modular architecture, through the dynamic weighting of the gated aggregation mechanism. It incorporates data preprocessing, honeypot interaction, dual-path detection, and decision fusion to more effectively capture diverse attack behaviors and strengthen overall detection reliability. The framework integrates a GBDT-based anomaly scorer with a dedicated Denial-of-Service (DoS) detector, and combines multidimensional evidence using a gated aggregation mechanism. Moreover, theoretical analyses establish a lower bound on feature distinguishability, prove model convergence, and demonstrate effective false-alarm suppression, providing formal guarantees for feasibility. Finally, experiments on real-world smart grid datasets show that the proposed framework accurately detects DoS, replay, and integrity attacks while maintaining strong overall performance. The results confirm its effectiveness as a theoretically grounded and proactive defense solution for smart grid security.

title: Hierarchical Gradient Preserved Network for High Resolution Hyperspectral Fusion Imaging_supp1-3680385.pdf
authors: Meiling Wang
abstract: High resolution hyperspectral fusion imaging (HRHFI) is an important topic in the remote sensing related tasks, which integrates advantages of hyperspectral images (HSIs) and multipectral images (MSIs) to generate the high resolution HSIs (HR-HSIs). Traditional methods fail to maintain multi-scale change information and directional information of HSIs and MSIs during the HRHFI process, limiting the performance. To solve this challenge, a novel hierarchical gradient preserved network (HGPN) is proposed for HRHFI. Concretely, we begin by establishing the foundational observation model, which serves to make an initial estimate of HR-HSI so that the basic observation loss is thus constructed. Subsequently, we integrate both multi-scale spectral gradient preservation loss and multi-scale spatial gradient preservation loss into the basic observation loss, which results in the overall loss function of the proposed HGPN method. Further, an optimization strategy based on alternating directions is devised to solve the overall loss and derive the corresponding solutions, which finalizes the imaging fusion mapping. Different from traditional approaches, the proposed HGPN method can achieve hierarchical fusion, which retains multi-scale gradient information of input HSI/MSI for HRHFI. Extensive experiments have been implemented to verify that the proposed HGPN method achieves more competitive performance than several state-of-the-art approaches in the HRHFI task.

title: Hybrid GNN-Centric Architectures for AI-Native 6G Wireless Networks: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Mostafa Rahmani Ghourtani, Sajedeh Norouzi, Jinxuan Chen, Hamed Ahmadi, Torsten Braun, Kaushik Chowdhury, Alister Burr
abstract: The growing complexity, scale, and heterogeneity of 6G wireless systems call for a shift toward AI-native architectures that are not only data-driven but also topology-aware, adaptive, and distributed. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), with their native support for graph-structured data, are well-suited for modeling the irregular and dynamic relationships inherent in wireless communication systems. However, standalone GNNs may be insufficient to address key 6G challenges such as continual learning, data scarcity, and dynamic adaptation. This survey, therefore, explores the emerging synergy between GNNs and complementary AI paradigms, including deep reinforcement learning (DRL), federated learning (FL), meta-learning, generative models, mixture-of-experts (MoE), and world models, enabling hybrid AI–GNN architectures for intelligent control, predictive adaptation, and scalable optimization across the wireless stack. We systematically review how these GNN-centric AI models can support intelligent functionality in next-generation network architectures such as O-RAN, as well as core 6G domains, including edge computing for wireless systems, advanced MIMO, traffic prediction, and digital twins. The survey also highlights key challenges in hybrid AI–GNN adoption, particularly scalability, generalization across dynamic topologies, interpretability, and symbolic reasoning, and discusses emerging strategies such as graph causality learning, dynamic GNNs, and neurosymbolic integration to address them. By consolidating recent advances and outlining open research directions, this work positions hybrid AI–GNN architectures as a promising approach toward enabling intelligent, energy-efficient, and context-aware wireless systems for 6G and beyond.

title: Algorithm Design and Regret Analysis for Aggregative Optimization in Open Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Yuxuan Liu, Maojiao Ye, Lei Ding, Qing-Long Han
abstract: This paper studies the problem of aggregative optimization in open multi-agent systems (OMAS), where agents are allowed to join and leave the system in a free manner during the decision-making process. A multi-aggregator communication mechanism is proposed to facilitate information exchange among agents, in which the aggregators are responsible for collecting information from agents and exchanging them with neighboring aggregators. Based on the multi-aggregator communication mechanism, a novel semi-distributed algorithm is designed. Dynamic regret is taken as a metric to evaluate the performance of the proposed algorithm. It is analytically shown that the dynamic regret can be bounded by the sum of agents' individual regrets, each of which grows sub-linearly with its active period length under locally diminishing step-sizes and a slowly changing environment. Moreover, to quantify the impact of agents' joining and leaving on algorithm performance, the regret is analyzed in the case of agent replacement, wherein some of the agents are replaced by new ones though the number of agents remains constant over time. Additionally, the algorithm is simplified in some special cases, resulting in a tighter regret bound. Finally, two numerical examples on target surrounding problems and price based energy management are given to verify the effectiveness of the proposed methods.

title: Retraction Notice: A Reflection of Driver Drowsiness Detection Techniques
authors: Rehna Baby Joseph, R. Satheesh Kumar, Chidambaranathan, M.B Lakshmi

authors: Ayyavaraiah Monelli, Shoban Babu Sriramoju

title: Retraction Notice: Advances in Vision based Lane Detection Algorithm Based on Reliable Lane Markings
authors: P. Priyadharshini, Pulugurtha Niketha, K. Saantha Lakshmi, S. Sharmila, R. Divya
publish: 2019 5th International Conference on Advanced Computing & Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: This paper advances in a vision based driver assistance system for effective lane detection Technique. First method involves image processing technique initially to convert the Red, green and blue (RGB) into grayscale images takes place by removing the effects of shades and immersion data of the images by maintaining the brightness of the images. Then to remove the sound available in the images and the obtained video is allowed to pass through the Gaussian filter in-order to remove sound from the images. The edges recognition is an important function to identify and detect the sharp break in an image by canny edge detection algorithm. Then Hough transform technique is applied to obtain the image pixels in the form of long straight line in the Hough space. So we can detect the lane if the vehicle tends to deviate from the lane in a complex situation and take corrective measures to change the lane.

title: Retraction Notice: Implication Centered Learning Mechanism for Exploring Analysis of Variance by Means of Linear Regression in Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Akey Sungheetha, Rajesh Sharma R., Jemal Nuradis

abstract: The resolution of this learning is to explore the client-provision supplier rapport in the insurance production by means of artificial neural networks and linear regression. Data Analysis visualization is suggested to alleviate calculation difficulties in terms of knowledge discovery. Also, a category of currency data is presented to addresses the practical application by combining F ratio and p value to compute currency data. This shows that N way ANOVA produce good performance in the case of computing the currency data set and computes faster than the evolution strategy for the computing system.

title: BFX: An Interpretable Statistical Tool for Anomaly-Oriented Examination of BGP Features
authors: Marian Kyryk, Stanislav Maruniak, Leonid Uryvsky
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Research on Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) anomalies frequently introduces new update-derived features, yet demonstrating their behavior around incidents often relies on ad hoc scripts that hinder comparison. In this study we propose BGP Feature Examiner (BFX), an interpretable statistical tool that examines BGP feature behavior over time spans containing an anomaly using precomputed snapshots. It provides raw and normalized time-series views and concise, test-based summaries of variability, distributional change, separability, and change-point evidence, presented with compact charts. The design goal is a unified, pre-model assessment step that helps authors and operators demonstrate and compare feature behavior consistently before training detection models, while allowing additional measures to be added when needed.
