title: Improving the Dynamics of a Lifting Mechanism Based on Fuzzy Control Approaches
authors: Yaroslav Paranchuk, Roman Paranchuk, Oleksiy Kuznyetsov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The feasibility of using artificial intelligence approaches to improve the dynamics of lifting mechanisms of various production and technological processes has been substantiated. A fuzzy speed controller for the control system of the lifting mechanism of an excavator bucket has been designed. A simulation model of the electromechanical system of the lifting mechanism of an excavator bucket has been designed in Matlab/Simulink. A set of simulation studies of the dynamics of the bucket movement in different operating modes of the lifting mechanism and various setting and disturbances has been carried out. The study has been carried out using the proposed fuzzy speed controller in comparison with a traditional precise proportional controller in the structure of the automatic control system for this mechanism. A comparative analysis of the obtained results demonstrated a decrease in the amplitude of speed fluctuations and dynamic torque in different operating modes of the lifting mechanism when using a fuzzy controller.

title: Methodology for Evaluating the Occurrence, Multiplication, and Spread of Errors in Artificial Intelligence Models
authors: Yuriy Danyk, Valery Shestakov, Roman Shyrshov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents the results of a study regarding the problem of error occurrence in artificial intelligence (AI) models, which leads to the generation of incorrect, false, non-existent, or contradictory information. The research proceeds from the hypothesis that errors are intrinsic to the nature of AI model training. The authors analyze typical scenarios and models through which errors ensue and spread. Analogies are drawn: the multiplication and scaling of errors relative to the number of AI interactions is compared to Moore's Law regarding the doubling of transistors in integrated circuits over certain time intervals, while their dissemination is compared to the spread of a virus through a population. Methodologies for evaluating the rate of error accumulation and scaling in AI models are formalized using the Lyapunov exponent. To ensure the safe deployment of AI systems in critical infrastructure, the authors have developed a comprehensive methodology for evaluating the occurrence, multiplication, and spread of errors in AI models.

title: Full-Scale 3D Models for Training in Rhinoscopic Interventions
authors: Andrii Sokoltsov, Nataliia Shushliapina, Oleg Avrunin, Yana Nosova, Oleksandr Avrunin, Ihor Kandaurov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper presents the development and implementation of full-scale 3D-printed models for training in rhinoscopic and endoscopic sinus surgery. Based on patient-specific CT data, anatomically accurate nasal cavity models were created using segmentation and additive manufacturing. These models provide realistic simulation of surgical procedures, enhancing spatial orientation and technical skills without patient risk. The study highlights the advantages of 3D printing in surgical education, including reproducibility, safety, and cost efficiency, as well as the potential for remote learning. Experimental results confirm the high educational value and applicability of 3D models for training otorhinolaryngologists and improving clinical outcomes.

title: The Impact of Stress on Cognitive Performance in University Students: EEG-Based Study
authors: Hnin Ei Hlaing, Wanus Srimaharaj
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study explores the correlation between academic stress and cognitive performance among university students during exams using the publicly available EEG (electroencephalogram) data. The dataset includes recordings of 26 University Students (19 females, 7 males; mean age <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.23 \pm$</tex> 1.14). EEG signals were analyzed from Alpha, Beta, and Theta frequency bands, while cognitive performance is determined using Theta/Alpha Ratio (TAR) and Theta/Beta Ratio (TBR). Stress levels were categorized as No, Low, Moderate, and High based on perceived stress scores. Spearman's rank correlation was applied to assess associations between stress and EEG-derived cognitive performance of students. The results indicated weak negative correlations between stress and both TAR (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\rho=-0.063, p=0.760$</tex>) and TBR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\rho=-0.078, p=0.705)$</tex>. Students with low stress levels show stable TAR and TBR values, which indicate consistent cognitive performance. In contrast, those with moderate stress showed a tendency toward the higher mean TAR and TBR values, suggesting optimal engagement and mental effort. University students under high stress and no stress demonstrated a significantly wider range of values and large in EEG patterns, which reflected increased brain activity variability and cognitive strain. The results demonstrate that stress primarily influences cognitive performance through non-linear processes, leading to destabilized brainwave patterns and affecting cognitive performance. The results demonstrate the importance of monitoring academic stress and related cognitive factors to support and maintain optimal learning performance.

title: Comparing Independent and Centralized Multi-Agent Learning for District Energy Systems
authors: Dmytro Voitekh, Anatolii Tymoshenko, Volodymyr Pavlenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) offers promising coordination strategies for district-scale energy management, but explicit coordination methods like Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MADDPG) come with significant computational overhead. Prior work has primarily evaluated small-scale deployments (9-17 buildings), leaving the scalability-performance tradeoff uncharacterized at realistic district scales. We address this gap by comparing Independent Learners using Soft Actor-Critic (IL-SAC) against centralized MADDPG across four scales (10, 20, 50, 100 buildings) using CityLearn's Texas Travis County dataset. We find that independent learners capture 85-90% of coordination performance while requiring only 1/6 the training time. At 10 buildings, IL-SAC achieves 18.3% peak demand reduction versus MADDPG's 21.1%. As districts scale to 100 buildings, this performance gap narrows to under 2 percentage points while computational costs increase 6-fold, making independent learning the practical choice for large-scale deployments.

title: SPI Enabled 64x8 SRAM Memory Design for Biomedical Applications
authors: Shivangi Mishra, M Chandu, Santosh Kumar Vishvakarma
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this paper, a Static Random Access Memory (SRAM) architecture with a 512-bit RAM is implemented, and verified together with a Serial Peripheral Interface (SPI) protocol. The design was realised using Cadence Virtuoso EDA tools of schematic capture, simulation, layout validation and carried out using 180 nm CMOS technology. The improved architecture employs a popular industry standard of serial communication, the SPI to coordinate memory access, such as read, write and control, as opposed to standard parallel SRAM modules. The architecture is a compact and energy-efficient memory system as SPI reduces the amount of pins significantly, interconnect complexity, and overall power consumption of 1.34 mW with a power-delay product (PDP) of 24.1 pJ. The design has been specifically optimized to suit the requirements of low-power applications with moderate access rates with low energy usage. Specifically, such a memory design can be applied to IoTbased environmental sensors, wearable computers, and batteryoperated embedded systems, in which energy efficiency is more important than high-speed access to data. Functional verification establishes the stability of the SRAM to the constraints of the SPI protocol, enabling the successful completion of all memory operations without any failure in the conditions of varying process variations in the 180nm CMOS. SPI-based interfacing of the microcontroller (MCU) not only eases microcontroller interfacing but also enables scalability to memory sizes that are larger, is a solid foundation for future developments of energyconscious battery-operated applications.

title: Noise Reduction in High Slope Deformation Data Based on CEEMD-PCA
authors: Hua Xia, Juan Wang, Qingda Duan
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: The stability of high slopes is critical to engineering safety, and the accuracy of deformation data is key to assessing this stability. However, deformation data from actual high slope monitoring is often contaminated by noise, compromising data quality and the reliability of analysis results. To address this issue, this paper proposes a noise reduction method for high slope deformation data by combining Complementary Ensemble Empirical Mode Decomposition (CEEMD) and Principal Component Analysis (PCA). By applying CEEMD decomposition to the deformation data, multiple intrinsic mode function (IMF) components are obtained. These IMF components are then subjected to PCA processing to extract principal components, thereby achieving data denoising. Comparative analysis with wavelet denoising methods using different thresholds demonstrates the reliability and significant advantages of this model.

title: Adaptive Sigmoid-Parameterized Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Cold-Start Recommendation
authors: Tanveer Kaur, Naina Yadav
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Personalized recommendation systems require working with a wide range of users from diverse backgrounds and interaction histories. Sparse interaction histories of users require different inference methods than those of users with dense interaction histories. In this paper, we propose an Adaptive Sigmoid Parameterized Retrieval-Augmented Generation (ASR-RAG). This integrated framework dynamically balances three information streams: graph-centric collaborative filtering (LightGCN), semantic retrieval (Sentence-BERT), and genrespecific evidential constraints. The main part of this methodology is an adjustable sigmoid function that adjusts the weight of collaborative and semantic messages based on the user's maturity level. The MovieLens dataset (26.5 million ratings, 32,028 items, 265,072 users) demonstrates that it is effective: users with dense histories achieve an NDCG@10 of 0.4206, representing a 6.51 % improvement over collaborative filtering, with a hit rate of 0.7300. Users with sparse histories achieve an NDCG@10 of 0.0614, representing a 2.0 % improvement over semantic baselines. ASR-RAG eliminates algorithmic bifurcation by employing methodical and adaptive signal aggregation.

title: AI-Assisted IoT Smart Battery Management System with Peltier-Based Thermal Control
authors: Harsh Kumar, Savita Nema, Punjan Dohare
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper introduces a five-layer Smart Battery Management System (SBMS) that is AI-supported and IoTenabled, with Peltier-based active cooling, cloud-processing of data, and real-time fault analysis to control lithium-ion battery stacks commonly used in electric vehicles and modern electronics. The conventional Battery Management System (BMS) is largely based on reactive control approaches, which highly suffer from delays and ineffectiveness during fast charging operations or sudden temperature disturbances in the battery stack. The proposed SBMS overcomes these issues by leveraging sophisticated control, data analysis, and monitoring functions. The critical elements are learning-assisted temporal modeling with behavior inspired from LSTM for supporting robust estimation of State of Charge (SOC) and health-related parameters, and the anomaly detection module with decision logic inspired from Random Forest for identifying abnormalities in battery behavior. The IoT platform integrated within the system enables continuous monitoring through an MQTT communication system. Simulation results obtained using MATLAB/Simulink show temperature stability and reliability of the battery system. The results show that the Peltier module helps to keep the battery temperature between a window at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5 \&} \boldsymbol{3} \mathbf{5}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}} \mathbf{C}$</tex>, and also helps to overcome thermal risks compared to the conventional fan cooling system.

title: Efficient Ensemble Machine Learning Framework for Early Prediction of Thyroid Cancer Using Clinical Data
authors: Sandeep Kumar Hegde, Rajalaxmi Hegde, Thangavel Murugan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Thyroid cancer is considered as one of the global health burden that occur due to the abnormal growth of cells in the thyroid gland. One of the remedies to improve survival rates and provide successful treatment is to diagnose the disease at an early stage. The traditional diagnostic approach depends on the clinical findings and imaging-based techniques. Hence, it is recommended to adopt a data-driven approach by incorporating Machine Learning (ML) for the early prediction of diseases. According to the literature findings, machine learning applied to predict thyroid cancer using clinical data has demonstrated high precision in predicting the disease and has also improved personalized treatments. The XGBoost is an ML model that can handle complex features in the dataset by analyzing the feature interaction, and LightGBM will work efficiently on an imbalanced dataset. In the proposed research work, an ensemble-based machine learning framework has been implemented by combining the Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) and Light Gradient Boosting (LightGBM). The main objective of the proposed work is to predict thyroid cancer with higher accuracy by applying an ensemble-based machine learning approach. The proposed work also aims to improve the model bias and generalization by utilizing the capabilities of two powerful ensemble algorithms. The experimental results illustrated that the proposed ensemble framework achieved greater accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC score compared to traditional ML algorithms. Hence, the proposed ensemble framework can be effectively used to predict thyroid cancer disease early.

title: A GAN-Driven Framework for High-Quality Image Restoration and Enhancement
authors: Jayapal Lande, Ganga Singh Chauhan, Vishal Verma, Rakesh Kumar, Somya Shukla, Rachna Rathore
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Superior images have become essential in a number of various industries such as entertainment, surveillance, medical diagnosis and satellite surveillance. Despite such increase of demand, the hardware and environment constraints make most pictures to be ruined with low-resolution, motion blur, noise, and compression artifacts. Despite the advances of the deep learning, particularly the Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) that have brought a considerable progress to the image super-resolution process, most of the existing algorithms are concerned with the structural accuracy, without paying much attention to the overall attractiveness of the image. To overcome such a weakness, the following paper introduces a novel approach known as Neural Aesthetic Super-Resolution GAN (NASR-GAN), which could be employed to make the perception and art quality clearer. The structure created is 3 stage in design. First of all, Restoration GAN (R-GAN) eliminates noise and restores damaged information. Second, it has a Detail Enhancer GAN (D-GAN) that makes textures and edges more detailed and sharpens fine-grained features. Finally, it has an Aesthetic Super-Resolution GAN (A-GAN), which enhances the image globally. In addition, an Aesthetic Loss Function is new and is added to the model to ensure that it drives the output to create not only sharp or realistic outputs but also aesthetically pleasing. NASR-GAN, according to the findings of experimental measurements, outperforms the rest of traditional approaches to super-resolution in producing more natural, clear, and aesthetically-polished images. The proposed method can find much use in areas such as photographic enhancement, restoration of the digital artworks, satellite images, and medical imaging as well as can be considered as a good direction to aesthetically driven image super-resolution research.

title: Design and Implementation of an Artificial Intelligence—Based System for Stock Market Prediction and Investment Insights
authors: Priyanka Kaushik, Jigar Soni, Bhawna, Neeraj Varshney, Anshika Tyagi, Krishan Kumar Garg
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Stock market forecasting is a complicated yet important task to investors and other financial experts. Traditional methods of prediction do not typically attempt to describe the complex and non-linear phenomena in financial market data. This paper presents an investment insight and stock market prediction framework, based on machine learning, specifically the Support Vector Machine (SVM), to increase the precision of stock prices prediction. SVM is chosen due to its high ability to deal with high-dimensional data and its effectiveness in regression and classification. The suggested system examines the past data in the stock market and incorporates technical indicators and macroeconomic variables in training the SVM model. The evaluation of the performance is carried out with the help of real-world financial data and the results show that SVM-based model has a higher predictive accuracy than the traditional forecasting models. In addition, the system provides useful investment information by analyzing the market trends and classifying stocks based on their level of risk and returns. This study contributes to the literature on financial technology by helping investors make smarter decisions using AI-based predictive analytics.

title: Analysis and Control of Multi-Input Zeta Converter fed from Renewable Sources for Grid-Tied Inverters
authors: Sayantan Chowdhury, Sujoy Bhowmik, Arkendu Mitra, Amritesh Kumar, Chayan Bhattacharjee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Sustainable Technology in Energy and Power Systems (STEPCon)
abstract: Now-a-days, the world is shifting towards the use of emission-free power sources. The installation of Photovoltaics (PVs) has become a necessity in order to utilize green power as well as to mitigate the increasing power demand at cheaper costs. However, the power available from PVs is primarily influenced by weather conditions, results in a major challenge in ensuring reliable power delivery.To overcome this issue, this work proposes a multi-input DC-DC converter that uses PV as the primary source of power and a battery for back-up purposes. The back-up power is activated when the power available from PVs fails to meet the desired power demand. The proposed topology is based on DC-DC Zeta converter comprising of an additional power source. The work focuses on the analysis of the different modes of operation of the proposed Multi-Input Zeta Converter (MIZC). A control strategy emphasizes harnessing power from both sources at the same time to meet a maximum load power demand of 2.5 kW at 400 V accordingly. Various results obtained from the simulations are depicted to validate the proposed work.

title: Event-Driven IoT Node: A Low-Power Asynchronous Microcontroller Architecture
authors: Amrutha, Kolluru Taraka Rama Shriya, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The Event-Driven IoT(Internet of Things) Node presented in this project was created for ultra-low power sensing applications which uses asynchronous microcontroller concept. In general traditional clock-driven microcontrollers run continuously which results in wastage of energy so, to over come this drawback our proposed system operates on an event-driven concept, where the node stays in sleep state and only activates when triggered by major sensor events. Software based implementation is done using python for our system. In the proposed system Temperature, Humidity, and soil moisture values are monitored by the node, which only transmits data under unusual or threshold-crossing circumstances, this results in significant energy saving due to this selective activation which cuts downs significantly cuts down on unnecessary processing and transmission. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively extends system longevity and power efficiency, offering a scalable and sustainable solution for IoT based smart agriculture and other remoting applications.

title: IoT based Automated Irrigation System for Aquaponics and Land Farming
authors: M. Kavitha, C. H. Hussaian Basha, B. Janani, B.S. Gopika, S. Sivamani, S. Senthilkumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: To monitor essential environmental parameters in real time, an IoT based smart irrigation system is proposed. pH and TDS sensors in aquaponics systems ensure that the water quality maintains itself at ideal levels for fish and plants, encouraging effective nutrient absorption and water recirculation. In land farming, soil moisture and NPK sensors provide precise data to enable precision irrigation and fertilization. Based on threshold-based decision-making and predictive analytics, the system dynamically triggers water pumps and actuators to regulate irrigation cycles. Cloud integration stores and analyzes historical data, enhancing system reliability and optimizing performance through data-driven insights. Real-time remote monitoring enables farmers to control and manage the system from anywhere, ensuring timely intervention and improved operational efficiency. The system’s performance is enhanced by predictive analytics models that forecast soil moisture and plant growth, enabling adaptive irrigation schedules. Fault detection mechanisms ensure system reliability by preventing sensor drift, communication failures, and power disruptions. By addressing challenges such as sensor calibration, power consumption, and system redundancy, this IOT-based irrigation system ensures consistent and accurate operation. It promotes sustainable agriculture by reducing water waste, lowering energy consumption, and improving overall crop health. The integration of IOT technologies in Aquaponics and land farming offers a scalable, efficient, and environmentally friendly solution for modern agricultural practices.

title: Predicting and Preventing Fraud in Supply Chain Networks using Deep Neural Networks and Clustering Techniques
authors: K Anil Kumar, K. Srinivasan, Nanthini L, S. Poongothai, M. Keerthi Priya, Pawan Mandal
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: There is a lack of empirical evidence on the occurrence and causes of fraud in SCN, which poses a hidden but serious hazard to business effectiveness. There is a clear link between interorganizational fraud and relational risk in the SC, but because it is often covert, it goes undetected, which has serious operational and financial consequences. Using the viewpoints of agency theory, transaction cost economics, and the fraud triangle—frameworks commonly used in auditing and accounting—this research investigates five main causes of fraud in the supply chain. Organizations suffer a median revenue loss of 9% due to interorganizational fraud, according to survey data collected from 151 professionals in industrial supply chains. This study introduces a new technique to fixing the problems with traditional methods of fraud, which have high FPR and low TPR. Following extensive data processing, which included normalization and imputation of missing values, a CGOA was used to mimic natural grasshopper activity in order to optimize data features. The developed DCNNLSTM had a fraud detection accuracy rate of 94.60%. In addition to demonstrating the effectiveness of complex algorithmic models in improving fraud detection and prevention, our results stress the critical nature of addressing fraud in SCN.

title: Joint Optimization and Leakage-Proof Strategies for Imbalanced Data
authors: Guoqing Meng, Qingqing Cao, Liang Sheng, Yihua Wang, Yuling Jiang, Yumeng Li, Lijun Deng, Xianjun Yang, Lisheng Gao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In disease risk prediction using real-world health examination data, class imbalance and information leakage are critical challenges often exacerbated by traditional stepwise optimization that overlooks synergistic interactions among modeling stages. To address this, we propose a Bayesian-based joint optimization framework. This approach constructs a unified parameter search space to simultaneously optimize preprocessing parameters—such as feature selection size and SMOTE neighbors—alongside XGBoost hyperparameters, guided by a weighted composite metric within a rigorous leakage-proof pipeline. Applied to hyperuricemia risk prediction on 18,164 records, the optimized model achieved an ROC-AUC of 0.8512 and PR-AUC of 0.5950, significantly outperforming an SVM-RBF baseline (ROC-AUC: 0.8182). Notably, comparative analysis revealed that neglecting strict train-validation segregation could lead to a systematic performance overestimation of approximately 0.20. These results demonstrate the method’s robustness and reliability for clinical modeling applications.

title: Research on Intelligent Matching Algorithm of Red Archives Content Adaptation to Generation Z Cognitive Preference Based on BERT Pre-training Model
authors: Li Zhang, Jianxin Liu, Jie Tang, Kaiyuan Zheng
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: In the era of digital dissemination of red archives, the cognitive preferences of Gen Z significantly influence the spread of red culture. Intelligent matching algorithms play a crucial role in enabling precise content dissemination of red archives. This paper first reviews the foundation of BERT pre-training model, the cultural consumption characteristics of Gen Z, and the principles and applications of intelligent matching algorithms, providing a theoretical basis for subsequent research. Subsequently, a BERT-based content adaptation scheme for red archives was designed, encompassing key stages such as dataset construction and preprocessing, model selection and tuning, and user profile establishment. Through experimental design and result analysis, the proposed scheme demonstrated its performance advantages across various scenarios. The findings demonstrate that this approach effectively aligns with Generation Z's information consumption patterns, thereby enhancing the dissemination impact of red archival content. The study also identifies existing limitations and proposes directions for future technological improvements, application expansion, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

title: Quantum Resilient Cryptography for Secure Ultra Dense 6G Terahertz Networks
authors: S. Aghalya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The advent of 6G terahertz (THz) communication networks offers unparalleled data rates and ultra-dense device connectivity but introduces tremendous vulnerabilities to quantum computing attacks. Conventional cryptographic techniques such as RSA and ECC are becoming increasingly vulnerable to quantum attacks and are thus a serious threat to network integrity and data confidentiality. The research here addresses the challenge of protecting 6G-THz networks from quantum adversaries by designing a quantum-resilient cryptographic protocol for ultra-dense network scenarios. The main objective is to offer resilient, scalable, and low-latency encryption and authentication to enable high-throughput THz communications. The system leverages lattice-based post-quantum cryptography combined with a light-weight key distribution protocol and hierarchical identity management to support massive device connectivity. The novelty lies in the adaptive blending of post-quantum primitives optimized to the bandwidth and latency limitations inherent in THz systems, combined with context-aware key negotiation to remove quantum threats in real time. The protocol significantly enhances future network resilience in quantum-enabled adversarial networks.

title: Design and Development of Venn Diagram Shaped Microstrip Patch Antenna for Female Breast Tumor Detection
authors: Hemant Kumar, Vandana Vikas Thakare
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This work proposes a near-field ISM band venn diagram shaped antenna for detecting tumors in the female breast. The concept employs venn diagram shaped antenna that shows a narrow frequency response in the 2.45 GHz ISM frequency band, which is highly sensitive to changes in the breast tissue's electromagnetic properties. The existence of a tumor is determined by measuring the magnitude and phase response variations of the received signal. In this paper, a compact Venn diagram-shaped microstrip patch antenna (MPA) is designed and simulated for breast tumor detection. The suggested antenna feeds the radiating patch via a stepped feed line and a complete ground plane. Its <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60 \text{mm} \times 6 0 \text{mm x} 1.6 \text{mm}$</tex> low-profile shape makes it easy to integrate into compact devices. At 2.42 GHz, the antenna had a return loss of −12.89 dB, a peak gain of 2.95 dBi, and a suitable bandwidth for biomedical applications. The results of the experiments and numerical testing strongly suggest that the detection method described here may lead to routine screening for breast tumours and a low-cost, portable, early-detection tool.

title: Edge-Scale Coranking: Azure-Ready Dataset Ranking with DevOps Discipline
authors: Uday Kumar Gannu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: This paper implements coranking and tensor-based multiranking over heterogeneous author–paper–journal graphs as a production-minded service, emphasizing reproducible pipelines, scalable computation, and operational rigor. Data ingestion, graph/tensor construction, and iterative solvers are packaged as containerized stages with CI/CD gates to ensure deterministic builds and safe promotion. Deployment targets a managed Kubernetes footprint on Azure for elastic execution of convergence-heavy jobs, while environment configuration is codified to keep preprocessing and ranking runs consistent across rings. Telemetry and release automation are integrated from the outset, enabling canary rollouts of ranking logic, fast rollback on drift, and continuous tracking of runtime efficiency and result stability. The result is a robust, cloud-oriented ranking system that couples network-theoretic models with DevOps practices—turning algorithm prototypes into reliable, maintainable, and scalable services suitable for Azure-backed research compute.

title: Energy-Aware LoRa-Based Remote Health Monitoring System with Edge Intelligence for Extended Field Operation
authors: Neha Ingle, Rajashree Raut
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Remote patient monitoring (RPM) is becoming increasingly important for healthcare delivery in rural settings and under-resourced scenarios wherein conventional wireless technologies often lack in range or power-related capacities. LoRa technology offers an attractive alternative for low-power, long-range communication of health data. This article presents the design, implementation, and assessment of a LoRa-based RPM with edge ML for real-time anomaly detection. In this system, sensor nodes with physiological sensors, a low-power microcontroller, and an SX1276 LoRa transceiver communicate with a gateway connected to a cloud dashboard, the latter providing health condition monitoring. To reduce power consumption while meeting reliability requirements, adaptive event-triggered transmissions together with dynamic spreading factor (SF) control schemes were implemented. Field experiments over 100-1,200-m link distances indicated stable communication characterized by a 5.4 % packet loss maximum and an average RSSI of -92 dBm. Under a typical duty cycle, battery life is up to 16.4 days. The edge ML model (Random Forest) attained 94.2 % accuracy with an inference latency of 118 ms on a microcontroller. The results proved the system feasible for continuous RPM, laying down the possibility of extending healthcare services into areas with scarce connectivity while being operationally efficient and reliable.

title: Machine Learning-Based Analysis of Multi-Source Network Packet Captures
authors: Kirubavathi G, Karthika Biju M, M A Niranjani Nair, Sreedhanu Surendran, Sanjana S
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: In this research paper, we introduce an unsupervised network traffic labeling method based on K-means clustering to distinguish between benign and malicious flows. The CTU-55 malware dataset consists of 55 malicious traffic samples and 9 benign samples. Following preprocessing and feature extraction, KMeans clustering is utilized to generate unsupervised labels with integrated outlier detection. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) with correlation matrices to understand feature relationships and inform feature selection. Subsequently, supervised machine learning classifiers, including Random Forest, XGBoost, KNN, and Naive Bayes, are trained on the labeled dataset to evaluate malware detection performance. Experiments demonstrate that cost-sensitive classifiers such as Random Forest trained with Gini impurity achieve the highest classification accuracy; XGBoost trained with binary cross-entropy loss, Naive Bayes optimized for negative log-likelihood, and KNN minimizes distance in feature space, whereas cross-model error agreement analysis reveals common and complementary misclassification trends are crucial for robust network traffic malware detection.

title: MDVP: Fo, Spread2, and PPE Emerge as Reproducible Biomarkers for Early Screening of Parkinson's Disease
authors: Aastha Kaushik, Pravir Kumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Parkinson Disease (PD) is hard to detect at an early stage. It is mainly because the clinical symptoms are revealed after some level of neurodegeneration. But in such a case, voice-based biomarkers are a pocket-friendly and noninvasive method of identifying PD through assessment of minor motor impairments. In this paper, we have suggested an interpretable machine learning (ML) based model that could be used to identify PD observing the acoustic characteristics that are provided by sustained vowel phonation. We used three ML models, which are, Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and XGBoost, so as to enforce robustness and generalization. They were all aggressive in their performance in terms of discrimination. AUCs ranged from 0.87 to 0.97. The maximum accuracy and AUC were displayed by XGBoost. The explainability based on SHAP to detect clinically relevant vocal biomarkers was verified and resulted in the detection of MDVP:Fo(HZ), MDVP:Fhi(Hz), spread2, PPE, and MDVP:RAP as consistent biomarkers of PD. With the help of a multi-model approach, analysis of feature stability, and explainable AI, the present research intends to offer a consistent framework to identify PD on time, and it may be applied to healthcare.

title: Detecting Fake Job Advertisements Using CNN, RNN, DNN, and Multilingual Transformers
authors: K. Sireesha, Dharani Dasari, Reshma Shaik, Pavani Marripalli, Bondala Ramakrishna, N. Srihitha Gunapriya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Fraudulent job advertisements are increasingly reported on online recruitment platforms, posing both financial and emotional risks to applicants. This study employs the publicly available Fake Job Postings dataset, which contains around 18,000 labeled records, to develop a hybrid detection framework. The preprocessing pipeline addressed missing values, cleaned textual content, and balanced classes using SMOTE. Text was represented using TF–IDF features alongside tokenisation. We used multiple neural architectures, including fully connected deep networks, convolution-based models (CNN), and recurrent models (RNN). In addition, a multilingual transformer, XLM-RoBERTa, is used to evaluate its capacity for cross-lingual generalisation. Experimental findings reveal that DNN and CNN architectures achieved the highest accuracy and F1-scores, while XLM-RoBERTa showed notable cross-lingual adaptability. The proposed framework can be integrated into recruitment systems to strengthen fraud detection, improve reliability, and build greater trust among platform users.

title: Breaking and Securing Vision-Language Models: An Adversarial Robustness Study
authors: Md Zarif Hossain, Abdur R. Shahid, Ahmed Imteaj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved remarkable performance in various downstream tasks, such as image captioning and visual question answering (VQA), by learning joint representations from paired image-text data. However, the robustness of VLMs against adversarial attacks is a critical concern as these models become widely adopted in real-world applications. This study provides a comprehensive overview of the current state of research on the adversarial robustness of VLMs. We introduce a novel taxonomy that categorizes the diverse landscape of adversarial attacks on VLMs, considering factors such as the modality of the attack, the level of model access, and the attack objective. We explore various types of attacks, including jailbreak, backdoor, data poisoning, gradient-based, and patch-based attacks, and discuss their underlying principles and assumptions. Furthermore, we present a comparative analysis of existing defense strategies and their strengths and limitations. By identifying open challenges and future research directions, we aim to stimulate further exploration of this crucial area and contribute to the development of robust and trustworthy VLMs for real-world applications.

title: Hybrid Deep-Spiking Neural Architectures for EEG-based Brain Signal Analysis
authors: Vedavalli S, Bagavathi Lakshmi R
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: The analysis of brain signals via Electroencephalography (EEG) will be essential to build trusted brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) and clinical neurodiagnostics. While deep learning models have shown high accuracy in the analysis of brain signals, they require substantial computational resource and energy, and they do not offer meaningful interpretability, whereas spiking neural networks (SNNs) have shown energy efficient approaches in the task of brain signal analysis, however in many contexts they have not displayed success with high-dimensional EEG signal. The approach in this paper is a Hybrid Deep-Spiking Neural Network (DSNN) model that fuses CNN-LSTM deep encoders with multi-layer SNN modules. The EEG signal is preprocessed, encoded via CNN-LSTM, and features are extracted containing spatial and temporal information (rich spatio-temporal features), transformed into spike trains fed into the DSNN. The CNN-LSTM layer extracts features from the EEG signal which can be implemented in the Employer SNN layers which are event-driven, converting temporal signals into spike trains. Additionally, a feature fusion mechanism adds attention that is utilized with both types of extracted features. Performance metrics were evaluated on EEG datasets, using the proposed DSNN model achieved highest accuracy (91.2%), improved F1-score (0.90) and reduced energy for inference (2.1mJ) versus other effective models. The analysis and training confirm that DSNN framework could allow for real-time, interpretable and energy efficient EEG based brain signal analysis, further work must be conducted to assess robustness of the model).

title: Strategic Agility in Turbulent Markets: Dynamic Capabilities for Rapid Pivoting
authors: Shuchi Gautam, Mishu Tripathi, Rajkumar A Gupta, Pankaj Nandurkar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: During periods of stochastic environmental turbulence such as the COVID-19 shock, structural inertia and dependence on ex-post indicators make traditional static financial ratios inadequate for detecting the lag between strategic intent and operational pivoting. This study seeks to operationalize and quantify “Strategic Agility” as a dynamic capability through high-dimensional semantic analysis of corporate disclosures. MD&A text from NSE Nifty 500 firms for FY2019-FY2023 was extracted and integrated with CMIE Prowess financials and SEBI annual filings; FinBERT was finetuned to identify semantic clusters representing sensing and seizing behaviors and was compared with a TF-IDF + SVM baseline. The transformer classifier attained a Macro-F1 of 0.89, exceeding the SVM baseline (0.76); firms in the top semanticagility quantile experienced a statistically significant (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<0.01$</tex>) approximately 14 % faster recovery in Return on Equity (ROE) during the post-shock period. Robustness checks including lags and survival models confirm these findings. The findings indicate that NLP-derived measures function as valid, near-real-time proxies for dynamic capabilities and constitute superior leading indicators of firm resilience in emerging markets.

title: Deep Learning for Unrelated-Machines Scheduling: Handling Variable Dimensions
authors: Diego Hitzges, Guillaume Sagnol
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Deep learning has been effectively applied to many discrete optimization problems. However, learning-based scheduling on unrelated parallel machines remains particularly difficult to design. Not only do the numbers of jobs and machines vary, but each job-machine pair has a unique processing time, dynamically altering feature dimensions. We propose a novel approach with a neural network tailored for offline deterministic scheduling of arbitrary sizes on unrelated machines. The goal is to minimize a complex objective function that includes the makespan and the weighted tardiness of jobs and machines. Unlike existing online approaches, which process jobs sequentially, our method generates a complete schedule considering the entire input at once. The key contribution of this work lies in the sophisticated architecture of our model. By leveraging various NLP-inspired architectures, it effectively processes any number of jobs and machines with varying feature dimensions imposed by unrelated processing times. Our approach enables supervised training on small problem instances while demonstrating strong generalization to much larger scheduling environments. Trained and tested on instances with 8 jobs and 4 machines, costs were only 2.51% above optimal. Across all tested configurations of up to 100 jobs and 10 machines, our network consistently outperformed an advanced dispatching rule, which incurred 22.22% higher costs on average. As our method allows fast retraining with simulated data and adaptation to various scheduling conditions, we believe it has the potential to become a standard approach for learningbased scheduling on unrelated machines and similar problem environments.

title: Southwest Power Pool Adoption of IEEE Std 2800-2022 Reactive/Active Power and Consecutive Voltage Ride-Through Standards
authors: Mostafa Sedighizadeh, Steve Purdy, Nick Parker, Sunny Raheem
abstract: This paper summarizes Southwest Power Pool (SPP)’s 2025 efforts to implement IEEE Std 2800-2022 requirements, specifically Clause 5 (Reactive Power–Voltage Control), Clause 6 (Active Power–Frequency Response), and Subclause 7.2.2.4 (Consecutive Voltage Ride-Through). Stakeholder feedback emphasized the complexity of implementation and associated concerns, leading to a decision to prioritize near-term voltage and frequency control capabilities. These initiatives enhance grid reliability and establish consistent performance expectations amid increasing inverter-based resource penetration. To support compliance with these new requirements, a PSCAD simulation was performed for an IBR plant proposed for connection to the SPP grid. The simulation results demonstrate the capability of this new SPP interconnection customer to meet the SPP-adopted IEEE Std 2800 requirements.

title: Nematic Liquid Crystal-Embedded Optical Fiber Cryogenic Temperature Sensor Amplified by Vernier Effect
authors: Jingxuan Xu, Hailiang Chen, Bing Ma, Sa Zhang, Xuan Zhang, Shuguang Li
abstract: Accurate cryogenic temperature sensing is critical for superconductivity, space technology, and low-temperature biomedicine. We present a high-sensitivity fiber-optic cryogenic temperature sensor based on Vernier effect amplified cascaded Sagnac interferometers (CSIs). The sensing arm incorporates a nematic liquid crystal (NLC)-embedded Sagnac interferometer (SI), while the reference arm uses a polarization-maintaining fiber (PMF)-based SI with a slightly detuned free spectral range (FSR), generating the Vernier effect for sensitivity enhancement. A fiber Bragg grating (FBG) with monotonic linear cryogenic response is integrated into the sensing loop to provide wavelength markers for coarse temperature estimation and FSR period identification, overcoming spectral aliasing and extending the measurement range. Experiments show an average sensitivity of 14.43 nm/K over 260–210 K, a 6.36-fold improvement over a single SI. These results validate NLCs as effective cryogenic thermometric materials and demonstrate the sensor's potential for applications such as short term biobanking of temperature-sensitive biomaterials and low temperature mechanical testing in industrial environments.

title: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis Based on Dynamic Language Enhancement and Synergistic Cross-Modal Transformer
authors: Linqin Cai, Daohong Liu, Lanrui Liu, Renyun Mou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) is a challenging task that utilizes verbal, visual, and acoustic cues to infer human sentiment and has garnered substantial research attention in recent years. However, due to the diversity of multimodal data, current MSA methods often fail to adequately leverage the rich semantic knowledge present in the linguistic modality while also overlooking the issue of informational redundancy within the visual and auditory modalities. In addition, the intermodal heterogeneity and spurious cross-modal interactions also pose huge challenges for effective multimodal fusion. To address these issues, we propose an MSA approach based on dynamic linguistic enhancement and synergistic cross-modal Transformer (LESCT). Our LESCT constructs a dynamic language enhancement network (LEN) for feature extraction. The proposed LEN enables visual and auditory features to dynamically capture contextual cues from multigranularity language representations via guided attention mechanism, thereby mitigating intramodal redundancy and noise interference. On this basis, the LESCT builds a new synergistic cross-modal Transformer (SCT) and local-to-composite multimodal fusion strategy. The SCT network employs a bimodal generator to produce composite features for each pair of modalities, transferring the composite information from the bimodal features to complementary unimodal features to facilitate rich intermodel and intramodal interaction. Extensive experiments were performed on three popular MSA benchmark datasets CMU-MOSI, CMU-MOSEI, and CH-SIMS. The overall accuracy of our LESCT is 86.43% on CMU-MOSI, 86.38% on CMU-MOSEI, and 81.35% on CH-SIMS. Experimental results demonstrate that our proposed LESCT is superior to the state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/jhwvh/LESCT</uri>

title: Enhancing Local Differential Privacy Accuracy by Exploiting Inherent Uncertainty
authors: Peng Tang, Xiya Shao, Rui Chen, Ning Wang, Shanqing Guo
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Management of Data
abstract: None

title: Attention-Enhanced High-Precision Model for Ground Radiation Field Reconstruction from UAV-Acquired Sparse Radiation Data
authors: Zheqi Liu, Yang Liu, Xinglong Li, Jianhua Wu, Chuangao Wang, Zhiping Luo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Accurate radiation field reconstruction from sparse UAV measurements is challenging in nuclear emergencies, especially in low-activity environments with scarce effective features. This paper proposes a Multi-level Window and Cross-Attention Network (MWCA-Net), a neural network integrating multi-scale coordinate encoding, hierarchical window attention, and high-dose mask cross-attention to enhance sparse weak signal feature extraction. A log-space dual-threshold loss function provides differentiated supervision across dose regions. Experiments show that at 2.78% sampling rate, MWCA-Net achieves a full-scene MAPE of 4.35%, 98.16% and 97.72% lower than inverse distance weighting (IDW) and Bayesian Poisson Kriging (BPK), respectively. Field validation confirms 100% source identification accuracy with 0.211 m average positioning error. MWCA-Net effectively addresses sparse sampling and weak feature constraints, offering a practical solution for rapid radiation field reconstruction in emergencies.

title: Research on Forest Fire Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv8
authors: Jian Huang, Tutu Sun, Rui Wang, Shanshan Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of low efficiency of manual patrol, insufficient recognition accuracy of small targets, and poor adaptability to low-light environments in traditional forest fire detection, an improved YOLOv8 forest fire detection algorithm is proposed. A four-scale detection architecture is constructed by adding a 4× downsampling detection head to enhance the capture ability of tiny fire and smoke; the CBAM attention mechanism is fused with the improved HSV image enhancement algorithm to improve the feature extraction accuracy in low-light environments; meanwhile, a high-robustness training set covering multi-angle, multi-distance, multi-landform and low-light scenarios is constructed to further enhance the generalization ability of the model; the anchor box configuration is optimized to adapt to the characteristics of forest fire targets. Experimental results show that the mAP value of the improved algorithm reaches 92.7% on the public forest fire dataset and the field-collected robust dataset, which is 8.3 percentage points higher than the original YOLOv8. Among them, the detection accuracy of small targets is increased by 12.6%, and the detection accuracy in low-light environments is improved by 15.2%, which can meet the practical needs of early and rapid warning of forest fires.

title: AgriCloud Framework: Improving Crop Production by Integrating Cloud and Machine Learning
authors: K Vijayalakshmi, G. Ramesh, Saroo Raj R. B, Rajeswari Rajesh Immanuel, P.Nirmala
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Agri Cloud is a cloud-based system that combines cloud computing, machine learning algorithms, and Internet of Things (IoT) sensors to improve agricultural management. The technology gathers data in real time from a variety of agricultural sensors, analyzes it in the cloud, and gives farmers insights they can use. Agri Cloud seeks to maximize resource use, boost agricultural yields, and promote sustainable farming methods by utilizing scalable cloud infrastructure. For contemporary agriculture, Agri Cloud is a dependable, accurate, and expandable solution. By giving farmers access to real-time insights, it improves crop management, lowers waste, and boosts yields. For more thorough decision-making, this basis can be extended to incorporate drone integration, satellite imaging, and sophisticated analytics. integrating cloud computing, machine learning, and the Internet of Things into a single framework for agriculture; facilitating real-time decision support by giving farmers data-driven, timely advice to maximize crop management; facilitating scalability by utilizing cloud infrastructure to manage massive data sets and assist with extensive agricultural operations; and emphasizing user-centric design to make it accessible to farmers with different levels of technological expertise. The simulated data is used to train a Decision Tree Classifier. Data is divided into test and training sets. Assesses model accuracy in this instance, 98%, which shows that crop conditions were well predicted.

title: Automated Configuration of Polymorphic Networks Based on Wafer-Scale Chip
authors: Yangxue Shao, Bo Ding, Jun Yu, Wei Zhao, Ke Song, Yu Wang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Traditional networks face rigidity issues due to their monolithic architecture, which struggles to adapt to dynamic demands for diverse applications and networking technologies. To address this, polymorphic networks have been proposed, but existing solutions lack core device support. This paper focuses on the core device requirements of polymorphic networks, demonstrating the feasibility of wafer-scale chips as a solution. We propose a wafer-scale chip architecture for polymorphic networks along with a modality auto configuration algorithm. Experimental results show that the algorithm compared with simulated annealing algorithm with random initialization achieves average improvements of 11.38% in average packet latency and 7.75% in throughput rate, offering an effective approach for polymorphic network infrastructure development.

title: FMVT-AH: Federated Multi-View Vision Transformers with Diffusion-Augmented Histopathology for Breast Cancer Diagnosis
authors: Srinivas Pasupula, Pulime Satyanarayana, Rajendra Kumar Jadi, Yerram Sneha, Shanti Priya, Kokkula Shiva Prasad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The progressive differentiation of mammographic and histopathology images through the use of deep learning DNN has led to the determination of breast cancer in the future, and the quality of a diagnosis and the cost-effectiveness of mammography and histopathological limits the development of new diagnostic tools. This paper suggests a combined system that combines multi-view mammography fusion, diffusion-augmented histopathology, and federated learning that conserves privacy based on Vision Transformer (ViTs). Transformer encoders are used to process craniocaudal (CC) and mediolateral oblique (MLO) views, and cross-attention is performed to allow complementary structural structure details to capture, whereas a conditional diffusion model is used to generate high-fidelity synthetic patches of histopathology to address the issue of class imbalance. The integrated features are incorporated into a federated ViT architecture, which allows training on the multi-institution without exchanging patient information. Experiments on DDSM, INbreast, CAMELYON and TCGA indicate that the proposed model can perform the best with the accuracy of 95.1%, sensitivity of 93.8% and specificity of 95.6% as well as an AUC of 0.947, and they have better results compared to mammographyonly, histopathology-only, early fusion, and federated baselines. These findings demonstrate multimodal fusion and generative augmentation as having the potential to be used to result in robust and privacy-preserving breast cancer detectors.

title: Designing an Adaptive Color Morphology Operator for Exudates and Microaneurysms Detection in Color Fundus Images
authors: Milad Rostamian, Motahare Shekari
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy is a leading cause of preventable blindness, making automated lesion detection critical for early screening. However, traditional unsupervised methods often fail due to image variability and ignore critical color information, while modern deep learning models require vast annotated datasets. This paper introduces a fully rule-based, unsupervised framework requiring no training or labeled data that integrates the detection of exudates and microaneurysms to overcome these challenges. The core of our method is a new Adaptive Color Morphology operator that leverages the perceptually uniform CIE L*a*b* color space for robust exudate segmentation using fixed a priori parameters shared across datasets. By first identifying and removing exudate regions, our pipeline improves the specificity of the subsequent multi-scale microaneurysm detection stage by reducing inter-lesion confusion. Evaluated on public datasets, our method achieves a competitive unsupervised exudate detection accuracy of 0.93 on DIARETDB1 (DDB1) and microaneurysm detection F-Scores of 0.460 on e-Ophtha and 0.291 on the challenging Retinopathy Online Challenge dataset. Our results demonstrate that this transparent, color-aware pipeline is an interpretable and practical alternative to data-dependent models for automated diabetic retinopathy screening. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/milad67/Adaptive-Color-Morphology-DR.

title: Temperature-Dependent Modeling and Optimal Design of LLC Resonant Converters Considering High-Voltage Field-Stop IGBTs Turn-Off Transient
authors: Shuiyuan He, Ruiqi Ma, Chongshan Xie, Yunhao Guo, Jiepin Zhang, Yingtao Ma, Lijun Diao
abstract: In high-voltage high-power rail transit auxiliary power supply (APS) systems, ensuring zero-voltage switching (ZVS) under discontinuous conduction mode (DCM) is critical for LLC resonant converters (LLC RCs). However, the temperature sensitivity of the turn-off transient in high-voltage field-stop insulated gate bipolar transistors (FS IGBTs) is often neglected, posing a risk of ZVS failure under thermal stress. In response to this challenge, this article proposes a temperature dependent analytical model and an optimal design method. Specifically, an analytical model is derived to capture the characteristic two-stage dVCE/dt of FS IGBTs during the near zero-current turn-off (near-ZCT) transient. Based on this model, a parameter design methodology for LLC RCs is developed, incorporating constraints on junction temperature and turn-off current. Unlike conventional approaches, the proposed method optimizes dead-time selection to ensure ZVS and DCM operation across the full operating temperature range. The approach is validated on a 150-kW prototype, achieving a peak efficiency of 98.97% at full load.

title: Multimodal Population Graph Networks with Self-Attention Mechanisms for Autism Spectrum Disorder Identification
authors: Yaning Liu, Weiyang Chen, Yi Pan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) identification is challenged by data heterogeneity across sites, unclear population-level inter-subject relationship modeling, and a lack of biologically meaningful brain-region analysis. To address these issues, this paper proposes GPGATNet, a cross-site ASD identification framework that introduces learnable mechanisms at three levels—individual representation alignment, population graph construction, and stable propagation learning—to achieve robust classification while enabling biomarker discovery. A three-layer self-attention unsupervised graph pooling module, SAGNet, is designed to adaptively extract brain-feature subgraphs via node-importance scoring and hierarchical Top-k selection. This strategy alleviates cross-site scale discrepancies and simultaneously provides interpretable estimates of regional contributions. In addition, a population-graph modeling scheme based on a “phenotypic skeleton + functional-connectivity edge-weight updating” paradigm is employed to enhance the robustness of inter-subject relationship modeling. Furthermore, a graph convolutional network, MH-GCAT, is constructed by integrating multi-branch convolutions with multi-head attention fusion to strengthen population-level discriminative capability. Experiments on the ABIDE dataset demonstrate that GPGATNet outperforms existing methods. Moreover, by combining attention-based localization with analyses of BOLD signal fluctuations, we observe functional abnormalities and activity instability in key regions such as the thalamus and hippocampus in ASD, providing neuroimaging evidence for understanding impairments in emotion regulation and social behavior.

title: Radargrammetric 3D Reconstruction of Multiple Moving Ship Targets via Multi-angle Spaceborne SAR Imagery
authors: Bin Liu, Yicheng Jiang, Zitao Liu, Jin Wei, Yong Wang, Yun Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The increasing complexity of the marine environments imposes higher demands on the surveillance of maritime ships. Radargrammetry can extract the height information of targets from the amplitude of multi-angle synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery, which provides a more specific and accurate description of the ship targets to efficiently enhance maritime surveillance capabilities. However, existing radargrammetric methods are limited to stationary targets, which cannot meet the demands for 3D reconstruction of multiple moving ships in maritime surveillance. To address this problem, a radargrammetric three-dimensional (3D) reconstruction principle and a detailed reconstruction procedure for multiple moving ships via multi-angle spaceborne SAR imagery are proposed in this paper. First, a radargrammetric 3D reconstruction principle for moving targets based on the multi-angle moving-target projection (MAMTP) model is proposed, and its theoretical height reconstruction accuracy is derived. Based on this principle, a multi-angle spaceborne SAR image registration algorithm for moving targets is proposed. Then, a whole radargrammetric 3D reconstruction procedure for multiple moving targets via multi-angle spaceborne SAR imagery based on the proposed principle is developed. This procedure includes the following steps: (i) division and refocusing of received signals from multiple moving targets; (ii) estimation of targets' velocities; (iii) multi-angle spaceborne SAR image registration; (iv) radargrammetric 3D reconstruction. This procedure can achieve accurate 3D reconstruction of multiple moving ships. Simulation results including both scatterer targets and electromagnetic targets verify the correctness of the proposed radargrammetric 3D reconstruction principle and the effectiveness of the proposed reconstruction procedure.

title: Making Sense of AI Agents Hype: Adoption, Architectures, and Takeaways from Practitioners
authors: Ruoyu Su, Matteo Esposito, Roberta Capuano, Rafiullah Omar, June Sallou, Henry Muccini, Davide Taibi
abstract: To support practitioners in understanding how agentic systems are designed in real-world industrial practice, we present a review of practitioner conference talks on AI agents. We analyzed 138 recorded talks to examine how companies adopt agent-based architectures (Objective 1), identify recurring architectural strategies and patterns (Objective 2), and analyze application domains and technologies used to implement and operate LLM-driven agentic systems (Objective 3).

title: Revolutionizing Smart Farming with IoT and AI-Driven Technologies
authors: P.Pandiaraja, Karthik K, C.Selvarathi, J.Raj Karkee, B.T.Annapoorani, P.Vikram
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Smart farming applies technology incorporation in agricultural practices, to try to improve the productivity by solving issues such as water shortage and resource inefficiency. This project involves an enhanced automated irrigation system that inefficient, and effective in using water according to the state of the environment and the climate. The system includes a micro controller to determine signals such as moisture of the soil, temperature, rain, and water level. Water is supplied in the crop only when soil moisture levels are low and there is no rainfall so water is used efficiently in the production of crops. On the other hand, the system shuts off the water, pump when there is enough moisture, or rainwater available in the environment. Other requirements include control of water storage, water supply signal systems and opening of drainage systems when there is a looming water logged situation. Use of a smartphone application or a web application to manage the system increases the usability to the farmers since they can manage the system from wherever they are. Real-time data processing and display are done on a local interface and to a cloud interface which can be altered in real time; notifications may be sent to the user in case of any problem to enable early system intervention. It helps to use as fewer amounts of water as possible, the time consuming process of watering crops is addressed and crops yield is considered. According to its design, it is flexible to suit different crops and its application is suitable for planting in large tracts of arable land. Such further enhancements can be development of additional sensors for the environment and the optimization through the application of machine learning techniques. This project demonstrates the ideas of how one can conduct sustainable agriculture and how smart technologies and automation helps in solving water management issues sufficiently.

title: University in the Age of AI: Vectors of Transformation
authors: Evgeny V. Kunts
publish: 2026 Systems of Signals Generating and Processing in the Field of on Board Communications
abstract: The article examines the outcomes of employing generative artificial intelligence in university education. The ongoing technological revolution around AI adoption has triggered a crisis in higher education, caused by the declining relevance of key elements of the traditional learning process. At the same time, the rapid development of technology and growing global instability are increasing public demand for high-quality higher education. Based on the study of eighty publications, the article examines the reasons for and the content of educational transformation brought about by the spread of generative AI. It also explores promising teaching practices that involve AI in the learning process. The author concludes that generative AI technology opens up new opportunities to enrich both educational theory and practice in higher education. The article contributes to the discussion on how AI affects higher education, on developing a comprehensive set of measures that universities can take in response to the spread of this technology, and on ways to use AI effectively in the teaching process.

title: Data-Driven Temperature Prediction of Electric Vehicle Brake Discs Using BP Neural Network
authors: Yihong Ru, Jianjun Wu, Liuwen Huang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Real-time temperature prediction of brake discs is critical for ensuring braking safety in electric vehicles (EVs) under high-demand boundary scenarios where regenerative braking is limited. Here, hydraulic braking must compensate for the loss of electric torque, leading to concentrated thermal loading on the friction pair. Although regenerative braking reduces average thermal stress in urban driving, the higher curb mass of EVs increases the kinetic energy that must be dissipated during these extreme events. To enable rapid thermal assessment for intelligent brake thermal management, this study proposes a data-driven surrogate framework based on a Back Propagation (BP) neural network to predict the maximum temperature of EV brake discs within milliseconds. A parametric finite element thermal model is first developed in ANSYS to generate training data using an orthogonal experimental design. A three-layer BP neural network is then trained using ambient temperature, vehicle curb mass, initial speed, and deceleration as input features. Test results show a mean relative error of 1.40% and a maximum error of 3.45%, with a single-sample prediction time of less than 1 ms. The proposed surrogate provides a fast and accurate temperature input for EV intelligent thermal management strategies and offers a generalizable approach adaptable to diverse braking system designs.

title: MeshCentral-FogHealth: A Hybrid Supervision Architecture for Distributed Telehealth Systems
authors: Mamadou Ba, Massamba Seck, Alie Ndiaye, Ibrahima Ngom, Samuel Ouya
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Faced with Senegal’s epidemiological transition, where non-communicable diseases account for 45% of deaths and hypertension affects 24.5% of the adult population, this research proposes an innovative centralized supervision architecture for distributed telehealth systems. The integration of MeshCentral into the FogHealth ecosystem addresses the operational challenges of national-scale deployment, particularly the coordination of a distributed network of connected care points in a context of variable connectivity. Our contribution lies in the design of a hybrid solution combining distributed fog computing and centralized supervision, enabling remote maintenance, proactive monitoring, and secure management of remote access. The results demonstrate operational stability with 271,993 continuous readings, validating the effectiveness of this approach in reducing hospital burden while maintaining an optimal level of medical surveillance. This architecture paves the way for a hybrid care model adapted to the constraints of developing countries, aligned with the vision of Senegal Strategy 2050.
