title: RISE: Resource-Efficient RFID Security Protocol in IoT with S-Box and Elliptic Curve Cryptography
authors: Haradhan Ghosh, Pramod Kumar Maurya, Satya Bagchi, Minho Jo, Bhaskar Krishnamachari
abstract: Ensuring the security and privacy of RFID systems is paramount to prevent unauthorized access and data breaches. In addition, addressing the insecurity of public keys in RFID tags and preventing server-side tag distinction requires a multifaceted approach. This paper proposes an RFID-enabled protocol, called RISE, Resource-Efficient RFID Security Protocol in IoT with S-Box and Elliptic Curve Cryptography, that integrates S-box and elliptic curve cryptography (ECC) to enhance the security of RFID communication. The protocol leverages S-boxes to introduce non-linearity and confusion, thereby thwarting potential attacks such as cryptanalysis and replay attacks. Additionally, ECC is employed for key exchange and authentication, leveraging computational efficiency and strong security properties. Formal proofs and analysis results demonstrate that RISE provides robust security and can effectively resist various types of attacks. RISE presents notable improvements, with an average 51.18% reduction in computational overhead compared to similar protocols. This advancement not only boosts performance but also enhances resource efficiency.

title: FAR-RIO: A Fast and Robust Radar-Inertial Odometry with Isotropic Uncertainty Model and Dual-Observation Update Pipeline
authors: Hang Zhen, Zhi Gao, Ronghe Jin, Xinyu Guo, Feng Lin
abstract: Due to the ability to provide point clouds and Doppler velocity, as well as the adaptability in harsh weather conditions, 4D Radar has emerged as a new option for Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM). However, there is limited research on both robustness and computational efficiency, which are essential requirements for deploying 4D Radar as a specialized sensor in harsh weather. This paper proposes a fast and robust Radar-Inertial-Odometry (RIO) approach, named FAR-RIO. Specifically, we propose a dynamic point filtering based on full covariance propagation, along with an isotropic uncertainty model for accurate registration. In particular, leveraging the dual-observation capability of 4D radar, we design a novel dual-observation update pipeline for the iterated error-state Kalman filter (IESKF), coupled with a corresponding keyframe selection strategy, significantly reducing computational load while minimizing accuracy loss. Our method approaches state-of-the-art performance on various sensors and runs significantly faster than existing methods. Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/heqi-zh/FAR-RIO.git</uri>.

title: Continuous representation of tactile information by an unsupervised liquid state machine_supp1-3680544.pdf
authors: Katsunori Kitano

title: AI-Assisted Semiotic Analysis of the Cultural Absence of the Zulfikar Sword in Chinese Islamic Visual Culture
authors: Qiling Huang, Rui Sun
abstract: The Zulfikar sword is a highly standardized sacred weapon symbol in Shia visual culture, forming a stable iconographic lineage across the Middle East and the Irano-Ottoman world. However, it is notably absent from Chinese Islamic visual traditions, resulting in a phenomenon of "cultural absence." While existing studies interpret this absence through religious history and iconography, they lack verifiable visual-structural explanations. This study proposes a cross-cultural semiotic framework by constructing a triple-layered symbolic structure—semantic core, visual grammar, and cultural vehicle—and introduces the Cross-Cultural Symbolic Structure Adaptability Framework (CSSAF). An integrated visual corpus of Zulfikar representations, Chinese Islamic artefacts, and decorative patterns was assembled. Using pretrained visual models, high-dimensional features were extracted and analyzed through dimensionality reduction, clustering, and discriminant methods to examine their relationships within latent space. A structurally and semantically constrained diffusion model was further employed to test the symbol ’ s potential integration into Chinese visual grammar. Results reveal that the Zulfikar symbol forms a stable, isolated cluster, lacking transitional continuity with Chinese Islamic decorative systems. Generative experiments show that attempted visual fusion results in superficial combinations rather than intrinsic integration. These findings demonstrate that the symbol ’ s absence arises from structural incompatibility between its strong semantic directionality and the decorative, de-subjectivised visual grammar of Chinese Islamic art. This study provides a quantifiable framework for examining cultural absence and offers a methodological reference for AI-assisted cross-cultural symbol analysis and generation.

title: NaviGate app: a Digital Catalyst Supporting Coordination in a Smart Port Ecosystem
authors: Juliana Basulo-Ribeiro, Raquel Gil-Pereira, Bernardo Macedo, Ezequiel Dias, Leonor Teixeira
abstract: The increasing complexity of port logistics and the growing need for coordinated data-driven operations have highlighted the importance of integrating truck drivers into digital transformation initiatives. This study presents the development and validation of NaviGate, a mobile application designed to improve communication, planning and operational synchronisation between truck drivers, transport companies and a Portuguese port (aiming to be a Smart Port). This paper reports the design and validation of a NaviGate prototype (interface, interaction flows and functional logic), which is being designed to support driver interaction with the future smart gate that is currently proposed for development. Using a user-centred design methodology, requirements were gathered through interviews with transport companies and questionnaires administered to drivers, forming the basis for a prototype that was subsequently validated by terminal managers and port authorities. Results indicate that drivers express a strong interest in adopting a mobile solution, recognising the need for reliable real-time information, better coordination and reduced operational uncertainty. The developed prototype integrates scheduling, real-time notifications, Guidance (process-oriented operational navigation) inside the terminal, continuous GPS-based monitoring, safety information, feedback mechanisms and a 24/7 chatbot. Theoretically, the study contributes to the conceptualisation of ports as socio-technical digital ecosystems, reinforcing evidence that digital maturity depends not only on technological infrastructures but also on the integration and acceptance of human actors, particularly truck drivers in this case, who are often overlooked in strategic planning. By demonstrating how mobile applications can operationalise this alignment, the research advances discussions on user-centred innovation in port digital transformation and supports emerging literature on collaborative smart gate ecosystems. Practically, NaviGate establishes a replicable model for ports seeking to enhance transparency, safety and efficiency through integrated, user-oriented mobile solutions. By aligning people, processes and technology, the application illustrates how digital platforms can strengthen coordination and operational resilience across port ecosystems.

title: Real-time Balancing Control of Reconfigurable Battery Packs using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Ali Irshayyid, Wanqun Yang, Jun Chen
abstract: This paper proposes a reinforcement learning (RL)-based control framework for real-time state-of-charge (SOC) balancing in reconfigurable lithium-ion battery packs. Traditional battery management strategies, such as passive and active balancing, suffer from energy inefficiency and limited adaptability. Reconfigurable battery systems offer enhanced flexibility through dynamic switchable topologies. However, to fully exploit the potential of the battery pack, an intelligent control policy to determine the optimal battery configuration in real-time is needed, which is a difficult task due to the large optimization space. To address this challenge, the reconfiguration problem is formulated as a Markov Decision Process, and a policy optimization framework is proposed to guide switch decisions based on observed cell states, such as the SOC of the individual cells in addition to the current pack configuration. The proposed RL agent operates on discrete action space and is guided by a multi-objective reward function tailored for battery management application. To avoid the high cost associated with physical battery training, we construct a linear surrogate model for efficient simulation-based learning. The proposed RL controller is evaluated against rule-based and greedy-based baselines, achieving superior SOC balancing performance with reduced switching frequency and improved runtime. In addition, comparison to true optimal obtained by dynamic programming demonstrates that the proposed RL controller achieves competitive results with significantly lower computational cost.

title: A Hybrid Model for Enhancing 3D Medical Image Segmentation with Self-Attention Volumetric Transformer and Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Ankit Srivastava, Santosh Rajak, Munesh Chandra, Ashim Saha, Sanjoy Chakraborty
abstract: In modern healthcare, accurate diagnosis, treatment planning, and surgical guidance are affected mainly by precise segmentation of 3D medical images. Clinicians can more precisely identify complex anatomical structures and detect abnormalities in 3D medical images than in 2D images. Most segmentation work is based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs). CNNs are good at capturing local image details, but struggle to represent broader spatial contexts, whereas ViTs excel at finding long-range dependencies within an image but require substantial processing power. This paper presents a hybrid segmentation approach that combines the strengths of both paradigms. This study employs a Volumetric Transformer Encoder (VN), which shows strong performance in capturing the non-local dependencies across 3D space, and a CNN-based decoder reconstructs fine-grained segmentation maps, showing strong spatial fidelity. The proposed model also uses hierarchical attention and skip connections, which support multiscale learning and maintain semantic consistency. The proposed model’s performance has been evaluated using the BraTS 2020 benchmark dataset, and the results show its superiority over the state-of-the-art existing models based on a higher average Dice score and a lower Hausdorff Distance.

title: TemporalFED: A Software Module for Detecting Cyberattacks in Industry 4.0 Time-Series Data Using Decentralized Federated Learning
authors: Ángel Luis Perales Gómez, Enrique Tomás Martínez Beltrán, Pedro Miguel Sánchez Sánchez, Alberto Huertas Celdrán
abstract: Industry 4.0 has brought numerous advantages, such as increasing productivity through automation. However, it also presents major cybersecurity issues, such as cyberattacks affecting industrial processes. Federated Learning (FL) combined with time-series analysis is a promising cyberattack detection mechanism proposed in the literature. However, having a single point of failure and network bottleneck are critical challenges that need to be tackled. Thus, this article explores the benefits of the Decentralized Federated Learning (DFL) in terms of cyberattack detection and resource consumption. The work presents TemporalFED, a software module for detecting cyberattacks in industrial environments using FL paradigms and time series. TemporalFED incorporates three components: time series conversion, feature engineering, and stationary conversion. To evaluate TemporalFED, itwas deployed on Fedstellar, a DFL framework. Then, a pool of experiments measured the detection performance and resource consumption in a chemical gas industrial environment with different time-series configurations, FL paradigms, and topologies. The results showcase the superiority of the configuration utilizing DFL and Semi-Decentralized Federated Learning (SDFL) paradigms, along with a fully connected topology, which achieved the best performance in anomaly detection. Regarding resource consumption, the configuration without feature engineering employed less bandwidth, less usage of the Central Processing Unit (CPU), and less usage of the Random Access Memory (RAM) than other configurations.

title: Efficiency-aware Federated Learning for Image Classification via Model Adaptation
authors: Yiheng Lu, Ziyu Guan, Maoguo Gong, Wei Zhao, Zhuping Hu, Fenlong Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Massive client training and bandwidth occupation are two main efficiency issues in Federated Learning (FL) for image classification. In this paper, we propose a model adaptation technique to alleviate the efficiency concerns of the FL. The model adaptation technique incorporates two key components: a Spectrum-Tempered Filter Pruning Framework (STFPF) and an Adaptive Accuracy-Based Selection Mechanism (AABSM), which are designed to effectively reduce client-side training costs and minimize bandwidth consumption, respectively. STFPF employs spectral analysis of the weight matrices to dynamically determine the optimal pruning rate. Specifically, the framework analyzes the eigenvalue distribution of each layer’s weight matrix, enabling the identification and removal of filters based on the magnitude of their corresponding eigenvalues. This spectral-based pruning mechanism allows STFPF to effectively reduce the scale of local models, thereby significantly decreasing computational costs throughout the FL process. Moreover, AABSM intelligently selects a subset of clients for model aggregation based on their individual model performance metrics. This performance-driven selection approach significantly reduces the consumption of required communication time between the central server and local clients, thereby effectively conserving bandwidth resources throughout the FL process. We extensively evaluate our model adaptation technique on three prominent architectures with AlexNet, VGG-16, and ResNet-50, using the FashionMNIST, CIFAR-10, and CIFAR-100 datasets, respectively. For AlexNet, out approach can reduce the computation cost obviously while with tiny accuracy sacrifice. For VGG-16 and ResNet-50, our approach not only achieves superior accuracy in the central model compared to baseline methods but also yields significant reductions in computational complexity (FLOPs), model parameters, and bandwidth consumption throughout the complete federated learning cycle.

title: Full W-Band HDI Antenna-in-Package Array Based on a Coplanar Magnetoelectric Dipole
authors: Pei Gan, Yuxiang Zheng, Yunyan Zhou, Gang Song, Xinyu Zhang, Chenglin Yang, WenWen Zhang, Jun Li, Qidong Wang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A full W-band antenna-in-package (AiP) array implemented in a six-layer high-density interconnect (HDI) package is presented. The radiator is a coplanar magnetoelectric (ME) dipole in which the electric dipole current and the equivalent magnetic current are synchronously excited on the same layer by a TM10-mode coupling patch. Unlike previously reported slot-based via-free ME dipoles using a resonant λ0/2 ground slot, the proposed magnetic source is a localized subwavelength gap, which suppresses vertical phase detuning and avoids electrically long-slot behavior at the upper W-band. A dual-layer artificial magnetic conductor (AMC) is further embedded as both an in-phase reflection boundary and a functional copper-density compensation structure for multilayer fabrication. Based on the proposed element, an 8×8 array is designed and fabricated. Measurements show an impedance bandwidth of 75–110 GHz (37.8%), a peak realized gain of 22.7 dBi, and stable symmetric broadside radiation across the full W-band. The proposed architecture is well suited for compact W-band HDI-AiP systems.

title: Hybrid Hierarchical Federated Learning over 5G/NextG Wireless Networking
authors: Haiyun Liu, Jiahao Xue, Jie Xu, Yao Liu, Zhuo Lu
abstract: Today's 5G and NextG wireless networks are moving toward using the coordinated multi-point (CoMP) transmission and reception technique, where a client can be simultaneously served by multiple base stations (BSs) for better communication performance. However, traditional hierarchical federated learning (HFL) architectures impose the constraint that each client can be associated with only one edge server (ES) at a time. If we keep using the traditional HFL architectures in modern hierarchical networks for model training, the benefits of the CoMP technique would remain unexploited and leave room for further improvements in training efficiency. To address this issue, we propose hybrid hierarchical federated learning (HHFL), which allows clients in overlapping regions to simultaneously communicate with multiple edge servers (ESs) for model aggregation. HHFL is able to enhance inter-ES knowledge sharing, thereby mitigating model divergence and improving training efficiency. We provide a rigorous theoretical convergence analysis with a convergence upper bound to validate its effectiveness. Experimental results show that HHFL outperforms traditional HFL, particularly when the data across different ESs is not independent and identically distributed (non-IID). For example, when each ES is dominated by only two of the ten classes and 15 out of the 57 clients can connect to multiple ESs, HHFL achieves up to 2× faster convergence under the same configuration. These results demonstrate that HHFL provides a scalable and efficient solution for FL model training in today's and NextG wireless networks.

title: JaiLIP: Jailbreaking Vision-Language Models via Loss Guided Image Perturbation
authors: Md Jueal Mia, M. Hadi Amini
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have remarkable abilities in generating multimodal reasoning tasks. However, potential misuse or safety alignment concerns of VLMs have increased significantly due to different categories of attack vectors. Among various attack vectors, recent studies have demonstrated that image-based perturbations are particularly effective in generating harmful outputs. In the literature, many existing techniques have been proposed to jailbreak VLMs, leading to unstable performance and visible perturbations. In this study, we propose Jailbreaking with Loss-guided Image Perturbation (JaiLIP), a jailbreaking attack in the image space that minimizes a joint objective combining the mean squared error (MSE) loss between clean and adversarial image with the model’s harmful-output loss. We evaluate our proposed method on VLMs using standard toxicity metrics from Perspective API and Detoxify. Experimental results demonstrate that our method generates highly effective and imperceptible adversarial images, outperforming existing methods in producing toxicity. Moreover, we have evaluated our method in the transportation domain to demonstrate the attack’s practicality beyond toxic text generation in specific domain. Our findings emphasize the practical challenges of image-based jailbreak attacks and the need for efficient defense mechanisms for VLMs.

title: LoRaDoctor: LLM-Driven Diagnosis and Adaptive Policy Optimization for Reducing Packet Error Rate in LoRaWAN Networks
authors: Dongyi Ma, Aitian Ma, Sirui Luo, Martin de Jode, Andrew Hudson-Smith, Mo Sha
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: LoRaWAN is widely used for large-scale Internet of Things (IoT) deployments, but real-world reliability is often affected by high packet error rate. Existing optimization methods, such as heuristics or supervised learning, cannot fully capture the effects of environment, spatial layout, and network dynamics, which limits their adaptability. In this paper, we present LORADOCTOR, the first framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to optimize LoRaWAN. LORADOCTOR performs causal analysis, generates adaptive transmission policies, and predicts network performance in an interpretable way. We perform simulation-based evaluations using a year-long dataset collected from eight sensors deployed in East London. The results show that LORADOCTOR can significantly reduce packet error rates compared to both the default LoRaWAN settings and standard machine learning methods. Our evaluation also identifies distance-based path loss, temperature effects, and human activity as the main causes of packet error rate, showing its potential to support more reliable and adaptive LoRaWAN deployments in future urban environments.

title: Operational Efficiency Assessment of Wind Power Generation Across Indian States: An SBM-DEA Approach
authors: Jyoti Luhaniwal, Shivi Agarwal, Trilok Mathur
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Earth's average temperature has increased by approximately 0.06° Celcius per decade since 1850, amounting to a total rise of about 1.1° C. Electricity generation is a major source of greenhouse gas emissions in India, which has increased the focus on low-emission renewable energy options. Wind power is the second-largest source of renewable electricity generation in the country. Therefore, this study employs a non-oriented slacksbased measure data envelopment analysis (SBM-DEA) model to evaluate the relative efficiency of wind power generation across nine Indian states for the years 2022 and 2023. Wind power density and installed capacity are used as inputs, while capacity factor and power generation are considered as outputs. The results reveal notable variations in efficiency across states and time, with Rajasthan, Kerala, Madhya Pradesh, and Telangana achieving full efficiency throughout the analysis period. A Tobit regression analysis further shows that site elevation positively affects efficiency, whereas wind curtailment significantly reduces performance. The findings offer practical insights for improving wind power plant operations and policy planning.

title: Task-Aware Facial Micro-Dynamics Modeling for Dementia Detection using Face-Mesh Motion Signatures
authors: Hemantha Kumar H S, Tanuja R, S H Manjula
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: Finding dementia early and correctly is still hard because cognitive decline is so subtle and different in different people. Recently, facial motion analysis during tasks that require a lot of thinking has been shown to be a promising non-invasive sign of dementia-related impairment. This paper suggests a new way to use facial mesh dynamics to find dementia called task-aware facial motion degradation modeling framework (TA-FMDM). The suggested method uses a Facial Motion Degradation Index to track how facial motion changes in different regions and combines task-aware attention with cross-task consistency learning to make it more robust across a range of cognitive tasks. We did a lot of tests on the PROMPT dataset using deep learning models like 1D convolutional neural networks, long short-term memory networks, and Transformers. A systematic evaluation of the effects of several data augmentation strategies showed that shifting-based augmentation gives the most consistent performance improvements. The suggested TA-FMDM framework did better than strong baseline models on all evaluation metrics, with an accuracy of 80.3%, a recall of 70.4%, and an area under the ROC curve of 0.821. Ablation and task-wise analyses showed that facial motion degradation modeling and task-aware learning worked well. These results show that task-aware facial motion analysis could be a useful and understandable way to screen for dementia. It could also be a non-invasive and scalable way to assess cognitive function early on.

title: CBF-YOLO: A Multi-scale Feature Enhancement Algorithm for Underground Helmet Detection
authors: Yibo Li, Zhijing Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Neural Networks, Information and Communication Engineering (NNICE)
abstract: Helmet detection in underground mining is challenging due to poor lighting, dust, complex backgrounds, and significant scale variations. This paper proposes CBF-YOLO, an enhanced YOLOv8 model incorporating three key improvements: a Coordinate Attention (CA) mechanism for better localization, a Weighted Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN) for adaptive multi-scale fusion, and Focal Loss to handle class imbalance. Experiments on a self-collected dataset show that CBF-YOLO achieves 91.5% precision and 86.2% mAP@0.5, outperforming the baseline by 3.5 and 6.7 percentage points, respectively. The method maintains low computational cost, making it suitable for edge deployment in underground environments.

title: The Pinnacle of Drone Innovation with Cutting Edge Camera Integration
authors: Md. Khaja Farman, S. Saravanan, Md. Zeeshan Ali, D. Vinay Goud, N.Aashish, D. Gireesh Kumar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: This work sets up a low-cost, made-by-hand drone using an APM 2.8 flight board and 1000kV brush-free DC motors. The drone is put together on an F450 body and runs on a 5200mAh LiPo cell, with 30A ESCs for good motor work. A UBEC part keeps power even to the on-board tech, including an LST S2 camera for live video send. Control comes from an FS-i6S send and get system, giving sure talk and smooth flight work. The drone works well for both hand-run and self-run tasks, with GPS for way finding. Made for air watch, check, and test work, the setup brings together parts that fit well, steady flight, and strong flying skills. Its build can grow, with plans for block-skip and AI add-ons. This project shows a good way to make an able and change-friendly drone setup for school and half-pro uses. The proposed system shows that a drone that is both affordable and modular can provide a flight that is stable, a FPV that is reliable and adaptability for various applications. The framework, while it is limited in several aspects such as endurance, GPS accuracy, and lack of obstacle avoidance, still offers a good starting point for the development of the next generation of drones. The anticipated improvements, including AI-assisted tracking, sophisticated obstacle avoidance, more efficient energy systems, and IoT integration, will only widen its capabilities, thus transforming it into a multifunctional platform for scientific and industrial applications.

title: Machine Learning Based Approaches in the Education Sector to Impart the Quality Among Students
authors: Rajalaxmi Hegde, Sandeep Kumar Hegde, Thangavel Murugan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The concept of Machine learning (ML) algorithms can be significantly applied in the area of academic research, which has the capability to impact teaching and learning processes, and research by offering a platform for individualized learning, responsive evaluation, and also providing information on student achievement, advancement, and learning practices. ML is transforming the way in which educational institutions work, communicate knowledge, and educate students. In the present era, young minds are being exposed to a wide range of global concepts. This would help the students to tune their mindset to the technologically advanced world of tomorrow. Due to the breakthrough in technology, machine learning has influenced the education sector by enabling facilities such as transcription, text-to-speech, and personalized learning. By applying ML, it is possible to determine whether a student is at risk of failing out or not graduating on time. These kinds of issues can be framed as binary classification problems, where the solution has two possible outcomes: the student either graduated on time or not. The proposed ML algorithms aim to find students who are at risk of not graduating in time by applying the concept of prediction from a set of training data. In this paper, the performance of supervised machine learning algorithms such as SVM, RBF, and Naïve Bayes algorithms is analysed by applying adaptive learning patterns to predict the students who are at risk by dynamically monitoring the progress of students. The proposed machine algorithms aim at identifying the learning gaps and also to create a personalized learning path that every student receives instruction tailored to their individual needs. The experimental investigation revealed that the SVM algorithm in combination with the RBF kernel attained a higher accuracy rate of 96 % compared to other ML models. It can also be analysed from the experiment that the proposed algorithm also improved the learnability of the students by 10 %.

title: Reduction in Motor and Generator Life due to Buildup of Residue in Water Heat-exchangers
authors: Henk De Swardt
abstract: The reliability and efficiency of large electric machines are vital to sustainable energy systems, particularly in wind and hydroelectric power generation. While water cooling offers superior thermal performance over air cooling, the buildup of mineral residue in heat exchangers and water jackets poses a critical risk to machine longevity. This paper investigates the thermodynamic impact of fouling on generator performance using thermal circuit modeling and Arrhenius life-cycle analysis. Results show that even a 0.5 mm layer of residue can elevate internal temperatures enough to reduce insulation life by up to 30%. These findings reveal that conventional maintenance practices overlook hidden degradation mechanisms. The study provides a quantitative foundation for adopting more rigorous, cleaning protocols to preserve the operational lifespan of renewable energy generation assets.

title: Bounomodes: the grazing ox algorithm for exploration of clustered anomalies
authors: Samuel Matloob, Ayan Dutta, O. Patrick Kreidl, Swapnoneel Roy, Ladislau Bölöni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: A common class of algorithms for informative path planning (IPP) follows boustrophedon ("as the ox turns") patterns, which aim to achieve uniform area coverage. However, IPP is often applied in scenarios where anomalies, such as plant diseases, pollution, or hurricane damage, appear in clusters. In such cases, prioritizing the exploration of anomalous regions over uniform coverage is beneficial. This work introduces a class of algorithms referred to as bounomōdes ("as the ox grazes"), which alternates between uniform boustrophedon sampling and targeted exploration of detected anomaly clusters. While uniform sampling can be designed using geometric principles, close exploration of clusters depends on the spatial distribution of anomalies and must be learned. In our implementation, the close exploration behavior is learned using deep reinforcement learning algorithms. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms several established baselines.

title: Cascaded 7 Level Inverter with Built-In Fault Tolerance
authors: Beemidi Karthik Reddy, Azra Zaineb, Yamijala Sai Praneet, Gangadhari Ganesh
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: We proposed a model design of 7 level inverter which can identify and overcome faults itself. The design uses latest techniques to find and isolate faulty side network, thus ensure uninterrupted operation. It helps in analysis of data in real-time and ensure inverter to adapt to rapid changes and faults. Our topology improves system efficiency and reliability. This has high usage in renewable energy, to maintain continues power supply and decrease the downtime. This also provides better power quality and reduced distortion, which made it suitable for grid- integrated systems The advantages of this topology are improving overall THD to 7.07%, reduce running costs, and improving longevity. Fault-tolerant system ensures that the system would be in operational state even in any failure. This makes the system more useful in power systems and various other applications.

title: Polarization Conversion Metasurface for Dynamic Reconfigurable Multi-Beam
authors: Xiaoxuan Guo, Yang Yang
abstract: A dynamically reconfigurable metasurface is proposed to achieve switching between dual-beam and triple-beam through polarization conversion. The proposed metasurface consists of multilayer dielectric unit cells with high transmission amplitude and broad transmission phase range, whose transmission phases under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex> - and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex>-polarizations are relatively independent. By introducing 0° and 90° or 0° and 180° periodic phase profiles for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex> - and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex>-polarizations respectively, the metasurface realizes switching between dual-beam and triple-beam configurations. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed metasurface can achieve high-performance dynamic reconfiguration of multi-beam radiation. The proposed method is also applicable to scenarios with a larger number of beams.

title: BoP-X: A New XAI Approach to Explain Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Driving
authors: Gulsum Alicioglu, Bo Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is commonly used in artificial intelligence (AI) applications. Explaining how a DRL agent learns and makes decisions is crucial in gaining the trust of end users. In this paper, we introduce a new explanation technique, Bag-of-Pattern for eXplanations (BoP-X), supporting lightweight policy summary. The proposed approach adopts a multivariate bag-of-pattern method to extract and summarize the learned behaviors and strategies of the DRL. The BoP-X captures the temporal dynamics of an RL by highlighting the policy strategies and events over time, along with their contribution to the reward. We tested BoP-X in an autonomous driving environment and detected all the learned strategies to show the driving behaviors of the agent. Our approach extends traditional explainable AI (XAI) techniques by providing an exploration-based technique where we can easily present the learned driving behaviors to general public.

title: Dual-Band Anisotropic Metasurface for Polarization-Decoupled Feed Separation in K/Ka Bands
authors: Michael Thompson, Christophe Fumeaux, Amin Abbosh
abstract: A dual-band anisotropic metasurface designed to redirect signals from two separated and orthogonally polarized feed antennas operating at 20 GHz <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(K$</tex>-band) and 30 GHz (Ka-band) is presented. The metasurface employs a multi-layer interleaved anisotropic dipole architecture that minimizes crosscoupling between horizontally and vertically polarized elements, thereby enhancing polarization isolation and reducing undesired interactions across bands. Unlike many dual-band designs that assume co-located feeds, this multi-layer structure enables spatial feed separation while maintaining coherent beam formation. The design demonstrates simultaneous redirection of both feed's radiation, each incident at a mirrored 30° angle, into a unified broadside beam direction. The design also supports beam-splitting functionality, offering a versatile solution for polarization-based feed management in multi-band antenna systems.

title: Data-Driven Control of Continuous-Time LTI Systems via Non-Minimal Realizations
authors: Alessandro Bosso, Marco Borghesi, Andrea Iannelli, Giuseppe Notarstefano, Andrew R. Teel
abstract: This article proposes an approach to design output- feedback controllers for unknown continuous-time linear time-invariant systems using only input-output data from a single experiment. To address the lack of state and derivative measurements, we introduce non-minimal realizations whose states can be observed by filtering the available data. We first apply this concept to the disturbance-free case, formulating linear matrix inequalities (LMIs) from batches of sampled signals to design a dynamic, filter-based stabilizing controller. The framework is then extended to the problem of asymptotic tracking and disturbance rejection—in short, output regulation—by incorporating an internal model based on prior knowledge of the disturbance/reference frequencies. Finally, we discuss tuning strategies for a class of multi-input multi-output systems and illustrate the method via numerical examples.

title: Characterization of Patterned Self-Assembled Electrets for Vibrational Energy Harvesters
authors: Yuya Tanaka, Ryu Kumagai, Ayato Jingu, Suzaku Kimura, Syuuma Kawamura, Yuji Takata, Takaaki Suzuki, Daisuke Yamane
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Electret-based vibrational energy harvesters (E-VEHs) are promising power sources for wireless sensor nodes. However, conventional E-VEHs require a dedicated charging process for the dielectric, which complicates high-throughput manufacturing. Our precious work introduces the use of self-assembled electrets (SAEs), which are polar organic semiconductors exhibiting spontaneous orientation polarization (SOP), and demonstrates an SAE-based VEH that does not require a charging process. For this device, the effect of the surface potential of the patterned SAE (p-SAE) on the output current must be understood; however, the relationship between them remains unclear. In this study, the surface potential and output current of p-SAEs with various structures are precisely evaluated using a Kelvin probe. A linear relationship between the surface potential and output current is observed in the modeled p-SAE VEHs, indicating that the output current can be enhanced merely by increasing the average film thickness of the SAE. Furthermore, we find that the surface potential of p-SAEs originates from the SOP and can be controlled by changing the average film thickness, density, and occupancy rate of the pattern. These findings suggest that p-SAEs are suitable for the mass commercialization of E-VEHs, as they eliminate the need for a dedicated charging process and enable the deposition of SAEs after the movable electrode is fabricated.

title: Masthead
authors: N/A

title: Disturbance-Observer-Based Sliding Mode Controller for Physical Human-Robot Interaction in Multi-Joint Exoskeleton Robots
authors: Mostafa Mogharabi, Iman Kardan, Ali Moradi, Alireza Akbarzadeh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: A robust and stable physical Human-Robot Interaction (pHRI) controller is crucial for robotic systems, particularly for rehabilitation devices that enable continuous human-robot interaction. A key challenge is to achieve a precise interaction torque that enables the robot to provide active assistance or remain transparent to the user. This paper proposes a novel direct force controller, termed as Integral Sliding Mode Controller with a Disturbance Observer Based on the Interaction torque model (ISMCDOBI), for pHRI control of multi-joint exoskeleton robots. A spring-based contact model is explicitly integrated into the robot dynamics to account for human–robot interaction effects and to compensate for human motion. Unlike SEA-based or position/velocity-based methods that indirectly regulate interaction forces, the proposed approach operates directly in the force/torque domain. An integral sliding mode controller is combined with a disturbance observer that employs a nominal interaction model to account for uncertainties and disturbances. Gravity, friction, and human motion compensators are also incorporated to reduce control effort and improve the tracking of the desired interaction torque. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated through realistic MATLAB–OpenSim co-simulation on a coupled human–exoskeleton biomechanical model. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed interaction controller by maintaining interaction torques less than 0.42 N·m and 0.52 N·m in transparent mode for the hip and knee. Also, a one-way ANOVA analysis depicts the superiority of the proposed controller over two common torque control methods in various modes.

title: A Practical On-state Resistance Estimation Scheme of SiC MOSFETs in High-frequency Resonant Converters
authors: Quanjie Wang, Yingzhou Peng, Wei Wang, Xiaoliang Yu, Yi Zhang, Zhikang Shuai
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: The on-state resistance (Rdson) of silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFETs serves as a critical indicator of device degradation and performance. Traditional estimation methods based on Ohm's law face challenges in high-frequency applications due to phase shifts caused by parasitic inductance and sampling delays. To address this problem, this paper proposes a power-loss-based Rdson estimation method for SiC MOSFETs in high-frequency LLC resonant converters. It is immune to the phase shift issue and can obtain the Rdson in real-time. Validated across four typical control modes in an LLC converter, the proposed approach demonstrates <5.0% estimation error, significantly outperforming conventional solutions.

title: Quantum compressed sensing tomographic reconstruction algorithm
authors: Arim Ryou, Kiwoong Kim, Kyungtaek Jun
abstract: Computed tomography (CT) is a non-destructive technique for observing internal images and has proven highly valuable in medical diagnostics. Recent advances in quantum computing have begun to influence tomographic reconstruction techniques. The quantum tomographic reconstruction algorithm is less affected by artifacts or noise than classical algorithms by using the square function of the difference between pixels obtained by projecting CT images in quantum superposition states and pixels obtained from experimental data. In particular, by using quantum linear systems, a fast quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) model formulation for quantum tomographic reconstruction is possible. In this paper, we formulate the QUBO model for quantum compressed sensing tomographic reconstruction, which is a linear combination of the QUBO model for quantum tomographic reconstruction and the QUBO model for total variation in quantum superposition-state CT images. In our experiments, we used sinograms obtained by using the Radon transform of Shepp-Logan images and body CT images. We evaluate the performance of the new algorithm by reconstructing CT images using a hybrid solver with the QUBO model computed from each sinogram. The new algorithm was able to obtain a solution within 5 projection images for 30 × 30 image samples and within 6 projection images for 60 × 60 image samples, reconstructing error-free CT images. We anticipate that quantum compressed sensing tomographic reconstruction algorithms could significantly reduce the total radiation dose when quantum computing performance advances.

title: Dynamic Key Management in Blockchain using AI
authors: Nirav Changela, Shrey Mehta, Kashish Golwala, Keval Dhabalia, Naina Parmar
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Dynamic key management plays vital role to enhancing both the security and the scalability of blockchain systems. Common approaches to key management are often very static, and thus become easy targets for attacks while being inflexible in the face of evolving threats. Such an approach introduces massive security vulnerabilities particularly in decentralized environments where cryptographic keys authenticate transactions and guarantee data integrity. A dynamic key management framework is introduced and further decomposed into four distinct phases namely as, Key Generation Phase, which describes the generation of symmetric keys, Key Verification Phase, incorporating DEC+GMM and VAE models to verify any produced symmetric keys have the required strength and to detect anomalies, Key Storing and Distribution Phase, utilizing AES encryption and on-chain storage with Ethereum smart contract, and finally, Key Revocation and New Key Generation Phase, which automatically revokes expired or compromised keys and enables new keys to be generated. Artificial intelligence AI can tie into blockchain systems to help address the issue with more secure, adaptive key management solutions. This provides better security by reducing the risk of key theft or compromise, and better resource efficiency by adapting to dynamic network conditions. When AI is integrated with blockchain, the whole system is more proactive and reactive with smarter, more robust protection of transactions and sensitive information. This is a forward-thinking, dynamic approach to key management that meets the key vulnerabilities faced in the increasing demand for secure, scalable blockchain applications across all industries, thus unlocking the potential for safer decentralized networks.

title: The Pandemic Paradigm Shift: Analyzing Pre-and Post-COVID Effects on Life Insurance
authors: Amrin Samar Sultana, Aishwarya V. Kamble, Rekha R Nair
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Interdisciplinary Approaches in Technology and Management for Social Innovation (IATMSI)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic is the core research issue of the present study, and the major shifts occurring in the life insurance industry are explored before and after March 2020. It examines changes in new consumer perspectives, underwriting and economic positions, products and service portfolios, and insurers' financial status. Before the pandemic, the industry growing with precise risk assessment parameters in place. The sudden outburst early this year led to several impacts such as; a higher mortality rate, economic fluctuations in investments, and the advancement of digitalization throughout the insurance value chain. Specific mortal risks were more visible to the consumers due to the pandemic if this is a growth driver for the life insurance segment. Thus, it also raised price-sensitive levels as a result of economic volatility. This has led to the change of underwriting and pricing models, and the repositioning of products to address issues of long-term morbidity and mortality, in future pandemics. Information major of the work also outlines a growing trend of products that blur the lines between life and health insurance; instead of calling them life or health insurance products, several companies are labeling their products as life and wellness or health. Drawing data from India Stats, this research applies regression analysis to analyze demographic factors affecting insurance in the backdrop of the COVID-19 pandemic. In light of this, the study establishes that insurance markets for the employed population are largely in urban centers while in rural areas expansion is mostly by government initiative. Thus, this research identifies the following prospects as the key to the industry's evolution: the creation of new, customer-centered solutions, increasing financial awareness, and the integration of innovative technologies and behavioral changes.

title: Monte Carlo-Type Neural Operator for One-Dimensional Differential Equations
authors: Salah Eddine Choutri, Prajwal Chauhan, Othmane Mazhar, Saif Eddin Jabari
publish: 2025 International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: The Monte Carlo-type Neural Operator (MCNO) introduces a framework for learning solution operators of one-dimensional partial differential equations (PDEs) by directly learning the kernel function and approximating the associated integral operator using a Monte Carlo-type approach. Unlike Fourier Neural Operators (FNOs), which rely on spectral representations and assume translation-invariant kernels, MCNO makes no such assumptions. The kernel is represented as a learnable tensor acting on the latent features at sampled points, with sampling performed once uniformly at random from the discretized grid. This design enables generalization across multiple grid resolutions without relying on fixed global basis functions or repeated sampling during training, while an interpolation step maps between arbitrary input and output grids to further enhance flexibility. Experiments on standard 1D PDE benchmarks show that MCNO achieves competitive accuracy with efficient computational cost. We also provide a theoretical analysis proving that the Monte Carlo estimator yields a bounded bias and variance under mild regularity assumptions. This result holds in any spatial dimension, suggesting that MCNO may extend naturally beyond one-dimensional problems. More broadly, this work explores how Monte Carlo-type integration can be incorporated into neural operator frameworks for continuous-domain PDEs, providing a theoretically supported alternative to spectral methods (such as FNO) and to graph-based Monte Carlo approaches (such as the Graph Kernel Neural Operator, GNO).

title: Enhancing Additive Manufacturing Security: A Novel Approach using Zero Trust, Blockchain, and AI-Powered Watermarking
authors: Mary Shreya, R. Chitra, D. Brindha
publish: 2025 6th International Conference on Smart Electronics and Communication (ICOSEC)
abstract: Additive Manufacturing (AM), commonly referred to as 3D printing, is a groundbreaking technology revolutionizing industrial manufacturing by enabling instant prototyping, on-demand production, and intricate geometries. The increasing interconnectedness and application of digital instructions in AM systems, however, make them vulnerable to a myriad of cybersecurity attacks such as unauthorized access, data tampering, intellectual property (IP) theft, and sabotage. The existing security measures are unable to address these issues in an all-encompassing manner. This paper presents an innovative, multi-layered cybersecurity model for improving the security of the AM process end to end. The new approach employs Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) for strict user authentication and access control, AI-driven G-code watermarking through deep-learning-based steganography for ensuring data integrity and protection against malicious tampering, Federated Learning (FL) to enable threat intelligence sharing across decentralized systems without compromising data privacy, and Blockchain technology for tamper-evident storage and enhanced authentication. Integrating these technologies, the proposed system offers a robust defense mechanism against advanced cyber-attacks for AM systems while ensuring security without compromising scalability, efficiency, or performance. This paper presents the proposed system's architecture and demonstrates its effectiveness through a simulated deployment.

title: VMD and Gramian Angular Field Empowered CNN for Drone RF Signal Recognition
authors: Yuanhua Fu, Chunjin Zhang, Zhiming He
abstract: The explosive growth of drones raises significant safety concerns due to their illegal use. Radio frequency (RF) based drone detection and recognition plays a key role for the drone surveillance and control. This letter proposes a novel drone RF signal recognition method with high performance. First, a variational mode decomposition (VMD) method is introduced for the raw RF signal denoising. Specifically, the raw RF signals after VMD and then the effective intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) are selected and summed as the denoised sample. Then, in order to enhance the differentiation between samples, Gramian angular field (GAF) is used to transform the 1D signals into 2D images, which preserve the temporal correlations between each data point of the 1D signals. Finally, we construct a convolutional neural network (CNN) to learn image features and identify drone types. Experimental results on an open-source CardRF dataset show that the proposed method can achieve over 90.6% recognition accuracy with SNRs greater than 0 dB.
